Summary: The problem-based workflow might error when using a Global Optimization Toolbox solver with UseParallel=true
Description: When using the problem-based workflow and selecting one of the Global Optimization solvers ga , surrogateopt , or patternsearch , with UseParallel=true , the solve  function might error. For example: x = optimvar('x',2, 'LowerBound', -5, 'UpperBound', 10);prob = optimproblem; prob.Objective = 4; prob.Constraints.con1 = x(2) <= 1;prob.Constraints.con2 =  1 <= x(1) + x(2);gaOptions = optimoptions('ga', 'UseParallel', true);x0.x = [-2 3];[xga, fga, efga, outga] = solve(prob, x0, 'solver', 'ga', 'options', gaOptions); The returned error can appear as follows:? Error using fcnvectorizerFunction to evaluate must be represented as a string scalar, character vector, or function_handle object.Error in makeState (line 69)        Score = fcnvectorizer(state.Population(initScoreProvided+2:end,:),FitnessFcn,1,...Error in galincon (line 24)state = makeState(GenomeLength,FitnessFcn,Iterate,output.problemtype,options);Error in ga (line 420)            [x,fval,exitFlag,output,population,scores] = galincon(FitnessFcn,nvars, ...Error in optim.problemdef.OptimizationProblem/callSolverError in optim.internal.problemdef.ProblemImpl/solveImplError in optim.problemdef.OptimizationProblem/solveCaused by:    Failure in user-supplied fitness function evaluation. GA cannot continue.
Workaround: As a workaround, you can use the prob2struct function to generate the function files for the objective and constraint in the current folder, and then call the solver directly on the output structure.For example:probStruct = prob2struct(prob, x0, 'solver', 'ga');probStruct.options = gaOptions;[xga, fga, efga, outga] = ga(probStruct);
Fixed In: R2021b Update 7, R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 7, R2023a Update 6, R2023b

Summary: Schema-based file headers written by the cdfx function are not compliant with the ASAM standard
Description: The header section of a CDFX-file produced by the cdfx  funtion contains the following statement: xsi:schemaLocation="http://www.asam.net/schema/CDF/r2.1 cdf21.xsd" This line is not compliant with the ASAM standard. The correct statement is: xsi:schemaLocation="http://www.asam.net/schema/CDF/r2.1 cdf_v2.1.0.sl.xsd"
Fixed In: R2021b Update 7, R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 6, R2023a Update 4, R2023b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Calls to enumeration?function with string input
Description: For MATLAB code containing calls to the enumeration  function with a string input, the generated code returns an empty matrix instead of the contents of the enumeration. This bug does not affect calls to the enumeration  function? using character vector input.  For example, consider this MATLAB code: classdef LightColors < uint8    enumeration        red(1)        yellow(2)        green(3)    endendfunction out = getColors()out = enumeration("LightColors");end? In MATLAB execution, calls to getColors  return the contents of the enumeration. If you generate a MEX for getColors , calls to getColors_mex  incorrectly return an empty matrix.? ?This issue affects MATLAB Coder, MATLAB Function blocks or MATLAB System blocks in Simulink, and MATLAB functions in Stateflow.
Workaround: In your MATLAB code, replace string input to the?enumeration?function with character vector input.For example, in the code in the bug description, replace double-quoted input to the?enumeration?function ("LightColors") with single-quoted input ('LightColors').?
Fixed In: R2021b Update 7, R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 6, R2023a Update 4, R2023b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Array indexing might be incorrect for inplace MATLAB functions that accept variable-size arrays as inputs
Description: Incorrect code might generate from a MATLAB function when all of the following conditions are true: The MATLAB function is an inplace function (that is, uses the same input and output variable) whose input is a variable-size array. Inside the MATLAB function, an inplace operation changes the size of the input/output variable. The code in the MATLAB function performs some inplace operations on the input/output variable. You generate code that uses the row-major array layout. You generate code that uses N-dimensional indexing by setting the?PreserveArrayDimensions ?property to  true .  In R2021b or later,  EnableImplicitExpansion  is turned off.  Consider this MATLAB function that uses the same input and output variable x  and accepts a variable-size array as an input. The size of x ?is changed inside of the function through an inplace operation. function x = foo(x)? ? ? ? % In-place function with variable-sized input/output x = x(1,4:1:5);? ? ? ? ? ? % The size of x is changed  x = x + (x + ones(1,2));? ?% Inplace operation  end   When you configure the code generator to use row-major array layout and N-dimensional indexing,?you may see these code lines in the generated code: x_data[0] += x_data[0] + 1.0;  x_data[1] = x_data[0] + (x_data[0] + 1.0);   The generated code calculates the value assigned to? x_data[1] ?using?x_data[0] ?incorrectly.
Fixed In: R2021b Update 7, R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 8, R2023a Update 6, R2023b

Description: Incorrect code generated for the?Weighted Sample Time?and?Weighted Sample Time Math?blocks under these conditions: Operation  parameter is set to +  or - .? Input signal is a  large number. Input signal type is set to either double  or single . Output data type  is set to a fixed-point data?type.  For example, if a Weighted Sample Time?or Weighted Sample Time Math block has these settings:Operation ?parameter is set to?+ .   Input signal value is set to  1e+20 .  Input signal data type is set to?single. Output datatype is set to fixdt(1,32,12) .   The output in the generated code does not match that of the normal simulation output.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generated for A(idx,:) = []?does not remove the intended elements if?A is N-D array (N>2) and idx is logical column vector or matrix
Description: The generated code does not remove the intended elements from the array?A ?if all  ?these conditions are true:? Your MATLAB code contains patterns like A(idx,:) = [] ?or ?A(idx,:,:) = [] .  A  is an array of dimension greater than or equal to 3. idx  is a logical array. idx  is either a column vector or a matrix. (This bug does not occur if idx  is a row vector.)  This issue affects MATLAB Coder, MATLAB Function blocks or MATLAB System blocks in Simulink, and MATLAB functions in Stateflow.
Workaround: Convert the logical array idx?to a row vector.For example, replace the code line?A(idx,:) = []?with the following two lines:?idx2 = reshape(idx,1,[]);A(idx2,:) = [];

Summary: When exporting a Simulink model as an FMU using the Standalone FMU Export feature,?the resulting modelDescription.xml file contains incorrect values for the min/max attributes of the bus element when the model includes a bus element with min/max values
Description: When a Simulink model uses a bus structure and includes a bus element with min/max values, the FMU's exported modelDescription.xml file incorrectly writes the /VendorAnnotations/Tool/Simulink/SimulinkModelInterface/BusObject/BusElement/min  or max? attributes as random symbols, which might lead to issues when importing the FMU in Simulink.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Simulink parameter with non-Auto storage class might be incorrectly overwritten in initialize function when used to specify constant value of bus element signal connected to the outport of a conditionally-executed subsystem.
Description: The code generator might dynamically initialize a statically-initialized Simulink parameter when?all ?of the following are true: A Simulink.Parameter  is used as a constant value for a constant block. This Constant block?is connected as an input port of a Bus Creator block. The output port of the Bus Creator block is driving an Outport block of a conditionally-executed subsystem such as Enabled Subsystem, Triggered Subsystem, or Function-Call Subsystem. The parameter Source of initial output value  of the Outport block is set to 'Input signal' .  When the above conditions are met, Simulink might generate code similar to the following. /* Exported block parameters */  uint8_T myParam = 15U;? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ?/* Variable: myParam?  /* Model initialize function */  void simplify1_initialize(void)  {   /* SystemInitialize for Outport: '<S1>/Raw_Bytes' incorporates:   ?*? Constant: '<S2>/Const_dummy_Byte_8'   */   ?myParam = simplify1_ConstP.Raw_Bytes_Y0.Raw_Byte_8;?    }?   Note that the Simulink.Parameter  myParam   is statically initialized with value  15U , but it gets dynamically initialized? incorrectly?to some other value?inside the model initialize function.
Fixed In: R2021b Update 7, R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 6, R2023a Update 4, R2023b

Summary: MATLAB fails to authenticate SSH connections using RSA keys to remote machines running OpenSSH server 8.8 onwards
Description: When you authenticate an SSH connection to a remote machine with an identity file in the RSA format, MATLAB Parallel Server fails to establish the connection and produces an error like the following: Identity file not accepted by remote host?'machine32.mycompany.com'. Check the identity file is valid and has correct permissions.  Error from library: Username/PublicKey combination invalid  This error occurs when authenticating an SSH connection to a machine running OpenSSH server version 8.8 or later. The RSA private key might work when you authenticate using OpenSSH client on the command line. A private key is in RSA format if it begins with the following line: -----BEGIN RSA PRIVATE KEY -----
Workaround: Do one of the following:Use a private key in a format other than RSA, such as Ed25519.Use a password to authenticate.
Fixed In: R2021b Update 7, R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 6, R2023a Update 4, R2023b

Summary: SS_OPTION_PLACE_ASAP?option for an S-Function block is not honored when another such S-Function block is placed inside a virtual subsystem at the same level of model hierarchy
Description: When you specify the SS_OPTION_PLACE_ASAP  option for an S-Function block, the block is supposed to be placed before other blocks in the sorted list. However, if you specify the same option for another S-Function block and place it inside a virtual subsystem at the same level of model hierarchy as the S-Function block outside the subsystem, the SS_OPTION_PLACE_ASAP  option for the S-Function block outside the subsystem is not honored.?
Fixed In: R2021b Update 7, R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 6, R2023a Update 3, R2023b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: reshape function with dlarray input
Description: Code generated for the?reshape ?function that is passed a?dlarray ?input might return incorrect answer if?all ?these conditions are true: The size argument of reshape  is a row vector. The number of elements of this row vector is equal to the number of dimensions of the input.? One or more elements of this row vector?are identical to the sizes of certain dimensions of the dlarray  input.  For example, this code might produce incorrect answer in code generation: dlx = dlarray(rand(3,4,5), 'SCB'); y = reshape(dlx, [1 3 20]);
Workaround: Call the extractdata function on the input before calling the reshape function.dlx = dlarray(rand(3,4,5), 'SCB'); y = dlarray(reshape(extractdata(dlx), [1 3 20])); Alternatively, pass the size arguments as a comma separated list to the?reshape?function.dlx = dlarray(rand(3,4,5), 'SCB'); y = reshape(dlx, 1, 3, 20);
Fixed In: R2021b Update 7, R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 6, R2023a Update 2, R2023b

Description: SimBiology does not support forward compatibility of its project format. The apps might try to load projects that were saved in later versions without displaying any warnings or errors, but the integrity of the project cannot be guaranteed and the project data structures might become inconsistent with any particular version.? You should not attempt to load projects saved in later versions of SimBiology Model Builder  and SimBiology Model Analyzer .
Workaround: You can save and export models to MAT-files and use them in any release from R2019b and later.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results might occur for a model that contains a referenced model with a feedback loop
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets all of these conditions: The model contains a referenced model. The top model contains a feedback loop where the output of the referenced model is directly fed into its input signal. The referenced model contains a function-call subsystem whose execution is controlled by a?Trigger port. The input port inside the referenced model is configured to latch the input value and prevent it from changing during the execution of the function-call subsystem by selecting the inport block parameter?Latch input for feedback signals of function-call subsystem outputs .?  If you generate code from a model that meets the above conditions, the code generator might eliminate temporary variables incorrectly. Incorrect elimination of local variables occurs when a function in the generated code has pointer arguments, and the caller of the function passes a memory address more than once as the pointer arguments. For example, if a top model calls a function defined in the referenced model as?botModel(&a, &b, &b) , where the memory address?&b ?is passed to the function?botModel ?twice, the generated code might be incorrect.
Fixed In: R2021b Update 7, R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 6, R2023a Update 5, R2023b

Summary: Passing global variables of unnamed struct or enum type to custom code functions my produce incorrect error in C Function block.
Description: Your simulation may produce an incorrect mismatch error if the following is true: Your C Function block has a custom code function call that accepts global variables of unnamed struct  or enum  type  For example, consider the following custom code header file: // foo.h   struct myStruct {? ? ? ? int p;  };  extern struct myStruct gVar;  int myFcn(const struct myStruct *in);  Inside the C Function block,? y = myFcn(&gVar);  simulating the model produces the following incorrect error: Passing argument 1 of external C function 'myFcn' in the C Function block 'myModel/C Function' from incompatible pointer type. Ensure the pointer argument is compatible with the external C function's parameter type. Occurred in line:  y = myFcn(&gVar);
Workaround: If possible, name the structs and enums in the custom code.

Summary: Incorrect execution of timed events in Stateflow charts that are inside function-call subsystems with multiple initiators having different sample times

Workaround: Set the same sample time on all initiators of the parent function-call subsystem.

Summary: Incorrect results for a model containing an Observer Reference when both the models have?floor or ceil rounding operations

Summary: Generating PDF report containing nonprinting control characters in document heading or caption crashes MATLAB
Description: If you generate a PDF report that contains nonprinting control characters in a document heading, the following error occurs and MATLAB crashes. Unsupported control characters include characters with ASCII codes between 1 and 31, except horizontal tab (ASCII code 9), line feed (ASCII code 10), and carriage return (ASCII code 13). Error using mlreportgen.internal.fop.FOPObject/executeCannot convert "C:/workspace/root.fo" to "C:/workspace/test.pdf". Reason: Invalid Unicode character: U+001B? ? If the nonprinting character appears in a table of contents or list of captioned report elements, such as figure captions or table titles, MATLAB crashes.? For example, the following code adds the escape character to a document. MATLAB crashes when the document is closed. d = mlreportgen.dom.Document("output");append(d,mlreportgen.dom.TOC);append(d,mlreportgen.dom.Heading1(char(27))); close(d);
Workaround: Remove unsupported characters from the input string before adding the string to a DOM component or document. For example,inputChar = ['line feed: ',char(10),'escape character: ',char(27)];supportedChars = [9, 10, 13]; % Horizontal tab, line feed, and carriage return are supportedtoRemove = ~ismember(inputChar,supportedChars) & (inputChar < 32);inputChar(toRemove) = []
Fixed In: R2021b Update 7, R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 6, R2023a Update 3, R2023b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: duration operator returns incorrect results in some atomic subcharts

Summary: Performance issues for Simulink models with many instances of models referenced in normal mode
Description: Simulating, generating code, or updating the diagram might have poor performance for model hierarchies that have many Model blocks that simulate in normal mode and reference the same model. The behavior depends on the size of the referenced models and the number of Model blocks that reference them. When a model hierarchy has hundreds or thousands of instances of the same referenced model, and the instances simulate in normal mode, MATLAB might hang or crash.
Workaround: For most of the Model blocks, set the?Simulation mode?block parameter to Accelerator. Alternatively, in the Simulink Toolstrip, change the simulation mode of the top model from Normal to Accelerator. When a model simulates in accelerator mode, its referenced models also simulate in accelerator mode.In R2021a Update 8, you can enable an optimization manually that improves the performance of models with many instances of referenced models that simulate in normal mode. Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Size of variable-size struct field incorrectly modified prior to generated code that reads and writes the same data
Description: MATLAB Coder might generate incorrect code when all ?of these are true: An assignment statement completely writes a variable-size struct field The right-hand side of the assignment statement contains an access of a single element of the same variable-size struct field using a subscript  MATLAB Coder might incorrectly generate code for the statement. The generated code modifies the size of the variable-size struct field prior to the code that reads the variable-size data. This causes incorrect behavior when the read of the variable-size struct field also uses the modified size to compute a subscript. The generated code might read uninitialized memory or incorrect numeric results. The attached example ReproductionModel.zip  demonstrates this issue. This code snippet from the example shows the coding pattern described above: s.f1 = s.f1(2,2) - temp;   See this corresponding generated code: i = s->f1->size[0] * s->f1->size[1];  s->f1->size[0] = temp->size[0];  s->f1->size[1] = 1;  emxEnsureCapacity_real_T(sp, s->f1, i, &d_emlrtRTEI);  b_s = s->f1->data[s->f1->size[0] + 1];  for (i = 0; i <= loop_ub; i++) {  ? s->f1->data[i] = b_s - temp_data[i];  }   The expression? s->f1->data[s->f1->size[0] + 1] ?in the generated code?corresponds to the MATLAB expression? s.f1(2,2) . Note that the size of the struct field  s->f1  has been incorrectly modified prior to this expression. The generated code should update the size of  s->f1 ?after that expression.
Fixed In: R2021b Update 7, R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 6, R2023a Update 2, R2023b

Summary: Variants are applied in incorrect order in Group Simulation or Fit Data programs
Description: The SimBiology Model Analyzer  app sometimes applies variants in the incorrect order when you run the Group Simulation  or Fit Data  programs. The incorrect order might be used when you run the program after making a change to the variants, such as unchecking the box to use a baseline variant. The order of variants might also differ when running the program versus viewing the program code. If you have fewer than ten variant columns in the variant and dose setup table, then variants are in the correct order when you view the program code even if they are not applied in the correct order when running the program. If you have ten or more variant columns, then the variants will never be in the correct order when you view the program code. Note that the order of variants matters only if multiple variants modify the same quantity. In such cases, the last value is taken from the last variant to modify the component.
Workaround: To avoid this issue, update the variants to remove any overlapping content. Then, the results do not depend on the order in which variants are applied.If it is not practical to remove overlapping content, you can ensure correct results when running?a program by saving, reloading, and then rerunning a program after making any changes to the configuration of variants in that program. Reloading the project avoids the issue by updating the variant information stored in the SimBiology Model Analyzer app. However, if you have more than ten variant columns in the variant and dose setup table, variants are still applied in the incorrect order when viewing the program code.
Fixed In: R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 4, R2023a

Summary: datetime time zones do not reflect recent changes to the IANA time zone database, including time zones in Mexico
Description: MATLAB uses the IANA time zones database for up-to-date time zone information. MATLAB is missing recent updates to this database. As of the R2022b general release, MATLAB includes database version 2022a. However, in R2022b MATLAB does not include the 2022f version of the IANA database, which also captures time zone updates from database versions 2022b, 2022c, 2022d, and 2022e. Most notably, these updates include changes to a number of time zones which no longer observe daylight saving time (DST). For example, in October 2022 the government of Mexico announced that the country no longer observes DST except near the US border.?The time zone data in MATLAB does not reflect this recent change. IANA Time Zone Database:?https://www.iana.org/time-zones  In versions of MATLAB that do not have these changes, datetime  arrays in what used to be the DST period will have incorrect UTC offsets. Specifically, they will be one hour ahead of the actual times in those time zones.
Workaround: If possible, update to a release with an IANA time zone database version of 2022f or later.To check the IANA time zone database version in MATLAB, use the second output of the timezones function. When the database is up to date, the second output is '2022f'.>> [~,vers] = timezones vers =    '2022f'https://www.mathworks.com/help/matlab/ref/timezones.htmlIf you are unable to update to a newer release, you can obtain the correct time zone information for datetime arrays on or after October 30th 2022 (where the desired time zone is in Mexico) by using?the corresponding fixed time zone offset. For example, replace the time zone 'America/Chihuahua'?with the time zone 'UTC-6' on or after?October 30th, 2022.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 4, R2023a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Root outports that are directly driven by a bus signal with ExportedGlobal storage class might generate incorrect initialization
Description: The code generator might generate an incorrect initialization when?all ?of the following are true: The model contains a root outport that is driven directly by a bus signal. That bus signal has?ExportedGlobal ?storage class.  The code generator might assign an uninitialized value to the root outport in the model initialization function. For example, consider this generated code:? / * Model initialize function */  void simple_initialize(void)  {  / * Registration code */    / * block I/ O */    / * exported global signals */  {  myBus temp = {   0. 0F    };     myOut = temp;   }    {  real32_T rtb_speed;     / * SystemInitialize for BusCreator: '<S1>/ Auto Bus Creator v2' */   myOut. speed = rtb_speed;   }  }  ?The generated code initializes the bus signal?myOut.speed ?with the local uninitialized variable?rtb_speed .

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated code that uses deep learning libraries might produce incorrect answers if 1x1?convolution2dLayer?post-dominates?maxPooling2dLayer
Description: Generated code that uses MKL-DNN, ARM Compute or cuDNN library might produce incorrect answers if your deep learning network satisfies all  these conditions: The network contains a  maxPooling2dLayer ?followed by a  convolution2dLayer .  All paths in the layer graph that start at the  maxPooling2dLayer ?go through the  convolution2dLayer ?(that is, the? convolution2dLayer ?post-dominates ?the?maxPooling2dLayer ). The  convolution2dLayer ?has  FilterSize  equal to  [1 1], ?and ?has equal strides that are greater than  1 , and ?has no spatial padding .  The  maxPooling2dLayer ?has  FilterSize  greater than  1 , or ?has strides greater than  1 , or  has spatial padding.  The only layers between the? maxPooling2dLayer ?and  convolution2dLayer ?are  reluLayer, additionLayer, clippedReluLayer, leakyReluLayer.   This is an example network layer graph that might produce incorrect answers in code generation:? ?lg = layerGraph([imageInputLayer([5 5 3], Normalization = 'none', Name = 'input')?  ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? maxPooling2dLayer([2 3], Name = 'maxpool')?  ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? reluLayer(Name = 'relu')?  ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? convolution2dLayer(1,3,Stride = [2 2], Name = 'conv')]);?  This issue affects code generated by using any  of these products: MATLAB Coder  Simulink Coder  GPU Coder when using  TargetLibrary='cudnn'?
Workaround: For code generation targeting Intel, ARM, or AMD based CPUs, use?TargetLibrary='none'.For code generation targeting NVIDIA GPUs, use TargetLibrary='tensorrt'.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 4, R2023a

Summary: MATLAB Function block might fail to compile after adding breakpoints

Summary: Unexpected license checkout when using "Other uses of functionName" links in help text
Description: After calling help on a function that is overloaded in several classes, clicking on the "Other functions named functionName  " link at the bottom of the help text can cause licenses to be checked out.
Workaround: Installation instructions  Log in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.  Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps. On Mac systems, the folder name will have a .app extension, e.g. /Applications/MATLAB_R20XXa.app  Exit MATLAB.  Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.Windows Systems: You may need administrative privileges to save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder. To do this, open this Bug Report in a browser started in administrator mode, then from the browser save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.Mac Systems: If you are using Safari on a Mac, disable automatic unzipping of the downloaded file by unchecking Open Safe Files After Downloading in Preferences > General.   For each file that appears in the .zip file, find the corresponding file in your existing <MATLAB> folder and make a backup of it. To see the contents of the .zip file:          Windows Systems: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.      Linux/Mac Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Mac):        unzip -l <MATLAB>/<.zip file>On a Mac, include the .app extension in the folder name.         Extract the files from the .zip file into the <MATLAB> folder.          Windows Systems:                  Open the .zip file.          Extract the contents of the file into your <MATLAB> folder. You may be prompted to provide Administrator privileges when extracting the .zip file.                    Linux/Mac Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Mac):         cd <MATLAB>        unzip <.zip file>On a Mac, include the .app extension in the folder name.        Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 3, R2023a

Summary: CAN blocks drop floating-point digits in DBC file
Description: The CAN Pack, CAN Unpack, CAN FD Pack, and CAN FD Unpack blocks can be configured to use CANdb specified signals, in which case numerical parameters are read from a DBC file to perform the conversion from messages to signals. These rounded values are used in simulation and in any generated code.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Constant-folding expressions that accept nonvirtual buses as inputs might generate incorrect results
Description: The code generator might generate incorrect results when all  of the following are true: ?You generate code for a model that contains a MATLAB Function block. The code in the MATLAB Function block has an expression that accepts a nonvirtual bus containing a matrix as an input. The code uses the matrix in the nonvirtual bus input to initialize a matrix variable. A value is assigned to an element of the initialized?matrix variable. The code uses the coder.const  directive to constant-fold the expression.   Constant-folding the expression in the generated code might produce incorrect results. For example, consider the function  loca l. The function is called by passing the input  u, ? which is a nonvirtual bus that contains a matrix of complex numbers in the field f . function y = fcn(u)  ? ? u.f = repmat(0+2i,2,1);  ? ? t=coder.const(local(u));  ? ? y=t.f;  end  function ovar = local(ivar)  ? ? ovar.f = ivar.f;  ? ? temp = ivar.f;  ? ? temp(1) = 5+5i;  end  The function local ? initializes the variable temp  using the input and assigns the value 5+5i  to a matrix element. The generated code constant-folds the expression local(u) ,?which ?produces the incorrect result?[0+2i,5+5i], ?where t he correct result is [0+2i,0+2i] .
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 4, R2023a

Either a Sine Wave block or a Uniform Random Number block is inside the For Each subsystem. At least one of the block parameters of the Sine Wave block or Uniform Random Number block references the partitioned For Each subsystem mask parameters.

Description: MATLAB crashes if you change the Scope  property of a port on a Stateflow block from the Data tab of the Property Inspector . Summary: Wrong initial values in simulation results for export-function model with bus element port and Mux block at the root level

The model contains an In Bus Element or Out Bus Element block at the root level. The model contains a Mux block at the root level. The Data Import/Export > Format ?configuration parameter is set to Array , Structure , or Structure with time . Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect behavior when periodic function-call subsystem uses invalid periodic sample time
Description: Updating a model produces an incorrect error when all of these conditions are true: A periodic function-call subsystem has a Trigger block with Sample time type  set to periodic . In this periodic function-call subsystem, a block explicitly specifies a different periodic sample time. This periodic function-call subsystem is one of multiple subsystems in the block diagram.  The incorrect error is similar to this: The enabled subsystem 'subsystem-name' executes in multiple tasks.  This can cause corrupted data or non-deterministic behavior in a real-time system.  Building a model that produces this incorrect error might generate incorrect code.

Summary: Simulation results might be incorrect for export-function model with State Writer block that writes to Outport block
Description: Simulating an export-function model produces incorrect results when all these conditions apply: The?Data Import/Export > Format ?configuration parameter is set to?Dataset . The model contains two root Inport blocks that output function-call signals. Each function-call signal connects to a function-call port of a function-call subsystem. The first function-call subsystem contains a State Writer block. The State Writer block writes to an Outport block in the second function-call subsystem. The corresponding output port directly drives a root Outport block.  The output of the root Outport block is logged each time either function-call subsystem executes. When the first function-call subsystem executes, the output of the root Outport block is logged incorrectly. When the second function-call subsystem executes, the output of the root Outport block is logged correctly.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 2, R2023a

For example, inside a choice of a variant subsystem, with the FunctionPackaging  set to Inline . This variant region is triggered by a function call output from a reusable Stateflow chart. Summary: SimBiology projects lose data and become corrupted when apps are left open overnight
Description: If you leave a SimBiology project open overnight in the SimBiology Model Builder  app or the SimBiology Model Analyzer  app, the project might lose data and become corrupted. This data loss can lead to errors when saving or loading the project or when trying to load data or results from the project. This corruption happens because the operating system sometimes deletes temporary files needed by the SimBiology project.
Workaround: You can prevent the operating system from deleting SimBiology temporary files by changing the MATLAB temporary folder. Set the appropriate environment variable to a different location, such as your documents folder. On Windows, set the environment variable TMP. On macOS or Linux, set the environment variable TMPDIR.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 1, R2023a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: The Identity Matrix block might produce incorrect numerical results during simulation for fixed-point data types with non-zero bias
Description: A model might produce incorrect numeric results when all of the following conditions are true: Model contains an Identity Matrix block. The Output Data Type  parameter of the Identity Matrix block is set to Inherit: Inherit via back propagation . The Identity Matrix block output is connected to a Data Type Conversion block. The Output Data Type  parameter of the Data Type Conversion block is set to a fixed-point data type with a non-zero bias which can represent the value zero.  Under these conditions, the output of the identity matrix block might contain the bias value on the non-diagonal elements instead of zero.
Workaround: Set the?Output data type parameter of the Identity Matrix block to double.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Variant control names in the mask/model workspace for a Variant Subsystem block duplicated in base workspace for Variant Source/Sink? will cause wrong results.
Description: The Variant Subsystem will not pick the right active choice in a Simulink model if:  A Variant Subsystem uses the variant control from the mask/model workspace while another Variant Source/Sink uses the same variant control variable defined in the base workspace.

Summary: SIL and PIL simulations might throw error message Found multiple matching Parameters
Description: Running a SIL or PIL simulation might throw the error message Found multiple matching Parameters ?when all ?of the following conditions are met:  You run a SIL or PIL simulation on a component that contains Prelookup blocks. The Prelookup blocks share Simulink.Breakpoint ?data between multiple lookup tables.

Summary: Generated code for interp1 with interpolation method set to?'pchip' or 'makima'?might run slower than MATLAB
Description: Suppose that: You generate code for the function interp1  with interpolation method set to 'pchip'  or 'makima'  During code generation, you disable the configuration parameter Check memory integrity ?(IntegrityChecks )   In such situations, the generated code might run slower when compared to MATLAB. The performance issue affects both MEX and standalone code generation.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 4, R2023a

Summary: Compatibility checker incorrectly reports the usage of enum with prefix "str" as incompatible
Description: The Simulink Code Inspector compatibility checker incorrectly reports a warning when a model uses an?enum  type variable whose name starts with the prefix "str" . For example, if a model uses a workspace variable named "Str apdownEqICBusOb j", the compatibility checker incorrectly reports the variable as incompatible. However, the variable passes code inspection.

Summary: Error when running parsim on a cluster that uses a third-party job scheduler
Description: When you run parsim  on a third-party scheduler cluster such as a Slurm cluster, Simulink is unable to find the project on the parallel workers. The simulation fails with the following error message:? Error loading project on parallel workers.  ? ? Error using parallel.pool/getPerHostFolder  ? ? The folder project was not found.
Workaround: Modify the communicatingJobWrapper.sh wrapper script found in the PluginScriptsLocation property of the cluster profile. Add the following line immediately before the call to mpiexec:unset HOST HOSTNAME
Fixed In: R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 4, R2023a

Summary: Increase in build times for Simulink Real-Time models that reference other models with a large number of blocks
Description: For a Simulink Real-Time model that references models containing a large number of blocks with tunable parameters, you might observe that the R2021b build time for the model is greater than the R2021a build time. You can use slbuild  to build the model heirarchy. If you use the MATLAB profiler to profile the R2021b build process, the MATLAB profiler indicates that the?updateCodeDescriptor  and locUpdateModelCodeDescriptor ?functions take a long time to execute.
Workaround: For all models in the model hierarchy, set the Default parameter behavior?configuration parameter to Inlined.
Fixed In: R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 5, R2022b, R2023a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Charts using MATLAB as the action language and configured to generate coverage report may see the duration operator return an incorrect result
Description: Simulation results might be incorrect for a Stateflow chart that: uses MATLAB as the action language is configured to generate a coverage report has a state that uses the?duration? operator to count elapsed time for some condition  the elapsed time returned by the?duration? operator for that condition may be incorrect after that condition first evaluated to true? continuously for a time period longer 255 simulation steps, and a transition was taken out of that state at the end of that time period. C code generated from these charts is not affected by this bug.
Fixed In: R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 1, R2023a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Under certain conditions, lookup tables with table data defined via signal might generate code with output incorrectly set to zero
Description: Incorrect zero output might occur in code generation for models for which all of these conditions are true: The model contains an n-D Lookup Table block with: Data specification  set to Table and Breakpoints  Table data: Source ?set to Input port   The  Optimize using the specified minimum and maximum values ?configuration parameter in Model Configuration Parameters  > Code Generation  > Optimization  > Advanced parameters  is set to On

Description: If you run a Simulink model that contains a PID Controller block and has the Underspecified data types ?configuration parameter set to error , Simulink incorrectly throws an error if the PID Controller block uses one of the following configurations:? Integral-only control?  Discrete clamping anti-windup method

Summary: Definite integrals with parameters might be incorrect for some parameter values
Description: Definite integrals that share the same parameters in the integrands and the bounds might be incorrect for some parameter values. For example, the following result is only correct for x >= 2 : >> syms x u>> res = int(sign(u-x+2),u,[0 x])res =4 - x

Summary: Model Advisor check?Check for usage of text inside states? (Check ID:?mathworks.jmaab.jc_0739)?gives incorrect warnings
Description: Check for usage of text inside states? (Check?ID : mathworks.jmaab.jc_0739 ) gives warnings when the State label  does not exceed its boundary. These warnings are incorrect as the check?verifies the objects inside state such as transitions, junctions, substates and all the text labels that do not exceed state boundary.

Summary: Unable to change the block parameter values of the Min or Max blocks in Japanese environment
Description: On a PC configured to use a Japanese environment, Simulink throws the following error when you try to change the Function  parameter of the MinMax block using the drop-down list that opens when the block is selected and you click  min? or  max  at the center of the block:  ĄŃĄéĄá©`Ąż 'Function' ¤ËŚť¤ą¤ë MinMax Block 'MinMax' ÄÚ¤ÎÔO¶¨¤¬źo„ż¤Ç¤ą
Workaround: Use the Block Parameters dialog box instead.

Summary: Data type Propagation Block may result in numerical mismatch
Description: HDL Code generation from a subsystem containing Data type Propagation block may result in numerical mismatch. This is due to incorrect reference type data from the Data type propagation block in the generated model. However, the generated HDL Code is correct. This only occurs if there are no optimizations enabled in the model.?
Workaround: consider enabling any of the optimizations like Distributed pipelining, streaming/sharing, Adaptive pipelining...
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022b Update 5, R2023a

Summary: MATLAB slows down when using echotcpip or UDP functions
Description: Due to a memory leak, MATLAB might slow down and become unresponsive when using echotcpip  or UDP functions. The issue is more prominent when you send and receive large quantities of data (e.g., data sizes of 1 GB).  This issue can occur when using the following functions and possibly other functions: echotcpip  echoudp  udpport("byte")
Workaround: Restart MATLAB to free up the leaked memory. However, the issue might reappear if you use the affected functionality again.
Fixed In: R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 5, R2022b

Summary: HDL Coder generated model throws a compilation error when the initial value of the Delay block has floating-point complex input values which have an imaginary part as zero
Description: When you provide the initial value of Delay block with reset and enable via 'Input port' as a complex input of floating-point datatype, during the HDL code generation, a Constant block is created in generated model to hold this initial value. If the complex value has imaginary part as 0, then generated model throws a compilation error.

Summary: MATLAB might crash when simulating large model with fast restart enabled
Description: Simulating a large model with fast restart enabled might crash MATLAB during the initialization phase of the simulation.?

Summary: Compatibility check fails for a Simulink Test harness associated with a Subsystem Reference when block replacement is turned on
Description: When a Simulink Test harness, associated with a Subsystem Reference , has block replacement  turned on, Simulink Design Verifier fails to construct the correct block replacement model, and compatibility checking fails.  The following error is reported in the Diagnostic Viewer:  Simulink Design Verifier failed to initialize: Block Replacement failed

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect simulation and code generation result for Truth Table block that directly connects to a conditionally executed subsystem Outport block
Description: The initial value of the Truth Table block output is overwritten by the Outport block when all these conditions apply: The model contains a conditionally executed subsystem, such as an enabled or triggered subsystem. The subsystem contains a Truth Table block. An output of the Truth Table block directly connects to an Outport block of the subsystem. The parameter Initial value ?of the Truth Table block output port has a different value than the parameter Initial output ?of the Outport block.
Workaround: Insert a Signal Conversion block between the Truth Table block and the Outport block.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 2, R2023a

Summary: MATLAB might crash when generating code from models containing nested buses and signals resolved to Simulink.Signal objects that are initialized with parameter structures
Description: MATLAB might crash when generating code from a model that meets these conditions:The model contains a nested bus whose cumulative compile size is greater than 2560 bytes. The model also contains a root-level signal that resolves to a Simulink.Signal  object initialized with a parameter structure.  When this issue occurs the crash contains the following stack trace message: [snip]  Fault Count: 1  Abnormal termination:  std::terminate()  [snip]?  [ ?9] 0x00007ff9cd90aece CG::Const::cloneValue+00000030 (C :\..\win64\dist\bin\win64\cg_ir.dll) [ 10] 0x00007ff9d0088e2c CG::LoweringBaseImpl::lowerConst+00000140? (C :\..\win64\dist\bin\win64\cg_ir.dll) [ 11] 0x00007ff9d0089ac1 CG::LoweringBaseImpl::lowerSymbol+00000385 ?(C :\..\win64\dist\bin\win64\cg_ir.dll) [snip]?
Workaround: Use an Initial Condition block to initialize the Simulink.Signal object.
Fixed In: R2021b Update 7, R2022a Update 7, R2022b, R2023a

Summary: Full-duplex audio can cause MATLAB to crash
Description: Full-duplex audio can crash MATLAB if audioDeviceReader  is set up before audioDeviceWriter , or when an overrun or underrun occurs, in particular with devices that have more than four channels. This bug affects audioPlayerRecorder , or the simultaneous use of audioDeviceWriter  and audioDeviceReader  with the same device (with Driver  set to 'ASIO'  if using Windows).
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 4, R2023a

Summary: MATLAB might crash or produce an error when generating code for a model containing Simulink function or Graphical function in Stateflow chart
Description: MATLAB might crash or produce an error when generating code for a model containing a Stateflow chart under the following circumstances: The Stateflow chart has a Simulink function exported from Stateflow. The Stateflow chart contains a graphical function.  When generating code, you may get this code generation error: ValidCore violated: Failed assertion: fcnType->numInputs() == nInputs || (fcnType->isVariadic() && fcnType->numInputs()".   MATLAB might crash. The stack trace generated by MATLAB includes these lines:[ 0] 0x00007ff8bd15fd64 C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2022b\bin\win64\rtwcg.dll+06356324 RTWCG::RTWContext::getModule+00000020  [ 1] 0x00007ff8bcc5924a C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2022b\bin\win64\rtwcg.dll+01086026 CG::GenericTagCategory<CG::RTWCGMatlabFcnInplaceTag,0>::GenericTagCategory<CG::RTWCGMatlabFcnInplaceTag,0>+00000714
Workaround: For Simulink function, set the Function Packaging parameter to Inline and for Graphical function, set the Function Inline option to?Inline.?
Fixed In: R2021b Update 7, R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 6, R2023a Update 3, R2023b

Summary: Simscape diagnostic mistakenly identifies PS-Simulink Converter block when model has unequal number of variables and equations
Description: When a Simscape model has an unequal number of variables and equations, a compilation error is issued, saying: "Number of variables exceeds number of equations" or "Number of equations exceeds number of variables." The list of blocks identified by the error message sometimes mistakenly includes a PS-Simulink Converter block, for example: ?Error compiling Simscape network for model model.?Caused by:  Number of variables exceeds number of equations. Click on any Simscape blocks identified below for more detailed diagnostics.?  model/PS-Simulink Converter   model/Block     Clicking the link for the PS-Simulink Converter block returns either: " Error: Character vector is not terminated properly. " or? " Update the Simulink block diagram and try again. Simscape file with package path 'simscape.builtin.components.output' is not on MATLAB path and not in import context declared by import statements. "?   PS-Simulink Converter blocks should never appear in the list of Simscape blocks with more detailed diagnostics. The top-level error is still correct and the model does have unequal number of equations and variables, so inspect other blocks in the diagnostic list instead.
Workaround: Ignore any PS-Simulink Converter blocks identified in the diagnostic list when a Simscape model has an unequal number of variables and equations.

Summary: Simulink.Parameter with expression value has a different behavior than Simulink.Parameter with numeric value in mask initialization
Description: When you use aSimulink.Parameter  object as the value of a mask parameter, if the value of the parameter object is an expression, the behavior of the initialization code is different than if the value of the parameter object is a numeric value. When the value of the parameter object is an expression the parameter is not restored as a Simulink.Parameter  object after exiting the initialization code.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 4, R2023a

Summary: MATLAB Job Scheduler workers might get stuck in the 'busy' state after rerunning a communicating job
Description: Workers of a MATLAB Job Scheduler cluster might get stuck in the 'busy ' state when jobs created with createCommunicatingJob() or batch(...,'Pool',N)    fail and are automatically rerun. Consequently, the workers are unable to run jobs and tasks again.  The likelihood of a worker getting stuck increases with the number of workers that the job uses.
Workaround: Restart workers stuck in the 'busy' state with the stopworker and startworker scripts.To prevent workers from becoming stuck, disable the automatic rerun for communicating jobs. Use one of the following:For jobs created with createCommunicatingJob(), set the MaximumRetries property on the first task of the communicating job to 0 before submitting the job using the following syntax.? ? >> task = createTask(...)            ? ? >> task.MaximumRetries = 0Set the MaximumRetries property of your cluster profile to 0 in the Cluster Profile Manager.  This option works for all communicating jobs created with the cluster profile, including jobs created with batch(...,'Pool',N), but also disables the automatic rerun for independent jobs.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 5, R2022b

Summary: Generated model does not reflect optimizations for masked Library or Subsystem.
Description: When you enable optimizations for your design that contains masked Library or masked Subsystem and generate a model. The generated model is incorrect as it does not reflect optimizations for? Masked Library or Masked Subsystem. This?may result in numerical mismatch. However, thegenerated HDL code is correct.

Description: MATLAB might crash when the conditions below are TRUE:  The model contains branched bus signals. The?VariantConditionMismatch  (Diagnostics  -> Modelling issues related to variants  -> variant condition mismatch at signal source and destination ) option is set to either?error/warning    Stack snippet:? Workaround: Change the VariantConditionMismatch option?to the default setting:Set (Diagnostics?->?Modelling issues related to variants?->?variant condition mismatch at signal source and destination) option to none.Command line: set_param(gcs,'VariantConditionMismatch', 'none')

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Nonvirtual bus element at Outport block accessed by State Writer block might cause incorrect SIL mode simulation results
Description: SIL mode simulation produces incorrect results when all these conditions apply: A State Writer block?sets the state of an Outport block in a conditionally executed subsystem. The State Writer block is in an Initialize Function block. The Outport block receives a signal from a Mux block. The Mux block receives a signal selected from a nonvirtual bus.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Variant?Bus Element Out without signal names may generate incorrect code when output is driven from a subsystem
Description: A Simulink model containing conditional?Bus Element Out ? may generate incorrect code when the below conditions are true: The variant condition on the Bus Element Out  is propagated through a Variant Source  or Variant Sink . The Bus Element out  does not have a signal name assigned. The model contains Bus Creator  and Bus Selector  blocks for routing the conditional bus signals. The output is driven from an atomic subsystem or a virtual subsystem with Treat as grouped when propagating variant conditions  parameter is turned on  In the generated code, you can notice the output (e.g.:?rtb_VM_Conditional_Signal_Out1_BusCreator_0 ) is incorrectly reset to zero.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 3, R2023a

Summary: 4-Way 3-Position Valve (IL) block pre-parameterization option might cause unexpected behavior
Description: The 4-Way 3-Position Valve (IL) block pre-parameterization option Bosch 5-.WE J19  lacks the minimum information to pre-parameterize the block. The option can cause unexpected behavior of the block.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 3, R2022b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: The model parameter Allow clock-rate pipelining of DUT output ports can result in inconsistent I/O between the original model and HDL code
Description: When using the model parameter?ClockRatePipelineOutputPorts , the ordering of the outputs in the generated model and HDL code may not be the same as what was modeled originally. If these outputs have differing amounts of data rate latency, the validation model can show a mismatch.  Additionally, if input signals inside the DUT are left unconnected or drive unconnected logic that is considered dead and removed by optimizations, the signals are missing from the generated model and HDL code.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b

Summary: heatMapSFGalleryItem warning thrown for Stateflow charts calling functions containing protected models
Description: If you have a Stateflow chart containing a Simulink function which in turn contains a protected Model block, Simulink throws the following warning when you update or run the model: Warning: An error has occurred during refresh of Action named heatMapSFGalleryItem.  Warning: Unable to access model name for Model block 'topmodel/ Chart/ wrapper/ Model' because it references a protected model ('refmodel. slxp'). Use parameter 'ModelFile' to get the file name of the protected model.

Summary: cd errorswhen navigating to an empty folder on an SFTP server
Description: cd  errors when navigating from the current folder to an empty folder on an SFTP server. >> mkdir(S,"EmptyFolder")>> cd(S,"EmptyFolder")Error using matlab.io.sftp.SFTP/cd"EmptyFolder" is nonexistent or not a directory. Note: S ?is an?matlab.io.sftp.SFTP  object.
Workaround: You can work around this issue by creating a dummy folder in the empty folder.>> mkdir(S, "EmptyFolder/DummyFolder")>> cd(S, "EmptyFolder")Note:?S?is an?matlab.io.sftp.SFTP?object.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Models containing Variant Source blocks whose input signals have an?ExportedGlobal?storage class?and output signals have a reusable storage class might result in incorrect code
Description: Embedded Coder might generate incorrect code for a model meets the following conditions: The model contains a Variant Source block whose input signal uses an?ExportedGlobal ?storage class. The output signal of the block uses a?Reusable ?storage class. A root inport uses the same?Reusable ?storage class.  In the generated code, the global input buffer of the Variant Source block replaces its output buffer, which is also assigned for root input values. Replacing the output buffer causes the loss of runtime input data.
Workaround: Insert a Signal Conversion?block for the input signal of the Variant Source block,?and select the?Exclude this block from 'Block reduction' optimization?parameter of the Signal Conversion block. With the addition of the Signal Conversion block, an intermediate buffer is introduced, which prevents the global input buffer from replacing its output buffer.
Fixed In: R2021b Update 7, R2022a Update 5, R2022b, R2023a

Summary: False enumerated data type mismatch error between C and Simulink
Description: When you simulate or generate code for a model containing a C Caller block or a C Function block, Simulink throws a false enumerated data type mismatch error between C and Simulink enum  types when both of the following conditions are true:  C enum  type has type name as prefix for all enum  names. Simulink enum  type sets addClassNameToEnumnames  to true.  You see an error message similar to the one below:  Value of enumeration elements specified for argument 'u1' for C Caller block?'C Caller1' does not match the argument's type in the custom code.?Check the enumeration definition for 'myEnum' in Configuration Parameters > Simulation Target pane.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b

Summary: buildinfo.mat?error when MdlrefConfigMismatch check?is run with?parallel building of referenced models enabled
Description: When you run the Simulink Coder Model Advisor check,? mathworks.codegen.MdlrefConfigMismatch,  with parallel building of referenced models enabled ( EnableParallelModelReferenceBuilds  set to? 'on' ), the check produces an error indicating that it is unable to find? buildInfo.mat .

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generated for?hess might return incorrect results for ill-scaled matrices
Description: Code generated for generalized Hessenberg decomposition [aa,bb,q,z] = hess(a,b)  might return incorrect results if all  these conditions are satisfied: ?You generate a static library, a dynamic library or an executable that accepts real matrix inputs ?You do not instruct code generator to include calls to an external LAPACK library ?During run time, the input matrices a  or b  have elements with large magnitude ( > 1e137 for double precision and > 1e11 for single precision inputs)?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results are possible when initial conditions of a Stateflow chart are reset by the surrounding subsystem
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model containing a subsystem that meets the following conditions: The subsystem contains a Stateflow chart that has initial values for states and data.? The subsystem also contains one of these control port blocks:? Action Port block with the parameter States when execution is resumed ?is set to reset .? Enable Port block with the parameter States when enabling  is set to reset .? Trigger block with the parameter States when enabling ?is set to reset .? Reset block.
Fixed In: R2021b Update 7, R2022a Update 6, R2022b, R2023a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generator does not report an error when a non-inlined S-function accesses a global parameter with storage class
Description: The code generator does not report an error when a model that contains a non-inlined S-function accesses a parameter object in the base workspace or a data dictionary. This results in generated code that might behave incorrectly. This incorrect code generation can occur when any of the following conditions are true:The parameter object has a storage class other than Auto  or Model Default  The parameter object has storage class Auto  or Model default  and the default mapping for External parameters   is a value other than Default  The default mapping for Model parameters  is a value other than Default
Workaround: If a non-inlined S-function references a parameter object in the base workspace or a data dictionary, check that all of the following conditions are satisfied:The parameter object has storage class Auto or Model DefaultThe default mapping for External parameters is DefaultThe default mapping for Model parameters is Default
Fixed In: R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 4, R2022b, R2023a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Multi-Input Sum Block configured with vector/Matrix? inputs may results in a numerical mismatch
Description: HDL Coder generates incorrect HDL Code, when the 'Multi-Input Sum Block ' configured with vector/matrix inputs is used with possible signs combination other than (++). The generated HDL code may have a potential numerical mismatch.
Workaround: Consider modeling 'Multi-Input Sum Block' in terms of series of? 'Two Input Adder Blocks'.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code generated when export-function model has access functions and root level Model block with muxed input signals
Description: Incorrect code would be generated when all of following conditions are met: Inside an export-function model, data root Inport blocks are configured to have access functions. At least one of those data root Inport blocks has its output signal muxed with other signals to drive a Model block in the root level of the export-function model.?
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results are possible for?a model containing a?Unit Delay block inside a?For Each Subsystem block
Description: The generated code contains an incorrect initialization of a Unit Delay block state for a model that meets all of these conditions: The Unit Delay block is inside a For Each subsystem. The Initial Condition  of the Unit Delay block initialization is specified by a parameter instead of a fixed value. In the For Each Subsystem block parameters dialog box, the?Parameter Partition ?is selected .   There is a bug with the same symptoms introduced in release R2018b. See 2047809 for more information.?
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b, R2023a

Summary: Using?visdiff with models containing internally saved test harnesses might error if Simulink Test license not available
Description: Using?visdiff  with models containing internally saved test harnesses might error if a Simulink Test license is not available. For example, comparing the f14 and f14_2 models using visdiff('f14','f14_2')   might produce this error:? Unable to check out a Simulink Test license needed to use this feature.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated code might produce incorrect results if model contains For Each subsystem that accepts a nonscalar signal as input
Description: The generated code might be incorrect if your model satisfies?all ?these conditions: The model contains a For Each subsystem. The For Each subsystem accepts an input that is a nonscalar signal In the subsystem, at least one nonscalar signal connects to multiple output ports.  This is an example of generated incorrect code: void mRepro_ss_7(const real_T rtu_input[10], real_T rty_Out3[10]){? real_T tmp;? int32_T ForEach_itr;? int32_T i;? int32_T i_0;? int32_T tmp_0;? memcpy(&forEachInput[0], &rtu_input[0], 10U * sizeof(real_T));? memcpy(&shared_output2[0], &rty_Out3[0], 10U * sizeof(real_T));? for (i = 0; i < 2; i++) {??? for (i_0 = 0; i_0 < 5; i_0++) {????? forEachInput[i + (i_0 << 1)] = rtu_input[(i_0 << 1) + i];??? }??? for (ForEach_itr = 0; ForEach_itr < 2; ForEach_itr++) {????? tmp =? mRepro_no_2_19a_ConstP.ForEachSS_rtw_collapsed_sub_expr_0[ForEach_itr];????? for (i_0 = 0; i_0 < 5; i_0++) {??????? tmp_0 = (i_0 << 1) + ForEach_itr;??????? rty_Out3[tmp_0] = forEachInput[tmp_0] * tmp;????? }??? }? } }  In this code, the?memcpy ?operation for copying data from?shared_output ?to output port is executed before the?ForEach_itr ?loop. This code pattern produces incorrect results. The correct generated code for the above example looks like the following. Here the?memcpy ?operation for copying data from?shared_output ?to output port is executed after the?ForEach_itr ?loop. void mRepro_ss_7(const real_T rtu_input[10], real_T rty_Out3[10]) {  real_T tmp;  int32_T ForEach_itr;  int32_T i;  int32_T s1_iter;  int32_T tmp_0;  memcpy( & forEachInput[0], & rtu_input[0], 10 U * sizeof(real_T));  for (s1_iter = 0; s1_iter < 2; s1_iter++) {    for (i = 0; i < 5; i++) {      forEachInput[s1_iter + (i << 1)] = rtu_input[(i << 1) + s1_iter];    }    for (ForEach_itr = 0; ForEach_itr < 2; ForEach_itr++) {      tmp = mRepro_no_2_23a_ConstP.ForEachSS_rtw_collapsed_sub_expr_4847605804183166791_1[ForEach_itr];      for (i = 0; i < 5; i++) {        tmp_0 = (i << 1) + ForEach_itr;        rty_Out3[tmp_0] = forEachInput[tmp_0] * tmp;      }    }    memcpy( & shared_output2[0], & rty_Out3[0], 10 U * sizeof(real_T));  }}
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Models containing Stateflow Charts that have conditionally executed?while?or?do-while?constructs might result in incorrect code
Description: Incorrect code might occur for a model that meets the following conditions:The model contains a Stateflow Chart and a While Iterator block. The?Function packaging ?parameter of the Chart is set to Auto  or Inline.  The Chart also contains a conditionally executed while  or do-while  construct.  In the generated code, a part of the?while ?or do-while  loop is incorrectly hoisted.? For example, the correct code contains this while  loop that contains a do-while  loop while() {?  ? ? a =? inMatrix[1];  ? ? do {?  ? ? ? ? a++;?  ? ? ? ? // do something else  ? ? } while();  ? ? for(i=...) {?  ? ? ? ? outMatrix[i] += a? ? ?  ? ? }? }? In the incor rect code, the do-while  loop is incorrectly hoisted, and the outer while  loop becomes: do {  ? ? // do something else?  ?} while();?  while() {?  ? ? temp = inMatrix[1];?  ? ? for(i=...) {?  ? ? ? ? outMatrix[i] += temp ?} }?
Workaround: Set the?Function packaging of the Stateflow chart to Reusable function or Nonreusable function.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b, R2023a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generated for?eig might incorrectly return NaN values
Description: The eig  function uses an iterative algorithm to solve for eigenvalues and eigenvectors, and this algorithm might fail to converge in certain situations. In such situations, the function returns NaN ?values , and targets that support warnings also produce a warning. This issue is a possibility in both  MATLAB and the generated code. The implementation of the eig  function in the generated code is based on an older version of LAPACK and?might return NaN ?values when the output in MATLAB is finite. This issue can occur if both  these conditions are true:? ?You generate a static library, a dynamic library, or an executable by using MATLAB Coder or Simulink Coder ?You do not instruct the code generator to include calls to an external LAPACK library  This issue affects MATLAB Coder, MATLAB Function blocks or MATLAB System blocks in Simulink, and MATLAB functions in Stateflow.
Fixed In: R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 4, R2022b

Summary: Code generation error when code interface packaging is C++ Class and system target file is custom derived from ert.tlc
Description: C++ code generation might produce an error for a model with the configuration parameter CodeInterfacePackaging ?set to C++ Class? when the model uses a custom target file derived from ert.tlc. The error assertion might be similar to either of the following: Code generation assertion 'fpcArgSpecType != FPC_Value' failed in..  STD exception 'std::logic_error': 'basic_string::_M_construct null not valid' was caught.
Workaround: Review? the instructions to "Support C Function Prototype Control" for custom embedded targets and apply the steps found in section "Declaring C Function Prototype Control Compliance".
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 2, R2023a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect rapid accelerator mode simulation results for models using root-level bus element ports and function-call input ports
Description: Simulating a model results in incorrect answers when all ?of these conditions apply: The model simulates in rapid accelerator mode. The model has at least one In Bus Element or Out Bus Element block at the root level. The model has at least one Inport block at the root level that outputs a function-call signal and has a sample time of -1 . The ExternalInput  configuration parameter specifies time steps when the function-call input will run.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 1, R2023a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Export-function model with multiple function-call root Inport blocks driving a function-call subsystem with discontiguous output might result in incorrect code
Description: The signal memory for the root Outport block in the generated code might not be written to when all these conditions apply:? The model that contains the root Outport block is an export-function model. The model contains two root Inport blocks that output function-call signals. The root Inport blocks connect to the same Mux block. The output of the Mux block drives a function-call subsystem. The function-call subsystem drives a root Outport block. The signal that drives the corresponding Outport block in the function-call subsystem is discontiguous. A discontiguous signal can result from Mux, Selector, or Bus Selector blocks.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b, R2023a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code generated when a parameter or signal is an array of buses and specifies preservation of dimensions
Description: The code generated for a model might be incorrect when the model has a signal or parameter that meets these conditions. The parameter is an array of buses. The parameter specifies preservation of dimensions. A bus in the array has an element that is an array.
Fixed In: R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 4, R2022b, R2023a

Summary: MATLAB might crash when you select and move signal lines in a Simulink model multiple times using the arrow keys
Description: MATLAB might crash when you select and move signal lines in a Simulink model multiple times using the arrow keys. Moving signal lines does not include changing connections. The generated stack trace contains lines similar to the following: [ 0] 0x00007ffd4ab671cb Simulink::Routing::getPartialSegment[ 1] 0x00007ffd4ab7f598 `anonymous namespace'::getPartialSegmentKeyPairs?
Workaround: When moving signal lines using arrow keys, reselect the lines after each move or drag them using the pointer.
Fixed In: R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 2, R2023a

Summary: Having a large number of Data Dictionary objects causes a degradation in Model Explorer performance
Description: Having a large number of Data Dictionary objects can negatively affect the performance of the Model Explorer to the point that the Model Explorer becomes unresponsive.
Fixed In: R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 4, R2022b, R2023a

Summary: Accelerator mode simulation of an export function model with root-level Simulink function generates an incorrect result
Description: Accelerator mode simulation generates incorrect result for an export function model if all the following conditions are met: The model contains a Simulink function at the root level. The Simulink function is called from another export function within the model. The Simulink function contains a nested function-call subsystem.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 1, R2023a

Summary: Incorrect diagnostic error message with sltest.testmanager.createTestForComponent and SLDVTestGeneration
Description: sltest. testmanager. createTestForComponent  with?SLDVTestGeneration ? property  On , throws an incorrect diagnostic error:  ?A field named 'InputScenario' doesn't exist ?.  This happens when the invoked?SLDVTestGeneration ? ?generates 9 or more tests during the analysis.

Summary: Code generation for MATLAB code containing str2double function might fail with an error
Description: Code generation from MATLAB might fail if both  these conditions are true:  Your MATLAB code passes a character array to the?str2double  function?  The code configuration property Dynamic Memory Allocation for Variable-Size Arrays ('DynamicMemoryAllocation') is disabled   For example, consider this MATLAB function:? function out = foo(inData)idx = zeros(2,2);j = 1;idx(2,2) = 2;for i = 1:2    idx(j, 1) = i - 1;    j = j + 1;endc = char(inData(idx(2,1):idx(2,2)));out = real(str2double(c));end Suppose that you attempt to generate code for foo  by running these commands:? cfg = coder.config('lib');cfg.DynamicMemoryAllocation = 'Off';codegen -config cfg foo -args {[49 48]} -report In such situations, MATLAB displays the following error message: Computed maximum size is not bounded.  Static memory allocation requires all sizes to be bounded.  The computed size is [1 x :?].  Please consider enabling dynamic memory allocation to allow unbounded sizes.  Error in ==> str2double  When using Simulink Coder, this issue can affect code generation for a Simulink model that satisfies all? these conditions: The model contains a MATLAB Function block The code in the MATLAB Function block passes a character array to the?str2double ?function  Dynamic memory allocation in MATLAB functions disabled
Fixed In: R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b

Summary: Configuring UDP Send to broadcast address 255.255.255.255 causes error when loading real-time application
Description: If the To IP address  parameter in the UDP Send block is set to broadcast address 255.255.255.255 , Simulink Real-Time reports an error when loading the real-time application.
Workaround: In R2021b, you can set the To IP address in the UDP send block to the subnet broadcasting address.For example, if the local IP address is 10.10.10.15 and the subnet mask is 255.255.255.0, the subnet broadcasting address is 10.10.10.255.
Fixed In: R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a

Summary: String type custom attributes are imported from IBM DOORS Next as enumerated type
Description: When importing requirements from IBM DOORS Next to Requirements Toolbox, requirements with custom attributes of string data type are imported as enumerated data type instead. This is viewed as a combobox based custom attribute instead of a text edit based custom attribute.
Fixed In: R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 5, R2022b
