Summary: MATLAB might crash when simulating n-D Lookup Table block with fixed-point data types?in a model reference hierarchy
Description: MATLAB might crash when you simulate a model if all of the following conditions are true: Parent model contains a Model block with at least one breakpoint or table passed in as a model argument. Model block contains an n-D Lookup Table block with?Source? set to?Input port ?and ?at least one breakpoint or table input.? The model argument passed from the model block is set as a parameter value to the breakpoint or table parameter.  The n-D Lookup Table block inputs, breakpoints, and table data types are fixed-point.
Workaround: Replace the n-D Lookup table block with its equivalent Prelookup and Interpolation Using Prelookup block combination.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 4, R2022b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generated for svd might produce incorrect results if the input is an empty matrix
Description: In the generated code,?[U,S,V] = svd(A) ?might return incorrect results for the outputs U  and V  if: You specify the input matrix A ?to be variable-size during code generation, and  The input matrix?A  reduces to an empty matrix at runtime.?  This issue can arise if any  of these conditions are true:?  ?You generate a MEX file ?You use svd  in a MATLAB function block ?You generate a static library, a dynamic library, or an executable, and instruct the code generator to include calls to an external LAPACK library
Fixed In: R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 4, R2022b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect Simulation results for modelscontaining inline variants with?startup?activation and?updatediagram analyze all choices
Description: Incorrect Simulation results for modelscontaining inline variants with?startup ?activation combined when with?updatediagram analyze all choices? variant activations. The incorrect behavior is seen when all below conditionsare met: Model should contain variant source or sink withstartup ?activation and connected to another variant source or sink withupdate diagram analyze all choices? activation. Thevariant source or sink with update diagram?analyze?all choices  activationmust be inside the atomic subsystem.  Model is in Accelerator  modeSimulation or GRT code generation
Fixed In: R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 5, R2022b, R2023a

Summary: Test execution can error out in SIL mode
Description: If a test case is configured to run in Software in the Loop (SIL) simulation mode and has signals logged in the Simulation Outputs section, then the execution can error out.

Summary: Assigning a custom name to ERTHeaderFileRootName?and generating code for a?model that uses a storage class generates an extra header
Description: The code generator generates an extra header file model .h ?when both of the following are true:  The model uses a storage class You assign a custom name to the configuration parameter ERTHeaderFileRootName ?  No other generated files reference the model .h  file.?The file might incorrectly include the header file configured in the storage class HeaderFile  property.
Fixed In: R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 4, R2022b, R2023a

Summary: Generating an aggregated coverage report for test cases that use different test harnesses might error
Description: When generating an aggregated coverage report for coverage collected from different test harnesses the following error might occur:? Not Unique
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b

Summary: Multitasking analysis might fail when you redefine void type with typedef
Description: If you use typedef  to declare an alias for void  type, for instance: ?typedef void void_t;   Polyspace might not recognize functions of type? void_t  in your multitasking code and the analysis might fail. You see an error message similar to this:? Error in parameter -interrupts: interrupt has non-void prototype   For example, if you define a function isr ?in this way: typedef void void_t;?void_t isr(void_t)? {...}??   and you then use the option -interrupts  to specify that the function isr ?in an interrupt (-interrupts isr ), Polyspace does not consider that isr  has type void and the analysis fails.

Summary: Incorrect validation metrics when using the?trainNetwork?function and moving batch normalization statistics
Description: When you train a network with batch normalization layers using the?trainNetwork ?function with the BatchNormalizationStatistics ?training option set to?"moving" , ?the accuracy, RMSE, and loss validation metrics are incorrect.
Workaround: When you train the network, use an output function that reads from checkpoint networks and calculates the validation accuracy using the predict function.For an example, see the attached files.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 2, R2022b

Summary: IEC Certification Kit test case for Simulink Design Verifier fails to execute when Stateflow is not available
Description: The IEC Certification Kit test procedure for Simulink Design Verifier?requires Stateflow to execute test case TC_AOB_RPT_101 .?  In this situation, it is expected that the test procedure would exclude the tests that require Stateflow and return results? "EXCLUDED: no Stateflow license " in the validation report. Instead, the test returns unexpected failure message "EXCLUDED: Simulink Design Verifier fault ".
Workaround: If you don't have a Stateflow license:1. In the trace matrix artifact (certkitiec_sldv_trace.xlsx), remove the row that correspond to test case TC_AOB_RPT_101.2. Rerun the Simulink Design Verifier test procedure in the IEC Certification Kit.?

Summary: MATLAB Java engine crashes when called from user defined threads
Description: If a call to MATLAB engine is made from a user defined Java thread, then the Java application crashes.
Fixed In: R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 3, R2022b

Summary: ecdf?might return incorrect answer for empirical CDF, survivor function, or cumulative hazard function when input data is double-censored
Description: The ecdf  function might return an incorrect answer for the empirical CDF, survivor function, or cumulative hazard function if double-censored input data is specified by using the?"Censoring"  name-value argument. An incorrect answer is returned by ecdf ?if the double-censored input data contains values at which there are: many right-censored observations no fully-observed observations no left-censored observations
Workaround: Interval-censoring is a generalization of double-censoring. Use the interval-censoring format by specifying the input argument y?as a two-column matrix with rows specifying the range of possible values for each observation. For an observation that occurs at the value t, the corresponding interval-censored format is:[t,t]?ˇŞ Fully observed at?t[¨CInf,t]?ˇŞ Left-censored at?t[t,Inf]?ˇŞ Right-censored at?t
Fixed In: R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 5, R2022b, R2023a

Summary: Logged signals not shown for tests using block diagram harness and parameter overrides if model dirtied during compilation
Description: A test that uses a block diagram harness and parameter overrides does not show the logged signals in the Test Manager if the model is dirtied by a callback during compilation, such as by an InitFcn  on a mask.
Workaround: Set the Dirty flag of the model to off in the postload callback of the test case:?set_param('<model_name>','Dirty','off');
Fixed In: R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 3, R2023a

Summary: Numeric differences between SIL/PIL and normal mode simulations when logged output has constant sample time
Description: When a model or subsystem under test has an output with constant sample time, you might observe numeric differences between SIL/PIL and normal mode simulations for these scenarios. Scenario 1:  A Model block has these parameter settings: Simulation mode  - Software-in-the-loop (SIL)  or Processor-in-the-loop (PIL)  Code interface  - Model reference  Show model reset ports ?- on   In the model under test, the output signal? with constant sample time is mapped to the Auto  storage class. The parent or harness model triggers the Reset  port of the Model block at a time greater than zero.  After the Reset? port trigger, the logged signal for the output with the constant sample time is set to zero, which is incorrect. Scenario 2:  A Model block has these parameter settings: Simulation mode  -?Software-in-the-loop (SIL) ?or?Processor-in-the-loop (PIL)  Code interface  -?Model reference  Show model initialize port  - on  The parent or harness model triggers the Initialize  port at a time greater than zero.  At t = 0, the logged signal for the output with the constant sample time is set to its final value, which is incorrect until the Initialize  port trigger, the logged signal value must be zero. ?Scenario 3:  A Model block has these parameter settings: Simulation mode  - Software-in-the-loop (SIL)  or Processor-in-the-loop (PIL)  Code interface  - Top model  Show model initialize port  - on   The parent or harness model triggers the Initialize  port at a time greater than zero.  After the Initialize  port trigger, the logged signal for the output with the constant sample time remains zero.Scenario 4:  A test harness is created for a subsystem in a model. An output of the subsystem has constant sample time. The SIL/PIL Manager or Simulink Test runs a SIL or PIL simulation of the subsystem under test.  The value logged for the output with constant sample time might be zero instead of the true value from the generated code.?
Fixed In: R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 5, R2022b, R2023a

Summary: Creating a Raspberry Pi connection fails in Desktop MATLAB
Description: In Desktop MATLAB with the MATLAB Support Package For Raspberry Pi Hardware installed, creating a connection to a Raspberry Pi board using the raspi  object fails. MATLAB displays the following error: picam.c:19:10: fatal error: RaspiCamControl.h: No such file or directory Updated standards in GitHub now require secure authentication using HTTPS. As a result, the raspberrypi/userland  repository in GitHub is not accessible from the existing installation scripts of the support package.
Workaround: To work around this issue, use either of the following:Install the latest support package updates for R2021a, R2021b, or R2022a.?For other releases, before creating the raspi object in MATLAB, run the following commands in a Raspberry Pi terminal:cd /optsudo git clone https://github.com/raspberrypi/userland.gitcd /opt/userlandsudo git pull originTip: You can use any software to SSH into Raspberry Pi to get the terminal window, or you can use the below link to launch a Raspberry Pi terminal window.https://magpi.raspberrypi.com/articles/terminal-help
Fixed In: R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 4, R2022b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generated for the?==?operator that compares handle objects might be incorrect
Description: If your MATLAB code applies the?== ?operator on handle objects, the generated C/C++ code might be incorrect.?  Specifically, the generated code might report that compared objects are different when they are actually equal. For example, consider this MATLAB code snippet: x = SomeClass();  % This line creates a handle object SomeClassy = someFunctionThatReturnsItsInput(x); % y is the same object as xout = x == y;  In the generated code, out ?might? return false ?even though its correct value is?true . This issue affects MATLAB Coder, MATLAB Function blocks or MATLAB System blocks in Simulink, and MATLAB functions in Stateflow.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 3, R2022b

Summary: Model Advisor check Check position of signal labels (mathworks.jmaab.db_0097) displays an incorrect warning for signal lines
Description: Model Advisor subcheck?db_0097_a  displays incorrect warning for signal lines in Stateflow charts that are not controlled by the user, such as an output port in a Chart block.Warning  The following signals have labels which overlap otherobjects:?  Model/Chart/data

Summary: MATLAB might crash during GPU code generation when?the long long data type is not supported by the selected hardware
Description: GPU code generation requires the long long data type and tries to set the target long long mode to be true. But if target long long mode cannot be set to be true or production hardware is used for code generation (cfg.HardwareImplementation.ProdEqTarget =?true ), MATLAB might crash and throw the following error messages: In validateNumBits: Cannot emit constant with too many bits.?  In approximateRank: could not determine what integer type to produce.? Request was for 64 bits, but long long has 32 bits.?
Workaround: Set the following property of the codegen configuration object to avoid the crash,cfg.HardwareImplementation.ProdLongLongMode = true;If?ProdLongLongMode is immutable, you can generate code on the target (test) hardware to emulate the behavior of the production hardware by setting,cfg.HardwareImplementation.ProdEqTarget = false;Note this approach only works when the target hardware supports the long long data type.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: AUTOSAR Classic model hierarchies that reference models containing global Simulink functions might generate incorrect code
Description: For AUTOSAR Classic model hierarchies, the generated code might be incorrect if models referenced in the hierarchy include a global Simulink Function block that contains external input or output ports.?
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 6, R2022a Update 7, R2022b, R2023a

Summary: Generated C++ code fails to use coder::bounded_array type
Description: In the affected releases, the C++ code generated for your MATLAB code might represent bounded variable-size arrays by using?a struct type named something like? emxArray_real_T_1x10 . In previous releases, such arrays were represented by using a C++ class template called coder::bounded_array .  This bug might impact the public interface of the generated C++ code. As a result, you might have to change your hand-written code that interfaces with the generated C++ code to use this alternate data type.?

Summary: Duplicate IDs when exporting SimBiology model to SBML result in an invalid model
Description: When you copy and paste model components in the SimBiology Model Builder  app, the IDs of components used for exporting the model to SBML are duplicated. Consequently, the exported SBML model is invalid because it does not satisfy the SBML standard that identifiers need to be unique.
Fixed In: R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 3, R2022b

Summary: AUTOSAR compilation error: too many initializer values
Description: When there is a discrepancy between the arxml model regarding a structure layout (SwRecordLayout) and the corresponding structure, an error causes the compilation of a psar_prove_main.c  file to fail. This causes the failure of the verification of the corresponding AUTOSAR behavior.
Workaround: Edit the file psar_prove_main.c file to remove the incorrect initializer. For example, replace  static app_Appl_Map_Object const cst_init10264103644892694060 = Rte_InitValue_CData_Map_Object;with  static app_Appl_Map_Object const cst_init10264103644892694060 ; /* = Rte_InitValue_CData_Map_Object; */?Note that a file?psar_prove_main.c?is generated for every AUTOSAR software component. For each software component, the file is located in the subfolder of the results folder corresponding to the software component (the same subfolder as the file??psar_behavior.xhtml? for the software component). To locate this file:Open the file psar_project.xhtml that shows an overview of results for all software components.?Click on the Behaviors?tab to open detailed information about each software component behavior and then click the link to the appropriate psar_behavior.xhtml file. In your browser address bar, you can see the location of this file.Navigate to that location in your file explorer and find the corresponding??psar_prove_main.c.?Once you have the made the suggested edits to?psar_prove_main.c, rerun the analysis using the polyspace-autosar command. Use the option -do-not-update-autosar-prove-environment so that the file psar_prove_main.c is not regenerated and your edits are not overwritten.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Undefined MATLAB_MEX_FILE when you simulate custom code in a separate process (out-of-process execution)
Description: When you integrate custom code into Simulink using C Caller, C Function, Stateflow Chart, MATLAB Function, MATLAB System, or Truth Table?blocks and simulate that code in a separate process (out-of-process execution), the MATLAB_MEX_FILE  macro is not defined. As a result, code with an?#ifdef MATLAB_MEX_FILE  statement produces different results during simulation and code generation, with the expected answer during code generation.
Workaround: In the Configuration parameter dialog box, clear the?Simulate custom code in a separate process?parameter.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 3, R2022b

Summary: Analyzing a model with an Observer Reference crashes MATLAB if any of the Observer Port blocks is inactive

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Input array of buses might be incorrectly treated as scalar when using?Build this subsystem
Description: Right-clicking a subsystem and selecting?C/C++ Code ?>?Build this subsystem ?might produce incorrect generated code or an unexpected error during the build process when both these conditions apply: One of the subsystem input ports receives an array of buses. A For Each block drives that input port.  The array of buses is incorrectly treated as a scalar.

Summary: Propeller block might calculate the wrong thrust and torque coefficients
Description: When you set Parameterization ?to Polynomial fit  and Blade pitch type ?to Constant , the Propeller block incorrectly adds the last polynomial term more than once. The block adds the last polynomial term M-1 times, where M is the length of the?kT polynomial coefficients (pN...p0) ?and?kQ polynomial coefficients (pN...p0) ?parameters.
Workaround: Divide the last element of the kT polynomial coefficients (pN...p0) and kQ polynomial coefficients (pN...p0)?parameters by the vector length minus 1.?Alternatively, you can set Blade pitch type?to Controlled. Set the rows of the?kT table of polynomial coefficients (P/D, pN...p0) and kQ table of polynomial coefficients (P/D, pN...p0)?parameters to be duplicates of the kT polynomial coefficients (pN...p0) and?kQ polynomial coefficients (pN...p0) parameters, respectively.?

Summary: Some optimizations are disabled when using trainNetwork
Description: Some internal optimizations are disabled for trainNetwork . As a consequence, performance may be suboptimal when there are layer patterns that can be optimized, such as the Convolution+ReLU pattern.

Summary: The output from 'IsNew' port of ROS 2 Subscribe block stays true in code-generation
Description: The output from 'IsNew' port of ROS 2 Subscribe block stays?true ?in code-generation?even if no new messages are received. It must stay false ?until any message is received, and only change to true ?thereafter.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Restoring simulation from a saved operating point might give wrong answer for certain Stateflow charts
Description: Restoring simulation of a model containing a Stateflow chart from an operating point might give wrong answer when you save an operating point, modify the operating point by changing a chart data that is written only in entry actions, and restore the simulation from the modified operating point.  The restored simulation might not reflect your change in data.
Workaround: Put a breakpoint in the chart before restoring simulation.?

Summary: Stack overflow exception with Polyspace Code Prover on Windows systems
Description: On Windows, when analyzing applications that exceed certain thresholds of size or complexity, Polyspace Code Prover might crash at Software Safety Analysis Level 0 with a stack overflow message such as: exception Stack overflow(Stack overflow) raised.

Summary: Design Verifier incorrectly reports design errors in IRT Subsystems and System Objects as Valid
Description: In Design Error Detection workflow, for models containing design errors in the Init subsystem of IRT  models and initialization of System Object s,? Design Verifier reports them as Valid  even if they can be falsified .??

Summary: phased.Platform and Motion Platform block errors out in MATLAB and Simulink with multiple custom trajectories
Description: phased.Platform in MATLAB errors out when MotionModel property is set to Custom and multiple trajectories containing both position and velocity at each time stamp are specified. Similarly, Motion Platform block in Simulink errors out when the "Model of object motion" parameter is set to Custom. The error message reads "Too many input arguments.".

Summary: Setting for C++ mapping property 'Data Access' is not preserved in model
Description: For models with the target language set to C++, the 'Data Access' property configured in the code mappings is not written to the model file. If the parameter argument 'Data Access' is set to 'pointer', the value of the data access property is lost after the model is saved and reloaded.?
Workaround: The 'Data Access' property can be saved in a model workspace parameter by using the following steps:?1. Configure the model PostLoadFcn.a. Open Model Explorer.b. In the 'Model Hierarchy' panel, click the affected model.c. In the 'Model Properties' panel, select the 'Callbacks' tab.d. In the 'Model callbacks' panel, select 'PostLoadFcn'.e. Paste the following MATLAB code into the 'Model Post-Load Function' dialog box:hws = get_param(bdroot, 'modelworkspace');access = getVariable(hws, 'access');cm = coder.mapping.utils.create(bdroot);?cm.setData('ModelParameterArguments','DataAccess', access);2. Configure the model PreSaveFcn.a. Open Model Explorer.b. In the 'Model Hierarchy' panel, click the affected model.c. In the 'Model Properties' panel, select the 'Callbacks' tab.d. In the 'Model callbacks' panel, select 'PreSaveFcn'.e. Paste the following MATLAB?code into the 'Model Pre-Save Function' dialog?box:hws = get_param(bdroot, 'modelworkspace');hws.DataSource = 'Model File';cm = coder.mapping.utils.create(bdroot);access = cm.getData('ModelParameterArguments','DataAccess');hws.assignin('access',access);?

Summary: MATLAB crashes when loading a model that references a library that was saved before R2020b
Description: MATLAB crashes when all of these conditions are met:?Model Lib  is a library that has at least one signal object specification on one of its signal lines. The signal object specifies a non-Auto  storage class. Model?Lib  was? saved in a release prior to R2020b. Model A  references a block from library Lib . Model B ?uses model A  as a referenced sub-model.  In this case, MATLAB crashes when the find_mdlrefs  function is called on model B . A crash could also occur during model load.
Workaround: To avoid a crash, use one of these approaches:Open the referenced library before opening the model that references it.Open the referenced library and save it in a current release (R2020b or later). Then open the model that references the library.

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier compatibility check fails when?the model contains a root-level outport driven by an inactive state owner
Description: When a model contains a root-level outport driven by a state owner block that is located within an inactive variant subsystem, then the compatibility check fails with the following error message: Building model representation failed: The Outport block <block_path> has the parameter 'Ensure outport is virtual' turned on but Simulink cannot guarantee that the outport will be virtual in generated code due to the following reasons. One of the ways to correct this issue is to set the parameter 'Ensure outport is virtual' to 'off'.?  The error message states to set the parameter?Ensure outport is virtual  ?to?off  ? even? when the parameter is cleared.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 4, R2023a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated code might be incorrect for AUTOSAR model?with a multidimensional port parameter and ArrayLayout set to Row-major
Description: When a model is configured for AUTOSAR code generation, incorrect code might be generated when these conditions exist:Model is configured with ArrayLayout? set to  Row-major .  Model contains a parameter mapped as a port parameter.

Summary: JMAAB check for jc_0753?displays incorrect warning for?division operator used in transitions
Description: The?Sub-ID a1? of??Check condition actions and transition actions in Stateflow? (id:?mathworks.jmaab.jc_0753 )? displays incorrect warning when division operator is used in transitions in?Stateflow charts.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 4, R2022b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Using For Each subsystem in Switch Case Action or If Action?subsystem might produce incorrect simulation results in Accelerator mode
Description: Simulation results might be incorrect when a model uses a For Each subsystem inside an Action subsystem and all the following conditions are true: The model contains multiple instances of the For Each subsystem. The function packaging  parameter of the For Each subsystem is set to either Auto ?or Reusable function . The For Each subsystem contains signals with continuous sample time. The model runs in Just-In-Time (JIT) accelerator mode.?  Note that this problem occurs only in JIT accelerator mode. Normal and classic accelerator mode simulations produce correct values.
Workaround: Switch to classic accelerator mode by using:set_param(0,'GlobalUseClassicAccelMode','on')
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b

Summary: MATLAB might crash with a stack trace that includes 'sltopo::destinationBlocksOf'
Description: During model compilation or simulation, MATLAB might crash with a stack trace that includes the text 'sltopo::destinationBlocksOf' . This crash mostly happens for models that include port blocks, such as Inport and Outport blocks, or variants.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect C code generated when function-call subsystem writes to its output conditionally.
Description: The code generator produces incorrect C code when these conditions are true: The top model contains multiple function-call subsystems.? Signal lines that connect the function-call subsystems represent data transfers (a function-call subsystem consumes the output of another).  At least one of the data transfer signals is?updated conditionally. The top model is configured with an AUTOSAR system target file.  In the generated code, the variable or buffer for the transferred data is allocated on a stack and initialized and updated conditionally, but is used unconditionally.
Workaround: Add a Signal Conversion block before the outport of a function-call subsystem that writes to a data transfer signal that is updated conditionally. For the added Signal Conversion block, select block parameter Exclude this block from 'Block reduction' optimization.
Fixed In: R2021b Update 6, R2022a Update 6, R2022b, R2023a

Summary: Assignment statements in C charts might have incorrect number of condition coverage objectives
Description: Coverage results for a logical expression in an assignment statement found in a C chart might incorrectly show duplicated condition objectives. This issue occurs if the C chart is inside a For Iterator Subsystem or inside a model that is referenced multiple times in a model reference hierarchy. This issue does not occur for MAL charts. The number of duplicated condition coverage objectives increases each time you simulate the model in the same session, resulting in an incorrectly high number of total condition coverage objectives and an incorrectly low condition coverage percentage shown in the coverage report. The difference in the number of coverage objectives between subsequent simulations of the same model causes this error message during coverage aggregation:? Dimensions of arrays being concatenated are not consistent.   This error appears for any coverage aggregation method, including Simulink Test Manager or manually adding two cvdata  objects.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 2, R2022b

Summary: Bus struct typedefs appear in a nondeterministic order in generated header file
Description: If a model utilizes variants and references Simulink.Bus  objects with?DataScope ?set to Auto , then the corresponding typedefs may appear in the generated header files in a nondeterministic order that may change from one session to the next.
Workaround: Set the?DataScope?property of the Simulink.Bus objects to Imported or Exported.

Summary: Use as signal checkbox in the New Session Dialog does not work for single-member data in timetable format
Description: For single-member data being imported as a timetable into the Diagnostic Feature Designer  app, the default option is to import the timetable variables as features. The New Session dialog box provides the Use as signal  checkbox to instead import the data in the timetable variables as signals. Selecting this checkbox does not work and the data is imported as features into the app. Depending on the R2021a update version you are using, you might or might not receive warning messages.??
Workaround: To import the single-member data in the timetable as a signal,?create a cell array with the timetable as an element. For example, if TT is the timetable you want to import, then:TTC = {TT}Then import TTC using the New Session Dialog to import the data as a signal.

Summary: Analysis functions of dsp.Channelizer object return incorrect filters for non-maximally decimated cases
Description: For the dsp.Channelizer object, when DecimationFactor is less than NumFrequencyBands (non-maximally decimated channelizer), the getFilters, fvtool, and freqz object functions depict incorrect filters. To obtain the correct channelizer filter, the prototype filter is supposed to be shifted by 2¦Đ?M, where M is the number of frequency bands. In the case of non-maximally decimated channelizer, the getFilters, fvtool, and freqz object functions shift the prototype filter by 2¦Đ?D to calculate the filters, where D is the decimation factor.  Note that the Channelizer object uses the correct set of filters in the implementation in the step function. The issue occurs only when you use the getFilters, fvtool, and the freqz object functions.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 3, R2022b

Summary: DO Qualification Kit test procedure for Simulink Coverage fails to complete when Fixed-Point Designer or DSP System Toolbox is not available
Description: The DO Qualification Kit test procedure qualkitdo_slcov_sl.m  for Simulink Coverage requires Fixed-Point Designer and DSP System Toolbox to execute optional test cases for these products. When theses licenses are not available, the test procedure does not exclude the corresponding tests as expected. Instead, the test procedure fails to execute with the error Simulation failed . Test procedures in the DO Qualification Kit are expected to exclude tests when the license that is required to execute a test is not available. When this occurs, the results for the test states EXCLUDED: no <XYZ> license  in the qualification report.
Workaround: When you don't have a DSP System Toolbox license:Open test model mDiscreteSignalSize.slx and remove subsystem Discrete FIR Filter, which is intended to test blocks that require DSP System Toolbox.Save the test model and rerun the Simulink Coverage test procedure in the DO Qualification Kit.?When you don't have a Fixed-Point Designer license:?Open test models mLookupTables.slx?and mSignalAttributes.slx.?Attempt to compile the models. Remove subsystems that cause compilation errors (they are intended for testing fixed-point data types).When the fixed-point subsystems are removed and the models compile without error, save the test models.?Rerun the Simulink Coverage test procedure in the DO Qualification Kit.

The model runs into this sequence of steps during any part of the simulation:   ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ?Step 1? ? ? ?Step 2? ? ? ?Step 3 Layer 1's If block output:? ? ? ?Active? ? ? Inactive? ? ?Active   Layer 2's If block output:? ? ? ?Active? ? ? ?Active? ? ? ?Active   Triggered subsystem:? ? ? ? ? ?Active? ? ? ?Active? ? ? ?Active     Under these conditions, incorrect elapsed time might be calculated and used in the computation of the discrete-time integrator's output at Step 3.

Summary: Model Advisor checks requiring Simulink Design Verifier analysis do not work when combined with other checks requiring extensive analysis when using Advisor.Application API
Description: When using the Advisor.Application  API to run a Model Advisor analysis, if the provided configuration has a combination of checks requiring Simulink Design Verifier analysis along with other checks requiring extensive analysis, the checks requiring Simulink Design Verifier analysis will wrongly show a passing status.

Summary: Using group-specific variants in parameter estimation fails when computing confidence intervals of type bootstrap and profileLikelihood
Description: When you use group-specific variants in the estimation of parameters in SimBiology, the computation of confidence intervals of type bootstrap  and profileLikelihood  errors unexpectedly.

Summary: invalid vector subscript error?during code generation with? a variant?root outport
Description: invalid vector subscript ?error?during code?generation when all the below conditions are true:  Signal object is attached to the root outport block i. e.   get_param(gcb,   'MustResolveToSignalObject')  returns true for the root outport block.   The storage class of this signal object is custom.  The root outport has a variant condition.  There is at least one root outport "before" this root outport that has either no variant condition or the same variant condition as this root outport.

Summary: Unexpected error when selecting parameter or residual types to display for fit results in Simbiology Model Analyzer
Description: In the SimBiology Model Analyzer  app, when you create an?Act vs. Pred ?or Residuals  plot for the Fit Data  program results, you can turn on or off different parameter types (individual vs. predicted) or residual types (CWRES or IWRES) in the legend. ?This change to the plot might fail and show a dialog with the error Unrecognized field name CategoryBinValues .

Summary: Fit result plots might label axes with group index number instead of group name or identifier
Description: When you fit a model to data that has group identifiers that are not character vectors, the fit results plots might use the group index number instead of the corresponding group identifier. For example, you might encounter this issue when you are using strings or categorical as group names or IDs.
Workaround: Convert the group column in the dataset to a character vector or cell array of character vectors before performing fitting to automatically display the group identifiers. ?Otherwise, you can manually change the axes labels on the plot.

Summary: Model Advisor check "Check reuse of subsystem code" exits with warning when?checksum information is missing
Description: Model Advisor check "Check reuse of subsystem code" ?( ID: ?mathworks.codegen.SubsysCodeReuse) ?exits with a warning when a subsystem does not provide checksum information.
Fixed In: R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 6, R2022a Update 7, R2022b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Simulink fails to report inconsistent initial values between Outport block and signal object specified inside referenced model
Description: Simulink fails to report inconsistent initial values between an Outport block and a signal object specified inside a referenced model, and instead simulates the model. When this issue occurs, the simulation and code generation results might not be the same, depending on which initial value is applied last.?? This issue might occur if the model has these conditions: The model contains a conditionally executed subsystem. The conditionally executed subsystem has an Outport block with an explicit value specified for the Initial output  parameter, that is, not an empty matrix ([] ). The Outport block is driven by an output port of a Model block. Inside the referenced model, the signal driving the root Outport block corresponding to the output port of the Model block has a Simulink.Signal  object associated with it. The InitialValue ?field? of the signal object is not an empty string and the initial value is not the same as the initial output value specified for the Outport block in the top model.
Workaround: When a Simulink.Signal object with an explicit initial value is applied to a signal, make sure that the destination, conditionally executed subsystem Outport block uses an empty matrix ([]) as the initial value. Also, apply this workaround when the signal in question crosses a model reference boundary.
Fixed In: R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 4, R2022b

Summary: Incorrect results for custom block replacement rule when referring to sqrt block in sldvdemo_custom_blockreplib.slx
Description: Simulink Design Verifier may show incorrect results when: The BlockReplacement ?parameter is set On  in Design Verifier Configuration window. Custom block replacement rule in?BlockReplacementRuleList? refers to sqrt   block in?sldvdemo_custom_blockreplib  .    For example, sldvdemo_custom_blkrep_rule_sqrt.m   refers to sqrt  block in sldvdemo_custom_blockreplib  model ?and if it?is one of the rules in?BlockReplacementRuleList,? then SLDV analysis may return incorrect results.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 3, R2022b

Summary: polyspace-configure ignores diab compiler options file
Description: If your build system uses a diab  compiler that has an options file name that starts with the capital letter E or O, and you run polyspace-configure  to generate a Polyspace project or Polyspace analysis options file from that build system, the compiler options file is ignored and you see a debug message similar to this: DEBUG: Semantic option ignored: -@<OptionFile>   The issue occurs because Polyspace misinterprets the -@ErestOfFileName  and -@OrestOfFileName  options as redirect options -@E<restOfFileName> ?(redirect standard error) and -O<restOfFileName> ?(redirect standard output). Because the options file is ignored, if you run an analysis using the generated Polyspace project or analysis options file, the analysis might fail.

Summary: Models that use Bus?data type with elements smaller than double?might throw assertion during code generation for S-Function target
Description: If a model uses the Bus ?data type with bus elements that are of a type smaller than  double ?(e.g.,  single ) and there are blocks in the model that initialize that  Bus ?type to zero, then the model might throw an assertion during code generation for an S-Function target.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated code might be incorrect for models containing If Action subsystem connected to Memory block
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets all of these conditions: The model contains an If Action subsystem that connects to a Memory block. The model configuration parameter?Reuse global block outputs ?is selected.  If the If Action subsystem and the Memory block have initial conditions, the?Reuse global block outputs? optimization might cause their block buffers to be reused incorrectly leading to a loss of one initial condition in the generated code.
Workaround: In the Configuration Parameter dialog box, clear the Reuse global block outputs?parameter.

Summary: JMAAB check for na_0001?displays incorrect warning for logical operations
Description: The?Sub-ID a? of??Check Stateflow operators? (id:?mathworks.jmaab.na_0001 )? displays incorrect warning when logical operators &&  and ||  are used in Stateflow charts with C  action language.

Summary: Error loading aggregated coverage data from nested subsystem in linked library
Description: When you try to load coverage data that is aggregated from the coverage results for two or more harnesses, an error occurs when both of these conditions are true: At least one harness is a block diagram harness where the component-under-test is a model At least one harness is for a subsystem nested inside a linked library instance of a parent subsystem.  The error message might be similar to: failed assertion in B:\matlab\toolbox\slcoverage\src\slvnv\cv_root.cpp at line 661:  parentId = hier_locate_root_parent(rootId,tree)

Summary: Unexpected effect of rotational velocity on pressure and force in the Rotating Single-Acting Actuator (IL) block
Description: Increasing rotational velocity decreases rather than increases pressure and interface force magnitude in the Rotating Single-Acting Actuator (IL) block.
Workaround: This workaround is equivalent to the expected behavior of the Rotating Single-Acting Actuator (IL) block. Replace the Rotating Single-Acting Actuator (IL) block with these three blocks:Single-Acting Actuator (IL)Rotating Channel (IL)Rotating Cylinder Force (IL)Make the following connections. These connections match the attached picture:Rotating Channel (IL) port A to previous connections of the Rotating Single-Acting Actuator (IL) port A.Rotating Channel (IL) port B, Single-Acting Actuator (IL) port A, and Rotating Cylinder Force (IL) port X.Rotating Cylinder Force (IL) port C, Single-Acting Actuator (IL) port R, and previous connections of the Rotating Single-Acting Actuator (IL) port R.Rotating Cylinder Force (IL) port R, Single-Acting Actuator (IL) port C, and previous connections of the Rotating Single-Acting Actuator (IL) port C.Rotating Channel (IL) port W and Rotating Cylinder Force (IL) port W to previous connections of the Rotating Single-Acting Actuator (IL) port W.Make the following parameter updates:Parameters in the Single-Acting Actuator (IL) block should correspond to parameters on the Actuator, Hard Stop, and Initial Conditions tabs of your original Rotating Single-Acting Actuator (IL) block. The value of the?Piston cross-sectional area?parameter in the Single-Acting Actuator (IL) block equals the value of the annular area in-between the?Piston outer radius?and?Piston inner radius?parameters in the Rotating Single-Acting Actuator (IL) block.Parameters in the Rotating Channel (IL) block should match parameters on the Fluid Channel tab of your original Rotating Single-Acting Actuator (IL) block. These parameters in the Rotating Single-Acting Actuator (IL) block map to corresponding parameters in the Rotating Channel (IL)?with a different name:Fluid entry radius maps to Port B radius of rotation.Fluid channel cross-sectional area maps to Orifice area.The Rotating Cylinder Force (IL)?block parameter Mechanical orientation?should have the opposite setting of the Rotating Single-Acting Actuator (IL)?block. All remaining parameters in the Rotating Cylinder Force (IL) block map directly from parameters with the same names in the Rotating Single-Acting Actuator (IL) block.
Fixed In: R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 1, R2022b

Summary: MATLAB fails to start with system version of Java on macOS
Description: MATLAB might not find Java installed on a macOS computer when calling this command in MATLAB:jenv system  or calling this command from a terminal window: matlab_jenv system ?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generated for network containing global average pooling layer followed by?nnet.keras.layer.ZeroPadding2dLayer?might produce incorrect results
Description: Code generated for a deep learning network that contains the following two layers in succession might produce incorrect results: Global average pooling layer nnet.keras.layer.ZeroPadding2dLayer   This issue affects all these targets: MKL-DNN, ARM Neon, cuDNN, ARM Mali, and TensorRT. Therefore, this issue affects both MATLAB Coder and GPU Coder workflows.

Summary: Argument was not found or found more than 2 hits or Unexpected number of elements in GraphicalIndex?error from SIL/PIL simulations of AUTOSAR model
Description: ?A software-in-the-loop (SIL) or processor-in-the-loop (PIL) simulation of an AUTOSAR model fails if both of these conditions apply to the model under test: The configuration parameter?CodeInterfacePackaging  is set to?'Reusable function' . Function Caller blocks call functions defined in Simulink Function blocks that are outside the model.  The simulation produces one of these error messages: Argument was not found or found more than 2 hits  Unexpected number of elements in GraphicalIndex
Fixed In: R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 2, R2022b

Summary: Incorrect?SldvData with option?Include expected outputs selected and with?signals enabled for logging
Description: If the model contains signals enabled for logging, then the option?Include expected outputs ?logs incorrect values in SldvData .  This issue has been observed in following scenarios: When model contains a model reference block feeding root level outports and user is logging few signals in the model reference hierarchy. When model contains stateflow block and logging is enabled for stateflow local variables.

Summary: Atomic subcharts with parameter mapping in hierarchy of models with For Each subsystem might cause MATLAB to crash
Description: ?During simulation initialization, MATLAB might crash when all of the following conditions are met: The parent model contains at least two child models. One of the child models has an atomic subchart with parameter mapping. The other child model has either a Stateflow chart with mask parameters or an atomic subchart with parameter mapping inside a For Each subsystem. Both child models use at least one parameter that directly or indirectly resolves to the same global Simulink.Parameter  object with bus data type defined either in the base workspace or in a Simulink data dictionary.

Summary: Output to terminal is not displayed when calling?disp or any other function displaying output from Java Engine
Description: When using Java Engine, any call to a function to create terminal output such as disp  does not produce any terminal output. For example, the following code should print Hello World  to the terminal:? MatlabEngine eng = MATLABEngine.startMATLAB();  eng.eval("disp('Hello World');");  Instead this code produces no terminal output.

Summary: JMAAB check for jc_0641 reports incorrect warning for Delay and Trigger blocks
Description: Model Advisor check Check for sample time setting ?(id:?mathworks.jmaab.jc_0641)? does not report warning for?Function-Call Subsystem  and reports incorrect warning for Delay  block at root level.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 1, R2022b

Summary: Normal simulation mode is displayed instead of SIL or PIL in Test Manager results
Description: When you use the Test Manager to run a SIL or PIL simulation in a subsystem harness that contains a Reusable Library Subsystem or an Atomic Subsystem, the simulation mode in the test result is displayed as Normal, instead of SIL or PIL.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 3, R2022b

Summary: Embedded Coder might generate uncompilable code for a model that uses standards compliant casting
Description: Embedded Coder might generate uncompilable code when all ?of the following are true:? The model uses any of the following storage classes or access functions on the I/O of a referenced Model block or S-Function that uses TLC for code generation: A?GetSet  storage class. A user-defined storage class in an Embedded Coder Dictionary whose data access is Function.  A user-defined TLC storage class that uses different legacy APIs to get and set the data.  The Casting modes ?model configuration parameter is set?to Standards Compliant .  Embedded Coder might insert a void  cast before an access function call, leading to uncompilable C/C++ code.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 7, R2023a

Summary: Incorrect results with Run additional analysis to reduce instances of rational approximation?when?Enable timer optimizations is selected
Description: Results given in the?Run additional analysis to reduce instances of rational approximation ?may be wrong when:Simulink Design Verifier analysis is run with the timer optimizations enabled through the use of the?sldvtimer(1) .  The incorrect behavior is observed across Simulink Test Generation, Design Error Detection and Property Proving workflows. Results obtained when the setting?Run additional analysis to reduce instances of rational approximation ?is OFF  are not affected.
Fixed In: R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 1, R2022b

Summary: Error when using Simulink.SimulationInput to provide a new value for a variable in a data dictionary
Description: If Simulink.SimulationInput  object is used to provide a new value for a variable which is defined in a data dictionary of a reference model and the variable has a data type which is also defined in the same data dictionary, the following error is thrown:  Error using Simulink.Simulation.internal.DesktopSimHelperTo use variable 'variable_name', data dictionary dd_name.sldd must be a data source of data dictionary dd_name.sldd.  The error will be thrown if the top model does not use any data dictionary.??
Workaround: Use a data dictionary for the top model as well. The data dictionary can be empty.?Here?is the documentation for setting up a model to use data dictionary.

Summary: MATLAB may crash if the Spatial Contact Force block is used in a simulation that is over constrained.
Description: If the?Spatial Contact Force block is used to model contact between a File Solid geometry and another geometry type, MATLAB may crash when running the simulation. The issue may arise if the simulation is over constrained or mechanically invalid.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 4, R2022b, R2023a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: IP core register initial value is incorrect for fixed-point values with non-zero fraction length
Description: When mapping a Simulink input port to a register in the IP core workflow, you can optionally specify an initial value for the register. If the mapped Simulink port has a fixed-point data type with non-zero fraction length, then the register initial value specified is incorrect in the generated code.
Workaround: You can cast the Simulink port data type to be a fixed-point data type with zero fraction length at the DUT boundary (using a Data Type Conversion block in Stored Integer mode). You can then convert it back into the desired fixed-point data type inside of the DUT (using a Data Type Conversion block in Stored Integer mode). When setting the register initial value, set the value using the stored integer representation of the desired initial value.For example, if the original Simulink port had data type fixdt(0, 16, 15)?and the desired initial value is 0.5, cast the port data type to uint16 at the DUT boundary and set the initial value as?storedInteger(fi(0.5, 0, 16, 15)).
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Stateflow chart hard codes some size information of non-scalar symbolic dimension
Description: When generating code for a 2-D matrix that is defined by symbolic dimensions,  the resulting code inserts a numeric offset in the matrix assignment based on the current value of one dimension of the matrix.?This causes an issue if you specify a different dimension post code generation since the numeric offset will not match the matrix dimension.
Fixed In: R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 1, R2022b

Summary: The JMAAB check for?jc_0802?does not analyze enumerated data types correctly
Description: The Model Advisor check?Check for implicit type casting in StateFlow (id: mathworks.jmaab.jc_0802)? does not analyze enumerated data types correctly in a Stateflow Chart with MATLAB as the action language.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b

Summary: Calibration levels for acousticFluctuation and acousticRoughness are incorrect
Description: The?acousticFluctuation  and acousticRoughness  functions output inaccurate level values because of incorrect calibration.
Workaround: To compensate for the inaccuracy, set the microphone calibration factor to twice the measured value:      [x,fs] = audioread("Engine-16-44p1-stereo-20sec.wav");? ?   caliblvl = 6.1;? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ?     ?% Computed by calibrateMicrophone? ?   acousticFluctuation(x,fs,2*caliblvl)?     % Double the calibration factorIf you are using the default value of sqrt(8) for the microphone calibration factor, change all function calls to use twice that value:      acousticFluctuation(x,fs,2*sqrt(8))? ?     % Double the default calibration factorThis bug and workaround apply only to?acousticFluctuation?and?acousticRoughness.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Using gpucoder.reduce?with dimension specification might?produce incorrect answers for variable-sized inputs and row-major layout
Description: When using row-major layout with variable-sized inputs (bounded or unbounded), the code generated for gpucoder.reduce  containing a dimension ('dim' ) specification might initialize the output to a wrong value resulting in an incorrect final value.
Workaround: When using?gpucoder.reduce, always use column-major layout.

Summary: JMAAB check for?db_0126?reports incorrect warning for events in Stateflow charts
Description: Model Advisor check Check definition of Stateflow events ?(id:?mathworks.jmaab.db_0126) ?reports below incorrect warning for events used in parallel states. ? Warning  The following Stateflow events are not defined at theirsmallest scope:?  Event? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? Smallest Scope  model/Chart/E??? model/Chart/A/A2  model/Chart/F? ? model/Chart/A/A1?  This warning message is incorrect as the events are defined at the smallest possible scope.??
Fixed In: R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 8, R2022b

Summary: Error on Linux when working with?HDF5 files on NFS4 network locations
Description: An error is thrown when using Linux platforms and working with HDF5 files in network locations that are mounted as NFS4. For example, the error can happen when using h5writeatt  or h5write  after h5create . >> outFile = 'myfile.h5';>> h5create(outFile, '/mysignal', [10 1])>> h5write(outFile, '/mysignal', ones(10, 1))Error using hdf5lib2Unable to open the file because of HDF5 Library error. Reason:UnknownError in H5F.open (line 130)file_id = H5ML.hdf5lib2('H5Fopen', filename, flags, fapl, is_remote);Error in h5write (line 108)    file_id    = H5F.open(Filename,flags,fapl); It can also happen when re-opening the file for writing using HDF5 Library Packages, as shown in this call to H5F.open . >> fid = H5F.open('myfile.h5','H5F_ACC_RDWR','H5P_DEFAULT');Error using hdf5lib2Unable to open the file because of HDF5 Library error. Reason:UnknownError in H5F.open (line 130)file_id = H5ML.hdf5lib2('H5Fopen', filename, flags, fapl, is_remote);
Workaround: This issue is specific to Linux platforms. To work around it, set the environment variable HDF5_USE_FILE_LOCKING to FALSE before launching MATLAB. For example, to set the environment variable in a?C-shell terminal, and then launch MATLAB from the same terminal session, use these commands.% setenv HDF5_USE_FILE_LOCKING FALSE% matlabTo set the environment variable in a bash terminal, use the "export" command, e.g.:% export HDF5_USE_FILE_LOCKING='FALSE'% matlabSetting this environment variable to FALSE?disables the file locking behavior that was introduced in HDF5 1.10.x versions (more details can be found here:?https://docs.hdfgroup.org/hdf5/rfc/RFC_THG_2014-01-10.pdf). In terms of file locking and protection from file corruption, setting HDF5_USE_FILE_LOCKING to FALSE is equivalent to using a MATLAB release earlier than R2021b, when we upgraded to HDF5 1.10.7. But disabling file locking like this is not recommended by The HDF Group (the developers and maintainers of HDF5).

Summary: Renamed signal in Signal Editor not saved with correct name in MAT-file if signal dimension greater than 1
Description: In Signal Editor, if you rename a signal that has a dimension size greater than 1, the updated name is reflected in the Signal Editor interface, but is not saved with the new name in the MAT-file.

Summary: Unable to load saved model workspace parameter value during model load/open operation
Description: Unable to load saved parameter values from model workspace during model load/open operation for?Narrowband Transmit Receive Array  or Monopulse Feed  Blocks (from Phased Array System Toolbox ). The issue is specific to values coming from the model workspace and in this case the block is loaded with the default settings coming from the library?
Workaround: Right click on the model and choose Model PropertiesClick the Callbacks tab and modify PostLoadFcn and CloseFcn as given below:In PostLoadFcn, add the model work space variables into the base work space:? ? ? ? hws = get_param(gcs,'modelworkspace')? ? ? ? a = getVariable(hws,'a')? ? ? ? e = getVariable(hws,'e')? ? ? ? s = getVariable(hws,'s')In CloseFcn, clean up the base work space.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 5, R2022b

Summary: MATLAB crash during "Check for Run-Time Issues" step in the MATLAB Coder app
Description: In the MATLAB Coder app, the Check for Run-Time Issues ?step might cause MATLAB to crash. The error message mentions "CycleEquivalence. cpp line 216:?In getClass: edge has no entry in CycleEquivalence::getClass ". A similar error might occur when simulating a model that contains MATLAB System blocks that have the Simulate using  ?parameter set to Code generation.
Workaround: To avoid this issue, clear?Collect MATLAB line execution counts?in the MATLAB Coder app during the Check for Run-Time Issues?step.See the attached image.To avoid this issue in Simulink, change?Simulate using?of the affected MATLAB System block to Interpreted execution. Alternatively, download and extract the attached .zip file. Run ">> SimulinkWorkaroundG2681332" in MATLAB command window.

Summary: IEC Certification Kit test procedure for Simulink Coverage fails to complete when Fixed-Point Designer or?DSP System Toolbox is not available
Description: The IEC Certification Kit test procedure?certkitiec_slcov_sl.m ?for Simulink Coverage requires Fixed-Point Designer and DSP System Toolbox to execute optional test cases for these products. When theses licenses are not available, the test procedure does not exclude the corresponding tests as expected. Instead, the test procedure fails to execute with the error Simulation failed . Test procedures in the IEC Certification Kit are expected to exclude tests when the license that is required to execute a test is not available. When this occurs, the results for the test states EXCLUDED: no <XYZ>  license  in the validation report.?
Workaround: When you don't have a DSP System Toolbox?license:Open test model mDiscreteSignalSize.slx.?Remove subsystem Discrete FIR Filter, which is intended to test the blocks that require DSP System Toolbox.Save the test model and rerun the Simulink Coverage test procedure in the IEC Certification?Kit.?When you don't have a Fixed-Point Designer?license:?Open test models mLookupTables.slx and mSignalAttributes.slx.?Attempt to compile the models.? Remove any subsystems that cause compilation errors (they are intended for testing fixed-point data types).When all fixed-point subsystems are removed and models compile without errors, save the test models.?Rerun the Simulink Coverage test procedure in the IEC Certification?Kit.

Summary: Exporting models with mixed datatypes in bus outputs as tool-coupling FMUs might produce incorrect simulation results
Description: When a model is exported as a tool-coupling FMU, the simulation results from the FMU might be incorrect if the following conditions are true: The model has a bus or nested bus output. The bus or nested bus has elements or sub-bus with mixed datatypes (boolean, double, int...).
Fixed In: R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 1, R2022b

Summary: An atomic subsystem with no inports or outports and containing a referenced model may crash during code generation
Description: When an atomic subsystem with no inports or outports contains a referenced model with a bus input and no outports, the model may crash during code generation.

Summary: Tests that specify "Stop simulation at last time point" and use external inputs for a test harness different than Test Case inputs might error during execution?
Description: When you run a test that has?Stop simulation at last time point ?selected in the Test Manager?Inputs ?section, and you use an external input signal? from a test harness with both of these conditions: The harness fails to compile. The harness is not the one you specified in the Test Manager?System Under Test ?section.  This error occurs: Invalid Simulink object name: <harness_name>.  The block diagram '<harness_name>' is not loaded.
Workaround: Deselect Stop Simulation at Last Time Point in the Test Manager Inputs section.Specify a Stop Time in the Simulation Settings and Release Overrides subsection?of the System Under Test section.
Fixed In: R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 2, R2022b, R2023a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Root-level In Bus Element block with top-level virtual bus and nested nonvirtual bus might produce incorrect code
Description: A Simulink model might generate incorrect code when all the following conditions are true: A root-level In Bus Element block selects a nonvirtual bus from a virtual bus. A Bus Selector block selects a multidimensional leaf signal from the nonvirtual bus. A Selector block selects an element of the multidimensional leaf signal. The selected element of the multidimensional leaf signal passes to an atomic subsystem.
Workaround: Insert a Signal Conversion block after the Bus Selector block that selects the multidimensional leaf signal. Then, double-click the Signal Conversion block and select the?Exclude this block from 'Block reduction' optimization parameter.

Summary: SQO report generation fails when report generator version is older than Polyspace Access version
Description: If you generate an SQO report from Polyspace Access results, and if the version of the Polyspace product that you use to generate the report is older than the Polyspace Access version, the report generation fails. You see an error message similar to this:? Polyspace Report GeneratorLoading resultsError: internal - unable to find check definition for: Defect_AUTOSAR_LIB_NON_COMPLIANCEError: internal - unable to find check definition for: Defect_INCORRECT_VALUE_FORWARDINGError: internal - unable to find check definition for: Defect_NOEXCEPT_FUNCTION_THROWS...?  The issue occurs when the Polyspace Access SQO definition that you use to generate the report contains defect or coding rule checkers that are not available? in the older Polyspace product version.
Workaround: To work around this issue:Log into PolyspaceAccess.Create a custom SQO definition where you select only defect andcoding rule checkers that are available in the older Polyspace product version.Apply the custom SQO definition to the project for which you want to create an SQO report.?Even if you are able generate an SQO report with the original SQO definition, use the custom SQO definition instead to avoid seeing defect and coding rule checkers that are not available in the version of the product used to generate the report.?
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 5, R2022b

Summary: Simscape compilation might fail with the error message "Invalid parameter type set" the first time a model is compiled or simulated
Description: When a Simscape block has a mask initialization function that sets the value of a parameter on the block, simulating the model can result in a confusing error with the message:  Invalid parameter type set at <parameter name>. Expected type ({logical,real,integer,index},~). Got type string.  This happens when a parameter that controls the visibility of other mask parameters is not properly evaluated before compilation.
Workaround: There are two ways that you can work around this issue:1) Call set_param twice in the mask initialization function. For example:>> set_param('circular', 'false');>> set_param('circular', 'false');?2) Call SIM twice and ignore the first error, for example:>> try;? sim(mdl); end;>> sim(mdl)?

Summary: License checkout can fail for Audio Toolbox when using a borrowed license
Description: When using?audioDeviceReader ?with a borrowed license, you might get this error: Unable to check out license for Audio Toolbox.
Workaround: Explicitly check out the borrowed toolbox license before using?audioDeviceReader. Use capital initial letters for the product name:license('checkout','Audio_System_Toolbox') % With the capitalization of ASTAn alternative workaround is to call an Audio Toolbox function before using?audioDeviceReader. For example, callmel2hz(2595)before?audioDeviceReader?to check out the borrowed license.

Summary: Preserve array dimensions property not present in the Custom Storage Class Designer dialog box
Description: In the Custom Storage Class Designer dialog box, the Preserve array dimensions  property is not visible when For signals  property is selected and?For parameters ?property is not selected.
Workaround: Select For parameters even if the storage class is only used for signals.

Summary: MATLAB crashes when running MEX generated by GPU Coder with deep learning network if no GPU installed
Description: Running MEX generated by GPU Coder without a GPU causes CUDA errors. MATLAB crashes when running code generated for deep learning network during resource release. It prints out a message similar to the one below in the stack trace: 0x00007f89ee200ff0 [unknown function] at matlabroot/bin/glnxa64/libcublas.so.11+311280 (no debugging symbols found)

Summary: Reduced Order Flexible Solid block with ten or more interface frames gives erroneous?results
Description: When a Reduced Order Flexible Solid block has ten or more interface frames, the internal mapping between interface frames and connection frame ports on the block is incorrect, giving erroneous simulation results.

Summary: MATLAB might crash for models with multiple callsites of the same function or system object analyzed by Simulink Design Verifier
Description: Simulink Design Verifier might cause MATLAB to crash in rare instances when running Simulink Design Verifier with  Test Generation or Design Error Detection being run with MCDC objective enabled.  Multiple calls to the same function or multiple instances of the same system object in the model.
Fixed In: R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 2, R2022b

Summary: Incorrect simulation results for compartments when unit conversion is enabled
Description: SimBiology might return incorrect simulation results for compartments when all of the following conditions are met: Unit conversion is enabled. The model contains multiple compartments with different units. The model contains nested compartments.  In such cases, SimBiology might use the wrong unit multiplier to convert a compartment capacity from internal units to user-specified units. Internally, the simulation proceeds with the correct units, and the reported simulation results are correct for species and parameters.
Workaround: Work around the problem by doing any of the following:Disable unit conversion.Ensure all compartments have the same units.Convert all nested compartments to top-level compartments.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 3, R2022a, R2022b

Summary: Simulation 3D Probabilistic Radar mounts incorrectly when setting a non-zero?Relative rotation?parameter
Description: If you select the Simulation 3D Probablistic Radar block Specify offset ?parameter and set?Relative? rotation ?to a non-zero value, the block incorrecly mounts the sensor.
Workaround: Apply a degree-to-radian conversion to the?Relative rotation [Roll, Pitch, Yaw]?values. For example,? to provide a 60 deg roll, 45 deg pitch, and 10 deg yaw offset, set?Relative rotation [Roll, Pitch, Yaw]? to?deg2rad([60, 45, 10]).The conversion mounts sensor correctly in the 3D environment and corrects the sensor output detections. However, the Birds Eye Scope may not correctly provide the field-of-view (FOV).

Summary: Signal label mismatch during test harness compliation
Description: If you have a test harness for a subsystem whose output signal is explicitly named, and signal label propagation is enabled, this error might occur during compilation:Signal label mismatch: <harness_name>/Subsystem output port 1 label <signal_name>, should match <harness_name>/Output Conversion Subsystem output port 1 label <signal_name><propagated_signal_name>.
Workaround: 1) Manually set Show propagated signals?to ON?for the?signals that are outputs of the signal conversion subsystem.2)? Set Signal label mismatch?to none in the?Diagnostics?> Connectivity?category of Model Configuration Parameters.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 3, R2022b

Summary: Loss of test objectives when performing test case generation in C/C++ S-Functions and Simulink Custom Code blocks
Description: Loss of all test objectives for functions in Custom code/C Caller blocks that have name longer that 100 characters. You may notice different results for two functions with the same body but with different names. This scenario may happen in a workflow where function names are automatically generated/mangled and where the name are long.?

Summary: Reading map data from HERE HD Live Map web service can cause error
Description: MATLAB can issue an error when you read map data from the HERE HD Live Map web service using these options: The Driving Scenario Designer app The roadNetwork  object function of the drivingScenario  object The read  object function of the hereHDLMReader  object  An example of the error when constructing hereHDLMReader object is:? The '' protocol specified in URL, 'catalogs?verbose=1&organizationType=here&layerType=versioned&q=hdlm-protobuf', is not supported. Specify the URL with the protocol 'http://' or 'https://'.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 3, R2022a Update 1, R2022b

Summary: The Model output plot of the System Identification app may show the model response with zero initial conditions rather than estimated initial conditions
Description: If the user launches the Model output plot with no active models (all the models in the app are in the inactive mode), the plot initially shows no curves (as expected). Then, if the user activates a model by clicking the model icon, the app computes the response using a setting of 'zero' for initial conditions rather than 'estimate'. This is a bug since, by default, all the model responses should be computed by estimating the initial conditions to maximize the fit to the validation output data.
Workaround: Launch the Model output plot with at least one model active.

Summary: JMAAB check for?jc_0627?displays abnormal exit warning for Discrete-Time Integrator blocks
Description: The?Sub-ID b? of??Check usage of Discrete-Time Integrator block? (id:?mathworks.jmaab.jc_0627 )? displays abnormal exit warning for Discrete-Time Integrator  blocks when both Upper and Lower saturation limits  are defined by parameter objects whose data types are not auto .
Fixed In: R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 1, R2022b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Concatenating empty variable-sized signals might produce incorrect results
Description: Incorrect code generation and rapid accelerator simulation might occur when Vector Concatenate blocks accept empty variable-sized signals as inputs.See the attached model for an example. The model contains a Vector Concatenate block which accepts a variable-sized signal from an Enabled Subsystem as an input. The block concatenates the variable-sized signal with another signal. When the Enabled subsystem is disabled, it passes an empty output, and as a result the Concatenate block just passes through the other signal. The incorrect generated C code from the example model: ? repro_DW.concatenateBlk_DIMS1 = repro_DW.Enabled_SS._DIMS1[0] + 1;  ? loop_ub = repro_DW.Enabled_SS._DIMS1[0] * repro_DW.Enabled_SS._DIMS1[1] - 1;  ? for (i = 0; i <= loop_ub; i++) { // -------------------> When the output of Enabled Subsystem is empty, this loop is never executed  ? ? tmp_data = repro_B.Enabled_SS.u; // -----------------> This is trying to copy output of Enabled Subsystem to tmp_data  ? }  ? repro_B.concatenateBlk[0] = tmp_data; // --------------> tmp_data is still copied to output of Concatenate block  ? repro_B.concatenateBlk[1] = repro_P.Constant3_Value;  The generated code treats the empty signal as non-empty and incorrectly copies the signal to the output of the Concatenate block.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results might occur for?a model containing a Merge block and a Unit Delay block or signals specified to have a Reusable custom storage class
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets either of the following scenarios: Scenario 1: The model contains a Merge block and a Unit Delay block. The outputs of a set of conditionally executed subsystems are feeding into a Merge block. The output of the Merge block is connected to a Unit Delay block and then fed back to the input(s) of any of the conditionally executed subsystems. The input(s) of the conditionally executed subsystems is/are used in a computation generating an output different from the one connected to the Merge block. The execution of that output happens after the execution of the output connected to the Merge block.  Scenario 2: The model contains a Merge block. The outputs of a set of conditionally executed subsystem are feeding into a Merge block. The output of the Merge block is specified to have a Reusable custom storage class. The input of any of these subsystems is specified to have a Reusable custom storage class with the same variable name as the output of the Merge block. The input(s) of the conditionally executed subsystems is/are used in a computation generating an output different from the one connected to the Merge block. The execution of that output happens after the execution of the output connected to the Merge block.?
Workaround: Connect a Signal Conversion block to the output of the Merge block. In the Signal Conversion block parameters dialog box, select the Exclude this block from 'Block reduction' optimization parameter.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 2, R2023a

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier may incorrectly show?Incompatible for models with arrays of complex numbers as output or input to function calls
Description: Simulink Design Verifier may incorrectly show?Incompatible  for models that meet the following conditions:The model contains arrays of complex numbers? The complex array is either the input or the output of a function call The complex array is not a simple variable?  For example, the following pattern may cause the model to be incorrectly shown as incompatible:? obj.complexArray = functionCall(obj.complexArray);  However, the following pattern will not be shown as incompatible:? complexArray = functionCall(complexArray);
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 3, R2022a

Summary: MATLAB may crash if an invalid CAD File is input to a File Solid block and the Convex Hull?output is selected.
Description: If the?File?Name ?input refers to an invalid or unsupported CAD file, and the Convex Hull  output is used by a Spatial Contact Force block, MATLAB may crash when running the simulation. The File Solid block cannot read CAD drawing or assembly files, and supports the following versions of CAD formats:? ACIS - Up to 2019 Autodesk Inventor - Up to 2020 CATIA V4 - Up to 4.2.5 CATIA V5 - Up to V5-6 R2019 (R29) CATIA V6 - Up to V5-6 R2019 (R29) Creo-Pro/E - Pro/Engineer 19.0 to Creo 6.0 I-deas - Up to 13.x (Nx5), Nx I-deas 6 IGES - 5.1, 5.2, 5.3 NX - V11.0 to NX 12.0, and 1899 Parasolid - Up to v32.0 Revit - 2015 to 2020 Rhino3D - 4, 5, 6 Solid Edge - V19 - 20, ST - ST10, 2020 SolidWorks - From 97 to 2020 STEP - AP 203 E1/E2, AP 214, AP 242?
Workaround: Validate the CAD file in the File Solid block visualization pane prior to running the contact simulation.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a, R2022b

Summary: Unexpected error may occur during HDL Code Generation for a large model that uses the NCO HDL Optimized block
Description: HDL code generation may error out for a model that uses the NCO HDL Optimized block with frame-based output. This error happens for bigger models like the Wireless HDL Toolbox reference application models. Before R2022a, this block was named NCO HDL Optimized, and was included in the DSP System Toolbox HDL Support library. Starting in R2022a, the block is named NCO and is in the DSP HDL Toolbox library. The error message is:? Error using hdlcoder.pirctx/addZeroProtection  Unexpected Error during HDL Code Generation.  Contact support@mathworks.com  with reproduction? steps. Assertion Failed With Debug Message:  pir_transforms/PropagateInitVal.cpp:205:getInitVal(s) == InitVal::NOT_VISITED?
Fixed In: R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 1, R2022b

Summary: Highlighting analysis results may take long time or throw an error for models containing Simulink Function blocks connected in a loop
Description: If a model contains Simulink Function  blocks connected in a loop and if design error detection analysis is performed on one such block, then highlighting analysis results may have one of the following behavior: If highlighting is performed?from the Results Summary  window, then it may take a long time and the text InspectorUtils.extendRangeInfo  may be displayed in the MATLAB Command Window If highlighting is performed using sldvhighlight  API, then the following error may be thrown:Out of memory. The likely cause is an infinite recursion within the program.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: MinMax block might produce incorrect results in accelerator and rapid accelerator modes with NaN input and zero-crossing detection
Description: The MinMax block produces incorrect results in accelerator and rapid accelerator modes when the following conditions are true: Model solver is variable-step. MinMax block Enable zero crossing detection  is selected. MinMax block input has continuous sample time. Input to MinMax block has double data type. One of these is true: MinMax block has only one input and the first element of the input is NaN. For example, input [NaN 1] can output NaN, which is an incorrect result. MinMax block has multiple inputs and the first input has a NaN.
