For example, for a MinMax block with two inputs, the first input has an input of NaN and the second input has a 1, the resulting incorrect result is a NaN.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 3, R2022a, R2022b

Summary: Excessive array expression hoisting when generating PLC code
Description: Users may encounter statements like the following when generating PLC code for certain models: temp1 := testCycleNum;cycle_U := tb_U[temp1];temp2 := testCycleNum;cycle_Y := tb_Y[temp2];  Here, the temporary variables are unnecessary and yet they are created just so that every array index expression is hoisted.?

Summary: MATLAB might crash when it is launched in non interactive mode using -batch option
Description: When launching MATLAB in non interactive mode using -batch option, MATLAB might sporadically crash.

Summary: IEC Certification Kit may exclude some Simulink Design Verifier validation tests cases due to timeout
Description: The Simulink Design Verifier analysis time might exceed the IEC Certification Kit test execution threshold. In this situation, the IEC Certification Kit?excludes the affected test cases. In the validation test report, it sets the validation status for these tests to: Executed Tests Passed, Unexecuted Tests Need Review .

Summary: ?Import operation in?Mixed-Signal Analyzer?app is highly time consuming.
Description: Importing Cadence??simulation results in the?Mixed-Signal Analyzer ?app takes equal amount of time as generating those data in Cadence??ADE (Analog Design Environment). Currently, whether you import the data in the app by generating a mat file and then load it in the app or directly import the data through Cadence??database, both methods take significant amount of time.

Summary: When using a perpetual license, the License Borrowing UI does not allow you to borrow licenses for more than two days.
Description: When using a perpetual license, the License Borrowing UI does not allow you to borrow licenses for more than two days.
Workaround: Borrow licenses using the command linein MATLAB instead of using the License Borrowing UI. The borrow feature command allowsyou to borrow a feature for a length of time by specifying the returndate.To check out one feature at a time, run the borrow feature command in MATLAB :system_dependent('lmBorrowFeature',?'MATLAB',?'20-feb-2022 23:59')Replace the values 'MATLAB' and '20-feb-2022' with the feature name and the return date for the feature you want to borrow. Feature names are quite specific. To get a list of feature names, run the command? >>system_dependent('lmfeaturelist').The date must be in ˇ®dd-mmm-yyyyˇŻ format, and you must provide a timestamp.This command will throw an error for invalid arguments, for example, incorrect or invalid product names or dates, missing timestamp, or if the user is on an exclude list for borrowing.?

Summary: JMAAB check for jc_0753 does not report Transition Actions not enclosed in curly braces
Description: The?Sub-ID a1 ?of Model Advisor check jc_0753_a1: Check transition actions in Stateflow charts?(id: mathworks.jmaab.jc_0753)? doesn't report warning for Transition Actions  not enclosed within curly braces in C Action Language chart .
Fixed In: R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 3, R2022a, R2022b

Summary: After loading a System Composer model, this error appears: Uniqueness Violation
Description: When opening or loading a System Composer model, the error: Uniqueness Violation  is shown in the MATLAB command window or in the Diagnostic Viewer. This error can occur under the following conditions: The System Composer model contains at least one component which is a reference to another System Composer model. The referenced System Composer model has been updated by renaming a port at the root of the model or deleting and re-adding a root port with the same name.

Summary: Build Failure in LMS Filter System object and Block when 'DSP Intel AVX2-FMA' Code Replacement Library (CRL) is Usedˇ±
Description: When you use the  ˇ®DSP Intel AVX2-FMA (Windows/Linux/Mac)ˇŻ?code replacement library (CRL) to generate SIMD code from LMS Filter System object? and block for C++ targets, the build process fails with this error:   undefined reference to `MWSPCGlmsnb_S_avx2(float const*, float const*, float, unsigned int*, float*, float*, int, float, int, float*, float*, float*, unsigned char)'  collect2: error: ld returned 1 exit status
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 3, R2022a, R2022b

Summary: Using function syntax hints in a user-defined function can cause an error
Description: If the help text in a user-defined function has paragraphs that begin with tabs instead of being indented with spaces, typing the function name at the Command Window and waiting for the code suggestion causes an error in the Command Window.
Workaround: Replace tabs at the beginning of help text paragraphs with spaces.

Summary: Incorrect Unsatisfiable objectives for MCDC objectives where the overall condition is always false
Description: Simulink Design Verifier might report incorrect Unsatisfiable (or Dead Logic)  objectives for MCDC objectives when the following conditions are met: The MCDC objective is part of a MATLAB System or MATLAB Function block. The overall expression for a || or && operation is always false.?  When the overall expression is?A && B , when this issue is experienced, all MCDC objectives are reported as Unsatisfiable : A && B with A true  A && B with A false  A && B with B true  A && B with B false?  However,  if A can be false, "A && B with A false " should be reported as Satisfiable?  if B can be false (when A can be true), "A && B with B false " should be reported as Satisfiable    On the other hand, when the overall expression is?A || B , when this issue is experienced, all MCDC objectives are reported as?Unsatisfiable : A || B with A true  A?||?B with A false  A?||?B with B true  A?||?B with B false?  However, "A || B with A false " should be reported as?Satisfiable?  "A || B with B false " should be reported as?Satisfiable
Fixed In: R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a

Summary: JMAAB check for db_0032 reports incorrect warning for signal lines
Description: The Sub-ID b  of Model Advisor check db_0032_b: Check signal overlaps (id: mathworks.jmaab.db_0032)? displays the following warning for signal lines that do not overlap with other signal lines in the model diagram.Warning :  The following signals overlap with other signals in the diagram.

Summary: Cosmetic misalignment in comparison results for modified model configuration sets or internal test harnesses in published HTML reports
Description: When comparing Simulink models with modified model configuration sets or internal test harnesses, resulting changes in the published HTML report are untidy, with inconsistent alignment. This is easy to notice when it happens and does not affect the interpretation of the results of the comparison.

Summary: Error in computing higher order derivatives with dlfeval/dlgradient when using a convolution layer with 3D image inputs
Description: Computing higher order derivatives of a convolution layer with 3D inputs using dlfeval/dlgradient throws an error. This affects some workflows that require higher order derivatives with 3D images - for example, training a WGAN-GP model on 3D images.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a, R2022b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect simulation results in accelerator and rapid accelerator modes for?a model containing a Bus Creator block
Description: Incorrect simulation results are possible in accelerator and rapid accelerator modes for a model that meets these conditions: The model contains a Bus Creator block inside a Conditional or an Enabled subsystem. An input to the Bus Creator block has Inf  sample time.

Summary: Experiment Manager displays incorrect training loss values
Description: When you set the training option OutputNetwork to best-validation-loss , then Experiment Manager returns the trained network with the lowest validation loss, but displays the training loss for the last training iteration. The displayed training loss might not equal the training loss for the network. If you run a Bayesian Optimization experiment to optimize the training loss, then the Experiment Manager passes the incorrect training loss value to the optimization algorithm producing incorrect results.

Summary: C++ and AUTOSAR code generation may not reflect Shared utilities identifier format setting
Description: The model configuration parameter Shared utilities identifier format , also known asCustomSymbolStrUtil  when configured programmatically, may not be used properly by Embedded Coder to generate code for a model?if the following conditions are true: The model uses autosar.tlc  or system target files derived from it or uses ert.tlc  with the Language  parameter set to C++ . Code was previously?generated for the model and artifacts from that codegeneration still exist. The?CustomSymbolStrUtil ?setting was changed (by using set_param ) since the code was previously generated.
Workaround: Togenerate code correctly for an AUTOSAR model, delete the following artifacts that may have been produced when code was previously generated: The     code generation directory (for example,     <modelName>_autosar_rtw?) The     relevant subdirectory of the slprj directory (for example,     slprj/autosar/<modelName>) The     Simulink cache file for the model (for example, <modelName>.slxc)After you haveremoved the previous code generation artifacts, generate the code again.?To generate code correctly for an ERT C++ model, delete the following artifacts that may have been produced when code was previously generated:The code generation directory (for example,?<modelName>_ert_rtw?)The relevant subdirectory of the slprj directory (for example,?slprj/ert/<modelName>)The Simulink cache file for the model (for example,?<modelName>.slxc)?After you have removed the previous code generation artifacts, generate the code again.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 2, R2022b

Summary: Simulation mismatch between numerical SIL and normal mode for models that use AUTOSAR 4.0 code replacement library for the function Mfx_ConvertP2
Description: Numerical SIL and normal mode simulation results may be different when simulating an AUTOSAR model using an AUTOSAR 4.0 code replacement library that triggers code replacement for the function Mfx_ConvertP2  (for example, a fixed-point conversion from one radix to another).?

Summary: In Signal Editor, Draw Signal context menu stays visible even after clicking Close Draw Signal
Description: Upon closing the Draw?Signal  tab in Signal Editor, the corresponding context menu remains visible in the toolstrip with broken menu actions.

Summary: Root Inport Mapper freezes when saving MAT-file with same name as any variable in From Workspace link dialog box
Description: When importing data through the From Workspace link dialog, the Root Inport Mapper stops responding if the specified MAT-file name matches any of the workspace variable names being imported.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results might occur when a Matrix Concatenate?block is connected to a Model block
Description: The generated code might be incorrect when a model contains a Matrix Concatenate?block connected to a Model block.
Workaround: Insert a Signal Conversion block between the?Matrix Concatenate?and the Model block, and select the?Exclude this block from 'Block reduction' optimization?property of the Signal Conversion block.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a, R2022b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: The initial condition of an output port block may be ignored if the output port block is inside an inactive variant subsystem block.
Description: An output port block inside an inactive variant subsystem may be removed according to the configuration of the variant subsystem block. This may lead to the loss of initial condition and cause answer mismatch while simulating in normal or rapid accelerator modes as the behavior when the output port block is removed in these two modes could be different according to the setting.?
Fixed In: R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 2, R2022b

Summary: HDL IP core generation using Xilinx Vivado fails as of January 1, 2022
Description: Since January 1, 2022, IP core generation fails when the synthesis tool is set to Xilinx Vivado. The following error message can be observed in the HDL Workflow Advisor Task 3.2:? Failed Task "Vivado IP Packager" unsuccessful. See log for details. Generated logfile: hdl_prj\hdlsrc\<myModel> \workflow_task_VivadoIPPackager.log  The log file at \hdl_prj\hdlsrc\<myModel>\workflow_task_VivadoIPPackager.log  reports an error message similar to the following: bad lexical cast: source type value could not be interpreted as target  ? ?while executing"rdi::set_property core_revision 2201031458 {component component_1}"  ? ?invoked from within  "set_property core_revision 2201031458 [ipx::current_core]"  ? ?(file "vivado_ip_package.tcl" line 57)
Workaround: Installation instructions  Log into an account with the appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.  Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps. On Mac systems, the folder name will have a .app extension, e.g. /Applications/MATLAB_R20XXa.app  Exit MATLAB.Locate the following file under your <MATLAB> folder and rename the file to "IPEmitterVivado_backup.p":<MATLAB>\toolbox\hdlcoder\hdlcommon\+hdlturnkey\+ip\IPEmitterVivado.pWindows Systems:?You might be prompted to provide Administrator privileges when renaming this file.  Download and unzip the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report.In the unzipped folder, navigate to the subfolder that matches the release you are using. For example, if you are using the R2021b release, open the R2021b subfolder.If you do not see a subfolder that matches the release you are using,?contact MathWorks Technical Support.In this folder, there is a newer version of the?IPEmitterVivado.p file. Copy this file and paste it in the location mentioned above.Windows Systems:?You might be prompted to provide Administrator privileges when pasting this file.Restart MATLAB.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 3, R2022a, R2022b

Summary: SFStudio.internal.variantTransitionRF?error when selecting a transition in Stateflow
Description: Quickly changing the selection to and from a Stateflow transition might cause the MATLAB command prompt to show a spurious error message that contains the following: MATLAB stack trace:  > In SFStudio.internal.variantTransitionRF  Warning: An error has occurred during refresh of Action named variantTransitionAction.?  Warning: Generator should return a DAStudio.ToolSchema.?  Warning: object[id]->isa == isa

Summary: Aggregating coverage data for model and subsystem harnesses might error
Description: Performing unit-to-system test coverage aggregation might error if all the following conditions are true:  You have a test case for the top-level model. You have test cases for 2 or more subsystem harnesses. One of the subsystem harness test cases includes a change that causes coverage data to not be compatible for aggregation with the top-level model. An example of such a change is altering a variant control or changing a signal datatype  When you run your test cases using Simulink Test Manager, MATLAB shows an error similar to: Operands to the logical AND (&&) and OR (||) operators must be convertible to logical scalar values.  Use the ANY or ALL functions to reduce operands to logical scalar values.   Alternatively, you might see a warning such as:   Warning: Object 239 is not in the list of objects to be saved => saved as zero.   > In SlCov. CoverageAPI. saveCoverage>save_a_model (line 384)   In SlCov. CoverageAPI. saveCoverage (line 135)   In cvsave (line 19)

Summary: Error occurs when closing an App Designer app while execution is paused due to a call to uiwait in the startupFcn for the app
Description: The below error occurs when closing an app that has the HandleVisiblity  property of the figure set to 'callback' ?when execution is paused due to a call to uiwait  in the app's startupFcn . This is likely to be the case for multiwindow apps where the primary app launches a secondary app that calls?uiwait  in its?startupFcn .Warning: The following error was caught while executing 'onCleanup' class destructor:  Error using matlab. ui. Figure/ setInvalid or deleted object.   Error in appdesigner. internal. service. AppManagementService>@()set(fig, 'HandleVisibility', 'callback') (line 150)?c = onCleanup(@()set(fig,  'HandleVisibility',  'callback'));  Error in onCleanup/ delete (line 80) obj. task();
Workaround: There are two workarounds:In the app with the call to?uiwait?in the?startupFcn, change the HandleVisiblity property of the figure to 'on'.?If it is necessary to keep the HandleVisiblity?of the figure?set to 'callback', instead, in the app with the call to uiwait in the startupFcn do the following:Add a CloseRequestFcn callback to the figure.In the function body of the CloseRequestFcn callback, replace the call to delete(app) with uiresume(app.UIFigure).In the primary app, when calling the secondary app, get a handle to the secondary app and delete the secondary app on the next line.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 3, R2022a, R2022b

Summary: MATLAB crash at startup caused by an invalid preferences settings file
Description: MATLAB can crash at startup if there is an invalid settings file in the user's preferences folder.
Workaround: Remove the contents of the user's preferences folder.

Summary: MATLAB crashes during model update with Variant Connector, Simscape Bus and Variant Subsystem block with connection port
Description: MATLAB crashes during model update phase when all belowconditions are met: Model contains Simscape Bus  block and VariantSubsystem  with connection port. Model contains Variant Connector  block or PropagateConditions outside of Variant Subsystem  is on for Variant Subsystem . In the?Variant Subsystem  block, portsname on choice block and its corresponding port name on variant subsystem blockdiffers in whitespaces only.  Sample crash snippet : [? 0]0x00007f51b47e1eca sltopo::XformableTopo::adjacentPorts(slPort const*) const[? 1]0x00007f51b193e507 sltopo::adjacentPortsOf(slPort const*, sltopo::XformableTopoconst [? 2]0x00007f51b193e878 HierStringPropagator::PropagateHierStringFromBlk(SLBlockconst*)?
Workaround: Configure the Variant Subsystem such that the name of the ports on choice block matches the corresponding port name on variant subsystem block.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: When generating HDL code using the "MATLAB Datapath" architecture of a?MATLAB function block, HDL Coder can produce incorrect code for logical indexing expressions used in size identification functions
Description: When generating HDL code using the "MATLAB Datapath" architecture of a?MATLAB function block, HDL Coder can produce incorrect code for logical indexing expressions. A logical indexing expression used inside of a size identification function can result in incorrect code generation. Some examples of size identification functions are?length ,?numel , and?size .  For example, this expression produces incorrect generated code: numel(x(x>10))

Summary: Generated HDL code for RS Decoder block includes a combinational loop
Description: While running HDL synthesis for the RS Decoder block, the synthesis tool returns combinational loop warnings. This warning results in incorrect timing analysis. For reliable behavior on hardware, this condition should be avoided in HDL designs.

Summary: Performance regression in access, link, and fieldOfView functions when using satellite scenario
Description: A performance regression was introduced in the access, link, and fieldOfView functions. The regression is noticeable when calling these functions repeatedly in a loop, as demonstrated by the following code: % Create a satellite scenario object  sc = satelliteScenario;  % Add 16 satellites  sat = satellite(sc,ones(1,16)*10000000,zeros(1,16),ones(1,16)*80,[0:15]*22.5,zeros(1,16),repmat([45 135 225 315],1,4));  % Time the creation of 16 satellites?  tic  for idx = 1:16  ? ?for idx2 = idx:16  ? ? ? ?access(sat(idx),sat(idx2));  ? ?end  end  toc ?

Summary: Group-specific variants and doses are not included when copying a Fit Data program in SimBiology Model Analyzer
Description: If you create a copy of a Fit Data  program, the group-specific variants and doses specified in the original program are not copied over.

Summary: Simulink Real-Time simulation may produce an error if Measure function execution times is selected
Description: Consider a model that contains an S-function with methods that use an empty code block. If you select the model configuration parameter?Measure function execution times ?and run a Simulink Real-Time simulation, the simulation may produce this error: Array indices must be positive integers or logic values.

Summary: Incorrect error during code generation of Variant Subsystem with startup variant activation time
Description: Variantblocks (Variant Subsystem , Variant Source , Variant Sink ) in startup variantactivation time with variant control expressions containing TRUE /FALSE  keywordsthrows the below error message during code generationUnexpected or internal error encountered in "in CGIR: B:\matlab\src\cg_ir\export\include\cg_ir\tag\VariantIfExprToSLSymbolicExprTag.hpp line 61: In getSymbolicExprId: A if expr without tag found!

Summary: Aerospace 3DOF body axes blocks impose positive initial velocity
Description: The Aerospace Blockset 3DOF equations of motion blocks require that the?Initial Velocity ?parameter be positive for the body axes (i.e. 3DOF (Body Axes), Simple Variable Mass 3DOF (Body Axes), and Custom Variable Mass 3DOF (Body Axes)) blocks.?
Workaround: Try changing the inertial frame of reference of the block into one for which the initial velocity is positive.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Inconsistent simulation and code generation results when input to log or log10 is negative
Description: When a Simulink model contains computation of log  or log10   and the input to this computation is negative, The result of this computation is likely NaN  in Normal mode, and The result of this computation might be -inf  in Accelerator mode, Rapid Accelerator mode, and generated code.  This computation of log  or log10 might come directly from a Math Function block, or indirectly from other logic that computes   log  or log10.     This issue affects Simulink, Simulink Coder, and Embedded Coder.
Fixed In: R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 1, R2022b

Summary: Signal Multiresolution Analyzer does not update frequencies for all decompositions when Time is changed
Description: The Level Selection ?pane in the Signal Multiresolution Analyzer  app has a column that shows the frequency bands of the selected decomposition. If you change Time, the app only updates the frequency bands of the selected decomposition. The app does not update the frequency bands of the other decompositions. For example, suppose you have MODWT and EMD decompositions in the Decomposed Signals ?pane and are working in samples. If you select the MODWT decomposition and specify a sample rate, the app updates the frequency bands in the Level Selection  pane and expresses them in hertz. If you then select the EMD decomposition, the app updates the frequency units but not the frequency values. The reported values are still in cycles/sample.
Workaround: Select the decomposition, then change and set Time again. The app will update the Level Selection pane with the correct frequencies.

Summary: Assertion failure when generating code with incompatible variable sized matrices and?EnableImplicitExpansion?is turned off
Description: In rare cases, when generating code, MATLAB Coder may produce an assertion failure for dll/lib/exe  targets, if the MATLAB program contains the coding pattern satisfying the following conditions: Binary operation with one variable-sized operand, for example, A - B where B is variable-sized. Size of operand B is known at compile-time. Sizes of A and B are not compatible. EnableImplicitExpansion ?is turned off.  The assertion error contains the following text: Assertion at B:\matlab\src\cgir_support\dom_core\analysis\range\RangeValueMath.cpp line 230: In x_binary: Assertion failed: aLeftRange.isScalar() || aRightRange.isScalar() || aLeftRange.getNumberOfValues() == aRightRange.getNumberOfValues()  For an example of the issue, please see the attached example.

Summary: MATLAB may crash if invalid inputs are given to the Grid Surface block?
Description: If the input?X Grid Vector  or Y Grid Vector ?parameter is invalid, then it could cause the application to terminate when running the simulation. For example, the input?X Grid Vector  parameter?is invalid if it contains Inf  or NaN  entries, or if number of elements in the vector is not equal to the number of rows in the?matrix to?Z Heights  parameter.
Workaround: You can use the?griddedInterpolant?function to check the validity of inputs to the?X Grid Vector,?Y Grid Vector, and Z Height parameters. For example:F = griddedInterpolant({xGridVector, yGridVector}, zHeights)If the inputs are invalid, the griddedInterpolant?function issues an error message that highlights the problem.Passing valid data to the Grid Surface block will avoid the issue.?

Summary: An extra empty signal entry is created in the Dataset output when a model's root Outport has a forward slash in the block name
Description: An extra empty signal entry will be created in the Dataset  output of a model when the following conditions are all true:? The Simulink model has a root Output port with a "/" in the name e.g. my/out1 The model is configured such that the SaveFormat = "Dataset"  The mode is simulated using rapid accelerator mode?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: C++ code generation might produce an incorrect initial value setting for a root outport when using function prototype control
Description: C++ code generation might generate an incorrect initial value for a root outport for a model that meets these conditions: Uses function prototype control Propagates a non-zero constant sample time to a root outport
Workaround: Do not use function prototype control on models where an outport has a constant sample time.

Summary: Math Function block with reciprocal functiongenerates an incorrect HDL code for the?fixed-point inputs that have fractionallength greater than word length
Description: The generated HDL code for a Simulink model might be incorrect when the following settings are applied: The model contains a Math Function?block with reciprocal? function  Architecture is s et to? Math  in the HDL block property for Math Function Reciprocal block  The inputs to the block are fixed-point type with fractional length greater than word length?, such as? sfix8_E13 numerictype(0, 8, 13)  Output data type  parameter is set as other than 'Inherit: Inherit via internal rule'   The incorrect HDL code can result in simulation failures in ModelSim when verifying the generated code.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 1, R2022b

Summary: Logging might sporadically miss samples or contain duplicate samples when the Simulation Data Inspector is open during simulation
Description: Logging might sporadically miss samples or contain duplicate samples. The missing or duplicate samples occur in data logged to the workspace and data logged to the Simulation Data Inspector. Missing or duplicate samples are more likely to occur when the Simulation Data Inspector is open while a simulation is running or paused.?

Summary: During fast restart or runtime update diagram, Simulink reports an error when replacing a Simulink.Signal object with another signal object with the same property values
Description: When performing a fast restart simulation or an update diagram during runtime, Simulink reports an error when a Simulink.Signal  object is replaced with another one with the same property values. The reported error is similar to:The Simulink signal object 'SignalObject1' for output port 1 of block 'model/testBlock' was changed during simulation.
Workaround: If you use initialization scripts before every fast restart simulation run, consider these alternatives:Separate out the Simulink.Signal objects into a separate MAT-file and load them only once before the first simulation run.Write a wrapper around the Simulink.Signal object creation function that can check for equivalent signal objects already in the workspace and not replace them.
Fixed In: R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 3, R2022a

Summary: SimBiology projects saved with an empty plot between R2019b and R2020b do not load in later releases
Description: SimBiology projects saved with empty plots between R2019b and R2020b might error when you load them in later releases.
Workaround: Open the project in the release it was created in, delete any empty plots, and resave the project.

Summary: Warning when loading pretrained ivectorSystem?while using speakerRecognition
Description: When you attempt to download and use the pretrained ivectorSystem ?while using speakerRecognition , the following unnecessary warning appears: Warning: While loading an object of class 'audioFeatureExtractor':      A field named 'pFeaturesToExtract' doesn't exist.   You can ignore this warning.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Initialization code might be incorrect for?a model containing a block that has an output with non-zero initial conditions
Description: The generated code in the initialization function might be incorrect for a model that meets the following conditions: The model contains a block or a subsystem that has an output with non-zero initial conditions. The model also contains a Bus Creator block inside a Conditional or Enabled subsystem. The output with non-zero initial conditions is connected to the input of the Bus Creator block.

Summary: Running multiple test cases with iterations in Fast Restart throws errors.
Description: Running test cases that use the same model but different test harnesses errors when using Fast Restart with iterations.

Summary: Polyspace compilation issues when using compiler armclang
Description: If you run an analysis using compiler armclang  in Polyspace version R2021b, that analysis might fail to compile if your project uses CMSIS headers. You see an error message similar to this: error: #error directive: "ACLE intrinsics support not enabled."| #error "ACLE intrinsics support not enabled."|? ^  The issue occurs because these macros are not defined in Polyspace R2021b:? __ARM_ACLE:  Defines the ARM C Language Extensions (ACLE) level used by the compiler. ARM headers do not compiler if this macro is undefined. __ARM_ARCH : Specifies the version of the Arm instruction set architecture. __ARM_ARCH_PROFILE : Specifies the profile of the target architecture.
Workaround: Use option -D to manually define the macros:-D __ARM_ACLE=200 -D __ARM_ARCH=7?-D __ARM_ARCH_PROFILE='M'

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect simulation result might occur when using fixed point data type in S-Function Builder
Description: Simulink might produce incorrect simulation results when you use fixed point signals in your S-Function Builder block, and the data type override is set to a value other than off .
Workaround: First, manually edit the generated source file for the S-function.Open the generated source fileIn the mdlInitializeSizes method, update the data type override flag to 0. The override flag is the last field in function ssRegisterDataTypeFxpBinaryPoint or ssRegisterDataTypeFxpSlopeBias. For example:DTypeId DataTypeId_0 = ssRegisterDataTypeFxpBinaryPoint(?S,?OUT_0_ISSIGNED,?OUT_0_WORDLENGTH,?OUT_0_FRACTIONLENGTH,?0 );Then, recompile the source file before running the simulation.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 3, R2022b

Summary: Boolean type custom attributes are imported from DOORS Next as enumerated type
Description: When importing requirements from IBM DOORS Next to Requirements Toolbox, requirements with custom attributes of boolean data type are imported as enumerated data type instead. This is viewed as a combo boxbased custom attribute instead of a checkbox based custom attribute.
Fixed In: R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results are possible for a model containing an Enabled subsystem that has an Discrete-Time Integrator block
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets these conditions:The model contains an Enabled subsystem. The Enable port?block has the?States when enabling ?parameter set to?reset.  The subsystem contains an Discrete-Time Integrator block that has the Initial condition source  parameter set to external .  A Constant block serves as the external initial condition source of the Discrete-Time Integrator block.?
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 7, R2022a, R2022b

Summary: Linearization of Simscape model can return NaN
Description: Simscape models involving modecharts or events may produce NaN for the linearization if the model has a conditionally high differential index.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 3, R2022a, R2022b

Summary: When training data is a single cell array with multiple observations, Experiment Manager fails to generate a confusion matrix
Description: When you use a single cell array with multiple observations as training data, Experiment Manager trains the network correctly, but fails to generate a confusion matrix.

Summary: Out-of-memory error for two-output call to svd in some cases
Description: For an m -by-n  matrix X  with n >> m , it is possible for these svd  calls to throw an out-of-memory error: [U,S] = svd(X);[U,S] = svd(X,'econ');[U,S] = svd(X,0); The error occurs in cases where it is not possible to allocate a temporary matrix of size n -by-n .
Workaround: Use the command[U,S,~] = svd(X,'econ');instead, padding the matrix S with zeros if necessary.

Summary: Possible simulation failure when a flexible body with three or more frames is part of a kinematic loop.
Description: When a flexible body has three or more frames, and that flexible body is part of a kinematic loop, the following error may be thrown during the simulation: Kinematic constraints cannot be maintained. Check solver type and consistency tolerance in the Simscape Solver Configuration block. Check Simulink solver type and tolerances in Model Configuration Parameters. A kinematic singularity might be the source of this problem.  This can occur when there is in fact no constraint violation.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 3, R2022a, R2022b

Summary: Simulink simulations run in a parallel pool with the Bit Error Rate Analysis app can result in an unexpectedly high BER
Description: The Bit Error Rate Analysis  app can run Simulink simulations in parallel when Acceleration > Parallel pool  is set to Auto  and a Parallel Computing Toolbox license is available. When running in this parallel simulation configuration, the app results in a constant bit error rate (BER) of approximately 40%.

Summary: MATLAB crashes when cached?Simulink.SuppressedDiagnostic?objects are accessed when the model is closed
Description: When you query the diagnostic suppressions of a model using the MATLAB Command Window, Simulink creates Simulink.SuppressedDiagnostic  objects in the workspace. These objects contain information related to diagnostic warnings or errors that are suppressed from being thrown during simulation and update diagram.?Simulink.SuppressedDiagnostic ?objects?also contain information about the source of these suppressions. When you cache Simulink.SuppressedDiagnostic ?objects associated with a model, close the model, then access the cached?Simulink.SuppressedDiagnostic ?objects when the the model is no longer accessible, MATLAB will crash.

Summary: Generating code for a subsystem that takes input from a muxed source might cause invalid index error
Description: When you generate code for a model that contains a subsystem that takes input from a muxed source (for example, a Mux block or a virtual Bus Creator block), the code generator might throw an error like the following: Internal error while creating code interface description file: codeInfo.mat. Aborting code generation.     Caused by:     Invalid index.?
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: UTF-8 characters outside of ASCII range emitted as unexpected characters
Description: The presence of non-ASCII characters in a null terminated string causes unexpected characters to be generated?in MATLAB Code: function x = foo()   x = [char(124) char(125) char(126) char(127) char(128) char(0)]; % <------- char(128) is non-ASCIIend>> codegen foo  The code generator throws this warning:foo.c:18:31: warning: initializer-string for array of 'char' is too long? and produces this code in foo.c :static const char_T cv[6] = "|}~?";??// <-------- notice the unexpected character in generated code
Fixed In: R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 2, R2022b

Summary: MATLAB might throw an error when applying a coverage filter
Description: When you apply a coverage filter in the Coverage Results Explorer, you might get an error that looks like:  Error occurred when evaluating apply callback.   This error can happen for models containing charts, subsystems, or custom library blocks.

Summary: Initialization error when using Hysteresis MAT files generated from Transformer Hysteresis Design Tool app in Simscape Electrical Specialized Power Systems
Description: If you use the Hysteresis MAT files generated from the Transformer Hysteresis Design Tool  app in Simscape Electrical Specialized Power Systems blocks, MATLAB throws this mask initialization error when you run a simulation: Failed to evaluate mask initialization commands.   Caused by:   Unrecognized field name "Fj_sat".?
Workaround: To get the correct MAT file that does not cause error, use the attached function:?resavematfile ?This function receives the filename of the MAT file you want to fix and returns the corrected MAT file.For example, if you have a hysteresis MAT file, at the MATLAB command prompt, enter:? ?resavematfile('hysteresis')?The function generates a MAT file in your current path. The name of the output MAT file is filename_resaved. In this example, the generated MAT file is hysteresis_resaved.Please make sure you are in a writable directory before you execute the function.

Summary: Closing a MATLAB project takes a long time when many link sets are open
Description: If you open many link sets from a MATLAB project and then close the project, the project might take a long time to close.

Summary: Some gas blocks do not correctly update parameter visibility
Description: The Variable Orifice ISO 6358 (G) and Pressure ReducingValve (G) blocks do not correctly update parameter visibility. This occurs for all settings of Valve parameterization  for the? Variable Orifice ISO 6358 (G) block. For the Pressure Reducing Valve (G) block, this occurs when you set?Valve parameterization? to?Cv coefficient (USCS) ,?Kv coefficient (SI) , or?Restriction area ?and switch?Opening parameterization  between?Linear ?and?Tabulated data .
Workaround: To correctly update the parameters, find and execute the command that matches your settings.?If you set the valve or orifice parameterization?to?Cv coefficient (USCS)?and would like to set?Opening parameterization?toLinear:?>> set_param(gcb, ˇ®iso_opening_spec_CvˇŻ, ˇ®fluids.gas.enum.iso_opening_spec.linearˇŻ);Tabulated data:?>> set_param(gcb, ˇ®iso_opening_spec_CvˇŻ, ˇ®fluids.gas.enum.iso_opening_spec.tabulatedˇŻ);If you set the valve or orifice?parameterization??to?Kv coefficient (SI)?and would like to set?Opening parameterization?to?Linear:?>> set_param(gcb, ˇ®iso_opening_spec_KvˇŻ, ˇ®fluids.gas.enum.iso_opening_spec.linearˇŻ);Tabulated data:?>> set_param(gcb, ˇ®iso_opening_spec_KvˇŻ, ˇ®fluids.gas.enum.iso_opening_spec.tabulatedˇŻ);If you set the valve or orifice?parameterization?to Restriction area?and would like to set?Opening parameterization?toLinear:?>> set_param(gcb, ˇ®iso_opening_spec_areaˇŻ, ˇ®fluids.gas.enum.iso_opening_spec.linearˇŻ);Tabulated data:?>> set_param(gcb, ˇ®iso_opening_spec_areaˇŻ, ˇ®fluids.gas.enum.iso_opening_spec.tabulatedˇŻ);

Summary: Incorrect simulation results in rapid accelerator mode for?a model containing a Function-Call Subsystem block
Description: Incorrect simulation results are possible in rapid accelerator mode for a model that meets these conditions: The model contains a Function-Call Subsystem block. The Function-Call Subsystem has?an inplace specification (that is, the same input and output variable).
Fixed In: R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 3, R2022a, R2022b

Summary: MATLAB Parallel Server jobs on Windows HPC Pack 2019 frequently appear failed on completion
Description: Jobs submitted using Parallel Computing Toolbox to a MATLAB Parallel Server cluster running Windows HPC Pack 2019 will often appear to have failed at completion. This might happen for every job or only for some jobs. The job and tasks will have state 'failed'  in both MATLAB and in the HPC Cluster Manager, even if the submitted work has completed successfully. Any outputs are still available via the OutputArguments  property of the Task  object.
Workaround: To fetch the outputs of a job that is failed:outputs = [job.Tasks.OutputArguments].';

Summary: HDL Coder generates an internal assertion when Flatten Hierarchy is turned On for the Filter blocks.
Description: HDL Coder generates the following internal assertion when you turned On?Flatten Hierarchy ?for the Filter blocks. Compilation errors when generating code for: <Block_path> ? Error during MATLAB code compilation: Error Path: B:\matlab\src\cgir_hdl\emlinterface\EMLInterfaceForHDL.cpp:407 Error Location: (@Row: --> 15 @Column: --> 36) Undefined function or variable '<variable_name>' . The first assignment to a local variable determines its class.?
Workaround: Consider disabling Flatten Hierarchy. To disable hierarchy flattening by using the HDL Block Properties dialog box:Right-click the subsystem.Select?HDL Code?>?HDL Block Properties.Turn Off?Flatten Hierarchy block property.?

Summary: execute function returns uninformative error message when multiple artifacts are dirty
Description: If you collect metrics programmatically using a metric.Engine  object, the execute  function returns an uninformative error message when you have multiple artifacts with unsaved changes.
Workaround: Save all open models, test cases, requirements, and requirement link files.

Summary: jc_0732?check does not?report Stateflow event names that match state names
Description: The Model Advisor check?Check uniqueness of Stateflow State and Data names ?(id: mathworks.jmaab.jc_0732)  does not? report Stateflow event names that match state names.?

Summary: Code generation report fails to generate
Description: The code generation report does not generate correctly when the configuration parameter 'CheckMdlBeforeBuild'  is set to Warning  or Error . This error can cause SIL/PIL simulation failure.
Workaround: Set the configuration parameter?'CheckMdlBeforeBuild'?to 'Off'.

Summary: Model with custom code or simulated in SIL or PIL mode shows incorrect decision coverage in specific cases
Description: If your model contains custom C/C++ code, for instance, in an S-Function or C Caller block, the coverage results might show fewer number of decisions than you expect if the?Structural coverage level ?is set to?Decision  and?the Relational boundary  option is disabled. You might see the same issue when you collect coverage results after a simulation in SIL or PIL mode (which uses code generated from the model).  The reason for fewer decisions is that certain types of code constructs are not instrumented for decision coverage till you select a higher?Structural coverage level ?such as?Condition Decision ?or?Modified Condition Decision Coverage ?(or enable relational boundary coverage).
Workaround: Set the Structural coverage level to Condition Decision or Modified Condition Decision Coverage (MCDC), or enable the Relational Boundary option.?

Summary: Incorrect normal-mode simulation results when a Variable Selector block is in a model that is referenced multiple times
Description: Simulating a model hierarchy in normal mode might produce incorrect simulation results when all these conditions apply: The model hierarchy contains multiple Model blocks that reference the same model. These Model blocks are configured to simulate the referenced model in normal mode. The referenced model contains a Variable Selector block.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 3, R2022a, R2022b

Summary: jc_0802?check not reporting comparison with literals
Description: If you use MATLAB Action Language in your Stateflow chart, t he Model Advisor check?Check for implicit type casting in Stateflow ?(id: mathworks.jmaab.jc_0802)? does not report ?comparing a variable and a literal value which are of different data type.? ?

Summary: JMAAB check for?jc_0611?does not report warning for Product blocks?with?multiplication?operators
Description: Model Advisor check Check signs of input signals in product blocks ?(id:?mathworks.jmaab.jc_0611)? does not report warning for Product  blocks with multiplication  operators. Also, it reports incorrect warning for Product  blocks whose input signal data types are not Fixed-point?  data types.  Warning :  The following product blocks have input signals with different sign bits.? This warning message is incorrect as the input data types are not?Fixed-point?data types.?

Summary: The JMAAB check for?jc_0731?does not report use of slash in?Stateflow state name
Description: The Model Advisor check?Check usage of State names ?(id: mathworks.jmaab.jc_0731) ? does not report state names in Stateflow that contain a slash (/) character.?

Summary: Simulink Test Manager incorrectly reports test failure when using verify keyword
Description: When you use verify signals only and no logged signals, Simulink Test Manager incorrectly reports a test failure regardless of the verify  outcome.
Workaround: To work around this issue, log an output signal in the model.
Fixed In: R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 3, R2022a

Summary: MATLAB might stall during model update?with Variant Connector and Simscape Bus block
Description: MATLAB might stall during model update phase Compiling: refreshing bus cache : Completed when allbelow conditions are met: Model contains Variant Connector  and Simscape Bus  block. Simscape Bus  block have more than one port onits children (elements) side and all ports on children side connected together (makingloop).

Summary: Hyperlinks created in MATLAB R2021b that link to a location within the same document do not work in a previous version of MATLAB
Description: If a hyperlink that links to a location within the same document is inserted into a live script in MATLAB R2021b, the hyperlink does not work when the live script is opened in a previous version of MATLAB, for example, R2021a. When the hyperlink is clicked, the cursor does not move. If the live script is opened in MATLAB versions R2021b and later, the hyperlink works correctly.

Summary: JMAAB check for?db_0129?does not report?transition lines that are diagonally oriented
Description: The?Sub-ID d? of Model Advisor check?Check for Stateflow transition appearance ?(db_0129_d: Check orientation of transition lines)? does not report warning for?transition lines that are diagonally oriented and are not part of flowchart loops.

Summary: Incorrect warning message when merging coverage results in the Model Testing Dashboard
Description: When the Model Testing Dashboard merges coverage results for test cases that contains multiple iterations, the dashboard shows a warning message with an incorrect number of test case results. For example: 5 out of 4 results for 'model_name' have been dropped due to merged conflicts. Make sure all results originate from?the same model and tool version.

Summary: Local clusters are not supported in MATLAB Web App Server
Description: When you use the 'local' cluster from the Parallel Computing Toolbox in web applications deployed to MATLAB Web App Server, you see the error: 'Local' clusters are not supported in MATLAB Online.

Summary: The JMAAB check for jc_0802 does not analyze Simulink Function inside Stateflow Chart
Description: The Model Advisor check??Check for implicit type casting in Stateflow ( id : mathworks.jmaab.jc_0802)  does not analyze the Simulink Functions  inside Stateflow Chart .
Fixed In: R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 3, R2022a, R2022b

Summary: Loading the initial state in rapid accelerator for a model may error when a virtual bus only exists in the referenced model
Description: Loading the initial state in rapid accelerator for models with a virtual bus only in the referenced model will error with the following: ?"Data type mismatch in the initial state for model"   This issue is only observed when all of the following conditions are met: The 'SaveFormat' is 'Dataset' The initial state being loaded is in Dataset format A virtual bus only exists in the referenced model

Summary: Quickly  navigating in and out of MATLAB Function blocks?and Stateflow MATLAB functions may cause disappearance of function body
Description: When you enter and exit the editor used by MATLAB Function blocks or MATLAB functions in Stateflow charts before the editor fully loads, the function body might disappear. ?
Workaround: Before navigating out of the editor, wait until it fully loads.The behavior has been improved in R2022a and R2021b Update 3, and the bug is expected to occur less frequently in those releases.?

Summary: Using extractFeatures on SIFTPoints returns duplicate valid points
Description: While extracting features from?SIFTPoints , duplicate points are created. In the following workflow for instance,?validPoints ? contains some duplicate?SIFTPoints :  points = detectSIFTFeatures(image);  [features, validPoints] = extractFeatures(image, points);
Workaround: The?duplicate points have orientation as 2*pi. The following modified feature extraction workflow shows how to remove the duplicate points:points = detectSIFTFeatures(image);[features, validPoints] = extractFeatures(image, points);%% Now remove duplicate points using one of the following ways:% 1. Remove duplicates using a tolerance on orientationtol = 0.001;validPoints(abs(validPoints.Orientation - 2*pi) < tol) = [];% OR% 2. Remove duplicates using single precisionvalidPoints(single(validPoints.Orientation) == single(2*pi)) = [];?

Summary: JMAAB check for?db_0125?does not report warning for data objects in State Transition Tables
Description: Model Advisor check Check definition of Stateflow data ?(id:?mathworks.jmaab.db_0125)? does not report warning for data objects used in State Transition Tables .

Summary: The JMAAB check for na_0021, na_0031, and na_0037 are not available in JMAAB By Task folder
Description: The Model Advisor check?Check usage of character vector inside MATLAB Function block?(id: mathworks.maab.na_0021), Check usage of enumerated values?(id: mathworks.maab.na_0031),  and ?Check use of single variable variant conditionals?(id: mathworks.maab.na_0037)  do not appear in the JMAAB By Task folder in Model Advisor
Workaround: These checks can be run from the By Task > MAB folder in Model Advisor.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 3, R2022a, R2022b

Summary: The JMAAB check for na_0004?reports blocks that are excluded
Description: The Model Advisor check?Check for Simulink diagrams using nonstandard display attributes?(id: mathworks.maab.na_0004)? reports blocks that are excluded using the exclusion feature of Model Advisor.

Summary: While running analysis on a pcbStack created with single metal layer and two dielectric layers, the top metal layer is being removed and a rectangular ground plane is added below the dielectric layers.?
Description: During analysis of a pcbStack?created with single metal layer and two dielectric layers, the top metal layer is being removed and a rectangular ground plane is added below the two dielectric layers.?   Example:  ??? p = pcbStack;  ??? % Create a stackup of single metal layer between two FR4 dielectric layers.??  ??? p.Layers = { dielectric('FR4'), p.Layers{1}, dielectric('FR4')}; ??? % Assign the feed location at the center of the second layer.  ??? p.FeedLocations = [0,0,2];  ??? % Run analysis and mesh.?  ??? figure; impedance(p,1.7e9);  ??? % Notice that in the mesh plot a ground plane is added? and the metal layer in the middle is removed. ??? figure; mesh(p);?

Summary: On Windows 11, Polyspace Bug Finder might error out during the Function Analysis part of the analysis and provide only partial results
Description: If you run a Polyspace Bug Finder analysis on a Windows 11 host, you might encounter an error during the Function Analysis  part of the analysis.The error message in the log file is exception Fail(Fail: failure in job) raised.  The error does not mean that the entire analysis failed. You can still see the Polyspace Bug Finder results, but the results are from the previous level, Pre-Analysis . Because higher levels detect more defects, the results of Pre-Analysis  might contain fewer than the actual number of defects (more false negatives).

Summary: In Signal Editor, graphically removing data points of Boolean data type does not work as expected
Description: The erase and delete graphical editing tools do not work on signals of Boolean type. In some cases, the data points disappear and then return.

Summary: LPV System Block produces incorrect answer for LTI arrays with irregular grid and unsorted data in the SamplingGrid property.
Description: The LPV System block interpolates the model matrices and the offset data according to the chosen interpolation rule. However, when the LTI array corresponds to an irregular grid, the interpolation is always performed using the nearest neighbor approach. In this case, if the SamplingGrid  data is not monotonic for each variable, the interpolation may not be performed correctly. Example:?? sys = rss(2,1,1,1,3);  sys.SamplingGrid = struct('Alpha',(1:3)','Beta',[20 30 10]')
Workaround: Sort the systems in the LTI array based on their SamplingGrid field values before using it in the LPV System block.GridInfo =ltipack.SamplingGrid.getGridStructure(sys.SamplingGrid)sys = reshape(sys(:,:,GridInfo.SamplePerm),GridInfo.GridSize);?Reorder the offsets in states, state derivatives, inputs and outputs in a similar way (if present). For example, if the InputOffset is non-empty, reorder the data as follows:?InputOffset?= reshape(InputOffset(:,:,GridInfo.SamplePerm),GridInfo.GridSize);?

Summary: Lookup Table Dynamic block using Simulink.AliasType objects?might be unnecessarily stubbed during analysis
Description: If a model contains a Lookup Table Dynamic  block which uses Simulink.AliasType  objects, Simulink Design Verifier stubs the Lookup Table Dynamic  block and the model is incorrectly marked as partially compatible.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 2, R2022b

Summary: J1939 Transmit block does not inherit discrete sample time
Description: The J1939 Transmit block sample time is always set to continuous even if the inputs to the block are discrete in time. The block does not inherit discrete sample time.?This can unintentionally result in a multi-rate model.

Summary: JMAAB check for?jc_0009 gives incorrect result for signal connected to From block
Description: The Model Advisor check?Check for propagated signal labels  ?(id: mathworks.jmaab.jc_0009)  does not report the signal connected to From block when the Show?propagated signal ?parameter is not selected and the corresponding signal connected to?Goto block has signal label.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 3, R2022a, R2022b

Summary: The JMAAB check for jc_0641?reports Enable and Trigger blocks inside Subsystem blocks
Description: The Model Advisor check?Check for sample time setting?(id: mathworks.jmaab.jc_0641)? reports Enable  and Trigger  blocks for setting sample time other than -1 . When inside Subsystem, the sample time setting for these block is disabled and user cannot modify them.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 3, R2022a, R2022b

Summary: The JMAAB check for?jc_0641 does not report trigonometric?function block
Description: The Model Advisor check?Check for sample time setting (id: mathworks.jmaab.jc_0641)? does not report trigonometric function blocks even if the sample time is not set to -1.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 3, R2022a, R2022b

Summary: JMAAB check for?jc_0752?does not report warning for parentheses in condition actions
Description: Model Advisor check Check usage of parentheses in Stateflow transitions ?(id:?mathworks.jmaab.jc_0752)? does not report warning for p arentheses in a single line?having characters (comments or transition action) before and after curly braces.

Summary: The JMAAB check for?jc_0644 does not report Outport block
Description: The Model Advisor check?Check type setting by data objects  ?(id: mathworks.jmaab.jc_0644)? does not report an Outport? block that has a data type set on it even though the data type is specified in a signal object.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 3, R2022a, R2022b

Summary: The JMAAB check for?jc_0602?reports Inport blocks with prefixes
Description: The Model Advisor check?Check for consistency in model element names  ?(ID: mathworks.jmaab.jc_0602)  reports Inport  blocks if the connecting signal name and the Inport block name do not match even though the only difference in the names is that the Inport  block name has prefix specified as a check input parameter which is an exception to the rule.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 3, R2022a, R2022b

Summary: JMAAB check for db_0042 incorrectly flags hidden Simulink blocks
Description: Model Advisor?check? Check position of Inport and Outport blocks  ( ID: ?mathworks.jmaab.db_0042 ) incorrectly flags hidden Simulink blocks inside Stateflow charts.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 3, R2022a, R2022b

Summary: Custom attribute values are deleted when you move a requirement by clicking and dragging
Description: If a requirement has custom attributes and you move the requirement in the Requirements Editor ?by clicking and dragging, the software removes the custom attributes.

Summary: Opening project archive fails with PathNotFound exception
Description: Opening a project archive (.mlpro j) fails if both  conditions are true: The archived project references another project. The referenced project is stored in at least two-level deep subfolder inside the top-level project root.  MATLAB errors with the following message:?Caught "std::exception" Exception message is: fl:filesystem:PathNotFound

Summary: The MTPA Control Reference block and the ACIM Control Reference block do not limit the negative Iq Reference output to the specified Max current
Description: The MTPA Control Reference block andthe ACIM Control Reference Block in Motor Control Blockset output Id reference current and Iqreference current. When the Iq reference is negative and the motor is operating in the field weakeningregion, these blocks do not limit the output Iq reference according to the specified Max current.

Summary: JMAAB check for db_0129?does not report?transition lines that overlap one another
Description: The?Sub-ID b? of Model Advisor?check?mathworks.jmaab.db_0129 (db_0129_b:?Check for transition lines that overlap one another)? does not report warning for?transition lines that do not overlap one another entirely.

Summary: Empty XPath context node might crash MATLAB
Description: Passing an empty context node to the evaluate  method of a matlab.io.xml.xpath.Evaluator  object might crash MATLAB. For example, the following script crashes MATLAB: d = matlab.io.xml.dom.Document('book');?nl =?getChildNodes(d);n = item(nl,1);  % n is an empty object of type Node.b = evaluate(matlab.io.xml.xpath.Evaluator,'/',n);

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generated for dlarray indexing operations might produce incorrect answer
Description: Code generated for indexing operations on dlarray   objects using parenthesis might produce incorrect answer if the?dlarray  object is permuted (directly or indirectly) before indexing. The following are some example MATLAB code patterns that might be affected by this issue. Example 1: dlX  = dlarray(ones(5, 5, 16, 'single'));dlX  = permute(dlX, [3 1 2]);dlY  = dlX(:, :, 1:4); Example 2: dlX = dlarray(reshape(1:24, [2 3 4]));dlX = permute(dlX, [3 1 2]);dlX(1, 1, 1) = 1;
Workaround: Before indexing into the dlarray object, invoke the extractdata method. After the indexing operation, reconstruct the?dlarray object.Modified example 1:dlX  = dlarray(ones(5, 5, 16, 'single'));dlX  = permute(dlX, [3 1 2]);x = extractdata(dlX);dlY = dlarray(x(:, :, 1:4));Modified example 2:dlX = dlarray(reshape(1:24, [2 3 4]));dlX = permute(dlX, [3 1 2]);x = extractdata(dlX);x(1, 1, 1) = 1;dlX = dlarray(x);

Summary: The Clean Missing Data task can generate plot code that produces an indexing error in the Live Editor.
Description: In the Clean Missing Data task, the generated plot code can have an incorrect index. This can result in either an error or the plot visualizing the incorrect data.? The bug occurs in the case where all of these are true: The input data is a table or timetable The output format is "Create new table from selected variables" The specified variables are not the left-most variables in the table

Summary: If target.Processor?object is added to internal database in R2021a,?target?package functions cannot be used after upgrade to R2021b, R2022a, or R2022b
Description: Suppose you add a?target.Processor ?object?to an internal database in R2021a and then upgrade to R2021b, R2022a, or R2022b. For example, in MATLAB R2021a, you run: processor = target.create("Processor", "Name", "ExampleProcessor");  target.add(processor, "UserInstall", true);   Then, after an upgrade to MATLAB R2022a, you run: target.get("Processor", "ExampleProcessor");   The function produces this error:? Error loading Target Repository file ".../R2022a/TargetFramework/userregistrations.xml". Type 'target.profiling.XCPBuffer' has not been found in the registry.
Workaround: Navigate to the?userregistrations.xml?file in the?MATLAB?preferences folder.fullfile(prefdir, 'TargetFramework', 'userregistrations.xml')From each?target.Processor?element in the file,?remove this line:    <XCPBuffer type="target.profiling.XCPBuffer"/>The following processor definition provides an example:  <target.Processor type="target.Processor">? ? <XCPBuffer type="target.profiling.XCPBuffer"/> <--- REMOVE THIS LINE? ? <Name>ExampleProcessor</Name>  </target.Processor>

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results might occur for models containing custom TLC S-function blocks

The output port of the TLC S-Function block that creates an expression is connected to the input port of a Selector block, selecting elements other than the first element. The input port of the TLC S-Function block?that accepts an expression?is connected to the output port of the Selector block. Summary: Setting an ASCII format string with delimiters in a TCP/IP Receive block results in an error

Summary: App Designer MLAPP file may be corrupted by a custom component that contains an SLRT System Log component

Summary: Security Issue: Default configuration of matlab.io.xml.dom.Parser?with document type definitions (DTDs) is vulnerable to XML External Entity (XXE) attack
Description: The XML markup parser class matlab.io.xml.dom.Parser  parses documents with document type definitions (DTDs) by default, which makes the parser vulnerable to an XML External Entity (XXE) attack if you try to parse a malicious document. A malicious DTD might cause the parser to disclose potentially sensitive information in files local to the machine running the parser or other internal content that the parser has access to, for example, via http  requests. The following is an example of a malicious DTD that attempts to disclose a password file: <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>  <!DOCTYPE foo [  ? <!ELEMENT foo ANY >  ? <!ENTITY xxe SYSTEM "file:///etc/passwd" >]>  <foo>&xxe;</foo>?   In some cases, the DTD might instigate a denial-of-service attack by accessing a local resource and causing it to not stop returning data.
Workaround: Configure the XML Parser to not parse documents with DTDs:p = matlab.io.xml.dom.Parser;?p.Configuration.DisallowDoctype = true;?or??p.Configuration.AllowDoctype = false;?Only parse documents with DTDs from trusted sources.?

Summary: Polyspace analysis fails when using standard headers on Unix systems
Description: When you use the Polyspace desktop interface to analyze a project generated with polyspace-configure , the -dos  option is enabled by default. With this option, the paths of included files are resolved in a case insensitive manner. For instance, inc/myHeader.h  resolves to inc/myheader.h . This can create conflicts with standard headers on Unix systems where paths are case sensitive, which might cause the analysis to fail. The error message that you see depends on the system header that conflicts with the resolved case insensitive path.? For example, if you have empty file inc/Time.h  and this main.cpp  file:?     #include <ctime>    int main() {        return 0;     } The file compiles successfully on a Unix system if you use g++ -Iinc main.cpp .? If you generate a project with polyspace-configure g++ -Iinc main.cpp  and then run an analysis in the Polyspace desktop interface on this project, you see an error message similar to this: ?Error: ?the global scope has no "clock_t"  ? ?? using ::clock_t; ????????? ? ? ? ^  The error occurs because ctime  includes system header <time.h> , which is incorrectly resolved to case insensitive path inc/Time.h (inc/time.h) .
Workaround: Turn off the -dos option before you run the analysis:In the Configuration pane, go to Environment Settings under the Target & Compiler node.Uncheck the option Code from DOS or Windows file system.
Products: Polyspace Bug Finder Server, Polyspace Bug Finder

Summary: MATLAB Compiler does not package files associated?with certain support packages
Description: When applications that use files from certain support packages are packaged using Application Compiler, Library Compiler apps, the mcc  command, or the compiler.build  family of functions, the support package files are excluded. The list of support packages affected by this problem is: DCAM Hardware GenICam Interface Kinect for Windows Sensor NI Frame Grabbers OS Generic Video Interface Point Grey Hardware Teledyne Dalsa Sapera Hardware
Workaround: Use the includedSupportPackages.txt file generated by the mcc command to identify the support package that was included in the compilation. Execute the following commands, substituting the support package name listed in the includedSupportPackages.txt file for <spkgname>.>> spkgList = matlab.depfun.internal.DeployableSupportPackages;>> spkg = getSupportPackage(spkgList, '<spkgname>');>> getIncludeList(spkg)Include the output of getIncludeList(spkg) as an additional file during compilation.If using the mcc command, use the -a option and specify the additional file generated above.If using the Application Compiler or Library Compiler apps, click the Settings button and include the?-a?option and specify the additional file generated above in the Additional parameters passed to mcc field.If using one of the compiler.build.* functions, use the AdditionalFiles option to specify the additional file generated above.

Summary: Simulation of a Simulink model with multiple MATLAB Function blocks loading similar networks might produce incorrect answers
Description: Consider a Simulink model with multiple MATLAB Function blocks that load networks differing only in weight values. Simulation of such a model might produce an incorrect answer, if the networks share the same name. For example, suppose that?net1.mat  and net2.mat  contain networks that differ by weights only and are loaded in two different MATLAB Function blocks. The two blocks contain these two MATLAB functions respectively: MATLAB Function block 1?  function scores1 = fcn(u)persistent netCif isempty(netC)    netA = coder.loadDeepLearningNetwork('net1.mat', 'network');endscores1 = predict(netA,u);end MATLAB Function block 2 ? function scores2 = fcn(u) persistent netDif isempty(netD)    netB = coder.loadDeepLearningNetwork('net2.mat','network'); % network name is same as in first MATLAB function blockendscores2 = predict(netB,u);end  Simulating a model that contains both these MATLAB Function blocks might produce an incorrect answer.?
Workaround: Specify unique network names for unique networks.MATLAB Function block 1?function scores1 = fcn(u)persistent netCif isempty(netC)    netA = coder.loadDeepLearningNetwork('net1.mat', 'network1');endscores1 = predict(netA,u);endMATLAB Function block 2?function scores2 = fcn(u) persistent netDif isempty(netD)    netB = coder.loadDeepLearningNetwork('net2.mat','network2');% network name is different from first MATLAB function blockendscores2 = predict(netB,u);end Alternatively, do not provide any network name. MATLAB and Simulink will automatically create unique network names.?MATLAB Function block 1?function scores1 = fcn(u)persistent netCif isempty(netC)    netA = coder.loadDeepLearningNetwork('net1.mat');endscores1 = predict(netA,u);endMATLAB Function block 2?function scores2 = fcn(u) persistent netDif isempty(netD)    netB = coder.loadDeepLearningNetwork('net2.mat');endscores2 = predict(netB,u);end
Fixed In: R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 3, R2022a

Summary: Unable to use local cluster when username on Windows contains non-ASCII characters
Description: On Windows, the local cluster fails to start parpool or independent jobs if your username contains characters that are not in the basic ASCII range. This includes usernames that have extended ASCII characters such as "?". Attempting to use the local cluster results in failures that do not give much information, for example: >> parpool('local')  Error using parpool (line 151)  Failed to initialize the interactive session.
Workaround: Use the "-c" startup flag to override the default license path of MATLAB to one that contains only ASCII characters. The default license path covers all locations where a license file might be found: one of these locations contains the username and this needs to be modified.1) Obtain the current default license path using the following command in MATLAB:feature('lmsearchpath') 2) Look for paths in this string that contain your username. There should be exactly one such path, typically located at:C:\Users\%USERNAME%\AppData\Roaming\MathWorks\MATLAB\R2021b_licenses 3) Obtain the ASCII-safe version of this same path. This can be done in MATLAB using the command:!dir /X C:\Users\Alongside the path for your username, this command will list an ASCII-safe version that should end with a "~1" (or a "~2", etc). For example, username "Bj?rnson" might have the ASCII-safe version "BJRNSO~1".4)? Use the "-c" startup flag of MATLAB to use a license path where all references to your username have been modified to use ASCII-safe versions. For more information on how to do this, see:https://uk.mathworks.com/matlabcentral/answers/102520-how-do-i-change-the-license-search-location-for-matlab

Summary: compiler.package.docker fails with an error "COPY failed" when the value stored in the USER environment variable has a backslash "\" in it
Description: The MATLAB Compiler function "compiler.package.docker"  will fail when the value stored in the USER environment variable has a backslash "\" in it.? The error might look similar to: When creating the Runtime image, 'docker build' failed with the following log:  Sending build context to Docker daemon? 2.048kB  Step 1/3 : FROM matlabruntimebase/r2021a/release/update7  ?---> 00b2297ae2d3  Step 2/3 : COPY ./v910 /opt/matlabruntime/v910  COPY failed: stat /var/lib/docker/tmp/docker-builder680483356/v910: no such file or directory
Workaround: Instead of running compiler.package.docker, execute the following in MATLAB instead:clear classesoriguser = getenv('USER');oc = onCleanup(@()setenv('USER',origuser));newuser = strrep(origuser,'\','_');setenv('USER',newuser) % USER now has no backslashtry? ? compiler.package.docker(r)? ? clear occatch err? ? clear oc % when done? ? throw(err)end

Summary: Incorrect validation loss when using pixelClassificationLayer with class weights
Description: When training a network using the trainNetwork  function, the software computes the incorrect validation loss when the network contains a PixelClassificationLayer  object with a numeric ClassWeights  property and the MiniBatchSize  training option is strictly less than the number of observations in the validation data.
Workaround: Set the MiniBatchSize training option to the number of observations in the validation data using the trainingOptions function.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: In single clock mode, the timing controller can be incorrectly generated when requesting Oversampling and not utilizing Clock-Rate Pipelining.
Description: In rare instances, the timing controller generated by HDL Coder might not be appropriately clocking the design. The issue is evident during simulation of the HDL code because all or parts of the design have the clocking gated to 0. The issue occurs when the model has a base rate which is not used inside the DUT Subsystem. The model base rate, the fastest rate in the DUT, and oversampling factor all must have a certain relation in order for the timing controller to not be generated correctly. For example, if the model base rate is 1, the fastest rate in the DUT is 4, and the oversampling factor is 2, this issue can occur. The issue does not occur in most cases where an oversampling request creates an unused clock.
Workaround: To avoid the issue, choose one of these two workarounds:Alter the sample time of elements outside the DUT so that the DUT contains the fastest rate reported by the Simulink Timing Legend.Remove the oversampling request by setting Oversampling to 1, its default.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 1, R2022b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: When doing horizontal concatenation followed by vertical concatenation
Description: MATLAB Coder might generate incorrect code if your MATLAB code satisfies?all ?of these conditions: Generates code using row major layout. Completely initializes a variable-size array p . Writes to a piece of p . Concatenates p  horizontally. Concatenates p  vertically.   Incorrect generated code uses wrong indices to access p .? For example consider the following MATLAB code: function p = foo()p = [1 1];coder.varsize('p',[2 3]);p(1,1) = 2;p = [p, 1];p = [p; ones(1,3)];end In the generated code, function foo  uses out-of-bounds indices to access p : void foo(const emlrtStack *sp, real_T p_data[], int32_T p_size[2]){   (void)sp;   p_data[1] = 1.0;   p_data[0] = 2.0;   p_size[1] = 3;   p_size[0] = 2;   p_data[3] = 1.0;   p_data[6] = 1.0;   p_data[7] = 1.0;   p_data[8] = 1.0;} Output p  has size 2x3 but foo  is writes to p  using indices 6, 7, and 8 at the end.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 3, R2022a, R2022b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generation for dlnetwork?might return incorrect output shape for layers with singleton spatial dimensions in row-major layout
Description: Code generation for?dlnetwork ?might return incorrect output shape for layers with singleton spatial dimensions in row-major layout.   For example, a layer whose expected output shape is 1(S) x 1(S) x C x B might return C x B x 1(S) x 1(S) when generating code using the row-major layout.

Workaround: Open both Model Analyzer and Model Builder?apps before creating a model in Model Builder.

Summary: HDL code generation failure from a top-level model that contains a protected model
Description: HDL code generation for a Simulink model might fail if both  these conditions are true:? You attempt to generate code for a top-level model that contains a protected model The?.slx ?file for the protected model is open or on MATLAB path  If code generation fails, you get this error: The protected model has been changed since it was created,  or the file has been corrupted.  To use this protected model,  you need to recreate it.
