Description: Consider a model with a logged signal that is a matrix and maps to AUTOSAR typed per-instance memory (ArTypedPerInstanceMemory ). When you run a software-in-the-loop (SIL) or processor-in-the-loop (PIL) simulation of the model, during the build process you might see a memory access error for?xil_interface.c .

Summary: parquetread might incorrectly import columns whose PhysicalType is Date32
Description: parquetread  incorrectly imports Date32  values that exceed the date 9-Jan-2038 03:14:07 as the datetime  9-Jan-2038 03:14:07.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 2, R2022a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results for accelerator and rapid accelerator simulations for models containing continuous integrator blocks with limits enabled and wide signals.
Description: For models containing continuous integrator blocks with limits enabled, simulation results in accelerator and rapid accelerator may be inaccurate when using the block with a wide signal.

Summary: Showing enable port on Function-Call Generator block prevents implicit expansion of output signal
Description: When you configure a Function-Call Generator block to show the enable port, Simulink might throw unexpected dimension mismatch errors.? For example, if the block has the Show enable port  parameter selected, the Number of iterations ?parameter is specified as a scalar, and the block output is connected to a Demux block with two or more outputs, Simulink might throw a dimension mismatch error.
Workaround: Specify the Number of iterations parameter of the Function-Call Generator block as an N-element vector, where N is the intended dimension of the downstream signal. For example, if the Function-Call Generator block output is connected to a Demux block with three outputs, and you want to generate two function-call events at each time step, specify the Number of iterations?as [2 2 2] rather than 2.

Summary: Some images in the documentation for R2021b appear stretched and unreadable
Description: In the documentation for R2021b, some images are scaled incorrectly when their parent container is smaller than the image itself. This causes the image to appear stretched and unreadable. Most of these incorrectly scaled images are in documentation examples.?

Summary: Calling Python class methods error out when called from command-line and in some workflows from scripts.
Description: MATLAB displays this error when calling Python class methods from the command-line: Unable to return the number of named arguments if not in an active function.  This can occur for class methods that require an import statement in Python for access. For example, when you use this Python module in MATLAB: *** mymod.py *** class mycls:   def __init__(self) -> None:       pass   def myfun(self):       print(2)*** EOF mymod.py *** MATLAB displays: >> pobj = py.mymod.mycls();  >> pobj.myfun()Error using py.mymod.mycls/myfunUnable to return the number of named arguments if not in an active function. In some cases you can wrap the call in a MATLAB function, but this is not a verified workflow for all cases.

Summary: Database Explorer app crashes during test of data source connection
Description: MATLAB crashes when you test a data source connection using the JDBC Data Source Configuration, MySQL Data Source Configuration, and PostgreSQL Data Source Configuration dialog boxes from the Database Explorer app. The crash only occurs when the password field is left blank before pressing the Test  button.
Workaround: To test a data source connection with no password, enter?one or more characters in the?password field, and then delete them. The crash will not occur.

Summary: Incorrect Polyspace project generation when using polyspace-configure with compilation database
Description: If you generate a? Polyspace project or analysis options file by using polyspace-configure  with a compilation database (CCDB), the generated project or options file is incorrect if both of these are true: The CCDB "directory" fields contain folder paths with spaces in them.  The CCDB "command" fields contain relative paths.   For example, if you generate a Polyspace options file by using polyspace-configure  with this CCDB: [{? "directory": "\/home\/folder with spaces\/project\/src",? "command": "gcc -I..\/ main.c",? "file": "\/home\/folders with spaces\/project\/src\/main.c"}]  The generated options file contains this erroneous entry for the include folder path (-I ): -options-for-sources /home/folder with spaces/project/src/main.c;-I /home/folder with spaces/project/src/spaces/project/;  ...  Instead of the correct entry: -options-for-sources /home/folder with spaces/project/src/main.c;-I /home/folder with spaces/project/ ; ...  When you run an analysis with the generated options file, you see an error message similar to this: line 1: warning: could not find include file "foo.h"
Workaround: Try one of the following to work around this issue:Rename? your project folder to remove spaces in the folder path. For instance:/home/folder with spaces/project -->?/home/folder_without_spaces/projectUse absolute paths in the "command" field of the CCDB. For instance:[{? "directory": "\/home\/folder with spaces\/project\/src",? "command": "gcc -I\"\/home\/folder with spaces\/project\" main.c",? "file": "\/home\/folders with spaces\/project\/src\/main.c"}] ?Fix the incorrect entries manually in the generated options file.

Summary: When running in batch mode, MATLAB sometimes shows a Java error message when shutting down
Description: When running MATLAB with the batch  command flag, a Java error message displays when MATLAB is shutting down. The message begins with: java.lang.ExceptionInInitializerErrorat com.mathworks.mde.editor.plugins.editordataservice.debug.DebuggerInstaller.initializeListeners(DebuggerInstaller.java:78) at com.mathworks.mde.editor.plugins.editordataservice.debug.DebuggerInstaller.(DebuggerInstaller.java:64)   This message is benign and can be ignored.?

Summary: createInputDataset might return uninformative error message
Description: When using createInputDataset  on incorrectly configured models, the resulting error message is generic and uninformative.

Summary: Expected output field may not be present in the Simulink Design Verifier results for model having output ports as n-dimensional vector
Description: After the completion of Simulink Design Verifier analysis, the results will not have the expected output field if:There is an output port in the model which is a n-dimensional vector, and It is represented using either row matrix [1 n] or column matrix [n 1].

Summary: Compatibility check and block replacement API fails with error about incorrect block execution priorities for models with Initialize Functions and model references
Description: Simulink Design Verifier compatibility check fails with error about incorrect user-specified block execution priorities if the model consists of following types of blocks:Initialize Function  Model Reference  Global data such as Data Store Memory  or Simulink.Signal  objects  Similarly, the replacement model generated by sldvblockreplacement  API for such models also fails to compile with the same error about incorrect user-specific block execution priorities: The sample error message reported in such cases is as follows:  Unable to honor user-specified block priorities in task 0 to schedule block execution with a discrete or continuous sample time
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 5, R2022b

Summary: undefined reference compilation failure when building model reference hierarchy with MinGW
Description: Consider a top model that references a middle model that in turn references a bottom model. When you build the top model, a compilation failure might occur if all of these conditions apply:? The selected compiler is MinGW.? The build process for the middle model includes custom source code. The bottom model calls a function that is implemented in the custom source code included for the middle model.  You might observe a linker error. For example: undefined reference to my_custom_code_function  .
Fixed In: R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 3, R2022a, R2022b

Summary: Using mixed signal and message ports in the same reference component in an architecture model might produce incorrect error messages
Description: Compiling an architecture model that contains reference components with mixed signal and message input or output ports might produce incorrect error messages.
Workaround: Instead of mixing signal and message ports in the same reference component, place all signal input or output ports in one reference component and message input or output ports in another reference component.

Summary: JMAAB check for na_0001?does not report?~?and?^?used in logical operations
Description: The?Sub-ID a? of Model Advisor?check?mathworks.jmaab.na_0001 (na_0001_a:?Usage of bitwise operators in Stateflow )? does not report warning for bitwise operators? ~ ? and?^  ?used in logical operations in C action language ?chart   with?Enable C-bit operations   disabled.

Summary: Design Verifier compatibility check fails for a multi-rate model containing bus element ports
Description: Ifa model contains function-call subsystems with distinct sample times and buselement ports, Design Verifier fails to construct the correct block replacement model,and compatibility checking fails.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 3, R2022b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Update to Stateflow output may generate incorrect code
Description: When an output of a Stateflow Chart is resolved to a Simulink.Signal  object with GetSet  storage class may lead to incorrect code generation.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 3, R2021b Update 2, R2022a

Summary: MATLAB can crash when calling getadjacencymatrix or when using the Model Debugger on a very large SimBiology model
Description: MATLAB can crash when calling?getadjacencymatrix  on a SimBiology model that contains hundreds of reactions using null as reactant or product. The?Model Debugger for SimBiology  also relies on getadjacencymatrix  and can crash when used with such a model.
Workaround: Replace null reactants and products with a dummy species. You can adapt the following code, which updates a SimBiology model stored in variable modelObj by replacing null with a dummy species named NULL, while ensuring the reaction rate remains the same.reactions = modelObj.Reactions;nullPattern = regexpPattern('\<null\>');for i = 1:numel(reactions)    reactionString = reactions(i).Reaction;    if contains(reactionString, nullPattern)        allSpecies = [reactions(i).Reactants; reactions(i).Products];        oneSpecies = allSpecies(1);        oneCompartment = oneSpecies.Parent;        nullSpecies = sbioselect(oneCompartment, 'Name', 'NULL');        if isempty(nullSpecies)            nullSpecies = oneCompartment.addspecies('NULL', 'Units', oneSpecies.Units);        end        nullSpeciesQualifiedName = nullSpecies.PartiallyQualifiedName;        reactionString = replace(reactionString, nullPattern, nullSpeciesQualifiedName);        rate = reactions(i).ReactionRate;        reactions(i).Reaction = reactionString;        reactions(i).ReactionRate = rate;    endend
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 1, R2022a

Summary: Unable to authorize code generation for encrypted protected model
Description: Authorizing code generation for an encrypted protected model might fail when the encrypted protected model includes a protected model report. The Password entry for model dialog box rejects passwords for code generation even when the correct password is entered. The corresponding error is: The supplied password is incorrect! Please try again.   As a result of being unauthorized, code generation fails when a model hierarchy uses this encrypted protected model.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Variant Subsystem with model block as choice may cause missing?initialization?in the generated code
Description: Variant Subsystem with model block as choice may cause missing initialization when referenced models drive root outports with constant sample time.? This issue can be reproduced when all below conditions are met:Variant Subsystem with code-compile  activation time. Variant Subsystem has model block as choices. Inside the referenced models of model block, we have blocks with constant sample time (such as Constant  block) directly connected to outport.  ?
Workaround: Create a wrapper atomic subsystem on the model choice within the Variant Subsystem.Add a signal conversion block at the output of the model choice which is fed from constant rate. This ensures that the output of the wrapper subsystem is fed from the output of the signal conversion block.

Summary: Unnecessary?.lib?and?.exp?files created in code generation folder when building model reference simulation target on Windows
Description: When you build a model reference simulation target on Windows, the build process creates modelName _msf.lib  and modelName _msf.exp  files in the code generation folder, in addition to the mex  file modelName _msf.mexw64 . The .lib  and .exp  files are not required.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code generated for State Writer block writing to Outport block of reused conditionally executed subsystem
Description: The code generator might produce incorrect code for a model if these conditions exist: Model contains a State Writer block. State Writer block writes to an Outport block of a conditionally executed subsystem (for example, an Enabled Subsystem, Triggered Subsystem, or Function-Call Subsystem). Model is configured with these model configuration parameter settings: System target file ?is set to 'ert.tlc'. Pass reusable subsystem outputs as  is set to Individual arguments .  For conditionally executed subsystem, block parameter?Function packaging ?is set to Reusable function . Conditionally executed subsystem is inside another nonvirtual subsystem for which block parameter?Function packaging ?set to Reusable function .  When these conditions are met, the generated code for the State Writer block might write to a global variable instead of writing to the argument variable passed in to the reusable function for the subsystem, causing an incorrect answer.
Fixed In: R2021b Update 7, R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 6, R2023a Update 1, R2023b

Summary: JMAAB check for?jc_0231?displays an incorrect warning for hidden Simulink blocks inside Stateflow charts
Description: Model Advisor check?Check character usage in block names  ? (id:? ?mathworks.jmaab.jc_0231)? displays an incorrect warning for hidden Simulink blocks inside Stateflow charts.

Summary: Compiled report generator applications errors out at startup
Description: Compiled report generator applications might throw the following error at startup: Warning: an error occurred while parsing class rptgen.DAObject:  Invalid superclass handle.   > In rptgen.rpt_section/schema  In rptgen.coutline/schema  In RptgenML.CReport/schema  In rptgen/loadRpt

Summary: Code generation report shows empty content if build directory name contains a dot (.)
Description: If the build folder contains a dot (.) in its name, the code generation report might launch with a "Page Not Found " error message.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results from C Function or C Caller blocks in For Each Subsystem blocks with masked parameters
Description: Simulink can produce incorrect simulation results when a C Function or C Caller block inside a For Each Subsystem block uses masked parameters. If the masked parameter is sliced by the For Each Subsystem block, the C Caller and C Function block will retrieve an incorrect slice of data during simulation. This bug does not affect code generation behavior.?

Summary: Scenarios in Signal Editor block disappear when enumerated value name is changed
Description: If you have a scenario in Signal Editor block that uses an enumeration signal whose name in the enumeration data type class has changed, the entire scenario silently disappears from Signal Editor UI. If you revert the enumerated value name, the scenario reappears in the Signal Editor user interface.
Workaround: The best practice workaround for updating enumeration value definitions with existing data is to do the conversion in this order:Use the Signal Editor or a script to cast the enumerations to their numerical value equivalent.?Save the data or keep in the base workspace.Change your enumeration definition in the data dictionary.Use the Signal Editor or a script to cast your signals back to the enumeration value type, which now has its updated definition in the data dictionary.Save your data.Continue your workflows.

Summary: Equations do not display correctly when in a live script Heading 1 text line
Description: If a live script contains an equation in a text line with the Heading 1 text style applied, when the live script is opened, the equation does not render and is missing in the document.

Summary: Using a system hierarchy reporter to report on a Simulink model with a hidden MATLAB Function block might cause MATLAB to crash
Description: A hidden MATLAB Function block is a MATLAB Function block that has the attribute MaskHideContents  set to true . Using a system hierarchy  reporter to report on a Simulink model with a hidden MATLAB Function block might cause MATLAB to throw an Access Violation  error.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Chart outputs modified by exported functions might have different results in Accelerator mode
Description: When a block in your model calls a Stateflow exported function that modifies a chart output, the chart output might not be correctly updated in Accelerator mode.?This behavior occurs if the caller of the exported function comes after the chart in model execution order. As a result, simulation in Accelerator mode might produce different chart outputs than code generation or simulation in Normal and Rapid Accelerator modes.

Summary: Some Simscape models might crash target computer
Description: Real-time applications built from some Simscape models might exceed the available stack size. When run, these applications cause the target computer to crash.

Summary: Unable to fit data that has zero groups or one group in SimBiology Model Analyzer
Description: In the SimBiology Model Analyzer  app, the?Fit Data  program errors if the data being fit contains no groups or only one group.

Summary: Network Analyzer does not count learnable parameters in dlnetwork learnables of custom layers
Description: When you use the analyzeNetwork function to analyze a network, it can report an incorrect number of learnable parameters for each layer. The value is incorrect if: The network contains custom layers. Those custom layers define learnable properties which are dlnetworks. Those dlnetwork learnable properties contain other learnable parameters.  Network Analyzer ignores the learnable properties of those nested dlnetworks, so does not include their parameters in the total learnables in the network. The Network Analyzer shows a value for the "Total learnables" of each layer which treats a dlnetwork learnable as a single-element learnable parameter, and fails to count any of the learnable parameters inside the dlnetwork it contains.?This issue does not occur with networks containing custom layers whose learnable parameters are numeric arrays.

Summary: Compatibility check fails for models containing Stateflow chart with grouped states and Data Store Memory access
Description: If a model consists of a Stateflow chart  with grouped states and if the chart is accessing Data Store Memory (DSM),  then Simulink Design Verifier compatibility check fails with following error message:Simulink Design Verifier failed to initialize: Checking compatibility failed  Cannot change transitions inside a grouped state or a grouped graphical function
Fixed In: R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 2, R2022a

Summary: Pressing Ctrl+C?when working on a Simulink model and in debug mode might cause MATLAB to crash
Description: When working on a Simulink model and in debug mode, pressing?Ctrl+C? might cause MATLAB?to crash. The crash often happens when pressing?Ctrl+C? forces the model to close. For example, when you debug with a script that opens a model and press?Ctrl+C , causing the model to close, MATLAB might crash.

Summary: JMAAB check for?jc_0643?does not detect fixed point data type in Simulink data object
Description: Model Advisor check?Check usage of fixed-point data type with non-zero bias?  (ID: mathworks.jmaab.jc_0643)  does not flag warning when fixed point data type with non-zero bias is used inside a Simulink data object.

Summary: FTP connection error
Description: If you have an HTTP proxy configured in MATLAB, you might see an error when attempting to connect to an FTP server in MATLAB R2021b. To identify if you are affected by this issue, run this?MATLAB FTP example . If you see the following error message, then this issue affects you. >> ftpobj = ftp("ftp.ngdc.noaa.gov")Error using matlab.io.ftp.FTP (line 175)Could not open a connection to "ftp://ftp.ngdc.noaa.gov", port "21". This error might also appear when using Simulink Real-Time with Speedgoat targets in MATLAB R2021b: >> tg.update('force',true)Error using slrealtime.Target/throwErrorCannot update software on target 'Speedgoat': Cannot copy file'C:\ProgramData\MATLAB\SupportPackages\R2021b\toolbox\slrealtime\target\supportpackage\...'to '/home/slrt/...' on target 'Speedgoat': Could not open aconnection to "ftp://192.168.1.1", port "21". Similar FTP-related connection error messages might appear when using other functions in MATLAB with FTP URLs, such as: aeroReadIERSData  in Aerospace Toolbox getgeodata  in Bioinformatics Toolbox FTP-related examples in the Datafeed Toolbox, like "Determine Buy-Sell Imbalance Using Cost Index" or the costCurves  function example Data transfer functions in the Parrot Drone support package  Additionally, some functions might be slower to execute when using FTP URLs, such as: urlread ?and?urlwrite ?in base MATLAB kmlwrite ?in Mapping Toolbox

Summary: MATLAB crashes when a model reference uses a model argument with simulation input
Description: MATLAB crashes when all the following conditions are met :?The model has model reference with model arguments A Simulation input uses setVariable(myParam,ˇŻWorkspaceˇŻ,ˇŻmodelNameˇŻ)  You simulate the top level model with the simulation input  Sample stack trace:  C:\MATLAB\R2021b\bin\win64\sl_compile.dll+14200932 ParameterConditionHolder::computeNetConditionsForInstP+00000228  C:\MATLAB\R2021b\bin\win64\sl_compile.dll+14206451 ParameterConditionHolder::getVariantConditionForInstP+00000195  C:\MATLAB\R2021b\bin\win64\sl_compile.dll+09910125 ParameterConditionHolder::operator=+00048013  C:\MATLAB\R2021b\bin\win64\sl_compile.dll+09910856 ParameterConditionHolder::operator=+00048744  C:\MATLAB\R2021b\bin\win64\sl_engine_classes.dll+04281350 slplugin::addCompBdSignal+00018118  C:\MATLAB\R2021b\bin\win64\libmwsimulink.dll+08834791 SLModelSlicerUtils::getSegmentsInPath+00014391  C:\MATLAB\R2021b\bin\win64\libmwsimulink.dll+08824592 SLModelSlicerUtils::getSegmentsInPath+00004192  C:\MATLAB\R2021b\bin\win64\libmwsimulink.dll+09528769 sl_pir::copyContentBetweenModels+00055825  C:\MATLAB\R2021b\bin\win64\libmwsimulink.dll+10378573 RTWCG::RTWContextImpl::PopulateVarGroupIF::useRootOutportBuffer+00387613  C:\MATLAB\R2021b\bin\win64\libmwsimulink.dll+10397272 RTWCG::RTWContextImpl::PopulateVarGroupIF::useRootOutportBuffer+00406312  C:\MATLAB\R2021b\bin\win64\libmwsimulink.dll+10117765 RTWCG::RTWContextImpl::PopulateVarGroupIF::useRootOutportBuffer+00126805  C:\MATLAB\R2021b\bin\win64\libmwsimulink.dll+10167500 RTWCG::RTWContextImpl::PopulateVarGroupIF::useRootOutportBuffer+00176540  C:\MATLAB\R2021b\bin\win64\libmwsimulink.dll+07482929 slFullRTWGen+00000497

Model = rl.representation.model.rlDLNetworkModel(Model, UseDevice, ObservationNames, ActionNames);    Error in rlValueRepresentation (line 75)  Model = rl.util.createInternalModelFactory(Model, Options, ObservationNames, ActionNames, InputSize, OutputSize);    Error in ebr_21b (line 21)  rlValueRepresentation(lgraph,obsInfo,'Observation',["input_1" "input_2"])    Caused by:  ? ? Summary: LDRAcover in LDRA software integration?does not work
Description: Code coverage analysis with LDRAcover in a software-in-the-loop (SIL) or processor-in-the-loop (PIL) simulation fails because LDRAcover is not handled correctly in the LDRA software integration.
Workaround: Instead of LDRAcover, use LDRA Testbed, a component of LDRA tool suite.

Summary: HDF-EOS2 functions gd.ij2ll, gd.getPixels, and gd.readField return transposed values
Description: The?gd.ij2ll  and gd.readField ?functions return?lat  and lon  values that are the transpose of the corresponding values returned in R2020a and previous versions. Similarly, the?gd.getPixels  function?returns values of row  and col ?that are interchanged.
Fixed In: R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 1, R2022a

Summary: ROS/ROS 2 Subscriber's?receive?function?returns status?output as true in the generated code even when no message is received.
Description: While running a ROS or ROS 2 node generated from MATLAB, you may observe that whether a message is received or not, the receive ?function of the rossubscriber  or  ros2subscriber ?objects  always return the status  output as true .

Summary: A2l file is not generated for model containing a referenced model with a vector as input or output
Description: An A2l?file is not generated for a model that contains a referenced model, and the input or output to the referenced model is a vector. Note, that if the System target file   parameter is set to slrealtime.tlc , A2l file generation is part of the build process, and there is no an error. For other supported targets, generating the A2l file by using the Generate Calibration Files tool or the coder.asap2export  function results in an error.?

Summary: MATLAB might crash when you output internal variables of FMU
Description: MATLAB might crash if you select only some internal variables as outputs for an FMU block, or do not select all output variables as outputs. For example, MATLAB crashes if you select the third internal variable as an output, but not the first and second.

Summary: The category variables in a Fit Data program are ignored
Description: When you run a Fit Data  program in the SimBiology Model Analyzer ?app, the app ignores the variables specified in the Category Variable  column of the Estimated Parameters  table. Instead an unpooled fit is run.

Summary: MATLAB may throw "Unrecognized function or variable 'Settings'" error on exit in -nodesktop mode
Description: If MATLAB is started in nodesktop mode, this error might appear on exit: ? Error using exit ? Unrecognized function or variable 'Settings'.? This error can be ignored.?
Workaround: To avoid seeing this error during shutdown, disable the "Improve MATLAB by sending user experience information to MathWorks" option in the General section of the Preferences for MATLAB.

Summary: Simulink simulation failure for 'Discrete FIR Filter' and 'FIR Interpolation' blocks in MATLAB installed Ubuntu machines
Description: Simulation fails for? 'Discrete FIR Filter'  or 'FIR Interpolation'  blocks on Ubuntu machines with any of the following error messages: undefined reference to `halideInit'undefined reference to `processHalide_firInterpolatorRRD'?undefined reference to `processHalide_discreteFIR_RRD '  These error messages occur in the following simulation modes, and they occur due to the incompatibility of these blocks with the Ubuntu build gcc compiler.Rapid Accelerator mode Model Reference Simulation - Accelerator? Dataflow Domain

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated code for pspectrum gives different values than MATLAB for high sample rates
Description: The code generated by the pspectrum  function gives results different from those of MATLAB when the sample rate is high. One way to reproduce the issue is to use this function signature: p = pspectrum(ts,fs)  with? fs > 1e8.

Summary: Generated code might be incorrect for Terminate Function block connected to root Outport block
Description: ?Incorrect generated C code might occur for an AUTOSAR Classic model containing a Terminate Function block connected to a root Outport block.

Summary: Breakpoints in methods called by MATLAB Function blocks or Stateflow blocks are ignored
Description: When you use a MATLAB Function block or Stateflow blocks to call class methods defined in M-files, MATLAB and Simulink ignore the breakpoints set in those M-files and does not stop the simulation as expected.?

Summary: Different code might be generated when you change the code generation order of models that use the same reusable library subsystem
Description: The generated code of a reusable library subsystem might be different depending on the order in which you generate code for the models that include the reusable library subsystem.
Workaround: Disable the model configuration parameter Reuse local block outputs.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 3, R2022a, R2022b

Summary: Numerical mismatch in results between normal and fast restart simulations when solver is changed
Description: While changing solvers in some models initialized using fast restart, there can be numerical mismatch in logged information when compared to normal simulation.

Summary: While editing signal names in Signal Editor user interface, text field cursor can only be positioned using keyboard
Description: The signal name text field of the Signal Editor user interface disappears when you click in the text field.

Summary: Apollo Export: Lane 0 exported as inner neighbor for lanes with ids -1 and 1
Description: When exporting to Apollo, lanes with id -1 and 1 specify 0 as their neighbor rather than the lane on the opposite side of the road. This occurs for all road exported to Apollo.?

Summary: The Back, Forward, and Up to Parent arrows in a Simulink window might be incorrectly disabled
Description: Opening a subsystem or variant subsystem might disable the Back , Forward , and Up to Parent  arrows in a Simulink window.?
Workaround: Use other affordances to navigate among model components. For example, open the Model Browser by clicking the Hide/Show Model Browser button at the bottom left corner of the Simulink window.?

Summary: Crash when you call?listLength?with a single input
Description: MATLAB crashes when you call listLength  with a single input. The input can have any data type. For example, both of these calls to listLength  cause a crash: >> listLength(1) or >> c = {[]}; >> listLength(c{:})

Summary: Connecting to HTTP or FTP servers from MATLAB is slow
Description: Workflows in MATLAB that connect to HTTP or FTP servers are slow. ?For example, retrieving a file over FTP might take longer than expected. >> ftpobj = ftp('ftp. ngdc. noaa. gov')  >> mget(ftpobj,"README.txt");  This also impacts workflows that use HTTP or FTP indirectly - such as connecting to target hardware in Simulink Real-Time, or importing requirements into Simulink Requirements from third party applications.

Summary: Unexpected error when specifying group-specific variants to sbiofitmixed when using nlmefitsa
Description: If you use the Variants  name-value argument to specify group-specific variants to sbiofitmixed  when using nlmefitsa ?as the estimation function, the following error occurs: Error using horzcatDimensions of arrays being concatenated are not consistent.
Workaround: Use nlmefit instead of nlmefitsa with sbiofitmixed when specifying group-specific variants.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Wrong answer for model with Outports using GetSet or AccessFunction storage class and a referenced model containing a Function-Call Subsystem block
Description: Code generation of a model might result in a silent wrong answer when all of these conditions are true: The model contains a Model block that is directly connected to an Outport block. The Outport block uses a GetSet  storage class or other storage class with Data Access  set to Function  and Access Mode  set to Value . The referenced model contains a Function-Call Subsystem block that is connected to the Outport block.  Code generation might also be incorrect if the output signals of the Model block in the parent model use a GetSet  storage class.
Workaround: Insert a Signal Conversion block in the parent model between the Model block and the Outports that are mapped to?GetSet or Function?storage class.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 2, R2022a

Summary: Reopening model with Simulation 3D Camera Get block mounted on Simulation 3D Aircraft block reverts?Vehicle mounting location?to?Origin
Description: If you save and reopen a model with with a Simulation 3D Camera Get mounted on a Simulation 3D Aircraft block, the Simulation 3D Camera Get block parameter Vehicle mounting location  is set to Origin .

Summary: Copying and pasting a requirement does not copy custom attributes
Description: If you add custom attributes to a requirement and then copy and paste the requirement, the software deletes the custom attributes for that requirement. You must add them back manually.

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier compatibility check fails for models having custom code location specified using relative paths
Description: In models where custom code location is provided using relative paths, Simulink Design Verifier extraction workflow fails during compatibility check in the following scenarios:Running analysis directly on the model using sldvcompat  or sldvrun  Extracting the model first using sldvextract  and then running the analysis on the extracted model

Summary: Signal Editor user interface stops responding after signal data type is changed to enum
Description: The Signal Editor user interface stops responding if an enumeration data type is entered in the data type field and you do not click the Enter  key before clicking elsewhere in the signal tree area.

Summary: Crash with CreateSharedDataCopy on top of the stack
Description: MATLAB crashes with CreateSharedDataCopy  on top of the stack. This example shows a typical stack trace: Stack Trace (from fault):[ 0] 0x00007ff8e18258fa bin\win64\pgo\libmwlxeutilities.dll+00022778 MathWorks::lxe::CreateSharedDataCopy+00000010[ 1] 0x00007ff8e18f63bf bin\win64\pgo\libmwlxeindexing.dll+00615359 MathWorks::lxe::rparen_mcos_scalar_subscripts_reference+00020775[ 2] 0x00007ff8e188f298 bin\win64\pgo\libmwlxeindexing.dll+00193176 MathWorks::lxe::IsSetEnabledDefault+00041324[ 3] 0x00007ff8e187258f bin\win64\pgo\libmwlxeindexing.dll+00075151 MathWorks::lxe::IIndexingContext::FindOverloadedSubsrefWithoutValidation+00001579 The crash can result from an incorrectly configured MATLAB path, where matlab/toolbox/matlab/indexing  is not included on the path.
Workaround: If you encounter a crash like this, you can verify that the path is the problem:>> which matlab.indexing.IndexingContextIf the result of this command is the following output, then your path is incorrectly configured.'matlab.indexing.IndexingContext' not found.To configure your path correctly, use these commands:>> restoredefaultpath >> rehash toolboxcache>> savepathThese commands erase any custom paths you might have had. To backup custom paths, please follow the instructions mentioned in this MATLAB answer:?How will running the "restoredefaultpath" and "savepath" commands affect my custom paths and how do I back up my custom paths?If these instructions do not fix the problem, please contact MathWorks technical support.

Summary: JMAAB check for hisl_0016?does not report multiple?Compare To Zero?blocks
Description: Model Advisor?check?Check relational comparisons on floating-point signals? (id:?mathworks.hism.hisl_0016)? does not report warning for subsequent occurrences?of Compare To Zero  block s in a model with Automatic Names  and not renamed.

Summary: Conditional pause set on a signal in a multi-instanced model reference might not work properly
Description: A conditional pause in a multi-instanced model reference might be triggered even if that model block is set to "not visible".? In such situations, the correct behavior is that the conditional pause must be triggered only if that model reference is set to visible in normal model visibility.

Summary: Image Labeler app erroneously preserves pixel labels for deleted images
Description: Image Labeler app erroneously preserves pixel labels after images are deleted. Specifically, when images are selected in the app from right to left and then deleted, the app erroneously saves the corresponding pixel labels from the deleted images and reassigns them to existing images.
Workaround: To work around this problem, delete the images from left to right instead of right to left.

Summary: Updating model that has unset Sample time properties crashes Simulink
Description: Simulink crashes when you use the update diagram command on a Simulink model configured with these settings:  Sample time constraint  parameter is set to Specified?  Sample time properties ?parameter is unset
Workaround: Use either of these options to avoid crashing Simulink:Set the?Sample time properties parameter to?one or more valid sample time valuesSet the Sample time constraint?parameter to Unconstrained or Ensure sample time independent

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results might occur for a model containing a Lookup Table block having symbolic variable with negative sign
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets all of these conditions: The model contains a Lookup table block having a port with symbolic expression. The symbolic expression contains a symbolic variable with a negative sign (example [(-A<=B)+9] )  In the generated code, unwanted unsigned cast is inserted to the symbolic variable with a negative sign which might result in integer wrapping.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 4, R2022a, R2022b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Atomic subsystems that output the same signal multiple times might produce incorrect results
Description: Simulation and code generation might produce incorrect results when all of these conditions are true:The model has an atomic subsystem with multiple output ports. Two or more of the output ports have the same source. Bus Creator blocks group the signals from the output ports into multiple virtual buses and pass them to another atomic subsystem. Bus Selector blocks select two or more signals that have the same source and pass them to different destinations.
Workaround: Add a SignalConversion block before one of the Outport blocks in the atomic subsystem.

Summary: Merge results in corrupted Requirement Set
Description: When you load two copies of the same Requirement Set  file, the second file gets its internal IDs changed in memory. This can happen when user simultaneously opens Requirement Set files from multiple git branches. However, when the second file gets saved, new internal IDs are saved to the requirement set file. Upon merging the two files, the result requirement set file gets corrupted.
Workaround: While working on requirement set files in multiple git branches, invoke slreq.clear() before switching git branches. Note that this command will clear all requirement data in memory and close requirement editor.

Summary: Using the KernelDataType module property in Processor Configuration to set module data types does not throw error
Description: Until R2021a, the workflow to set module datatype in the Processor Configuration used the setModuleProperty  method on the 'KernelDataType'  property. % Create Processor Configuration object>>hPC = dlhdl.ProcessorConfig('Bitstream','zcu102_single')% Set Convolution and FC module datatypes to 'int8'>>hPC.setModuleProperty('conv','KernelDataType','int8');>>hPC.setModuleProperty('fc','KernelDataType','int8'); In R2021b, the KernelDataType  property was replaced with the ProcessorDataType  property. This property affects all modules (Convolution, FC and Adder). >>hPC.ProcessorDataType = 'int8'; This change was recorded in the release notes,?Release Notes for Deep Learning HDL Toolbox - MATLAB & Simulink (mathworks.com) . However, if you run a script that was created in R2021a, then the old code runs without erroring out, but it has no effect on the Processor Configuration.

Summary: Review Manager not displayed in AUTOSAR architecture model toolstrip
Description: The Review Manager is missing from the Simulink Toolstrip in AUTOSAR architecture models. Architecture models should display the Review Manager in the?Design ?gallery on the?Modeling ?tab.

Summary: edfwrite?does not create files when full path is provided or overwrite files on the MATLAB path
Description: The edfwrite  function does not create new files when the full path is provided or overwrite files that are on the MATLAB path. You can only create or overwrite files in the current directory. When you attempt to write the file anywhere other than the current directory, the function throws an error in the command window: Error using datestr (line 131)Not enough input arguments.Error in edfwrite/getFileProperties (line 1738)        obj.FileModDate =? string(datestr(fileInfo.datenum));Error in edfwrite (line 502)? ?     obj = getFileProperties(obj, fileInfo, true);
Workaround: To avoid this issue, you can either:Move to the relevant directory before using edfwrite and return to the current directory if needed.Work only in the current directory.

Description: MATLAB might crash when you run a model that contains a C Function block, if all the following conditions are true:? The C Function block uses an imported Simulink bus or enumeration.? Summary: Navigation link from external document to MATLAB/Simulink does not work
Description: When you create a link from lines of MATLAB code in a MATLAB function block to an external document such as an IBM DOORS Module or a Microsoft Office document, navigating from the external document to the linked lines in MATLAB code by using the backlink in the external document might not work. You might see this error: file not found  or invalid link .

Summary: Input argument error when trying to log a signal in Stateflow
Description: Stateflow might throw an error if a signal in Stateflow is either set as a logged signal twice or set and unset: Invalid input for argument 2 (rhs2):  Value must be a scalar.??
Workaround: Deleting the data for which signals are being logged and recreating it might alleviate the issue.

Summary: radarDetectionGenerator System object and Radar Detection Generator Simulink block do not generate detections for targets in its field of view when its yaw, pitch, and roll angles are not set to 0.?
Description: When a radarDetectionGenerator   is mounted such that its orientation is not aligned with the axes of the ego vehicle's frame, targets that are shown in the radar's field of view may not be detected. The problem can be demonstrated using the following code:  rdg = radarDetectionGenerator;  % Yaw the radar 20 degrees from the ego vehicle's axes  rdg.Yaw = 20;  ?  % Place a target 20 degrees off of the ego vehicle's heading  [x,y] = pol2cart(deg2rad(20),50); % Target at 50 meters from radar  tgt = struct('ActorID',1,'Position',[x y 0],'Yaw',0);  ?  % Generate detections with radar  simTime = 0;  [dets, numDets] = rdg(tgt, simTime);  ?  % Find the detections that are not false alarms  tgtIDs = zeros(1,numDets);  for iDet = 1:numDets      thisDet = dets{iDet};      tgtIDs(iDet) = thisDet.ObjectAttributes{1}.TargetIndex;   end  disp(tgtIDs)  ?  % False alarms will have a Target ID less-than 0. True target detections  % will have a target ID that matches the ActorID of the target that was  % detected.  isFA = tgtIDs<0;  ?  % Select the detections that were generated from the target  tgtDets = dets(~isFA)  ?  % No targets were detected  numTgtDets = numel(tgtDets)  ?  % Create a bird's-eye plot to show the target's location in the radar's  % field of view  bep = birdsEyePlot('XLim', [0 60], 'YLim', [-35 35]);  ?  % Plot the target car  olPlotter = outlinePlotter(bep);  profiles = rdg.ActorProfiles;  plotOutline(olPlotter, tgt.Position(1:2), tgt.Yaw, profiles.Length, profiles.Width, 'OriginOffset', profiles.OriginOffset(1:2));  ?  % Plot radar's field of view  caPlotter = coverageAreaPlotter(bep, 'DisplayName', 'Radar Coverage Area');  plotCoverageArea(caPlotter, rdg.SensorLocation, rdg.MaxRange, rdg.Yaw, rdg.FieldOfView(1));  ?  % Plot the radar detections  detPlotter = detectionPlotter(bep, 'DisplayName', 'Radar Detections');  if numTgtDets>0      detPos = cellfun(@(d)d.Measurement(1:2), tgtDets, 'UniformOutput', false);      detPos = cell2mat(detPos')';      plotDetection(detPlotter, detPos);   end
Workaround: If code generation is not required, then drivingRadarDataGenerator and its associated Simulink block can be used instead of the radarDetectionGenerator.

Summary: Incorrect results on export function models containing calls to Simulink Functions
Description: Simulink Design Verifier analysis may provide incorrect results when an export function model contains a call to a Simulink Function but the corresponding Simulink Function is not defined.  For example, if the Simulink Function caller outputs a uint8  value, the Design Error Detection analysis will report a single value of 0 as the range of that output instead of the entire uint8  range.

Summary: Signal Analyzer and Signal Labeler?show incorrect spectrogram plots for complex-valued signals longer than 1 million points
Description: The Signal Analyzer ?and Signal Labeler ?apps display incorrect spectrograms for complex-valued input signals when the signal length exceeds 1 million points.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 2, R2022a

Summary: Simscape Onramp chapter 7 "Simscape and Simulink", section 1 "Feedback Control" task 5 doesn't pass
Description: Simscape Onramp might incorrectly mark your submission to?chapter 7 "Simscape and Simulink", section 1 "Feedback Control" task 5 as wrong, even though the plot shows no errors.

Summary: Missing dimensional analysis of dimensionally inconsistent classifiers when using sbiompgsa
Description: If classifiers reference species or reaction-scoped parameters by their name, which is not qualified by its parent, then the dimensional analysis of the classifier is skipped. Therefore, if the classifier is not dimensionally consistent and unit conversion is on, the incorrect classifier is evaluated, leading to incorrect sensitivity results.  The function does not issue an error about the dimensionally inconsistent classifier.
Workaround: Ensuring classifiers are dimensionally consistent when using sbiompgsa always yields correct results.Classifiers are dimensionally consistent if units on the left-hand side and the right-hand side can be converted into each other.?To ensure SimBiology performs dimensional analysis for you, reference species or reaction-scoped parameters in classifiers via their qualified names.Qualified names of species consist of the owning compartment's name, followed by a dot, followed by the species name.Qualified names of reaction-scoped parameters consist of the owning reaction's name, followed by a dot, followed by the parameter name. For example, the qualified name of a species "S" in compartment "C" is "C.S".?

Summary: SIL simulation fails due to incorrect type definitions for some structure data types in Rte_Type.h
Description: SIL simulation produces a compiler error or warning for incompatible data types when all of the following conditions are true: The model was created by importing an ARXML description authored using a third party tool. The imported ARXML containsan ImplementationDataType ?with the?CATEGORY ?field set to? STRUCTURE  and its elements do not have the? CATEGORY ?set to  TYPE_REFERENCE .  There are changes in the Simulink model that require a change in the type definitions of structure elements in Rte_Type.h. The model configuration parameter RTWVerbose  is set to on .   The SIL simulation compiler error resembles the following: error: incompatible type for argument 1 of 'Rtw_IWrite_Out1_Out1' ?
Workaround: Open and build the affected model.?Unzip the attached folder.Run the following command at the MATLAB command line:? fixCorruptionInStructElementTypes(modelName)where modelName is the name of the affected model.Save the model.

Summary: Error borrowing licenses using the License Borrowing UI
Description: When using the License Borrowing UI, an error may appear in the command window when attempting to borrow products. This can happen when you select "Borrow selected products by choosing from a list" and all products are selected.
Workaround: You can select a subset of the products available to borrow or you can choose the option "Borrow products automatically as you work with them."

Summary: Diagnostic viewer does not show output if the search box is open
Description: If the search box inside the Diagnostic Viewer is opened, then the subsequent diagnostics will not be shown in the Diagnostic Viewer. This leads to the loss of data. However, the previous diagnostics will not be impacted.

Summary: App Designer Code Browser missing functions after section break (%%)
Description: Placing a section break (%%) into App Designer's Code View before a methods block or a function block can cause the Code Browser to stop displaying functions after that section break.? New functions added from the toolstrip or Code Browser may also be created with the same name as an existing function, when that function is placed after a section break.For example, with the following code, the Code Browser will display "func1" but not "func2" or "func3".  methods (Access = private)  function func1(app)  end  %%   function func2(app)   end   function func3(app)   end    end

Summary: JMAAB check for?jc_0630?displays an incorrect warning for Multiport Switch block
Description: The?Sub-ID a? of Model Advisor?check?mathworks.jmaab.jc_0630 (jc_0630_a:?Check number of inputs to Multiport Switch block )?displays the following warning for?Multiport Switch block when parameter Data port order  is set to Specify indices  .? Warning :? The following Multiport Switch blocks have less than 2 data ports.  This warning is incorrect as number of data ports is atleast two as per guideline because the setting doesn't support one data port case.

Summary: ?Clicking "Save to standalone FMU" shows an error when the parameter is a struct with unsupported data type.?
Description: When a parameter is a struct  with unsupported datatype as its element, user gets an error when they click "Save to standalone FMU". No dialog will be opened.

Summary: Exporting a Simulink model that contains a hidden MATLAB Function block might cause MATLAB to crash
Description: A hidden MATLAB Function block is a MATLAB function block with the attribute MaskHideContents  set to true .? Exporting a Simulink model with a hidden MATLAB Function block might cause MATLAB to throw an Access Violation  error.

Summary: AUTOSAR models having Simulink signals or parameters in the baseworkspace that use storage classes might generate uncompilable code.
Description: AUTOSAR models having Simulink signals or parameters in the base workspace that use storage classes might generate uncompilable code.
Workaround: Do not create signal or parameter data objects in the base workspace and associate them with a storage class.The recommended workflow is:1. Add a Simulink signal or parameter in the model workspace.2.?Use code mappings to configure the signal to "ArTypedPerInstanceMemory" or parameter to "SharedParameter" or "PerInstanceParameter".3. Use this signal or parameter in your model.
Fixed In: R2020b Update 7, R2021b Update 3, R2022a, R2022b

Summary: Loading a MAT-file containing a MAXP object crashes MATLAB
Description: When you load a MAT-file that contains a MAXP object, MATLAB crashes. For example, the following script crashes MATLAB: d = matlab.io.xml.dom.Document('book');e = getDocumentElement(d);save;load('matlab.mat','e');

Summary: Polyspace Access fails to start after upgrading from R2021a to R2021b
Description: After you upgrade Polyspace Access from version R2021a to version R2021b and click Restart Apps  in the Cluster Dashboard , the Polyspace Access  app fails to start due to an issue with the?Polyspace Access ETL  container. When you inspect the log of the?Polyspace Access ETL ?container, you see an error message similar to this: Cannot execute: INSERT INTO acl_sid (principal, sid) VALUES (false, 'PUBLIC_GROUP_FOR_ACCESS_USER')-!> [28-09-2021 at 10:09:39.071] *.datasource.etl.PvfUtilities.executeSqlCommands(...) at 751| PSQLException: ERROR: duplicate key value violates unique constraint "unique_uk_1"Detail: Key (sid, principal)=(PUBLIC_GROUP_FOR_ACCESS_USER, f) already exists. Depending on the size of your existing Polyspace Access database, the Cluster Dashboard ?might take a long time before displaying a status of Failed  for the Polyspace Access ?app, indicating that Polyspace Access did not start.
Workaround: To fix the issue, follow these steps:On the machine where you installed Polyspace Access, open a Bash shell and run this command:docker exec -it polyspace-access-db-main psql -U postgres -d prs_data -c "UPDATE \"Metadata\".\"Automation\" SET \"Status\" = 'COMPLETED' WHERE \"Step\" = 'Remove users and create public group-2021a_to_2021b.txt';"?The Polyspace Access DB container must be running before you run the command.On the Cluster Dashboard, click?Restart Apps.?You do not need to run the command again if, later, you restart or delete all the apps.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Logging of subsubbus is incorrect if subbus is not the first field in the main bus
Description: Simulink incorrectly logs data for certain bus signals that are in a hierarchy.? For example, create a bus with the following structure: MainBus?  ? ? int a1;  ? ? SubBus subBus;  ? ? ? ? int a2;  ? ? ? ?SubSubBus subSubBus;  ? ? ? ? ? ? int a3;  then set this bus type to output data y  and initialize it as y.a1 = 1 ,  y.subBus.a2 = 2 ,  y.subBus.subSubBus.a3 = 3 . Simulink logs the value for a3  as 2 , instead of 3  as expected.
Workaround: The workaround is either not to use subsubbuses or make sure their offset is zero. For example, the bus in the previous example can be defined as following:MainBus??? ? SubBus subBus;? ? ? ?SubSubBus subSubBus;? ? ? ? ? ? int a3;?? ? ? ? int a2;? ? int a1;In this case the offset of SubSubBus in the main bus is zero.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 2, R2022a

Summary: Simulink compilation slows down when a top model contains a Ground block or an Inport block using large nested buses
Description: Simulink model compilation slows down when the model contains a Ground block or an Inport block at the top level using large nested buses.
Fixed In: R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 3, R2022a
