Summary: Some heat exchanger blocks might exhibit incorrect specific heats under certain conditions
Description: The Simple Heat Exchanger Interface (G) block might incorrectly calculate specific heat when you set the Gas Specification ?parameter to Real . The block calculates a specific heat at constant pressure (cp) value of 1 kJ/kg/K, which might limit the value of the specific dissipation to an incorrect theoretical maximum.  This bug also affects the Simple variant of these blocks: Heat Exchanger (G) Heat Exchanger (G-G) Heat Exchanger (G-TL)?
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 1, R2022a

Summary: mdlTerminate?and mdlCleanupRuntimeResources?not called during model simulation for startup variant inactive block?
Description: Simulink model and block methods are executed in sequence?SetupRuntimeResources (mdlSetupRuntimeResources ), Start(mdlStart), Terminate (mdlTerminate ) and CleanupRuntimeResources (mdlCleanupRuntimeResources ).  But when a block is inactive by a Variant block whose?Variant Activation time ?is set to?startup , only SetupRuntimeResources?and Start methods are executed, while terminate and CleanupRuntimeResources methods are not executed.

Summary: Non virtual buses using update diagram mapping options might return incorrect error message
Description: Non virtual buses using the update diagram mapping options might return an incorrect error message as a result of the mapping operation.

Summary: A2L and ARXML generation might not be supported for models with lookup tables and breakpoint parameters which are fed through input ports
Description: A2L and ARXML generation might not be supported when the following conditions are true: The model contains a Prelookup block. The Prelookup block has a breakpoint with Specification  set to Explicit values ?and Source ?set to  Input port .
Workaround: For the Prelookup block, change the Source parameter to Dialog and specify the breakpoint data values in the provided field.

Summary: Import of File Log data from a target computer can report errors
Description: If a folder on the MATLAB path has the same name as the real-time application on the target computer from which File Log data is being imported, MATLAB reports an error. The error reports that MATLAB cannot change the current folder to a temporary folder on the development computer.
Workaround: Remove the folder with the same name as the real-time application.
Fixed In: R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 2, R2022a

Summary: The JMAAB check for?mathworks.jmaab.ar_0002?reports auto generated folder names which violate the guideline
Description: The Model Advisor check?Usable characters for folder names?(mathworks.jmaab.ar_0002)? reports auto generated folder names which violate the guideline. Example: The folders generated post code generation which are not compatible with guideline are also flagged.

Summary: Increase memory usage on development computer during File Log import
Description: During File Log import of large datasets into the Simulink Data Inspector, memory usage on the development computer may increase considerably. This usage increase may cause long wait times for the import process to complete or may cause MATLAB to close. This issue appears more often on development computers that have less memory installed.
Fixed In: R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 1, R2022a

Summary: JMAAB check for?jc_0628?displays an incorrect warning for?Saturation and Saturation Dynamic blocks
Description: Model Advisor check Check usage of the Saturation blocks ?(id: mathworks.jmaab.jc_0628 ) displays incorrect warning for Saturation and Saturation Dynamic blocks with same compiled input and output data types but has?type casting operations in their limits setting.

Summary: JMAAB check for jc_0623?does not report delay blocks used in?continuous type model or subsystem
Description: Model Advisor?check?mathworks.jmaab.jc_0623 (Usage of continuous-time Delay blocks and discrete-time Delay blocks) ?does not report?warning?for? delay ?blocks used in?continuous type model or subsystem as per the guideline.

Summary: Error when a function generated by matlabFunction is called with nonscalar input
Description: This error can happen only if both of these conditions are true: the input to matlabFunction  is not a scalar symbolic expression. the input to the function that is generated by matlabFunction  is also nonscalar.  Then MATLAB might throw the following error. "Error using reshape Number of elements must not change." For example, executing this code results in the error. A=sum(power(sym('a'),1:10));B=sum(power(sym('b'),1:10));C=sum(power(sym('c'),1:10));X=[A;B;C;2*A;2*B;2*C;3*A;3*B];f=matlabFunction(X,'File','Xfun',Optimize = false);f([1,2],[2,3],[3,4]) Meanwhile, f(1,2,3)  works as expected.?
Workaround: In R2021b you can work around this issue by using the name-value argument Legacy=true.For example, when you run the code example in the Description section, replace the call to matlabFunction with this line of code.f=matlabFunction(X,'File','Xfun',Optimize = false, Legacy = true);

Summary: Test file containing two or more test cases using different harnesses might crash MATLAB when run in Test Manager
Description: In the Test Manager, if you run a test file that contains two or more test cases and each test case uses a different test harness, then MATLAB might crash.

Summary: Inactive state writer block writing state to an outport block with initial condition may error when it receives propagated variant condition
Description: When?you perform an update diagram on a model, Simulink may throw an error stating that Outport block has inherited initial output value, though initial output value is specified.This error can occur when all below conditions are met: Outport block, with some initial value specified for initial output,  and connected from a block which has variant condition State Writer  block writes to the Outport block with the initial condition and propagated variant condition Outport block is inactive by variant block's whose 'Variant control mode'  is in 'expression' ?mode and 'Variant Activation time'  is not set to?'update diagram'   Sample error message shown below: Block 'model/Function-Call Subsystem/Out1' has attributes or configurations that are not supported by State Writer block 'model/Initialize Function/Subsystem/type_a/State Writer'.  Caused by: Outport block 'model/Function-Call Subsystem/Out1' is inheriting its initial output value from its sources(s). To correct this problem, ensure that the 'Source of initial output value' parameter is set to 'Dialog' and that the 'Initial output' parameter is set to a value different from the empty matrix([]).?

Summary: Collecting code coverage for a model might cause a compiler error
Description: When you collect code coverage for a model, you might get a compiler error similar to the following:  foo.c45:80: error: macro "__MW_INSTRUM_NODE_8" passed 2 arguments, but takes just 1   The error occurs on an instrumented expression when?both  these conditions are true: the expression contains one or more logical operator (&&  or || ) one of the logical operator's operands is an indexing expression involving a function call  For example, the following expression triggers the build error: tab[fun(x)] || (x > 0)

Summary: JMAAB check for jc_0626?does not report Lookup Table blocks not having recommended Interpolation method settings
Description: Model Advisor?check Check usage of Lookup Table blocks  (id:?mathworks.jmaab.jc_0626)? does not report warning for Lookup Table  blocks with Interpolation Method setting set to values other than recommended in the guideline.

Summary: 'Show Antenna' check box appearing along with a drop down that contains 'Show Antenna' when plotting the radiation using pattern function.
Description: A checkbox with text 'Show Antenna' is appearing above the dropdown with the same option when plotting radiation pattern.? For example,? Consider plotting the radiation pattern of a dipole antenna.? d = dipole;? pattern(d,70e6)?? The user will see a 'Show Antenna' check box above the drop down that contains the 'Show Antenna' option in the radiation pattern plot of the dipole antenna.?

Summary: Report Explorer Lookup Table component does not report table data and breakpoints specified by Simulink.LookupTable object
Description: To specify table data and breakpoints for a Simulink Lookup Table block, if you use a?Simulink.LookupTable ?object as the?Data specification  , the Report Explorer Lookup Table component does not report the data and breakpoints defined in that?object . Instead, it reports the data and breakpoints as if?Data specification ?were set to?Table and breakpoints . This causes incorrect data to be reported or the following error to occur:? Index exceeds the number of array elements. Index must not exceed 1 .??
Workaround: Set?Data specification?to Table and breakpoints and define the table data and breakpoints using the block dialog. Alternatively, if you are using release R2018a or later, use the Report API?slreportgen.report.LookupTable class to report information about Lookup Table blocks, instead of using the Report Explorer.?
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 1, R2022a

Summary: Aggregation of coverage data for subsystem into top-model might fail
Description: When you use Simulink Test  to aggregate subsystem-level coverage into the top-model, and one or more of the subsystems receives MCDC for cascaded logic, you get an exception similar to the following:Index in position n  exceeds array bounds (must not exceed m ).  This exception causes the coverage aggregation to fail and you do not receive the cumulative coverage results.

Summary: On Windows 11, Polyspace Code Prover might error out during the Software Safety Analysis Level 1?part of the analysis and provide less precise results
Description: If you run a Polyspace Code Prover analysis on a Windows 11 host, you might encounter an error during the Software Safety Analysis Level 1  part of the analysis. The error message in the log file is?exception SML_Pre_Basis.Fail("failure in job")(Fail: failure in job) raised . The error does not mean that the entire analysis failed. You can still see the Polyspace Code Prover results, but the results are from the previous level, Software Safety Analysis Level 0 . Because higher levels indicate greater precision, the results of level 0 might contain a greater number of orange checks (unproven results).

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Simulink Coder might generate incorrect code for models with reusable subsystems that have function-call output signals
Description: Simulink Coder might generate incorrect code for models under the following circumstances: The model contains identical atomic subsystems with function-call output signals. The subsystems have auto  or reusable  function packaging. The function-call output signals connect to identical function-call subsystems. The subsystems initiating the function call have data connections to the function-call subsystems. The order of the data connection does not match between the various identical function-call initiator subsystems and function-call subsystem instances.  Simulink Coder might generate one instance for the function-call initiator subsystem, where multiple instances are required.

Summary: SwSystemConstantValueSets contains NaN values in generated ARXML
Description: When code is generated from a model that is mapped to an AUTOSAR component containing variant behavior, the values of the SystemConstants ?in the ARXML appear as NaN. <SW-SYSTEMCONSTANT-VALUE-SET>                       <SHORT-NAME>Default</SHORT-NAME>                        <SW-SYSTEMCONSTANT-VALUES>                            <SW-SYSTEMCONST-VALUE>                                 <SW-SYSTEMCONST-REF DEST="SW-SYSTEMCONST">/SwSystemconsts/MySysConst</SW-SYSTEMCONST-REF>                                  <VALUE>NaN</VALUE>                              </SW-SYSTEMCONST-VALUE>                         </SW-SYSTEMCONSTANT-VALUES>                    </SW-SYSTEMCONSTANT-VALUE-SET>?

Summary: HDL code generation fails for Real Reciprocal HDL Optimized block
Description: HDL code generation fails if a model contains a Real Reciprocal HDL Optimized block with this error message: ˇ°The sample rate 4 of port 'Cout' in network ˇ®Real    Reciprocal HDL Optimized/Shift and cast to output type/Cast to Union of Types'    is different from the expected sample rate inf. Consider inserting a Rate    Transition block before the port.ˇ±
Workaround: Modify the sample time of the Constant block in Shift and cast to output type block from 'inf' to -1.1. Break the library link of Real Reciprocal HDL Optimized block:    a. Right click on the block. Navigate to Library Link then select Disable Link.    b. Right click on the block again. Navigate to Library Link then select Break Link. 2. Open the block by right clicking on the block, navigate to Mask and select Look Under Mask.3. Break the library link of Shift and cast to output type block and open it.4. Double click on the Constant 0 block and change the sample time from 'inf'?to?-1.A fixed block with the library link removed is attached.When updating to a newer version of MATLAB, the block with library link removed needs to be replaced with new block with library link to incorporate the updates.More information regarding Real Reciprocal HDL Optimized block and sample time setting can be found at the following reference pages.https://www.mathworks.com/help/fixedpoint/ref/realreciprocalhdloptimized.html    https://www.mathworks.com/help/hdlcoder/ug/check-for-infinite-sample-time-sources.html

Summary: MATLAB crashes when updating the model a second time during accelerator mode simulation
Description: Compiling a model twice during simulation in accelerator mode causes MATLAB to crash when the model contains a user-written S-function and one of the parameters of the S-Function block references a workspace variable. For example, during an accelerator mode simulation of the model with the simulation time set to inf , clicking Update Model  on the Modeling  tab twice causes MATLAB to crash.

Summary: Missing Simulation 3D Vehicle with Ground Following block Location and Orientation output ports
Description: If the Simulation 3D Vehicle with Ground Following block parameters are not defined, these block output ports are missing:Location  Orientation?
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 1, R2022a

Summary: MATLAB might crash when generating an Embedded Coder report
Description: MATLAB might crash when you use Embedded Coder to generate code and then enable option Generate static code metrics ?when creating a report. This option is available under Configuration Parameters > Code Generation > Report . The issue occurs when you generate code on Windows machines using the Visual Studio 2019 toolchain and the code contains a templated class with a static member variable whose initializer uses the ->  operator. This issue might also occur when using other toolchains.

Summary: JMAAB check for?db_0129?does not report supertransitions crossing states
Description: The?Sub-ID c? of Model Advisor?check?mathworks.jmaab.db_0129 (db_0146_c:?Transition lines shall not cross over states) ?does not report?warnings for? supertransitions crossing states.

Summary: JMAAB check for db_0129 does not report transitions crossing graphical functions or subcharts
Description: The?Sub-ID c? of Model Advisor?check?mathworks.jmaab.db_0129 (db_0146_c:?Transition lines shall not cross over states)  does not report?warning?for transitions crossing graphical functions or subcharts.

Summary: MATLAB might crash when undoing the deletion of a Simulink subsystem component
Description: Simulink subsystem components are used to implement subsystem behaviors in System Composer. You can delete these components on the composition canvas by selecting the component and deleting the component using the keyboard. MATLAB might crash if, after deleting the component this action is undone by using the keyboard shortcut CTRL Z  or selecting the Undo  button from the toolstrip.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Data Store Read block that reads an array of buses that contains a string gives incorrect simulation results
Description: A Data Store Read block gives incorrect simulation results when all of these conditions apply: The Data Store Read block reads the entire data store from a Data Store Memory block. The Data Store Memory block specifies a Simulink.Bus  object data type. The specified Bus  object contains an element with a string  data type. The Data Store Memory block specifies a dimension greater than 1.  The Data Store Read block correctly reads only the first element of the array of buses. The other elements of the array of buses are set to zero.
Workaround: Read individual elements of the array of buses from the data store.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Yolov2Resnet18 predict incorrect results when ConvThread Number = 9,?25,?36,?49
Description: Networks output layer that is connected to the convolution layer will produce incorrect inference results when using ConvThreadNumber = 9,25,36,49.
Workaround: There isn't a easy workaround for this issue. Please use other ConvThreadNum. (e.g: 4,16)

Summary: Lookup Table Editor returns Java error message when opened for empty model with no lookup tables
Description: Opening the Lookup Table Editor following these steps returns a Java error message on the MATLAB command line: Open a model that contains no lookup table blocks. In Simulink Editor, click Modeling  tab. In the Modeling , click Lookup Table Editor .

Summary: Error during IP core generation in SoC Builder when FPGA model has reference models
Description: When you run SoC Builder for a model that contains one or more reference models in the FPGA model, you get this error during the IP core generation step. Could not apply model's setting to the Target Interface Table inTask 1.2 of the HDL Workflow Advisor for the following reason: Cannot connectto model 'soc_hwsw_fpga0'; please try Update Diagram (Ctrl-D). Default or lastvalid setting is applied instead. If you are using the HDL Workflow CommandLine Interface, consider making these changes in the HDL Workflow Advisor andusing 'Export to Script...' to automatically generate the appropriate 'hdlset_param' commands.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: When using any deep learning Simulink block, changes to the specified network MAT-file might not be reflected in the simulation or code generation results
Description: Consider a Simulink model containing a deep learning block that loads a trained network from a MAT-file. If the network in the MAT-file is updated but the rest of the model remains unchanged, Simulink might not rebuild the model when performing simulation or code generation. As a result, the deep learning block might produce incorrect output.

Summary: fwrite silently ignores errors due to disk running out of space
Description: Writing to a file using fwrite ?silently ignores errors associated with the target disk running out of space. As a result, the operation appears to be successful. For example, in this operation, fwrite ?writes to a file on a target disk that is already at full capacity. Yet the operation generates no error and appears to have been successful. >> fid = fopen("hello.txt",'w')fid =?????5>> fwrite(fid,"text")ans =?????4>> fclose(fid)ans =?????0

Summary: Adaptive AUTOSAR header file for stringview uses wrong file name
Description: As per the AUTOSAR standard for adaptive platforms(18_10, 19_03 and 19_11), an ara::core implementation file for datatype stringview should be named as string_view.h , but AUTOSAR Blockset generates a file with the name?stringview.h .

Summary: Unrecognized field name warning when running a custom training experiment in Experiment Manager
Description: When you use the Experiment Manager app to run a custom training experiment, the MATLAB Command Window might show a warning of the form: Error occurred while executing the listener callback for event MetricsUpdate defined for class experiments.Monitor:  Unrecognized field name "METRIC_NAME" .   ?After this warning, the results table in the app might display incorrect values for the metric.??

Summary: False discovery rate method applies incorrect denoising in wdenoise2 function
Description: Selecting the false discovery rate (FDR) method for the DenoisingMethod  value?in the? wdenoise2 ?function results in an output image with similar noise levels to the input image. FDR denoising is not correctly applied to the  image.? For example:? load flower  wdenoise2(flower.Noisy,2,'DenoisingMethod',{'FDR',0.01});  The denoised image and the original image have the same levels of noise.
Fixed In: R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 1, R2022a

Summary: allcycles on a graph or digraph with many nodes is slow
Description: Using allcycles  on a graph  or digraph  with more than 10,000 nodes is slow. The computation may be slow even for graphs that have few or no cycles.
Workaround: Use isdag to determine whether a digraph has no cycles at all. This is faster than the allcycles call.You may be able to usebins = conncomp(g, "OutputForm", "cell");bins(cellfun(@isscalar, bins)) = [];which returns a cell array of all connected components that contain more than one node. The concept of connected components is related to, but not equivalent to, that of cycles.

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier incorrectly reuses model representation when?CovLogicBlockShortCircuit or CovMcdcMode parameter value is changed
Description: Simulink Design Verifier incorrectly reuses model representation in following scenario: ModelCoverageObjectives  option for Simulink Design Verifier analysis is set to MCDC  or EnhancedMCDC  and RebuildModelRepresentation  option for Simulink Design Verifier analysis is set to IfChangeIsDetected  and You toggle value of either CovLogicBlockShortCircuit  or CovMcdcMode  and rerun Test Generation analysis

Summary: When deleting points in Signal Editor user interface, spinner might display indefinitely
Description: When selecting and deleting multiple points that contain sparse selections, the Signal Editor user interface might display a persistent spinner.

Summary: Incorrect normal-mode simulation results for a referenced model with a parameterized for-each subsystem
Description: Simulating a model hierarchy in normal mode might produce incorrect simulation results when all these conditions apply:? The model hierarchy contains multiple instances of a referenced model that simulate in normal mode. That referenced model contains a parameterized for-each subsystem. That subsystem contains a Sine Wave block.

Summary: Wrong error when there is a type mismatch in block inputs and outputs that have inherited bus types
Description: Stateflow throws the wrong error when a type mismatch is detected when inferring bus types for inputs or outputs of MATLAB Function or Stateflow blocks. The error being thrown is of the form: Error using block2chart>is_an_sflink (line 49)block_diagram does not have a parameter named 'ReferenceBlock' Error in block2chart (line 8) if(is_an_sflink(blockH))Error in sfprivate (line 13)? ? ? ?[varargout{:}] = feval(fcnName,varargin{:});Error in Stateflow.Translate/Simulink_Bus_BusTypePropSrcDstMismatchBusSrc (line 14)? ? chartId = sfprivate( 'block2chart', chartPath );Error in Stateflow.Translate.translate (line 100)? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? sfException = obj.(methodName);   ?

Summary: Invoking Serial Link Designer, Parallel Link Designer, or Signal Integrity Viewer cannot be launched if NVDA is installed.
Description: The Signal Integrity Toolbox? apps (Serial Link Designer , Parallel Link Designer , and?Signal Integrity Viewer ) do not start successfully if the Windows NonVisual Desktop Access (NVDA) is installed in your system. This is true whether you invoke the app from the App Gallery or by typing at the MATLAB command prompt. The MATLAB window may flicker, but otherwise there is no observed behavior. To confirm that installed NVDA is the cause of the error: Attempt to invoke the app by typing: "pr = " followed by the app name (parallelLinkDesigner, serialLinkDesigner or signalIntegrityViewer). As before, the app should not open.  Type si.utilities.stdOutErrFromProcess(pr). The error message should contain the text:  java.lang.UnsatisfiedLinkError: no JavaAccessBridge-64 in java.library.path     ?Pausing NVDA does not avoid the problem.?
Workaround: Before invoking any of the Signal Integrity Toolbox??apps, rename the file .accessibility.properties file in your home directory to some other name. After finishing with the app, rename the file back to .accessibility.properties.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generation of a column-major model with a MATLAB Function block using a row-major layout might result in silent wrong answer
Description: The generated code for a Simulink model might produce a silent wrong answer when all of these conditions are true:The model has the configuration parameter Array layout   set to Column-major . The model contains a MATLAB Function block that uses a row-major array layout. The MATLAB Function block performs array operations using vectors. For example, matrix indexing using vectors, reshaping a matrix to a vector, or transposing a vector.
Workaround: Use the same array layout for both the model and the MATLAB Function Block.

Summary: Error when calling TLC library function LibBlockSampleTime
Description: When LibBlockSampleTime  is called from a custom TLC file, Simulink code generation may fail or the function might produce incorrect answers.
Workaround: In the function?LibBlockSampleTime?from?<matlabroot>/rtw/c/tlc/public_api/block_api.tlc  file, replace the following line of code:Incorrect version: %return CGMODEL_ACCESS("Block.SampleTime", BlockIdx[0], BlockIdx[2])Fixed version:? ? ? %return CGMODEL_ACCESS("Block.SampleTime", block.BlockIdx[0], block.BlockIdx[2])

Summary: Incorrect signal might be logged by XCP-based external mode simulation
Description: ?An XCP-based external mode simulation might log an incorrect signal when, for a given block, all of these conditions hold: A signal from one of the block's output ports is marked for logging. A signal into one of the same block's input ports is marked for logging. The logged signal at the block's input port is not contiguous. For example, the output from a Mux block.  The waveform obtained from the block's output port might contain the data from the input port.
Workaround: For the affected block, insert a Signal Conversion block before the input port or after the output port.Mark the signal from the output port of the Signal Conversion block for logging.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b, R2022a

Summary: min?or?max with ComparisonMethod="abs" return incorrect output for array containing realmin
Description: Calling min  or max  to compute the minimum or maximum of an array may return an incorrect result when all of the following criteria are satisfied:The call uses one input array rather than comparing two input arrays  The call uses the comparison method "abs"  The input array is a real single or real double a rray The input array contains the exact value realmax , or realmax('single')  if the input array is single

Summary: Bulleted list formatting lost when importing requirements from Microsoft Word
Description: When you import requirements from Microsoft Word  and, in the Importing Requirements dialog box you select Ignore outline numbers in section headers , any bulleted list formatting within the requirement is also ignored. This setting is selected by default and intends to avoid capturing automatic section numbers as part of the imported requirements, however the bullet points are also removed.

Summary: Running Model Advisor on MATLAB Function block in a library then opening it causes assertion failure
Description: MATLAB throws an assertion error when you run Model Advisor check himl_0002 on a library that contains a MATLAB Function block, then try to open the MATLAB Function editor: falseComponent: Stateflow | Category: Assertion error  followed by an error of the form?  Error calling eml_man(open_editor): Java exception occurred:java.lang.RuntimeException: java.lang.NullPointerException
Workaround: Before trying to open the MATLAB Function editor, close the library model (e.g. with bdclose command), then reopen it.?
Fixed In: R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b, R2022a

Summary: Connection error during XCP external mode simulation due to DWARF parser internal error
Description: The connection phase of an XCP-based external mode simulation fails for a model that has?all?of these configuration parameter settings: Transport layer  (ExtModeTransport) : XCP on TCP/IP ?or XCP on Serial  Build configuration  (BuildConfiguration ): Faster Builds  or? Faster Runs  Tool/Options? (CustomToolchainOptions ): Non-empty  Tool/Options ?will be non-empty if Build configuration ?has previously been set to Specify . The simulation produces this error: External Mode Open Protocol Connect command failed  Caused by:  DWARF Parser internal error: Parser::describeSymbol xcpDummyDoubleVariable is not a global variable?
Workaround: Perform one of these actions:Set Build configuration?(BuildConfiguration) to Debug or?Specify.From the Command Window, set Tool/Options (CustomToolchainOptions)?to empty:set_param(model, 'CustomToolchainOptions', '')?

Summary: Requirement links to test assessments are not recognized by the metrics of the Model Testing Dashboard
Description: When you link a requirement to a test assessment in Simulink Test Manager, the metrics in the?Model?Testing?Dashboard show the requirement as Unlinked . If you collect metrics programmatically using a metric.Engine  object, instances of metric.Result  show the requirement as not linked to test cases in the project.
Workaround: Instead of linking requirements to individual test assessments, link     requirements to?higher-level test cases in Simulink Test     Manager.
Fixed In: R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 1, R2022a

Summary: Invalid evaluation of classifiers in sbiompgsa
Description: The evaluation of classifiers in sbiompgsa  can fail or yield wrong values.?? When a classifier in sbiompgsa  references two or more model components whose names are a mix of qualified and non-qualified names, then the function can error. The error says that the classifier is invalid, even though the classifier is valid. An example of a qualified name of a species Drug  in the compartment named?Central ?is Central.Drug . When a classifier in sbiompgsa  references both constant and non-constant model components, then the function can return the wrong sensitivities without any error indication.
Workaround: To avoid the incorrect error about invalid classifiers, reference model components either by names or by qualified names, not a mix of names and qualified names in classifiers.To avoid wrong sensitivities being reported by sbiompgsa, specify numeric values instead of names of constant model components in classifiers.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b

Summary: Wavelet Signal Denoiser freezes when selecting or duplicating a signal denoised using 'Universal Threshold' method
Description: Wavelet Signal Denoiser freezes when selecting or duplicating a signal denoised?using the 'Universal Threshold' method. The spinning progress indicator appears and never disappears. The following steps trigger the bug. Load a signal into your workspace: load wecg  Start Wavelet Signal Denoiser. Import the signal into the app. The signal is denoised using default parameters.  Change the denoising method to  Universal Threshold  and denoise.  Duplicate the denoised signal. The Spinning Progress Indicator appears . The app is now blocked.

Summary: Unable to load LibraryLinkageCache.mat when starting Simulink or running parallel simulations
Description: Running parallel simulations by using parsim  might corrupt the file LibraryLinkageCache.mat . If the file is corrupted, the next time you start Simulink or run parallel pool simulations, Simulink reports an error similar to:?Unable to read MAT-file <user_home>/ . matlab/ <release>/ SLLibraryLinkageData/ LibraryLinkageCache. mat. ?Not a binary MAT-file.  Try load -ASCII to read as text.

Summary: Subsystem reference update fails if block pre-copy callback errors during synchronization
Description: When you edit a subsystem reference, the update process that copies the contents to the subsystem file fails if a pre-copy callback of a block errors.? When this occurs, the subsystem file is corrupted.

Summary: Mask parameter specified as a Simulink.Parameter whose value is an expression is treated as a Simulink.Parameter object in mask initialization code
Description: When you set a mask parameter to a Simulink.Parameter  whose value is an expression, the mask initialization code treats the mask parameter as a Simulink.Parameter  with value set to the resolved expression instead of as a MATLAB variable.? For example, if the following lines were evaluated in base workspace,? param = Simulink.Parameter;param.Value = slexpr("A+B");?A = Simulink.Parameter(3.2);?B = Simulink.Parameter(5.8);?  and mask parameter maskPrm  on a masked block is set to a value 'param', then inside the mask initialization code, maskPrm  is treated as Simulink.Parameter  with a value of 9.0 (this is resolved value of 'param'). So, its value can only be adjusted like so: maskPrm.Value = 15.0;?  The expected behavior is that maskPrm  be treated as MATLAB variable, so that its value could be adjusted as follows:? maskPrm = 15.0;??

Summary: Save As might check out a Simulink Test license for models containing test harnesses
Description: Using Save As  to save a model that contains one or more test harnesses might check out a Simulink Test license.
Workaround: Make a copy of the model, open the model, and then save it. Do not use Save As because it might check out a license.

Summary: XCP readSingleValue function is unable to receive messages of more than 7 bytes
Description: The XCP function readSingleValue  does not support receiving messages of more than 7 bytes. When you received a message exceeding 7 bytes, MATLAB errors with the following message: Index exceeds the number of array elements (7) Error in xcp.Channel/readSingleValue (line 992) value = xcp.Utility.unpackMeasurement(m, response(firstBye:lastByte)

Summary: Dependency Analyzer throws an error when exporting a project archive
Description: In the Dependency Analyzer, the "Package As Archive" export option throws an error when both  of these conditions are true:? You opened the Dependency Analyzer from a MATLAB project. The project has files that depend on files stored outside the project root.
Fixed In: R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 1, R2022a

Summary: JMAAB check for db_0141?doesn't have sub-id selection
Description: The guideline for the Model Advisor?check?db_0141?:?Signal flow in Simulink models ?has sub-ids but the Model Advisor check doesn't have the input parameters for sub-id selection.

Summary: Antenna radiation patterns in the Satellite Scenario Viewer have incorrect rotation
Description: Radiation patterns displayed in the Satellite Scenario Viewer using the pattern ?function are incorrectly flipped 180 degrees about the Z axis (the Z axis points toward nadir by default). As a result, if the antenna's pattern is asymmetric about the Z axis, the boresight will appear to point in the wrong direction. This issue is purely visual and it does not affect other pattern calculations.

Summary: MATLAB Live Editor might show blank page when window size is maximized
Description: When you enlarge the window to maximum size, the Live Editor might show a blank display. This can occur if the Live Editor is docked or undocked.
Workaround: To view the Live Editor content, resize the MATLAB window (if the editor is docked) or the Live Editor window so that it is not maximized.

Summary: Error when logging AUTOSAR software component signals during SIL or PIL simulation
Description: You can?test an AUTOSAR software component ?by using a harness model that references the AUTOSAR component model. If you run a simulation with the Model block?Simulation mode ?set to either?Software-in-the-loop (SIL) ?or?Processor-in-the-loop (PIL) ?and you log non-virtual buses containing signals that are mapped to an AUTOSAR sender-receiver interface, the simulation produces this error: Unrecognized method,  property,  or field 'Identifier' for class 'coder. descriptor. AutosarSenderReceiver'.
