Summary: packNGo or findIncludeFiles? might fail while parsing source file
Description: Consider an RTW.BuildInfo  object that specifies generated source code which uses utility functions in the shared location. If you specify the object as an argument for the packNGo  or findIncludeFiles  f unction, you might see an error or warning. For example: ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? -----------------------------------------         RTW:buildInfo:ignoreFindIncludeParseError        -----------------------------------------        While parsing the source file '.../slprj/ert/_sharedutils/rt_sfcn_helper.cpp'        the following error occurred                .../simulink/include/simstruc_compcond.h:329: #error        directive: Must define one of RT, NRT, MATLAB_MEX_FILE, SL_INTERNAL, or        FIPXT_SHARED_MODULE        |  #error Must define one of RT, NRT, MATLAB_MEX_FILE, SL_INTERNAL, or        FIPXT_SHARED_MODULE        |   ^ This error can occur when two models use the shared location and one model defines the preprocessor symbol RT ? and the other model defines the preprocessor symbol NRT .

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Simulink Coder generates incorrect code for reusable subsystem with array of nonvirtual Bus Element inputs
Description: Simulink Coder generates incorrect code for a reusable subsystem under the following circumstances: The input port of a reusable subsystem is connected to the output of a Vector Element Selector block, where an element is selected that is not the first element in the source vector The input port of the Vector Element Selector block has a array of nonvirtual buses as its input type The input port block of the reusable subsystem is connected to a Bus Element Selector block The Bus Element Selector block is selecting a non-scale element from this bus  In the generated code, the element offset from the array bus selection is incorrectly subtracted from the Bus Element selection. This may lead to negative indexing or compliable code if the selected Bus Element is connected to a Vector Element Selection block that selects an element that is not the first element.?
Fixed In: R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 1, R2022b

Summary: Data Store Read/Write blocks incorrectly inherit constant sample time when showing index port
Description: Simulating your model in normal mode may produce incorrect results when all of the following conditions are true: The model contains a Data Store Read or Data Store Write block. The Data Store Read or Data Store Write shows an index port. The index port is driven by a Constant block.?? The Sample time ?of the Constant block is set to inf .

Summary: Export script erroring out in PCB Antenna Designer due to extra tags in the names of copied shapes
Description: In PCB Antenna Designer, when a shape is copied multiple times,? an extra tag "_Copy(1)" is added to the name of the copied shape. If "Rectangle1" is copied and pasted the name of the copied shape will be "Rectangle1_Copy(1)"?  Ex:  Rectangle1 --> Rectangle1_Copy(1)?    If "Rectangle1_Copy(1)" is copied and pasted the name of the copied shape will be "Rectangle1_Copy(1) _Copy(1)"  Ex:  Rectangle1_Copy(1) --> Rectangle1_Copy(1)_Copy(1).    When the design is exported as script, the names of the shape objects are used as variable names.? The exported script for the shape "Rectangle1" will be:?  Ex:  Rectangle1 = antenna.Rectangle( <arguments> );    ?Similarly the exported script for the shape "Rectangle_Copy(1)_Copy(1)" will be.?  ?Ex:  Rectangle_Copy(1)_Copy(1)?= antenna.Rectangle( <arguments> );    Rectangle_Copy(1)_Copy(1)  is not a valid variable name and a syntax error is thrown in the editor.?

Summary: Incorrect visualization for some of the pcbStack arrays.
Description: Incorrect visualization of the pcBStack array ground plane. The ground plane dimensions are smaller than expected. Use case: patchAnt= patchMicrostripEnotch; pcbObj= pcbStack(patchAnt); % Array of pcb Stack? pcbLinArr= array(pcbObj, 'linear', 'NumElements', 5, 'ElementSpacing', 0.1); figure; %Ground plane dimensions will be incorrect when visualized. show(pcbLinArr);?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Embedded Coder might generate incorrect code with Optimize global data access set to Minimize global data access
Description: Embedded Coder might generate incorrect code for a model under the following circumstances: The Optimize global data access  option is set to Minimize global data access  The model contains nested reusable subsystems (more than 1 layer deep)  The optimization might replace global variables with local variables that do not contain the same value as the replaced global variable.
Workaround: Set the Optimize global data access model parameters option to none.
Fixed In: R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 1, R2022b

Summary: Model with a shift by an explicit negative constant may experience incorrect Unsatisfiable or Valid objectives
Description: Models with a shift by a negative constant can experience one of the two scenarios:? Simulink Design Verifier  analysis completes normally and report incorrect Valid or Unsatisfiable  objectives. Simulink Design Verifier analysis reports an internal error without providing the full analysis results.  A few ways in which a model may experience a shift by a negative constant amount are:  Shift Arithmetic  blocks with the following configuration: The shift amount is a negative constant. The Check for out-of-range 'Bits to shift' in generated code  option is off   MATLAB functions that contain calls to shift functions with negative constant shift values. Stateflow charts with shifts by negative constants
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b

Summary: FPGA prediction could stall due to MATLAB version mismatch
Description: The FPGA prediction could stall when:?  1. You upgrade to a newer version of MATLAB and do not upgrade the Deep Learning HDL Toolbox hardware support packages. For example when you upgrade MATLAB to R2021a Update 4 or newer, and didn't upgrade the corresponding Deep Learning HDL Toolbox support packages to latest version (version?21.1.3 or newer), the FPGA bitstreams shipped with the older version of the support packages are not compatible with newer MATLAB version.? 2. You upgrade the Deep Learning HDL Toolbox hardware support packages to the latest version, and did not upgrade MATLAB version R2021a to the latest version. The FPGA bitstreams shipped in the newer version of the hardware support packages are not compatible with the older MATLAB version.?  3. You build and generate an FPGA bitstream using an older version of MATLAB, and use a newer version of MATLAB to deploy the generated bitstream?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect Function-Call Subsystem may be called when model includes multiple Thread Trigger blocks
Description: When a model includes multiple Thread Trigger blocks, a condition exists where the model sends the function call to the wrong thread. The condition is a sorting action during code generation that depends on the Thread Trigger block names. If the name given to the 'Function Call Thread Trigger' block (the string that appears just below the block) is the auto-assigned name as you insert these blocks in a model, the names are 'Thread Trigger' and 'Thread Trigger1'. Using these names, the blocks send their thread wakeup command to the wrong function call subsystem thread. The same problem occurs when using the Async Interrupt block from the Speedgoat I/O Blockset.
Workaround: Use any of these workarounds:?For two Thread Trigger blocks that only differ in name by the presence of a number on one of them, a possible workaround is to add a '0' (zero) on the unnumbered block.?This workaround does not work if the blocks are in separate subsystems.Change the whole name string for either or both async thread trigger or async interrupt blocks. This would also be a readability issue to name the block with the condition it is checking.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: When targeting cuDNN with INT8 precision, the code generated for deep learning network having grouped convolution layers might produce incorrect results
Description: Generating cuDNN INT8 code for a deep learning network with grouped convolution layer might produce incorrect result if?all  these conditions are true  Number of filter groups is equal to 2?? Total number of filters from all the groups is not a multiple of 4 Number of filter channels is not a multiple of 4

Summary: Software interface model fails compilation for AXI4 Master and AXI4-Stream Video interfaces
Description: When using the AXI4 Master or AXI4-Stream Video interfaces in the IP Core generation workflow and generating a software interface model, the generated software interface model might fail compilation with the following error message:? ----------------------------------Coder:builtins:ClassMismatchAssign----------------------------------This assignment writes a 'embedded.fi {ufix1}' value into a 'logical' type. Code generation doesnot support changing types through assignment. Check preceding assignments or input type specifications for type mismatches.?
Workaround: In the generated software interface model, you can modify the data type(s) of the bus elements by looking under the mask of the generated subsystem. Under the mask, there are bus creator subsystems that recreate the control signals for each interface. These bus creator subsystems contain a constant block for each bus element. Each constant block with datatype "ufix1" should be changed to datatype "boolean" for the model to compile successfully.

Summary: MAXP document's getParent method crashes MATLAB
Description: Invoking an XML document's getParent method crashes MATLAB. For example, this script crashes MATLAB:import matlab.io.xml.dom.*d = Document('book');p = getParent(d);

Summary: PostgreSQL native interface returns incorrect data for double values with negative exponents
Description: The fetch  and sqlread  functions of the PostgreSQL native interface return incorrect values when the functions import data with the double  data type on the database and negative exponents. For example, the functions might import a 1e-6 value from the PostgreSQL database as 1.
Workaround: Create a connection to a PostgreSQL database using the database function to connect using either the ODBC or JDBC driver.Alternatively, for a PostgreSQL native interface connection, use the fetch function and cast the data from the?double?data type to a numeric data type in the SQL query. For example: SELECT CAST (doubleData AS numeric) FROM MyTable

Summary: Empty values in outputStruct.scan(index).precursorMz.value and loss of non-mzXML 2.1 attributes
Description: For an input mzXML file containing level 2 mass spectrometry scans (msLevel  ˇÝ?2), the function returns an empty array []  as the value for a field named "value" in the?precursorMZ  field of the output structure (outputStruct ) even if the value is not empty in the input file. That is, outputStruct.scan(index).precursorMZ.value = [] .? In addition, the scan  element attributes?that are not specified in the mzXML 2.1 specification document (http://sashimi.sourceforge.net/schema_revision/mzXML_2.1/Doc/mzXML_2.1_tutorial.pdf) were not included in outputStruct .

Summary: MATLAB on macOS 11.4 and later crashes while running?blelist
Description: MATLAB installed on macOS 11.4 and later versions can crash when using the following steps to scan for nearby BLE peripheral devices: After installation, double-click the MATLAB icon to launch MATLAB. At the MATLAB command prompt, run blelist .  MATLAB displays an error message about inadequate Bluetooth permission and then crashes.
Workaround: To work around this issue, follow one of these methods:Launch MATLAB only from the Terminal app. While running blelist for the first time, a prompt appears to allow Bluetooth permission for the Terminal app; click Allow.Change the privacy preferences to allow MATLAB to use Bluetooth on your macOS. Go to System Preferences > Security & Privacy > Privacy, and then click the Bluetooth tab. Add MATLAB to the list of apps requesting use of Bluetooth.

Summary: Code generation for custom deep learning layers using row-major layout might fail if?Inline between MathWorks functions is set to 'Never'
Description: For deep neural networks that contain custom layers, code generation using the row-major array layout might fail if the code configuration object or the MATLAB Coder app has the?Inline between MathWorks functions ?parameter set to 'Never' . For example, consider a network with the following layer array:? layers = [    imageInputLayer([28 28 1])    preluLayer(20,'prelu')    regressionLayer]; Assume a custom preluLayer ?is defined and included in the network as shown in?Define Custom Deep Learning Layer for Code Generation .? Code generation for this network fails when using the following code configuration parameters: cfg = coder.config('mex');??cfg.DeepLearningConfig = coder.DeepLearningConfig('mkldnn');??cfg.InlineBetweenMathWorksFunctions = 'Never';cfg.RowMajor = true; ?
Workaround: To generate code that uses row-major array layout for deep learning networks containing custom layers, use the default inlining options ('speed'). For more information, see?Control Inlining to Fine-Tune Performance and Readability of Generated Code.

Summary: Changing the rounding mode might produce incorrect results for some mathematical expressions
Description: The expression -(a*b)  might disregard a change in the rounding mode when it is evaluated in a loop that runs thousands of times. For example, when you run this function, MATLAB displays failure  in the Command Window. function testround  % a and b are floating-point numbers with several significand digits  a = 1 - 2^(-53);  b = 0.8712198487674925;  for i = 1:1e4  ? ? feature("setround",inf)? ? ?% Change MATLAB rounding mode  ? ? c1 = -(a*b);??  ? ? feature("setround",-inf)  ? ? c2 = -(a*b);  ? ? if c1 == c2? ? ?% c1 and c2 must not be equal  ? ? ? ? disp("failure")  ? ? ? ? return  ? ? end  end  disp("success")  end
Workaround: To take the change of rounding mode into account, evaluate the expression?-(a*b) in two steps.?function testrounda = 1 - 2^(-53);b = 0.8712198487674925;for i = 1:1e4? ? feature("setround",inf)? ? tmp1 = a*b;? ? c1 = -tmp1;? ? feature("setround",-inf)? ? tmp2 = a*b;? ? c2 = -tmp2;? ? if c1 == c2? ? ? ? disp("failure")? ? ? ? return? ? endenddisp("success")end

Summary: Rebuilding test harness slow when system being tested outputs bus objects and harness source is Chart or Test Sequence
Description: When a system under test has bus objects at its output interface, rebuilding its test harness whose source is a Chart or Test Sequence might be slower than test harness creation. If the number of bus objects at the output increases, the time taken to rebuild the harness also increases.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 1, R2022a

Summary: MATLAB crashes during simulations with 'SaveFinalState/SaveState'? is off, 'SaveFormat'?is 'Dataset' , and 'BlockReduction'? is on
Description: MATLAB may crash when the following conditions are met:? 1) The model contains a Bus Selector block which selects only a subset of input signals. 2) 'SaveFormat ' is 'Dataset ' format.? 3) Both 'SaveState ' and 'SaveFinalState ' configurations are turned off.? 4) 'BlockReduction ' is turned on. ?

Summary: Dependency Analyzer does not list Polyspace as a required product
Description: When using Polyspace functions in your code, the Dependency Analyzer does not list Polyspace as a required product?in the?Products  section of the?Properties ?pane. The Dependency Analyzer also reports Outside project root ?problems in the?Problems? section of the?Properties? pane.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 5, R2021b Update 1, R2022a

Summary: Rebuilding real-time application and then starting previously loaded version of application may halt MATLAB
Description: MATLAB may halt when this series of steps occur: Build a model, and load the generated real-time application. Re-build the model and run the previously generated version of the real-time application.  These steps may cause this error message: UUID is already set. It can not be overwritten .
Workaround: Press Ctrl+C,?then use the reset(tg) command.Load and run the newly generated version of the real-time application.

Summary: Antenna radiation patterns in Site Viewer have incorrect rotation
Description: When you change the elevation angle of a?txsite ?or rxsite  object by using the?AntennaAngle ?property, the antenna radiation pattern rolls instead of elevates. This bug affects Site Viewer radiation patterns in Antenna Toolbox(TM) and Communications Toolbox(TM).?

Summary: Unused local variables in generated code with Variant blocks and Simulink functions
Description: Variant blocks and Simulink functions may result in generation of additional local variables in the code as the below example. The variable?rtb_in2  is assigned the value 0, when Choice_1~=0  , however is never used.?void test_f(void)  {  uint16_T rtb_in2;  uint16_T rtb_in1;  #if Choice_1 == 0  rtb_in2 = test_U.in2;  #else  rtb_in1 = test_U.in1;  rtb_in2 = 0U;   #endif  #if Choice_1 == 0  test_Y.Out1 = (uint16_T)(((uint32_T)rtb_in2 << 15) >> 14);  rtb_in1 = 0U;  #else  test_Y.Out1 = rtb_in1;  #endif  }  This behavior may occur when the inports and outports of the Simulink functions become conditional due to variant sink/source block within.

Summary: Incorrect Unsatisfiable objectives for Saturation blocks with a vector of Inf bounds
Description: Simulink Design Verifier might report incorrect?Unsatisfiable (or Dead Logic) objectives ?for a Saturation  block if one of the following conditions is met:The Upper limit   block parameter has a vector of at least 2?Inf   values specified The?Lower limit  ?block parameter has a vector of at least 2 -Inf  ?values specified
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 3, R2021b Update 2, R2022a

Summary: Simulation 3D Camera, Simulation 3D Camera Get, and Simulation 3D Scene Configuration block error " (mask) does not have a parameter named 'ActorName'"?
Description: Reverting a deletion of the following blocks results in error " (mask) does not have a parameter named 'ActorName'". Simulation 3D Camera Simulation 3D Camera Get Simulation 3D Scene Configuration

Summary: Running a timer whose ExecutionMode property has value 'singleShot' does not update the TasksExecuted property
Description: Running a timer whose ExecutionMode  property is set to 'singleShot'  (which is the default value for that property) fails to update the timer's TasksExecuted  property. After running the example code below, the expected behavior is that tasksExecuted will be 1. Instead it is 0. t = timer('TimerFcn', @(src, evt) disp('1'), 'StartDelay', 0.001, 'ExecutionMode', 'singleShot');   start(t);  pause(1);  stop(t);  tasksExecuted = get(t, 'TasksExecuted')

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Tunable parameters in FMU Import block are discarded when you generate standalone FMU
Description: Tunable parameters in FMU Import block are discarded when you generate nested standalone FMU, but definitions are preserved in model description file. Tuned parameters in top-level FMU do not result in the updated values propagating to child FMU.

Summary: Clearing an XPath evaluator can crash MATLAB
Description: Using an XPath evaluator to evaluate a path on an XML file and then clearing the evaluator from the MATLAB workspace crashes MATLAB.? For example, this script crashes MATLAB:import matlab.io.xml.xpath.*xmlFile = fullfile(matlabroot,'toolbox/matlab/general/info.xml');ev = Evaluator;result = evaluate(ev,'//name',xmlFile);clear ev;n = getTagName(result);

Summary: Data Store Memory in referenced model with no tunable parameters might halt model build
Description: When a Simulink Real-Time model references a model using a model block, the model build might halt before compilation when all of the following apply: The referenced model uses a global or shared Data Store Memory The referenced model does?not?contain any blocks that would introduce a tunable Model parameter The default parameter behavior is?Tunable  The storage class for the model element category Model parameters ?in the Data Defaults ?tab of the Code Perspective is set to "PageSwitching"  For these conditions, the top-most model build halts after code is generated, before compilation with the error: The call to slrealtime_make_rtw_hook, during the after_tlc hook generated the following error:? ? Could not find pointer variable for type mg2546766_child_cal_type in header file
Workaround: For each model in the model reference hierarchy:?Open the Code Perspective.Select the Data Defaults tab of the "Code Mappings - C" view.Change the storage class for the model element category Model parameters from PageSwitching to Default.The model will then build, but the XCP Page Switching functionality will no longer be available.

Summary: Code generation fails for model with a root inport that outputs a nonvirtual bus
Description: Code generation can fail with an error for a model with a root inport that outputs a nonvirtual bus.? The error takes the following form: Simulink Coder Fatal: invalid SLTypeIdx -1 for struct

Summary: Clean Outlier Data can produce histogram plot with incorrect axes labels
Description: In the Clean Outlier Data Live Editor task, when a table is selected as the input and a histogram is selected as the plot type, the table variable name appears on the plot as the y-axis label when it should appear as the x-axis label.
Workaround: In the live script, add code directly following the Live Editor task (in the same section) to remove the y-axis label and create the correct x-axis label:ylabel("")xlabel("Var1")

Summary: When generating GRT code, a data store with an alias type might produce an incorrect error
Description: Generating code for a model hierarchy might produce an incorrect error about inconsistent data types when all of the following conditions are true:? Code generation uses a GRT-based system target file. Multiple models in the model hierarchy use the same global data store. The global data store uses an alias type.

Summary: Incorrect merged results when using three-way merge for Stateflow charts
Description: Three-way merge of Simulink models containing Stateflow charts creates incorrect merged charts for various chart objects. Incorrect merged result occurs when you perform these actions: Add, delete, or change properties of Stateflow data, event, or message objects.? Add or delete states, transitions, junctions under a box or a graphical function. Change junction positions. Add super-transitions crossing sub-chart boundaries.? Add or delete default and dangling transitions.?
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b, R2022a

Summary: Real-time applications that contain FMU blocks may cause a segmentation fault on target computers and report an issue with SLRT_setReal method
Description: When run on a target computer, a real-time application that contains an FMU block can cause a segmentation fault and report an issue with the SLRT_setReal  method. The development computer calls an initialize function when initializing the real-time application on the target computer. If an initialization issue occurs with loading functions, libraries, or instantiating the FMU, the initialize method returns a NULL pointer. This return ends the real-time application and causes a segmentation fault. Examining the segmentation fault log by using the slrealtime.getCrashStack  method, you find a reported issue with the SLRT_setReal  method. This report does not lead you to the initialization problem. For more information about the SLRT_setReal  method, see <matlabroot>\toolbox\slrealtime\target\kernel\dist\include\SLRT_FMU_target.h .

Summary: Vehicle Body 3DOF Longitudinal block has incorrect planar response for non-zero relative front and rear pitch angles
Description: The Vehicle Body 3DOF Longitudinal block incorrectlyapplies the suspension forces, resulting in incorrect planar response for non-zero relative pitch angles. Specifically, theblock uses the wrong sign for the internal longitudinal component of the front and rear axle forces. If you select?External forces , the issue does not apply.?
Workaround: Reroute these Info bus output port longitudinal suspension force signals:?BdyFrm.Forces.FrntAxl.FxBdyFrm.Forces.RearAxl.Fx?1. Subtract them.2. Multiply them by 2.?3. Input them to Fext input port.?See the attached example model (R2019a) and image.?
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 5, R2021b, R2022a

Summary: Running sldvcompat on an externally saved test harness might error
Description: If you run sldvcompat  on an externally saved test harness, an error might occur.
Workaround: Create the test harness as internally saved, or convert externally saved test harnesses to internal before running sldvcompat. To convert externally saved test harnesses:Open the Apps tab at the top of the main model.In the Model Verification, Validation, and Test section, click Simulink Test.On the Tests tab, click?Manage Test Harnesses?>?Convert?to Internal Harnesses.A dialog box provides information on the conversion procedure and the affected test harnesses. Click?Yes?to convert the test harnesses.

Summary: Reaction-scoped parameters are not displayed in the SimBiology Model Builder app after adding a kinetic law to the SimBiology model
Description: The Parameters  table in the Browser  pane of the SimBiology Model Builder  app does not display all parameters on the model in the following cases: A reaction has reaction-scoped parameters, and you change the kinetic law to "None". When you undo the change, the original reaction-scoped parameters are added back to the model. The parameters do not appear in the Parameters  table in the Browser  pane. You use?copyobj  ?at the MATLAB command line to copy a kinetic law from a reaction with reaction-scoped parameters to another reaction while the model is open in SimBiology Model Builder . The copied reaction-scoped parameters are added to the other reaction, but they do not appear in the Parameters  table.
Workaround: Save the project, then close and reopen the project in?the SimBiology Model Builder app.

Summary: Sporadic MATLAB crashes during compilation of models with nonlinear terms and derivatives in constraint equations
Description: MATLAB might crash when you run or update a Simscape model where a constraint equation  contains a nonlinear term, which in turn contains a derivative of any variable and at least one more variable. Consider the following network equations: ??????? der(x) * u == u * u;? ? ? ? ? ? ? (1)??????? r == der(x);? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? (2)??????? y == der(r) + cos(u) * der(u);??? (3)??????? u == sin(time);? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ?(4)  Equations (1), (2), and (4) are the constraint equations. When we differentiate equation (1) we get the following expression ? ? ?? der(der(x)) * u + der(x) * der(u) == 2.0 * u * der(u)   When applying derivative replacement method on this equation, der(x) * der(u)  term can cause the compilation to crash.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 5, R2021b

Summary: When debugging a live script in MATLAB Online, the Live Editor sometimes does not display embedded output
Description: In MATLAB Online, after stopping at a breakpoint in a live script, clicking the Run ?button to continue running causes the Live Editor to display output in the Command Window instead of in the live script.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Deep Learning processor stalls when in Continuous Streaming Mode
Description: The deep learning processor might stall when running in continuous streaming mode. In this mode the deep learning processor runs continuously consuming input data and providing output data. In certain situations the OutputValid handshaking signal does not go high and you will not receive any output data leading the deep learning processor to stall.

Summary: Setting Value or ItemsData with content of class timeseries or datetime results in an error
Description: Using complex datatypes like timeseries  might error when being used in the?Value  or ItemsData  property of a?Listbox , Switch , DiscreteKnob , or DropDown  component. For example, running this code results in an error. >> itemsData = [timeseries(2, 1); timeseries(0, 1)];>> c = uidropdown('ItemsData', itemsData); The first line of the error is:? Warning: JSON incompatible value has been used.

Summary: Simulink co-simulation with Unreal Engine??fails with 3D?simulation engine interface setup error
Description: If your user name contains non-ASCII characters, Simulink co-simulation with Unreal Engine fails with this error:3D simulation engine interface setup error: could not establish communication. Please restart the simulation.

Summary: Simulink Test Code Importer does not generate stub for undefined functions called with function pointer
Description: When using the Code Importer, if your code uses a function pointer and the function registered to it is outside the file, a stub is not automatically created. ?For example, for this C code, a stub is not generated in the sandbox auto_stub.c  file: Source.c  #include "Header.h"  FP fp = externalFunc; // pointer to undefined functionvoid function1(void){??fp(); //?externalFunc?function call}?  Header.h  typedef void (*FP)(void);extern FP fp;extern void externalFunc(void); ?

Summary: set_param during external mode?simulation results in error for non-tunable parameters even when value is not changed
Description: During external mode simulation, performing a set_param  results in a runtime error when the configuration parameter Default parameter behavior   is set to Inlined . The function reports an error similar to the following even when the call does not change the value of the parameter: Cannot change parameter '<parameter-name> ' of '<model-name>/<block-path> ' while simulation is running.  The error has also been observed in some library blocks that use an Assertion block inside them. In this case, during a runtime Update Diagram, the error reported would be similar to:? Error in '<model-name>/<block-path> ': Failed to evaluate mask initialization commands. Caused by: Cannot change parameter '<parameter-name> ' of '<model-name>/<block-path> ' while simulation is running.

Summary: Adding EtherCAT Async SSC/SoE Upload block to model generates errors
Description: Adding an EtherCAT Async SSC/SoE Upload block to a model generates errors or nonsense values. The error that the block returns vary depending on the configuration of block parameters.?In some cases, the block returns a nonsense value instead of an error.

Summary: Creating a test harness with a large number of input or output ports might result in unconnected ports
Description: Creating a test harness for a model with Initialize-Reset-Terminate (IRT) ports and a large number of inputs or outputs might create unconnected floating ports on the Component-Under-Test in the harness.

Summary: Function Caller block does not call scoped Simulink functions in SIL/PIL simulations
Description: Consider a parent model that contains two referenced models.  Model_A  is a referenced model that follows export-function rules and contains a Simulink Function block with Function visibility  set to scoped .??Model_B ?is a referenced model with a Function Caller block that is configured to call the scoped function defined in Model_A . If the Simulation mode ?parameter for the Model block that references Model_B ?is set to Software-in-the-loop (SIL) ?or Processor-in-the-loop (PIL) ,? when the parent model is run, the Function Caller block in Model_B ?does not call the scoped function defined in Model_A . Output signals from the Function Caller block? in?Model_B ?have a value of zero.
Workaround: If you want the?Model_B?Function Caller block to call the?Simulink Function, set?Function visibility?for the?Model_A?Simulink Function block to?global and change the Function Caller block to call the function using its unqualified function name.

Summary: Unexpected error when simulating a Multistage Nonlinear MPC Controller block with a constant terminal state and generated MEX function
Description: When user specifies a constant terminal state  in @nlmpcMultistage object and uses it in the Multistage Nonlinear MPC Controller block with Use MEX to speed up simulation  selected in the block dialog,? simulation aborts with an unexpected error:   Incorrect number of fields in expression 'onlinedata': expected 2 but found 3.Error in msnmpcblock_interface.m (line 55)        throw(ME)Error in '.../Multistage Nonlinear MPC/MPC/NLMPC' (line 28)
Workaround: Use one of the following workarounds:(1) Unselect Use MEX to speed up simulation which leads to slower simulation.or(2) Select Terminal state (x.terminal) in the 'Online Features' tab in the block dialog to enable an optional "x.terminal" input and then provide the desired terminal state value in a constant block attached to this input, which retains good simulation speed.

Summary: Assertion error when copy/pasting certain components from a Stateflow chart
Description: Copying (both copy-paste and drag-drop) some part of a Stateflow chart to an empty Stateflow chart within a separate Simulink model might cause the following error message: object[id] != nullptr?  Save your work and restart MATLAB now.? Please contact Mathworks Technical Support if you can reproduce this error.?  'object[id] != nullptr' found at 'fsm/passthrough/passthrough_hierarchy.cpp:1092'

Summary: Default optimization setting results in high execution time for Sliding Mode Observer block
Description: Creating a new model containing the Sliding Mode Observer block in Motor Control Blockset results in a high execution time when deployed on hardware targets. Because the default value of optimization setting named Default parameter behavior  is set to Tunable  for new models to be deployed on targets, the block generates code for all expressions.
Workaround: In the Optimization pane of Configuration Parameters dialog box, set the Default parameter behavior value to Inlined.Alternatively, run this command to change the setting:set_param(<model_name>,'DefaultParameterBehavior','Inlined')

Summary: Sliding Mode Observer block displays an error if the block parameters use fixed-point or single datatype
Description: The Sliding Mode Observer block displays an error if one or more block parameter values are type casted to fixed-point or single datatype.

Summary: Condenser Evaporator (2P-MA) and Heat Exchanger (TL-MA) blocks might exhibit lower than expected moist air outlet temperatures
Description: When the Condenser Evaporator (2P-MA) block or the Heat Exchanger (TL-MA) block simulate water vapor condensation in the moist air over a large temperature drop, the moist air outlet temperature might be lower than expected.

Summary: System Composer models might cause MATLAB to crash on model block refresh
Description: System Composer models might cause MATLAB to crash after refreshing model blocks. This can happen when referenced Simulink behaviors and linked architecture models on components are removed from the MATLAB path and then reintroduced multiple times.

Summary: Code generation using TensorRT library on a Windows host PC might fail to build on embedded NVIDIA GPUs
Description: On the Windows platform, code generated using TensorRT library might fail to deploy and build on embedded NVIDIA boards. The compilation failure might have the message: nvcc fatal?? : Don't know what to do with 'ToolkitCUDATensorRT-6.0.1.5lib' ?   This issue arises if the value for the?NVIDIA_TENSORRT  environment variable in Windows contains spaces.?
Workaround: Move the TensorRT installation libraries to a folder without spaces and update the NVIDIA_TENSORRT environment variable.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Bounding boxes returned by the code generator could be a few pixels off from MATLAB for yolov2ObjectDetector
Description: Code generation for yolov2ObjectDetector   having AlexNet-like backbone networks may return bounding boxes that have a few pixels off as compared to MATLAB simulation. This issue also affects code generation using the GPU Coder product.

Summary: JMAAB check for jc_0009 does not flag input signals across subsystem hierarchy
Description: Model Advisor?check?Check for propagated signal labels(id :?mathworks.jmaab.jc_0009)  does  not flag input signal across subsystem hierarchy.

Summary: AUTOSAR-C++14 M5-0-7 false positive when the output of a member function call is cast to float explicitly
Description: Polyspace incorrectly views member function calls as cvalue  expressions. This can result in a false positive violation of AUTOSAR-C++14 M5-0-7.
Products: Polyspace Bug Finder, Polyspace Bug Finder Server

Summary: customAntennaGeometry and customArrayGeometry support feed edge orientation only along Y-axis.
Description: Custom geometry models support feed edge orientation only along the Y-axis. Mesh generation is updated to support? feed edge along X-axis or an arbitrary direction. ant = customAntennaGeometry;? ant.Operation = 'P1+P2'; % FeedWidth ant.FeedWidth = 0.5e-3; % Define a rectangle rect1 = antenna.Rectangle('Length', 37e-3, 'Width', 37e-3); p1 = getShapeVertices(rect1); % Feed shape feed1 = antenna.Rectangle('Length', 0.5e-3, 'Width', 0.5e-3,? ? ? ? ? ? ... ? ? 'Center', [-17.25e-3 0]); p2 = getShapeVertices(feed1); ant.Boundary = {p1,p2}; ant.FeedLocation = [-17.5e-3,0,0]; figure;? % Feeding edge after meshing will be along Y-axis mesh(ant,'MaxEdgeLength',0.004); % Feeding edge is still along X-axis even after the direction is? modified. p2=[p2(:,2),p2(:,1),p2(:,3)]; ant.Boundary = {p1,p2}; ant.FeedLocation = [0,-17.5e-3,0]; figure;? mesh(ant,'MaxEdgeLength',0.004);

Summary: Simulation 3D Lidar block returns point cloud data from areas that do not have objects
Description: The Simulation 3D Lidar block returns point cloud data for samples in areas that do not have objects.?The block incorrectly processes points that are on different objects, returning point cloud data from the space between the objects.

Summary: 4-Way 2-Position Directional Valve (IL) block does not correctly represent a constant A-T orifice when you set both spool position parameters to?P-B and A-T
Description: 4-Way 2-Position Directional Valve (IL) block does not correctly represent a constant A-T orifice when you set?Positive spool position open connections  and Negative spool position open connections ?both to?P-B and A-T .
Workaround: Replace the 4-Way 2-Position Directional Valve (IL) block with two Orifice (IL) blocks.Add two Orifice (IL) blocks to the model canvas, and disconnect the 4-Way 2-Position Directional Valve (IL) block. For both Orifice (IL) blocks, set?Orifice type?to?Constant.If you set the 4-Way 2-Position Directional Valve (IL) block Orifice parameterization?parameter to Tabulated data - Volumetric flow rate vs. spool travel and pressure drop, set the?Orifice parameterization?parameter to Tabulated data - Volumetric flow rate vs. pressure drop?for both Orifice (IL) blocks. Otherwise, leave it set to Orifice area.Connect the ports of one Orifice (IL) block to the network nodes that were originally connected to ports A and T of the 4-Way 2-Position Directional Valve (IL) block. Set the parameters to match the 4-Way 2-Position Directional Valve (IL) block.Connect the ports of the remaining Orifice (IL) block to the network nodes that were originally connected to ports P and B of the 4-Way 2-Position Directional Valve (IL) block. Set the parameters to match the 4-Way 2-Position Directional Valve (IL) block.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Model with muxed root-level function-call signals connected to a Function-Call Subsystem might generate incorrect code
Description: Embedded Coder might generate incorrect code for model with the following condition: The model is using more that one root-level Inport that outputs a function-call signal. The function-call signals are muxed and connected to the trigger port of a reusable Function-Call Subsystem. The reusable Function-Call Subsystem might have a Bus Selector block of matrix elements connected to its Outport block. The subsystem output is connected to a root-level Outport block.  Embedded Coder might not generate the selection operation from the output function of the reusable Function-Call Subsystem.?

Summary: Using createInputDataset on model with input port of type string might fail
Description: The createInputDataset   function might return an error if the model has an input port with data type assigned as string.

Summary: DWARF parser fails to extract debugging information from ELF files compiled using Microchip's XC16 compiler
Description: Microchip's XC16 16-bit compiler generates binary ELF files that have a special structure in the .debug  sections. When a standard DWARF parser tries to extract debugging information from an ELF file, the structure causes the parser to fail. The ELF file appears as if it has been compiled without debugging information, which blocks XCP external mode workflows on Microchip 16-bit dsPIC target hardware. The workflows require the debugging information to infer memory addresses of global variables.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 1, R2022a

Summary: Polyspace in-product documentation not displayed on Linux
Description: If you use the Polyspace desktop interface on Linux, the in-product documentation might not open when you try one of these: Going to Help  -> Documentation.  Clicking a link on the Start Page. Clicking a link to open the context help for an option or a result.
Workaround: To work around the issue:Open the file polyspaceroot/polyspace/bin/polyspace?in a text editorComment out lines 35 and 36 in the file by adding a '#' character at the start of each line, like so:# LD_LIBRARY_PATH="$MATLAB_ROOT/sys/os/$ARCH:$MATLAB_ROOT/bin/$ARCH:$LD_LIBRARY_PATH"# export LD_LIBRARY_PATHHere, polyspaceroot is your Polyspace installation folder, for instance /usr/local/Polyspace/R2021b.

Summary: Double-Acting Servo Valve Actuator (IL) block incorrectly offsets spool position by half of the spool stroke
Description: The physical signal port S  reports a spool position of half the value of the?Spool stroke ?parameter when the spool is in the middle of the stroke. The value should be 0  at this point, to indicate the neutral position.
Workaround: Add a PS Subtract block and a PS Constant block to your model.Attach the?S?port of the Double-Acting Servo Valve Actuator to the +?port of the PS Subtract block.Attach the PS Constant block to the -?port of the PS Subtract block.Set the Constant?parameter for the PS Constant block to half of the value of the Spool stroke?parameter.The out port of the PS Subtract block is the correct spool position signal.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 5, R2021b

Summary: SIGSEGV (Invalid memory access) when running code generated targets from models with scalable compilation
Description: When a Simscape model has the?Reuse components during compilation ?configuration option selected, running code generated targets from such model might result in a SIGSEGV (Invalid memory access).

Summary: MATLAB may crash during code generation for a design file that creates copies of a network with states and calls predictAndUpdateState or classifyAndUpdateState
Description: For stateful networks such as networks with LSTM, BiLSTM, or GRU layers, the generated code may crash when the design file meets all of the following conditions: A stateful DAG or series network is copied. The copied network is updated with a multi-output call to?predictAndUpdateState ? or?classifyAndUpdateState . Inference is run on the copied and updated network.?  Example: function [out1, out2] = net_predict(in)   %#codegen?   persistent net;   if isempty(net)? ?   ? ? net = coder.loadDeepLearningNetwork('net.mat');   end   netCopy = net;   [netCopy, out1] = predictAndUpdateState(netCopy,in);   out2 = predict(netCopy, in);?   end     Note: According to condition 2, the crash will only occur if two outputs are queried from?predictAndUpdateState ?or?classifyAndUpdateState ?as in:  [netCopy, out1] = predictAndUpdateState(netCopy,in);  Replacing this line with? netCopy = predictAndUpdateState(netCopy,in) ? does not result in a crash.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results might occur for?a model containing an Enabled or a Triggered Subsystem block that contains a?Unit Delay block
Description: The generated code might contain incorrect initialization of a Unit Delay block state for a model that meets all of these conditions: The model contains an Enabled or a Triggered Subsystem block. The subsystem contains a Unit Delay block and a Stateflow chart. The output of the Stateflow chart feeds into the Unit Delay block. The Unit Delay block has a nonzero initialization value. The Enable or the Trigger port block has the States when enabling  parameter set to reset.

Summary: Code inspection might fail for a model containing a MATLAB Function block that includes a noninlined user-defined function
Description: Simulink Code Inspector fails to verify the code generated from a model containing a MATLAB Function block that includes a noninlined user-defined function when either of these is true: The noninlined function performs an assignment operation to copy data into a variable index array.?  The noninlined function contains a nested if  statement ?that has no else ?condition.  For example:? function y = foo(u, t)  ?coder.inline("never");  ?y[t] = u;  end  The function performs an assignment operation to copy the value of?u  into a variable index array y[t] . Code inspection fails to verify the code generated from the model.? Or for example:? function y = foo(u, t)  ?coder.inline("never");  ?if (c1)? ?  ? ?if (c2)? ? ?  ? ? ? y = t;  ? ?end?  ?end?  end?  The function contains a nested if  statement that has no else condition. Code inspection fails to verify the code generated from the model.?

Summary: MATLAB might crash for models with Code Mappings
Description: MATLAB might crash while opening,?building?or?closing?a model that has Code Mappings configured for signals, Data Store Memory blocks, or blocks with states. The generated stack trace contains lines similar to:[ 14] 0x00007ffcfdb71eab C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2021b\bin\win64\codemapping_c.dll+01187499 coderdictionary::ModelMappingBase::~ModelMappingBase+00000251  [ 15] 0x00007ffcfdaea0d3 C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2021b\bin\win64\codemapping_c.dll+00630995 coderdictionary::CoderDictionaryMapping::~CoderDictionaryMapping+00014483  [ 16] 0x00007ffd2de2f2ad C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2021b\bin\win64\codermapping_core.dll+00389805 sl::MappingManager::removeMappingsOfType+00000285  [ 17] 0x00007ffd2de29cac C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2021b\bin\win64\codermapping_core.dll+00367788 sl::MappingManager::destroyMappings+00000060  [ 18] 0x00007ffd2de262f8 C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2021b\bin\win64\codermapping_core.dll+00353016 sl::MappingManager::~MappingManager+00000024

Summary: Visualization and dashboard blocks may not display properly for some signals when real-time application is started from Simulink Editor
Description: Simulink visualization and dashboard blocks in a model may not display properly for some signals when the real-time application is started from the Simulink Editor.? Logging fixed-point signals or signals with dimensions greater than 2 may cause visualization and dashboard blocks to not display properly when connected to signals of the same sample time as the logged signals.

Summary: Assertion error when generating PLC code?'In setNameOrAssert: Assertion failed: found == this'
Description: When generating PLC code for a Simulink subsystem under the following conditions: The PLC Target IDE is configured for Generic.  The Simulink model contains Stateflow Chart(s) with State names that only differ by an underscore. For example,? my_state  and  my__state.   An unexpected assertion error might happen with error text like the following:?? Unexpected or internal error encountered in "in CGIR: B:\matlab\src\cg_ir\const\Const.cpp line 145: In setNameOrAssert: Assertion failed: found == this". Please report this to MathWorks if you can cause it to recur
Fixed In: R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 7, R2021b, R2022a

Summary: Asynchronous requests with large payloads fail when MATLAB Production Server deployment is behind a load balancer
Description: Asynchronous requests that clients send to MATLAB Production Server fail if all of the following conditions are true: The MATLAB Production Server deployment is behind any load balancer or web proxy server, such as Azure Application Gateway or AWS Application Load Balancer. The request payload is large, for example, larger than 1 MB.  The following workflow results in the error: Client sends an asynchronous request with large payload data, for example, over 1 MB:? POST <mps_endpoint>/<archive>/<function>?mode=async      Client retrieves the result of the asynchronous request: GET <mps_endpoint>/{request-uri-string}/result    The GET  request results in the following error: {    "error": {        "type": "httperror",        "code": 400,        "messageId": "InvalidJson",        "message": "Invalid JSON"    }}

Summary: Simulink.VariantVariable choice conditions involving equalities or inequalities and same variant control variable operands can be wrongly set to "false".
Description: Specifying a Simulink.VariantVariable  choice condition can lead to an incorrectly simplified condition being set under the following scenario. The condition is of type "V==V ", "V~=V ", "V<=V " or "V>=V ", where V is a Simulink.VariantControl  object.?  In this case, the condition is incorrectly simplified and set to "false".

Summary: Hex numbers are represented as integer literals in generated code
Description: Integer literals represented as hexadecimal in a Stateflow C Action Language chart might be represented as an equivalent decimal in generated code. This issue happens when a data type conversion is required in the statement containing the hexadecimal?literal.? For example, in a Stateflow chart containing a uint8_T  variable OutVal, ?the statement:OutVal = 0x01;  is generated as:OutVal = 1U;  instead of the expected:OutVal = (uint8_T)0x01;
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 7, R2021b

Summary: Code replacement for MATLAB Fast Fourier transform functions might cause build error or produce incorrect code replacement report
Description: This issue occurs if both ?these conditions are true:? Your MATLAB code or Simulink model contains MATLAB Fast Fourier transform functions?(fft , ifft , fft2 , ifft2 , fftn , ifftn ). When generating code, you either select multiple code replacement libraries (CRLs) or set the CodeReplacementLibrary  configuration parameter to 'ANSI_C' .   In such situations, the generated code replacement report might contain incorrect results. You might also get errors when building the generated code.

Summary: Code generation fails for AUTOSAR adaptive version R19-11 when used with third-party ara libraries
Description: When you generate AUTOSAR adaptive R19-11 version code theprevious version function signatures are generated in the main file to report theapplication execution state. These function signatures are compatible with MathWorks provided ara libraries, but if you use third-party ara libraries, execution creation fails with the following error: 'ExecutionReturnType' has not been declared

Summary: Indexed deletion with an empty list of indices might cause MATLAB to crash.?
Description: During an indexed deletion operation of an array, if the subscripts inside the parentheses are empty, then MATLAB crashes.? For example:? A = 1:10; idx = {}; A(idx{:}) = [];? % Causes MATLAB to crash Here, idx is an empty cell, and idx{:} generates an empty comma-separated list (CSL).?

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier might stall during the preprocessing stage for models with fixed-point 2-D Lookup Tables
Description: Simulink Design Verifier might experience stalls during the setup and compatibility stage for models that meet the following conditions:There is at least one 2-D Lookup Table with fixed-point breakpoints. The breakpoint ranges are close to the minimum and maximum values for that fixed-point type.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b

Summary: Adaptive Pipelining does not insert pipelines for Microsemi Libero SoC or Intel Quartus Pro devices
Description: When using Adaptive Pipelining with the Synthesis tool  set to Microsemi Libero SoC  or Intel Quartus Pro , no pipelines will be inserted from the optimization. When using Microsemi Libero Soc, a warning from Adaptive Pipelining is displayed saying that a default Xilinx device characterization will be used since none of the Libero families are currently characterized. Warning: Family igloo2 not characterized. Using default value, SynthesisToolFamily=virtex7 and SynthesisToolSpeedValue=-1, for Adaptive Pipelining.  However, no pipelines get inserted during code generation.?

Summary: The Enable property?has no visual impact on components created using uiswitch,?uigauge, and uiknob
Description: Components created using the following functions have no visual change when setting the Enable  property to 'off': ? uiswitch  uigauge  uiknob

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Mismatch in simulation results between normal and software-in-the-loop (SIL) modes in model that reads from and writes to data store
Description: The simulation results between normal and software-in-the-loop (SIL) modes might not match when all the following conditions are true: The model contains one or more Data Store Read and Data Store Write blocks. One of the Data Store Read or Data Store Write blocks is connected to an Outport block through a variant block. The connection of the variant block to the Outport block is inactive. The variant block has its Variant activation time  set to code-compile .  Under these circumstances, a mismatch between normal and SIL modes might result from inconsistency in the relative execution order of the Data Store Read and Data Store Write blocks.
Workaround: Set the relative execution orders of Data Store Read and Data Store Write blocks explicitly, for example, by using block priority.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results might occur for a model using S-function block and symbolic dimensions
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets all of the following conditions: The S-function block has two or more inputs, with first input to S-function being non symbolic and second or subsequent inputs contain symbolic dimensions. The S-function block uses?%roll ?TLC directive.  In the generated code, the symbolic dimension variables might appear incorrectly in the?for ? loop.
Fixed In: R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a

Summary: MATLAB crashes when?a classification experiment?uses?a large datastore for training or validation.
Description: When a classification experiment uses a large datastore (for handling more than around 2GB of data) for training or validation, a memory leak can cause MATLAB to crash. The memory leak happens when a trial is nearing completion.  Datastores are designed for working with collections of data that        are too large to fit in memory at one time. The problem here is caused by the readall  function incorrectly trying to load all the data in memory at once.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 2, R2022a

Summary: Orifice (IL) block might behave unexpectedly during a fault
Description: The Orifice (IL) block might remain partly or fully open rather than fully closing when a 'Closed' fault occurs. This issue occurs when you concurrently set Opening area when faulted ?to?Closed  Orifice parameterization  ?to?Tabulated data - Area vs. control member position  or Tabulated data- Volumetric flow rate vs. control member position and pressure drop  The first element of Control member position vector ?to a value below 0
Workaround: Make the Control member position vector start at 0 and offset the valve control signal to maintain the intended valve behavior:Subtract the value of the first element of?Control member position vector?from the original?Control member position vector. This results in a?Control member position vector?that starts at a value of?0.Add a PS Subtract block and a PS Constant block to your model.Attach the PS Subtract out port to the?S port of the Orifice (IL) block.Attach the PS Constant block to the -?port of the PS Subtract block.Set the Constant parameter of the PS Constant block to the original value of the first element of the?Control member position vector.Use the?+?port of the PS Subtract block as the control member control signal of the valve.

Summary: MATLAB crashes when using?importGeometry?for an STL file without valid triangles.
Description: The crash occurs in the?importGeometry  function when the STL file does not contain any valid triangles.  There are two possible causes: All vertices of each triangle are at the same location, that is, all triangles in the file are fully collapsed to a single point. The file is empty.  For example, STL files causing a crash, can contain these lines: solid CollapsedTriangles  facet normal 0 0 0    outer loop      vertex 0 0 0       vertex 0 0 0       vertex 0 0 0     endloop   endfacet   endsolid  An STL file with no facets also causes MATLAB to crash: solid EmptyFile  endsolid   Also note that the crash log file will contain the following function call at the top:? pde_CoplanarNormals?

Summary: Error if data input to root-level Inports is a Simulink.Signal object
Description: Simulink Real-Time errors out if the data input that you specify in the model configuration parameter?Data Import > Export > Input  is a Simulink.Signal  object.

Summary: Simscape HDL Workflow Advisor might generate an unexpected error about inconsistent array dimensions
Description: When performing the Code generation compatibility?> Check model?compatibility ?step, the Simscape HDL Workflow Advisor might generate this error: Error-checking model compatibility: Dimensions of arrays being concatenated are not consistent.  You might encounter this error if your model has multiple solver systems and multiple linked subsystems. It occurs when the Simscape HDL Workflow Advisor tries to create a list of non-compatible blocks.

Summary: Few incorrect samples are generated from?ccsdsTMWaveformGenerator  System object, when the property WaveformSource is set to flexible advanced coding and modulation.
Description: For the?ccsdsTMWaveformGenerator? System object, ?when the property? WaveformSource  ?is set to flexible advanced coding and modulation, ?the output waveform for some symbols may be incorrect. As a result, zero bit error rate (BER) may never be achieved at any signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) while performing the BER simulations. Neither the entire waveform nor the spectrum of the signal is impacted due to this problem. Only few samples of the generated waveform are incorrect.

Summary: Variation point proxies in the ARXML are incorrect when using the (default) variant keyword
Description: The variation point proxies generated in the ARXML file from a Simulink model, can be incorrect when the following conditions are met:?The model contains variant blocks in code-compile activation The variant blocks use (default ) keyword  When all the above conditions are true, the CONDITION-ACCESS  in the Variation Point proxy refers to a Rte_SysCon_  variable instead of a SystemConstant from the SystemConstant package.

Summary: Linear Circuit Wizard based loop filter causes glitches inside PLL system.?
Description: Linear Circuit Wizard based loop filter block reacts to short pulses which is not filtered at the output once inside a PLL system. This causes glitches in the VCO, causing spurs at the PLL output.

Summary: Navigation from Polyspace Access to Simulink blocks is not available
Description: When you review the results of an analysis on generated code in Polyspace Access, depending on the size of your Simulink model, you might be unable to navigate from the Source code  pane?in Polyspace Access to blocks in the model.
Products: Polyspace Access, Polyspace Code Prover Access

Summary: predict results are incorrect?when using bitstream with conv thread number = 9,?25,?36 and 49
Description: After generated the bitstream with Conv thread number = 9, 25, 36, and 49 using the DL HDL toolbox dlhdl.buildProcessor(hPC), and use this bitstream for networks prediction, it produces incorrect results.?
Workaround: There is no workaround for this. Please use other conv thread number except??9,?25,?36 and 49 (e.g: 4, 16, 64).

Summary: referencePathFrenet object's closestPoint method potentially returns incorrect results for query-points lying close to path-segments with small change-in-curvature
Description: Certain branches in the referencePathFrenet object's closestPoint algorithm may fail to converge when query points lie very close to clothoid-segments with extremely small change-in-curvature. This is most likely to occur when paths with zero change in curvature, e.g. an arc capable of being defined by two [x y theta] end poses, are represented by an discretized set of [x y] waypoints.?This issue may affect the following API: referencePathFrenet global2frenet closestPoint   One may verify if this error is present by converting a suspicious global state to Frenet coordinates, and then back to global coordinates. The error between pre/post transformation states should generally stay within sqrt(eps). refPath = referencePathFrenet(...)? initialGlobalState = ...;? frenetState =? global2frenet(refPath, initialGlobalState); restoredGlobalState = frenet2global(refPath, frenetState); assert(all(abs(initialGlobalState(:,[1 2 4 5 6]) - restoredGlobalState(:,[1 2 4 5 6])) < sqrt(eps))

Summary: HDL Workflow Advisor may display incorrect target frequency when switching workflows
Description: When changing workflows in the HDL Workflow Advisor,?sometimes the target frequencysetting displayed in the Set Target  >  Set Target Frequency ?task in the HDL Workflow Advisor does not update properly.?This causes the value shown in the SetTarget Frequency  task in the Workflow Advisor to be different than the value saved in the ModelConfiguration Parameters.?The value in the Model Configuration Parametersis used for actual code generation.
Workaround: If you explicitlyset a value for target frequency in the Set Target Frequency taskand click Apply in the HDL Workflow Advisor, the value will beupdated properly in both the HDL Workflow Advisor and the Model ConfigurationParameters.

Summary: timescope axes time limits will be out of sync if zoom is performed in an area that does not have any data
Description: If the input signals provided to the timescope object have different sample rates and plotted on multiple displays, zooming Into an area that does not have any data, might cause the axes time limits to out of sync.

Summary: JMAAB check for?db_0146?does not report iterator blocks positioned improperly
Description: The?Sub-ID b? of Model Advisor?check?mathworks.jmaab.db_0146 (db_0146_b:?Check position of iterator blocks ) does not report warning when annotations are placed above iterator blocks .  This behaviour?is incorrect because, by default the check expects the iterator   blocks to be placed at the top of the subsystem diagram.
