Summary: The high integrity check for hisf_0003 displays unexpected Stateflow warnings?for bit shift operations
Description: The high integrity check Check usage of bitwise operations in Stateflow charts ?(mathworks.hism.hisf_0003 ) throws warnings for shift operations where the second operand (number of bits to be shifted) is of signed type. ?

Summary: JMAAB check for jc_0741?displays an incorrect warning for variables used in transitions
Description: Model Advisor?check jc_0741 :?Timing to update data used in state chart transition conditions ?displays the following warning when variables used in transition condition action are used in state during action. Warning :? Stateflow states violate the guidelines for updating the variables used in state transition conditions.  This warning message is incorrect as variables are used in transition condition action and not in transition condition.

Summary: Code generation report missing search box
Description: When you generate a code generation report using HDL Coder, the search box is missing from the report.

Summary: JMAAB check for?jc_0171?displays abnormal exit warning for subsystems with unconnected source ports
Description: The?Sub-ID a? of??Check connections between structural subsystems? (id:?mathworks.jmaab.jc_0171)? displays abnormal exit warning for atomic subsystems with unconnected source ports.

Summary: Unconnected geometry ports on Spatial Contact Force blocks can cause MATLAB to crash
Description: When a geometry port on a Spatial Contact Force block is unconnected, MATLAB can crash during compilation at the beginning of simulation. The crash only occurs when one of the geometry ports is unconnected, not both.

Summary: Missing option to add Simulink.Variant object from the Model Explorer menu
Description: In the Model Explorer , no menu button exists to add a Simulink.Variant  object in the model workspace or the base workspace.

Summary: MATLAB and Job Monitor hang while other MJS cluster users create tasks
Description: When a user of a MATLAB Job Scheduler (MJS) cluster creates a task on a job, if you are connected to that cluster, your MATLAB client can temporarily hang. Until the cluster user has finished creating the task, your MATLAB temporarily hangs if you try to: Use the Update Now  button in the Job Monitor Access properties of the cluster user's job  Part of creating a task involves uploading the?InputArguments ?for the task. You might need to wait for a long time if there is a lot of data or if data upload is slow for the cluster user.
Fixed In: R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 4, R2021b, R2022a

Summary: Highlighting in Simulink model comparison report does not bring models in front of other windows
Description: Highlighting differences in?the model comparison report does not bring the Simulink Editor to the front?If both   these conditions are true:? The models are open The models are hidden behind other windows?

Summary: FMU simulation in Simulink errors out due to small step size and long simulation time
Description: When an FMU import block runs at a small communication sample time (for example, 0.0001s) in a long simulation (for example, 1000s), Simulink throws the following error: An error occurred while running the simulation and the simulation was terminated?   Caused by:?  Error in supplied FMU: An error occurred in function 'fmi2DoStep' for block '<block name>' during simulation.?    At the same time, the FMU logger prints out an error related to communication time inconsistency, for example: Log from FMU: [category:error, status:fmi2Error] Stepsize must be non-negative and divisible by 0.0001?

Summary: Data Type Propagation block does not support symbolic dimensions
Description: A Simulink model with Data Type Propagation  block?containing symbolic dimensions returns this error message:Modelname/Data Type Propagation does not support symbolic dimensions propagation.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 5, R2021b

Summary: Clicking the Restore View button?in the axes toolbar of some plots in?Classification Learner and Regression Learner?can result in empty or partial plots
Description: For some plots in the Classification Learner and Regression Learner apps, clicking the Restore View  button in the axes toolbar after changing some of the plot options can incorrectly set the axes limits. The plots then show no data or display only partial data. The affected plots are scatter plots in Classification Learner (when you select a different value from the X  or Y  lists under Predictors ), response plots in Regression Learner (when you select a different value from the X  list under X-axis ), and residuals plots in Regression Learner (when you select a different value under X-axis : True response , Predicted response , Record number , or a different value from the Predictor  list). This issue occurs when you click the Restore View  button, change the plot options, and then click the Restore View  button a second time.
Workaround: Close the affected plot and reopen it by clicking the corresponding plot button in the?Plots?section of the?Classification Learner or Regression Learner tab. Select the variables to display in the plot. Do not click the Restore View button in the axes toolbar before changing the variable selections.

Summary: Diagnostic error on simulation with variant control variables from the mask or model workspace used in an expression containing true or false
Description: During update diagram or simulation, if any variant blocks (Variant Subsystem, Variant Source, Variant Sink, or Variant Connector) in a model uses the variant control variable from the mask or model workspace, and if the variant expression on that block contains?true? or?false ?keywords, then this error is displayed.?Error message:? The variant condition on the block 'block name'?originate from different workspaces. ?This is not a supported workflow.?
Workaround: Consider using expressions like A==0 or A==1 instead of A==true or A==false.

Summary: MATLAB crashes while reading an HDF5 file containing compound variables with long duplicate field names
Description: When an HDF5 file with a compound variable containing several long (more than or close to 63 characters) duplicate field names is read in MATLAB using h5read  or has its contents queried using h5info , it results in a MATLAB crash. In some cases, if the field names are not long but are repeated many times then MATLAB truncates them by an abnormal length.

Summary: The HEVMM, HEVP0, and HEVIPS Reference applications have incorrect battery internal resistance calibration for ambient temperatures below -10C (263.15 K)
Description: The internal resistance of the battery models in the HEVMM, HEVP0, and HEVIPS reference applications have a unrealistically high battery internal resistance parameter when the ambient temperature setting at the top of the reference application is set below -10C (263.15K).
Workaround: Open BattHevP0.slx, BattHevIps.slx, or BattHevMM.slx for low ambient temperature simulationCopy row 3 of the RInt parameter and replace rows 1 and 2 with the row 3 values using copy/pasteSave the respective Batt*.slx file

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect std::copy and std::memset generated in C++ code when loop induction variable is unsigned int
Description: For sequential copy or initialization of an array inside for  loop, the code generator emits incorrect std::copy  and std::memset  which leads to incorrect result if all of the following conditions are true:Model configuration parameter Language  is set to C++.  Loop induction variable is unsigned int . Loop upper bound is not known at compile time. Subscript of source/destination array contains loop induction variable minus some offset, for example, A (k1 - 5) .  In the below function ebr_2527097_memcpy , there is a copy from array A to array B: function [B] = ebr_2527097_memcpy(A, B, in)    for k2 = uint8(6):uint8(1):uint8(in)        B(k2 - 5,1) = A((k2) - (5),1);    end end Following is the expected generated std::copy : if (u >= 6) {    std::copy(&A[0], &A[u - 5] , &B[0]);} Instead, the code generator produces the following which causes overflow for the subscript 'u + 251 ':if (u >= 6) {    std::copy(&A[0], &A[u + 251] , &B[0]);} Here is an example of std::memset  which initializes array B with int8(127) :function [B] = ebr_2527097_memset(B, in)        for k2 = uint8(6):uint8(1):uint8(in)        B(k2 - 5,1) = int8(127);    endend Following is the expected generated std::memset :if (u >= 6) {    std::memset(&B[0], 127, static_cast(u - 5)  * sizeof(signed char));}  Instead, the code generator produces the following:if (u >= 6) {    std::memset(&B[0], 127, static_cast(u + 251)  * sizeof(signed char));}
Workaround: Disable std::copy in coder.CodeConfig object.cfg = coder.config; cfg.EnableMemcpy = 0; There is no workaround for std::memset as?Use memcpy for vector assignment (EnableMemcpy) only disables std::copy.

Summary: Maximum number of elements decreased for deep learning operations on GPU
Description: For several deep learning functions, including layers, the maximum number of elements allowed for inputs and outputs on the GPU has decreased. Running affected functions using inputs and outputs with more than 2*10^9 elements results in the following error: Maximum variable size allowed on the device is exceeded.  This is a decrease from the maximum number of elements of? 2147483648 (2^31) in previous releases. Affected functions include, but are not limited to, the following:? convolution - convolution2dLayer , convolution3dLayer , dlconv?  batch normalization - batchNormalizationLayer , batchnorm?  activations -  reluLayer ,  relu
Fixed In: R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 5, R2021b, R2022a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code generated when model parameter argument uses a storage class that sets Data Initialization?to Auto
Description: Incorrect code might be generated when the mapping for a model parameter argument specifies a storage class that sets Data Initialization ?to Auto,  and ? Data Scope ?to Exported. ?In this case, the data for the parameter argument is uninitialized and typically has value 0 .
Workaround: Map model parameter arguments to Auto, Model default, or?a storage class that sets Data Initialization to Static.

Summary: Unable to use tall arrays in MATLAB Online before setting a default cluster profile
Description: When you have Parallel Computing Toolbox??and create a tall array in MATLAB Online, you sometimes encounter the following error when you try to use that tall array: ?Invalid tall array. The execution environment backing this tall array no longer exists.  You encounter the error if you have not set a default cluster profile before you use the tall array.
Workaround: As a workaround, you can use mapreducer to ensure that tall arrays are processed in serial before you open a parallel pool.?mapreducer(0)If you then open a parallel pool, use the following code to use the default behavior and run tall calculations on that parallel pool.?delete(gcmr)

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generated for?isprop does not return true for Constant properties of a class
Description: In MATLAB,   isprop(obj,PropertyName) ?returns?true ?if?PropertyName ?is a property of the object?obj, including  Constant properties.?   In the generated code,?isprop(obj,PropertyName) ? returns false ?if?PropertyName ?is a Constant  property of the object?obj.   For example, consider the following class A ?and function?main : % A.mclassdef A    properties (Constant)        property = 1    endend% main.mfunction result = mainx = A;result = isprop(x, 'property'); If you invoke?main ?in MATLAB, it returns?true : >> mainans =  logical   1 If you generate a MEX function for main  and invoke main_mex , it returns false : >> codegen mainCode generation successful.>> main_mexans =  logical   0 This issue affects MATLAB Coder, MATLAB Function blocks and MATLAB System blocks in Simulink, and MATLAB functions in Stateflow.

Summary: Importing C/C++ code with source files with same global variable declared more than once causes MATLAB to crash
Description: When using Simulink.CodeImporter or sltest.CodeImporter, if the custom C/C++ code declares the same global variable in different source files, MATLAB crashes while analyzing the code. This issue only occurs when the same global variable is declared multiple times, but is initialized once in different translation units. For example,?? Source File 1 ->??int myGlobal;?   Source File 2 -> int myGlobal = 1;

Summary: MATLAB issues warnings about missing fonts on startup on Mac OS 12?
Description: On macOS 12 open MATLAB and you can see the warnings. ? Warning: the font "Times" is not available,  so "Lucida Bright" has been substituted,  but may have unexpected appearance or behavor.  Re-enable the "Times" font to remove this warning. ? Warning: the font "Times" is not available,  so "Lucida Bright" has been substituted,  but may have unexpected appearance or behavor.  Re-enable the "Times" font to remove this warning.

Summary: Generation of ASAP2 (A2L) file fails for models containing opaque datatypes
Description: An A2L file is not generated for models which have an opaque datatype. The following error occurs during A2L file generation:Unrecognized function or variable 'format'? ?  For models with the?System target file  parameter set to slrealtime.tlc, ?the generation of the A2L file is a part of the build process. For these models, the A2L file is not generated due to this error, but the build process does complete successfully.
Fixed In: R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 4, R2021b, R2022a

Summary: Signal Editor appears to hang when deleting a large number of points
Description: When you attempt to delete a large number of points in the Signal Editor, it appears to hang. The apparent hang is actually a long delay between the delete action and removing the points.

Summary: Potential incorrect Dead Logic or Unsatisfiable objectives for MinMax blocks with large vector inputs with fixed-point or boolean arguments
Description: MinMax  blocks with the following configuration may incorrectly report Dead Logic  or Unsatisfiable  objectives:The MinMax  block has one of the following 2 configurations:  The block has exactly one inport and that inport is a vector with at least 31 elements. The block has at least 31 inports.   The inputs to the MinMax  block are of either fixed-point or boolean data type.

Summary: Input layers change when creating a dlnetwork with both featureInputLayer and image3dInputLayer objects
Description: When creating a dlnetwork object from a layer graph containing   both featureInputLayer  and image3dInputLayer  objects, the software returns a network containing only featureInputLayer  objects or only? image3dInputLayer  objects as input layers. In most cases, the network trains and makes predictions correctly. However, when interacting with the network layers, the software might throw an error such as: In 'nnet_cnn:layer:FeatureInputLayer:oneLineDisplayNoTransforms', data type supplied is incorrect for parameter {0}.
Workaround: In your code, replace featureInputLayer objects with imageInputLayer objects with input size [1 1 numFeatures], where numFeatures is the input size of the replaced featureInputLayer object. You must also reshape your input data to have have two spatial dimensions. To remove spatial dimensions in the network, you can use a flattenLayer object. For example, replace featureInputLayer(numFeatures) with imageInputLayer([1 1 numFeatures])flattenLayerand reshape the input data with format "CB" to have format "SSCB" using dlX = dlarray(dlX,"CBSS") Alternatively, in R2021a, you can remove the input layers completely and instead use the syntax dlnet = dlnetwork(layers,dlX1,...,dlXn) when creating the dlnetwork object. In this case, you must manually perform any normalization calculations done by the input layers.

Summary: An unconnected frame port on a variable mass block can cause MATLAB to crash
Description: When the frame port of a variable mass block is unconnected, and at least one input of the variable mass block is directly connected to the output of a Simscape Multibody block, MATLAB can crash during compilation at the beginning of simulation. This affects all the variable mass blocks of Simscape Multibody: General Variable Mass, Variable Brick Solid, Variable Cylindrical Solid, and Variable Spherical Solid.
Workaround: A workaround is to connect the frame port of the variable mass block to a frame port of another Simscape Multibody block. Another workaround is to remove the direct connection between the output of the other Simscape Multibody block and the input of the variable mass block. This can be achieved, for example, by inserting a PS Gain block into the signal.

Summary: MATLAB might freeze sporadically when running large live scripts that produce many figure outputs
Description: When running Live Editor code that produces many figure outputs, MATLAB might freeze and execution cannot be stopped. The freeze occurs most commonly when the many figure outputs contain image plots.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: A conditionally executed subsystem containing a C Function block can give different results in simulation and generated code
Description: Simulation results might not match generated code results when all of the following conditions are true: A model contains a C Function block placed inside a conditionally executed subsystem. The C Function block initializes the value of a block Output   symbol in the Start Code  of the block. The C Function block output corresponding to the Output   symbol is connected to an Outport block. The Outport block is configured to inherit its initial output value from the input signal.  In such a case, the initial value set by the C Function block is not propagated to the downstream Outport block during simulation.
Workaround: Explicitly specify the initial value in the Outport block parameters.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Numerical mismatch occurs in the output between normal simulation mode and SIL mode/Rapid accelerator mode when the Discrete-FIR Filter block is reset
Description: There is a numerical mismatch in the output between normal simulation mode and SIL mode/Rapid accelerator mode when the Discrete-FIR Filter block is reset in a model which has the following parameters set: 1) 'InitialStates' ?parameter is set to non-zero conditions.2) 'FilterStructure'?parameter is set to either 'Direct form symmetric' or 'Direct form antisymmetric'.Under these conditions resetting the Discrete-FIR block in the model will re-initialize the initial states to default (zeros) instead of setting it to the actual initial states.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Rapid accelerator mode simulation when For Each subsystem has large amount of output data might produce incorrect results
Description: Simulating your model in rapid accelerator mode might produce incorrect results when all of the following conditions are met: The model contains a For Each subsystem with an Outport block with a pre-concatenation signal size greater than 4,096 bytes, for example, a 513-by-1 signal of type?double . The?MultithreadedSim ?parameter of the For Each block inside the For Each subsystem is?set to its default value of auto . The?MultithreadedSim ?parameter of the model?is set to its default value of auto .

Summary: Typing in the desktop Search Documentation box on the desktop can cause MATLAB to become unresponsive
Description: The typeahead feature of the MATLAB desktop Search Documentation box can use a significant amount of memory. This can cause MATLAB to run out of memory and become unresponsive, which might result in loss of work.
Workaround: When searching the documentation, use the Search Documentation box in the Help browser instead of the Search Documentation box on the desktop.

Summary: Incorrect report of data dependency violation with asynchronous function-call initiator and Rate Transition block together with loop
Description: When both of the following conditions are met, you might see an incorrect report of a data dependency violation when updating or simulating your model: Either:?  The model contains an algebraic loop, and the model configuration parameter Diagnostics > Algebraic Loop  is set to none  or warning ; or  The model contains a loop formed by a graphical connection from the state port of a block with a state port, such as an Integrator block.   The model contains both an asynchronous function-call initiator and a Rate Transition block for that asynchronous rate in the same nonvirtual hierarchy as the loop.
Workaround: Separate the loop and the blocks with asynchronous rate into different nonvirtual hierarchies. For example, group the asynchronous function-call and corresponding Rate Transition block into an atomic subsystem.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 4, R2021b

Summary: HDL Workflow Advisor with Simulink Real-time workflow generates empty software interface model when using the command-line (hdlcoder.runworkflow)
Description: When using the Simulink Real-time workflow in the HDL Workflow advisor from the command-line using 'hdlcoder.runworkflow', the generated Simulink Real-time interface model is empty, i.e, all the inputs ports are connected to the terminators, and all the outputs ports are connected to the 'zeros' constant blocks.
Workaround: Use HDL Workflow Advisor UI for your task.

Summary: Adding multiple digital channels from a non-clocking device can generate an error
Description: When using the DataAcquisition interface with a device that does not support clocked operations, adding a second digital output channel can result in an error. However, the channels are successfully added. For example: dq = daq("ni");addoutput(dq,"Dev1","port1/line0","Digital")addoutput(dq,"Dev1","port1/line1","Digital")Error setting property 'ElementsAvailableEventCount' of class'matlabshared.asyncio.buffer.Buffer'. Value must be positive.dq.Channelsans =     Index    Type     Device       Channel       Measurement Type    Range           Name           _____    _____    ______    _____________    ________________    _____    __________________      1      "dio"    "Dev2"    "port1/line0"      "OutputOnly"      "n/a"    "Dev2_port1/line0"      2      "dio"    "Dev2"    "port1/line1"      "OutputOnly"      "n/a"    "Dev2_port1/line1"write(d, [0 0])
Workaround: You can avoid this issue with either of the following:Add multiple channels with one command when possible. For example, if the digital lines belong to the same port:dq = daq('ni')addoutput(dq,"Dev1","port1/line0:1","Digital")Otherwise, you can use the session interface:s = daq.createSession("ni")addDigitalChannel(s,"Dev1","port1/line0","OutputOnly")addDigitalChannel(s,"Dev1","port1/line1","OutputOnly")

Summary: Signal editor might display busy icon while importing complex signals from workspace
Description: If the Signal Editor imports a complex workspace variable, a busy icon appears and is not removed.

Summary: dlfeval errors when calling forward after modifying dlnetwork learnable parameters
Description: Modifying the learnable parameters of a dlnetwork  while inside a dlfeval  call can cause the forward  function to error.  For example, multiple calls to the following dlfeval  call errors. [gradients,loss] = dlfeval(@modelGradients,dlnet,dlX,T)  where modelGradients  is given byfunction [gradients,loss] = modelGradients(dlnet,dlX,T)dlY1 = T + 1;dlX1 = dlX + 1;dlX2 = dlX1.*dlY1;dlX3 = dlX2.^2;dlnet.Learnables.Value{1}(1:2,1:2) = dlX3(1:2)'*dlX3(1:2);dlZ = forward(dlnet,dlX3); % this will error when called a second timeloss = sum(dlZ,'all');gradients = dlgradient(loss,dlX);end?  In this case, the software throws the following error: Incorrect dimensions for matrix multiplication. Check that the number of columns in the first matrix matches the number of rows in the second matrix. To perform elementwise multiplication, use '.*'.
Workaround: In the call to forward, set the 'Acceleration' option to 'none'.For example, use:dlZ = forward(dlnet,dlX3,'Acceleration','none');

Summary: Spherical Harmonic Gravity Model and?Orbit Propagator blocks crash MATLAB when one or more positions is below Earth surface and Action is none or warning
Description: The?Spherical Harmonic Gravity Model ?block crashes MATLAB for array inputs when both: One or more position inputs is below the Earth surface Action  ?is set to?none  or warning   The?Orbit Propagator ?block crashes MATLAB when all of the following are true: More than one satellite is being modeled One or more satellites is below the Earth surface Gravitational potential model   is set to Spherical harmonics  Action ?is none  or warning ?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Simulink.VariantVariable with non-finite choice values generates incorrect code for code compile Variant activation time
Description: Specifying a Simulink.VariantVariable  with non-finite choice values for code compile Variant activation time  may generate incorrect code.? Consider that in a Simulink model you define Simulink.VariantVariable  as:V = Simulink. VariantControl('Value',  1,  'ActivationTime',  'code compile')?kv = Simulink. VariantVariable('Choices',  {'V == 1',  [1,  Inf],  'V == 2',  [1,  2]})  When you generate code from the model using Embedded Coder?, the model initialize function is generated as: void model_initialize(void) {  #if V == 1 || V == 2kv[1] = rtInf; #endif }In the model initialize function, the value of kv[1]  is incorrectly set to rtInf for both the variant choices. The value must be rtInf only for the condition V == 1 .

Summary: Deep learning training progress plot can incorrectly render values
Description: The deep learning training progress plot can sometimes incorrectly render values. For example, the training plot might display an accuracy as greater than 100%. This can occur when training using Deep Network Designer, Experiment Manager, or using the trainingOptions   function with the  'Plots ' option set to 'training-progress' .

Summary: Initialization of custom storage class data object by a non-owner model
Description: When you set the Owner  property of a Simulink.Signal  or Simulink.Parameter  custom storage class and select the Use owner from data object for data definition placement  configuration parameter, a model other than the one you specify in the Owner ?property might generate a?model_initialize ?method that initializes the signal.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Simulink might use an outdated enum definition when simulating or generating code for a referenced model
Description: Simulink might use the previous definition of a dynamic enumerated data type when all of the following conditions are true: The enumerated type is defined by using the?Simulink.defineIntEnumType ?function. The model hierarchy contains at least one referenced model. The Rebuild ?configuration parameter of the top model is set to If any changes in known dependencies detected . The enumerated type definition has changed since the model reference simulation or code generation target was last built.
Workaround: Set the Rebuild configuration parameter of the top model to If any changes detected.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b

Summary: When using arrayfun to call struct, passing multiple field names to struct as a string array crashes MATLAB
Description: If you use arrayfun  to call @struct , and pass multiple field names as arguments to struct ?in a string array, MATLAB crashes. For example:? arrayfun(@struct, ["name1","name2"], {[1 2],[3 4]}, 'UniformOutput', false);
Workaround: Use an intermediate anonymous function to wrap the struct function:?arrayfun(@(x,y) struct(x,y), ["name1","name2"], {[1 2],[3 4]}, 'UniformOutput', false)Alternatively, use a cell array of character vectors in place of strings, or use a loop in place of arrayfun.

Summary: Starting MATLAB generates a debug.log file in <matlabroot>\bin\win64
Description: Sometimes when MATLAB is started a debug.log file is generated in <matlabroot>\bin\win64 . Every time MATLAB is started, more content is appended to this file causing it to slowly grow over time.This generally happens when MATLAB is not installed into a read-only location on the file system.?
Workaround: This file can safely be deleted if it becomes too large but will be regenerated when MATLAB is restarted.Alternatively, change the <matlabroot>\bin\win64 directory to read-only to prevent the file from being generated.?

Summary: Pipe (IL) and Pipe (TL) blocks may incorrectly scale pressureloss
Description: For both the Pipe (IL) block and the Pipe(TL) block, you may encounter unexpected pressure loss values when you concurrently set:? Pipe wall specification ?to?Flexible  Viscous friction parameterization  ?to? Nominal pressure drop vs. nominal mass flow rate    The Pipe (IL) block may under-scale the pressure loss by a factor equal to the nominal area. The Pipe (TL) block may under-scale the pressure loss by a factor equal to the cubed ratio of the nominaldiameter to the the actual diameter.  ?

Summary: Invalid HDL code can be generated from General CRC Syndrome Detector HDL Optimized
Description: Invalid HDL code that does not compile can be generated when the General CRC Syndrome Detector HDL Optimized is used in these conditions:?? Synchronous semantics are used (from State Control block) Global reset type is set to 'synchronous' Minimize Clock Enables is on  The generated code looks like the below VHDL code which has a leading ELSE statement with no preceding IF statement.   datain_tapregister1_process : PROCESS (clk)    BEGIN  ? IF clk'EVENT AND clk = '1' THEN  ? ? IF reset = '1' OR end_valid = '1' THEN  ? ? ? datain_tapreg_1 <= to_unsigned(0, 32);  ? ? IF validIn = '1' THEN  ? ? ? ELSE   ? ? ? ? datain_tapreg_1 <= dataIn_unsigned;  ? ? ? END IF;  ? ? END IF;  ? END PROCESS datain_tapregister1_process;

Summary: Start values error for FMU Import block
Description: Simulink might throw an error related to FMU block start values when running a model even though the correct start values are provided in the FMU block dialog box. The error message varies depending on the application.

Summary: Copying an Inport or Outport? block in the rtwdemo_cppclass_expfcn?example model causes MATLAB to crash
Description: Copying an Inport or Outport? block in the?rtwdemo_cppclass_expfcn ?example model causes MATLAB to crash.
Workaround: Delete each Inport and Outport block from the model canvas of the?rtwdemo_cppclass_expfcn?example model. Add new Inport and Outport blocks to the model.

Summary: The iteration order selected in the Instantiate Architecture Model tool is not saved in the Analysis Viewer upon clicking Instantiate
Description: The iteration order selected on the Instantiate Architecture Model  tool does not carry over to the Analysis Viewer  upon instantiation after clicking Instantiate . This might result in incorrect analysis results when the analysis function is run after clicking Analyze .
Workaround: Re-select the desired iteration order on the Analysis Viewer prior to clicking Analyze to run an analysis using the analysis function.

Summary: dlquantizer validate step may fail when the selected C++ compiler is Visual Studio 2019
Description: dlquantizer  with ExecutionEnvironment  set to GPU  fails at the validate step with the following error dialog error when Visual Studio 2019 is the selected C++ compiler: dlquantizer workflow failed?
Workaround: Use?Microsoft Visual C++ 2017?or?Microsoft Visual C++ 2015?as the selected C++ compiler.

Summary: Aero.FixedWing?forcesAndMoments?method incorrectly calculates wind-to-body forces and moments as stability-to-body forces and moments
Description: When a coefficient object on a fixed-wing aircraft has ReferenceFrame  set to Wind , t he forces and moments calculation in forcesAndMoments ? incorrectly calculates the wind-to-body forces and moments as stability-to-body forces and moments.  The incorrect reference frame calculation affects these Aero.FixedWing  methods: forcesAndMoments  nonlinearDynamics  staticStability  linearize  datcomToFixedWing

Summary: Simulink.LookupTable?tabular interface displays incorrect error message when evaluating MATLAB expression for Table.Value
Description: A?Simulink.LookupTable  object with BreakspointsSpecification ?set to?'Explicit Values'  Table.Value  dimension set to an incorrect dimension number  displays an incorrect error message that contains?Must be 1D array , when a 2-D array is actually required.

Summary: MATLAB crashes when saving certain function handles created via Run Section
Description: If you use Run Section in MATLAB to run a section of code which: Includes a nested function definition, and Creates a function handle to that function  Attempting to save this function handle might cause MATLAB to crash. For example, take the following function f1.m  function f1%% section of codefh = @f2;?? ?function f2?? ? ? ?disp('in f2')?? ?end%%end  Using Run Section to run the section of code between the %% markers creates fh , a function handle to the function f2 . Then if you attempt to save fh , MATLAB crashes. >> save file.mat fh? % crash!

Summary: GPU acceleration on a Simulink model containing a deep learning network may crash MATLAB, if run-time error-checking is enabled
Description: Running a simulation on a deep learning Simulink model with GPU acceleration and run-time error-checking enabled, causes MATLAB to crash with the following message:  ====  failed assertion in LateBinding.cpp,?newInstanceVar    ?
Workaround: Disable run-time error-checking from the Simulation Target > GPU Acceleration pane on the Model Configuration Parameters window or by using set_param on the?SimGPUErrorChecks parameter.

Summary: Identical names for workspace variables and root inport blocks cause incorrect value for variable to be returned
Description: When a workspace variable and a root inport block have identical names, Simulink Real-Time Explorer or the getparam()  function can return an incorrect value for the workspace parameter.

Summary: Incorrect build error from model containing Inline variant block that has?variant control expression set to?true?(always true)
Description: Consider a model with these settings:An Inline variant block with two choices with variant control expression set to?true  and V==2 . The block has Variant activation time set to code compile . The variant control variable 'V 'is set to '1' in the base workspace or .sldd.  When you generate code from this model, this build error message is displayed.? At least one variant choice must be active.    This is incorrect build error because? one of the choice of the variant block is true  (always true).

Summary: pspectrum uses very low minimum threshold when plotting
Description: The pspectrum  function generates a plot with improper z -axis scaling when called without output arguments and without specifying the MinThreshold ?argument. The function chooses an unnecessarily small value for the minimum power, and as a result the power values do not occupy the entire range of the colormap. This code illustrates the bug: load batsignalpspectrum(batsignal,1/DT,'spectrogram')  The minimum spectral power displayed in the graph is lower than ¨C140 dB, as shown by the color bar.
Workaround: Use the MinThreshold?name-value argument to produce a spectrogram with a specified minimum value that does not display unwanted background content.This example uses pspectrum to plot a spectrogram with an adequate minimum threshold:https://www.mathworks.com/help/signal/ug/practical-introduction-to-time-frequency-analysis.html#TimeFrequencyAnalysisExample-2
Fixed In: R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 4, R2021b

Summary: On the Trailer Body 3DOF block mask Longitudinal pane, the?Initial longitudinal velocity, xdot_o parameter is not available
Description: On the Trailer Body 3DOF block mask?Longitudinal? pane, the Initial longitudinal velocity, xdot_o? parameter is not available. You cannot use it to specify an initial longitudinal velocity.
Workaround: To enable the?Initial longitudinal velocity, xdot_o parameter, follow these steps:Set Axle forces to External longitudinal velocity.Set Axle forces to External longitudinal forces.The Initial longitudinal velocity, xdot_o parameter is available on the Longitudinal pane.

Summary: TensorRT code generation for a network containing a pattern of?convolution, ReLU, and addition layers?might cause MATLAB to crash
Description: Consider a network containing?convolution2dLayer ,?reluLayer , and?additionLayer ?in this pattern:  ? ? ? ? ? ? _________|_________  ? ? ? ? ? ?|? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ?|  ? ? ? ? ?conv? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? layer? ? ?  ? ? ? ? ? ?|? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ?|  ? ? ? ? ?relu? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? |  ? ? ? ? ? ?|_____addition______|  ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ?|? ?? ?? Code generation for such a network using the TensorRT library might cause MATLAB to crash with a segmentation violation or assertion.  ?
Workaround: Update your existing MATLAB installations.Alternatively, replace?reluLayer?by an equivalent?leakyReluLayer?with zero-scaling and then proceed with code generation. For example, suppose that you have the following network architecture:?? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? image_input?? ? ? ? ? ? _________|_________? ? ? ? ? ?|? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ?|? ? ? ? ?conv1? ? ? ? ? ? ? ?conv2? ? ?? ? ? ? ? ?|? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ?|? ? ? ? ?relu1? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ?|? ? ? ? ? ?|_____addition______|? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ?|?? ? ? ? ? ? ? fully_connected?? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ?|? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? regression??The MATLAB code for this network is as follows:?lgraph = layerGraph( [? ? imageInputLayer([28 28 2],'Name','in','Normalization','none')? ? convolution2dLayer([3 3],2,'Padding', [1 1 1 1], 'Name','conv1')? ? reluLayer('Name', 'relu1')? ? convolution2dLayer([3 3],2,'Padding', [1 1 1 1], 'Name','conv2')? ? additionLayer(2,'Name','add')? ? fullyConnectedLayer(3,'Name','fc')? ? regressionLayer('Name', 'regOut')] );lgraph = disconnectLayers(lgraph, 'relu1', 'conv2');lgraph = connectLayers(lgraph, 'in', 'conv2');lgraph = connectLayers(lgraph, 'relu1', 'add/in2');To replace the layers, use the?replaceLayer?function.lgraph?= replaceLayer(lgraph,'relu1',leakyReluLayer(0,'Name','leakyrelu1'));In addition, if?the network?described by?lgraph?has already been trained and you would like to use it, you can reassemble it as:net = assembleNetwork(lgraph);

Summary: Dragging an LC Ladder block from the library into a model causes MATLAB to crash
Description: MATLAB crashes when you drag an LC Ladder block from the Simulink Library Browser into a model canvas.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: If two enumeration classes have same name but reside in different packages, C++ code generation might crash or produce incorrect code
Description: If your MATLAB code uses two enumerations the have the same name but reside in different packages, C++ code generation might crash or produce incorrect code. If the code generator produces incorrect code, one of the enumeration types is replaced with the other one in the generated code. For example, consider these two MATLAB enumeration files. Both enumeration classes are named myEnum2 . One of them is placed inside the package moo . ./MyEnum2.m   classdef MyEnum2 < int16    enumeration        Value1(1)        Value2(2)    endend ./+moo/MyEnum2.m   classdef MyEnum2 < int16    enumeration        Value1(5)        Value2(6)    endend Consider the this MATLAB function: function [out, out2] = test2out = int32(MyEnum2.Value1);out2 = int32(moo.MyEnum2.Value1);end Generate C++ code for test2  by running these commands:? cfg = coder.config('lib');cfg.TargetLang='C++';codegen test2 -report -c -config cfg The generated C++ code assigns the value of 1 to both out  and out2 , which is incorrect.

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier incorrectly reports decision objective for the last input index?of a Multiport switch block as Unsatisfiable.
Description: If a Simulink model contains a Multiport switch  block with following configuration:Number of data ports  set to 1 The data port is connected to the output of a?Mux ?block  then, Simulink Design Verifier always reports decision objective associated with the last input index?as Unsatisfiable  during the test generation analysis, even if it ?can potentially be covered by a simulation. Similarly, during dead logic analysis Simulink Design Verifier always reports this objective as Dead Logic .
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b

Summary: File upload operations in web apps may result in corrupt files
Description: In MATLAB Web Apps, files uploaded to the web using MATLAB uigetfile might not upload as expected.? Uploaded files on the MATLAB Web App Server may be corrupt or empty.

Summary: High-Integrity check MATLAB code with logical operators and functions does not analyze external MATLAB files at secondary and further levels
Description: The Model Advisor check? MATLAB code with logical operators and functions ? (ID:?mathworks.hism.himl_0010 ) does not analyze and flag the violations inside external MATLAB files at secondary and further levels.

Summary: RoadRunner crashes when exporting to Unity.
Description: When prop assets are missing or invalid, they show up as a pink barrel in the scene. When trying to export the scene with these missing props, RoadRunner crashes.
Workaround: To workaround this issue, users must clean up the scene so there are no missing props.

Summary: JMAAB check for?db_0097?displays an incorrect warning for signal labels
Description: The?Sub-ID b? of Model Advisor?check?db_0097 (db_0097_b:?Check position of signal labels )?displays abnormal exit warning for signal label not positioned according to the guideline.

Summary: Generated code contains usage of dynamic memory allocation when?Dynamic memory allocation in MATLAB functions?is disabled
Description: When generating code for a Simulink model containing a MATLAB Function Block, MATLAB System Block, or MATLAB Function in Stateflow that contains A binary operation where one of the operands is a concatenation expression. The concatenation expression contains bounded variable-size data.  Simulink Coder might produce generated code using dynamic memory allocation even if the?Dynamic memory allocation in MATLAB function  configuration parameter is disabled . For example when generating code for Simulink model containing a MATLAB function block with the content:? function out = issue_2462856(val,n)    boundedData1 = generateBoundedData(val,n,2);    boundedData2 = generateBoundedData(2*val,n,1);    boundedData3 = generateBoundedData(4*val,n,1);    out = boundedData1 .* [boundedData2,boundedData3];endfunction out = generateBoundedData(val,n,dim2)    coder.inline('never');    assert(n <= 255);    out = val .* (1:n)';    out = repmat(out,[1,dim2]);end with?Dynamic memory allocation in MATLAB functions  disabled, ?the?generated code incorrectly contains usages of?emxArray ?APIs that use dynamic memory allocation. See the call to modelName_emxEnsureCapacity_real_T ?in the following generated code snippet:? [SNIP]  loop_ub = b_size[0];  tmp_size = b_0->size[0] * b_0->size[1];  b_0->size[0] = b_size[0];  b_0->size[1] = 1;  modelName_emxEnsureCapacity_real_T(b_0, tmp_size);  for (tmp_size = 0; tmp_size < loop_ub; tmp_size++) {    b_0->data[tmp_size] = b_data[tmp_size + b_size[0]];  }[SNIP]? See also Bug Report ID 2462856 ?

Summary: Unexpected warning when importing FMU
Description: An unexpected warning  Warning: 'untitled/ FMU' is unable to map internal signals using 'structured' mode and will use 'flat' mode instead   might be returned when you try to import an FMU that has VariableNamingConvention  defined in the modelDescription.xml.

Summary: set_param?executed from mask initialization of a FromFile block with a bus object as datatype may result in error
Description: Consider a model having FromFile  block under a mask with a mask initialisation script. The mask initialisation script does a set_param  operation on the FromFile  block having bus object as its datatype, an error appears. The error says that bus data type specified on the FromFile  block does not match the structure of the input variable from the file.  The reported error message: Error in 'FromFileBlockName': Failed to evaluate mask initialization commands. Caused by:    Diagnostic reported from block 'FromFileBlock'        The Bus data type specified by the From File block 'blockName' does not match the structure of the        variable 'inputData' in file 'inputData.mat'. Field 'FieldName' of the structure is not an element of the bus.

Summary: Model Advisor check Check switch statements in MATLAB Function blocks?reports incorrect warning for?switch?statements
Description: Model Advisor check Check switch statements in MATLAB Function blocks ?(ID:?mathworks.hism.himl_0007 ) reports the following incorrect warning for?switch-case  statements containing two case  statements and an otherwise  statement with a meaningful comment.Warning  The followinginappropriate switch statements are found in embedded MATLAB code:  Block Path? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? Expression  TestModel/switch_case????? switch u...  RecommendedAction  Ensure that the switch statement has at least two case statements  This warningis incorrect as the check must report warnings only when switch  statements do notinclude at least two case  statements and an otherwise  statement with a meaningful comment.

Summary: Code generation might fail when SubsystemReuse parameter is set to 'Atomic and Virtual'
Description: HDL code generation might fail if both  these conditions are true:? The code generation parameter SubsuystemReuse  is set to 'Atomic and Virtual'  The model contains? reusable subsystems within a For Each subsystem inside of a model reference block  In such situations, HDL code generation might fail with the following error message: Error using getfullname (line 22)  Invalid Simulink object name: path/to/nonexistent/block.  Error in slhdlcoder.SimulinkFrontEnd/setupAndInitModel>computeCheckSumForTheModel

Summary: The ps_cxx_fe process crashes when the -code-metrics option is used with gnu or clang compilers
Description: When you use the -code-metrics  option with the gnu or clang compiler, the ps_cxx_fe  process crashes with an error like this: Error: A segmentation fault occurred in "ps_cxx_fe.exe"  The crash happens if your code uses gcc or clang built-in keywords, such as _exit  or?printf, ?as names for functions that have a body.

Summary: The edit time check does not flag Stateflow Junctions without default transitions.
Description: The edit time check does not flag Stateflow.Junction  object with missing default transitions.

Summary: The installer for the development version of MATLAB Web App Server in MATLAB Compiler cannot connect to the internet to download MATLAB Runtime
Description: On Windows systems, when running the installer for the development version of MATLAB Web App Server in MATLAB Compiler, the installation fails and an error dialog displays the following message: "Call to download server failed (HTTP error code: 12007). Try to rerun the installer."?
Workaround: Follow instructions in the Limited Internet Connectivity Installation section of the?documentation.?

Summary: Unable to select custom storage class when name is a partial match of built-in storage class
Description: When you create a custom storage class by using the Custom Storage Class Designer, you cannot select that storage class in the Simulink.Signal  or Simulink.Parameter  property dialog box when the name of the storage class is a partial match of a built-in storage class. For example, when you select the custom storage class Export  from the drop-down list, the resulting selected storage class is ExportedGlobal .
Workaround: Use the command-line API to set the storage class for Simulink signals and parameters.
Fixed In: R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 5, R2021b, R2022a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results for grt_malloc target and models containing continuous integrators with reset ports
Description: Code generation results may be incorrect for models that contain continuous integrator blocks that have the reset port enabled when the System Target File is set to use 'grt_malloc.tlc .'

Summary: XCP-based external-mode simulation fails when parameters are not found
Description: If certain parameters are not found during the connection phase of an XCP-based external-mode simulation, the simulation fails. For example: Tunable parameters in generated code from non-inlined S-Function blocks. Parameters associated with storage classes that do not support tuning (for example,?GetSet ).   The simulation produces this error message:? ?Unable to find parameter 'parameterName ' of block on path 'blockPath '.
Workaround: Set the?Default parameter behavior? configuration parameter to Inlined, which disables tunability of numeric block parameters in generated code.?You can make specific parameters tunable by assigning the 'ExportedGlobal' storage class to the parameters.

Summary: MATLAB crashes when opening the help browser or searching in it
Description: MATLAB might crash during either of the following actions: Opening the help browser Using the search string feature in the help browser on a machine behind a proxy server
Workaround: Use the following steps to work around this issue:Regenerate your MATLAB preferences using these instructions:https://www.mathworks.com/matlabcentral/answers/99625-how-do-i-regenerate-my-matlab-preferencesRestart MATLAB.Change the documentation location to "Installed locally":Open the MATLAB preferences by selecting Home > Environment > Preferences in the MATLAB Toolstrip.In the left panel, click Help, then select Installed locally under Documentation Location.Restart MATLAB.

Summary: The JMAAB check for jc_0009 flags signals originating from a subsystem linked to a Simulink library
Description: The Model Advisor check Signal name propagation  (mathworks.jmaab.jc_0009 ) flags signals originating from subsystem which is linked to Simulink library.

Summary: Running Simulink Design Verifier advisor checks when an external harness is open might crash MATLAB
Description: If an external harness is open and you run Simulink Design Verifier advisor checks on a model or?sldvextract ?on a subsystem, MATLAB might crash.

Summary: Real-time application crashes when stopped if UDP Send block and UDP Recieve block use the same IP address or port combination
Description: If the UDP Send block and the UDP Recieve block are configured to use the same IP address or port combination and send/receive data simultaneosly, the real-time application crashes when stopped.

Summary: Incorrect HDL code might be generated for a model that contains Blackbox subsystem followed by Complex to Real-Imag block
Description: ?Incorrect HDL code might be generated for a model that contains Blackbox subsystem followed by Complex to Real-Imag block if Output value ?is set to Real  or Imag.
Workaround: Use the Complex to Real-Imag block with Output value set to Real and Imag?and connect the unused one to a terminator.

Summary: JMAAB check for?db_0032?displays an incorrect warning for signal lines crossing over one another
Description: The?Sub-ID a2? of Model Advisor?check mathworks.jmaab.db_0032 (db_0032_a2:?Check if Line crossing style is set to 'Line hop' )?reports incorrect warning when line crossing style is set to Line hop , and also?inappropriately displays warning message of?sub-id a1.

Summary: Size of temporary DMR file that stores logged simulation data increases with each simulation run in rapid accelerator mode.
Description: Logged simulation data is stored in a temporary DMR file. When running simulations in rapid accelerator mode, the DMR file that contains logged data may grow unbounded, even when the Simulation Data Inspector is configured to automatically archive logged simulation data with a limit on the number of runs to retain in the archive.?
Workaround: To manually delete the data in the DMR file that stores logged simulation data, use the Simulink.sdi.clear function.?

Summary: HDL code generation may fail for a referenced model when the port name ends with a space
Description: For a referenced model, If the port name has any whitespace at the end, HDL code generation may fail. For example, having a port name like 'E1 ' results in the following error: For the block 'f1'  ? ?A new block named 'gm_tmp_fail/test_ref_f1/E1 ' cannot be added  Error in slhdlcoder.HDLCoder/reportMessages (line 21)

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Simulation of a MATLAB Function Block that accepts four or more input arguments might produce incorrect answers if at least one argument is of half type
Description: The simulation of a MATLAB Function Block might produce incorrect answers if?all ?these conditions are true: Simulation Mode is set to Normal. Your MATLAB code contains a function that accepts four or more input arguments. At least one of these arguments is passed data of half type.

Summary: dlquantizer?calibrate step will fail on a machine with GPU compute capability (CC) > 8.0
Description: Performing calibration on a  dlquantizer  object fails when the following conditions are met: The host development computer has a CUDA GPU device with a compute capability (CC) greater than 8.0. The ExecutionEnvironment   property of the dlquantizer  object is set to 'GPU'  (default).   Calibration fails with the following error message: dlquantizer workflow failed

Summary: IAR Embedded Workbench (iar-ew)?__section_begin?and?__section_end macros?always return a pointer to address 0
Description: When you run an analysis with option -compiler iar-ew ?(with any iar-ew target) and your code uses compiler macros?__section_begin()  and __section_end() , Polyspace models the macros as holding a null address.  Because the beginning and end of a section have the same address, the section has size 0. This is semantically incorrect and might cause issues, such as incorrectlyflagging some code sections as unused code.
Workaround: Go to? the folder?POLYSPACEROOT/polyspace/verifier/extensions/iar-ew/tmw_builtins/ and edit the file that corresponds to your architecture (arm.h,?avr.h,?msp430.h,?rh850.h,?riscv.h,, or?rl78.h).?POLYSPACEROOT is your Polysace installation folder, for instance?C:\Program Files\Polyspace\R2021a.After you open the file in a text editor, locate these lines:#define __section_begin(x) ((void*)0)#define __section_end(x) ((void*)0)#define __section_size(x) 0U#define __segment_begin(x) ((void*)0)#define __segment_end(x) ((void*)0)#define __segment_size(x) 0U#define __sfb(x) ((void*)0)#define __sfe(x) ((void*)0)#define __sfs(x) 0UAnd replace them with these lines:#define __section_begin(x) ((void*)0)#define __section_end(x) ((void*)100)#define __section_size(x) 100U#define __segment_begin(x) ((void*)0)#define __segment_end(x) ((void*)100)#define __segment_size(x) 100U#define __sfb(x) ((void*)0)#define __sfe(x) ((void*)100)#define __sfs(x) 100U?Note that the problem occurs in several files.
Products: Polyspace Bug Finder, Polyspace Bug Finder Server

Summary: C++ code generated using MATLAB Coder app and using codegen command might contain different declarative syntax for named constants
Description: C++ code generated by using the MATLAB Coder app might contain different declarations for named constants compared to the C++ code generated by invoking the codegen  command at the command line. In such situations, the MATLAB Coder app might prefer the usage of #define  semantics over the usage of?const . ?

Summary: Antenna radiation patterns in Site Viewer have incorrect rotation
Description: When you change the elevation angle of a txsite ?or rxsite  object by using the AntennaAngle  property, the antenna radiation pattern rolls instead of elevates. This bug affects Site Viewer radiation patterns in Antenna Toolbox(TM) and Communications Toolbox(TM).?

Summary: Rerunning sections in the Live Editor which contain subplot or tiledlayout can result in overlapping text and distortion in the figure outputs
Description: When using subplots or a tiled chart layout in the Live Editor, axes titles or labels created in one section can affect figure outputs in other sections after rerunning one of the sections. The result can be overlapping text and other distortions. This result can even occur if there is only one modified code section and that section is run individually (that is, even when not using the Run button on the toolstrip).
Workaround: In cases where you intend to run all the sections, resolve any overlapping text or other distortions by using the Run button on the Live Editor toolstrip rather than executing each section separately.In cases where you are able to clear all of the figures (including those in the Live Editor), avoid the issue by running the following code in the MATLAB Command Window before running sections in the Live Editor:delete(findobjinternal(groot,'type','figure'))

Summary: Simulink customization might fail when starting Simulink
Description: During Simulink startup, you might receive this warning: Warning: The following error occurred while evaluating simulinkrc: An error occurred during Simulink customization.  If you receive this warning, Simulink UI customization might fail and the Simulink Toolstrip might sporadically appear blank.

Summary: Compilation failure for generated Speedgoat interface model when the port is boolean/ufix data type
Description: When using the Simulink Real-time workflow in the HDL Workflow advisor, after running Step 5.1 Generate Simulink-Real Time Interface model  to generate the model, the generated model has a datatype mismatch on the PCIe write side when the port is boolean/ ufix data type causing compilation failure of the generate model:  Error message:? ?Data type mismatch. 'Input Port 1 ' of 'gm_model/DUT/Signal Specification ' expects a signal of data type 'boolean'. However, it is driven by a signal of data type 'uint32'.
Workaround: In the generated model, manually delete the 'dataTypeConversion' block that is connected to boolean/ufix data type port and directly connect that port to the next block that the 'dataTypeConversion' block connects to.

Summary: MATLAB might crash when a root level Inport block outputs a function call with minimum or maximum values set
Description: When a root level Inport block emits a function call with minimum or maximum values set, MATLAB might crash.
Workaround: Follow these steps:Clear the Output function call signal attributeEnter empty brackets for the Minimum and Maximum valuesReselect the Output function call signal attribute

Summary: Simulink blocks with fixed-point input might report incorrect relational boundary coverage results
Description: If your model contains a block with fixed-point inputs, you might get incorrect coverage results. For example, consider a Switch block whose control input signal is fixdt(1,3,0) , with threshold u > 3 ,  and data input signals 1  and 4 , respectively. For this case, the expected result is trigger - threshold = -4 , but the coverage report shows that +LSB  (which is +1 ) is satisfied instead:  trigger - threshold? 33%  ? ? -LSB? 0/  2  ? ? ? 0 ? 0/  2  ? ? +LSB? 2/  2
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 2, R2022a

Summary: MATLAB crashes when calling the?input function with the 's' option and multiple output arguments
Description: Calling the input  function with the 's'  option returns the entered text. For example: str = input(prompt,'s') % Returns entered text  MATLAB crashes if you call the function with this option and request multiple output arguments. For example: [str1, str2] = input(prompt,'s') % Crashes MATLAB because 2 outputs

Summary: The estimateCheckerboardCorners3d function returns incorrect value for pattern detection flag.
Description: The? estimateCheckerboardCorners3d ?function returns incorrect value for pattern detection flag. When you use the pattern detection flag to find the input images used for detecting the checkerboard corners as shown in the code, the results will be incorrect.  [imageCorners3d,boardDimensions,imagesUsed] = estimateCheckerboardCorners3d(imageFileNames,cameraIntrinsic,checkerSize);?  imageFileNames(imagesUsed);?

Summary: Simulink might crash while editing multiple blocks and lines as a group
Description: Editing certain arrangements of blocks and lines as a group might cause Simulink to crash.  For example, dragging a selection box around the blocks and lines and moving them as a group might cause Simulink to crash.

Summary: MATLAB hangs when communicating GPU data on A100 in MIG mode
Description: When you use NVIDIA's multi-instance GPU (MIG) mode to partition an A100 GPU device into multiple GPU instances, MATLAB hangs when GPU data is communicated between workers that are assigned different GPU instances. For example, if you have a pool of two workers that are assigned different GPU instances, then the following code causes MATLAB to hang:?? A = gpuArray(10);?  spmd  ? ? if labindex == 1?  ? ? ? ? labSend(A,2);?  ? ? else?  ? ? ? ? B = labReceive(1);?  ? ? end?  end?
Workaround: To avoid this issue, use gather to retrieve the data from the GPU before communication to other processes. For example, if you have a pool of two workers that are assigned to different GPU instances, use the following code:A = gpuArray(10);?spmd? ? if labindex == 1? ? ? ? tmp = gather(A);?? ? ? ? labSend(tmp,2);?? ? else?? ? ? ? B = labReceive(1);? ? end?end??

Summary: Transpose-multiply operation errors on GPU for sparse real matrices
Description: Transposing and multiplying matrices on the GPU using? x'*y  produces an unexpected error under the following conditions:? x  is a real sparse matrix y  is real dense matrix at least one of x  or y  is a gpuArray  The error occurs?on the GPU device with error code?CUSPARSE_INVALID_VALUE .
Workaround: To avoid the error, use the following code instead:transpose(x)*y

Summary: Simulink Real-Time model build generates error message license check failed
Description: During the model build, Simulink Real-Time checks for the installed QNX Neutrino tools license in the Simulink Real-Time Target Support Package. If the support package is not installed or if an issue during support package install caused an incomplete installation, model builds can fail with the build log error message:license check failed

Summary: cuDNN INT8 code generation may fail when using the Visual Studio 2019 compiler
Description: On a Windows platform, INT8 code generation with the cuDNN library may fail with the following undefined identifier message when using the Visual Studio 2019 compiler: ?MWBfpScaleLayerImpl.cu(21): error #3113: identifier "__roundf" is undefined in device code   ?

Summary: MATLAB might crash when using SIL/PIL Manager in?Automated Verification or Simulation Only mode
Description: MATLAB might crash when using the SIL/PIL Manager in?Automated Verification  or Simulation Only ?mode for a model hierarchy with at least three levels of hierarchy.
Fixed In: R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 4, R2021b, R2022a

Summary: Documentation in the Modbus Explorer app is blank when adding a device
Description: When you add a new device to the MODBUS Explorer app, the documentation in the app is not visible. This occurs when your help preference is set to use documentation from the MathWorks website. A warning in the MATLAB command window begins: Warning: Error executing listener callback for PostSet event on AddDeviceHelpDocumentReady dynamic property... This issue has no impact on the app functionality or workflow.
Workaround: To avoid this issue, set your MATLAB preferences to use locally installed documentation instead of the MathWorks website:Open the Preferences dialog with the command:preferencesClick the Help node.For Documentation Location, select Installed locally. Click OK.Your preferences settings are retained between MATLAB sessions.

Summary: Error when logging AUTOSAR software component Inport or Outport signals during SIL or PIL simulation
Description: You can?test an AUTOSAR software componen t by using a harness model that references the AUTOSAR component model. If you run a simulation with the Model block Simulation mode  set to either?Software-in-the-loop (SIL)  or?Processor-in-the-loop (PIL) ?and you log signals for AUTOSAR??component Inport or Outport blocks where DataAccessMode  is either ExplicitReceive  or ExplicitSend , the simulation produces this error: Unrecognized property 'Owner' for class 'coder. descriptor. AutosarSenderReceiver'.

Summary: DMA Tx does not work in RFSoC IP core generation workflow
Description: The DMA Tx does not work in the RFSoC IP core generation workflow. The RFSoC IP core generation workflow reference design has both DMA Tx and DMA Rx,  but the device tree files do not have mm2s information. So, if you run the software application that has stream transmit,  you get the following error in the diagnostic viewer.  Error occurred while executing External Mode MEX-file 'ext_comm': Failed to connect to the target. Possible reasons for the failure are:      a) The target is not switched on.      b) The target is not connected to your host machine.      c) The application for the model is not running on the target. You might have clicked the Stop button. If the Run button is not dimmed, click it. Otherwise, click the Build button, which downloads and runs your application on the target.     Caused by: An error occurred attempting to open an rtIOStream. More detail may be reported in the MATLAB command window.     Component: Simulink | Category:Block diagram error     Unable to connect to the 'Xilinx Zynq UltraScale+ RFSoC ZCU111 Evaluation Kit' target for <software model>.?      You can confirm the above error scenario by?checking the software application log file (which is located in the /tmp/  folder) on the hardware board. The log file contains the following error message. ? ? ? ? ? "Failed to find device: mwipcore0:mm2s0".?
Workaround: 1) Make sure that the ZCU111 hardware is connected to your system.2) Download and copy the attached devicetree.zip?file into your working folder.3) Unzip the devicetree.zip file.?4) Run the?UpdateSDImage.m?script.

Summary: HDL code generation may fail for Simple Dual Port RAM block when Address port width is set with variable under certain conditions
Description: HDL Code generation for a subsystem containing Simple Dual Port RAM block may fail under the following circumstances: The?Address port width ?is set with variable. The variable is set with same name in the base workspace and masked parameter.  The error message is: Error in 'DualPortRAM_Geck/ReadRAM/Simple Dual Port RAM': Failed to evaluate mask initialization commands. Caused by: Address port width should be between 2 and 29.?
Workaround: Use non-conflicting variable name for 'Address port width' of Simple Dual Port RAM block

Summary: Exported ASAM OpenSCENARIO file shows XML schema validation errors
Description: If you export a driving scenario to ASAM OpenSCENARIO file version 1.0 by using the Driving Scenario Designer  app or the export ?function of the drivingScenario ?object, the exported RouteCatalog  file fails to validate when any actor in the scenario has less than three waypoints.  Exported VehicleCatalog  file shows validation errors about PositionX  and PositionZ  attributes of FrontAxle  and RearAxle  elements. For example: The attribute 'PositionZ ' is not allowed. The attribute 'positionZ ' is required but missing.

Summary: Certain MATLAB Window applications including App Designer, Add-On Explorer, and Simulink Start Page appear blank and are not usable
Description: Certain MATLAB Window applications appear blank when launched. These tools include: App Designer Add-On Explorer Add-On Manager Simulink Start Page UIFigure-based apps  The windows for these applications open and can be closed, but there is no content in the windows.
Workaround: Run these commands before running any other commands in a new MATLAB session:>> wm = matlab.internal.webwindowmanager.instance; >> wm.setStartupOptions('ExternalProcess', '--proxy-server=direct:// --proxy-bypass-list='); These commands must be run every time MATLAB starts. To avoid having to enter the commands manually every time, add them to your startup.m file.

Description: This issue might occur if the existing code generation output folder (for example,?codegen/lib/myFunction ) was generated by using an older version of MATLAB Coder.? In such situations, if you generate code into the existing code generation output folder, MATLAB might crash with a stack trace like:? [? 0] 0x00007f556623172a MATLABROOT/bin/glnxa64/builtins/rtw_core/../../../../bin/glnxa64/libmwdmr.so+01660714  [? 1] 0x00007f554c6a445b MATLABROOT/bin/glnxa64/builtins/sl_graphical_classes/../../../../bin/glnxa64/libmwdmr_datasource.so+00336987 _ZN14dmr_datasource10DataSource10addObjectsERSt13unordered_mapIN7mwboost5uuids4uuidENS0_16DMRNewObjectDescEN2mf4zero8internal10UUIDHasherESt8equal_toIS4_ESaISt4pairIKS4_S5_EEE+00007851  [? 2] 0x00007f554c6a5331 MATLABROOT/bin/glnxa64/builtins/sl_graphical_classes/../../../../bin/glnxa64/libmwdmr_datasource.so+00340785  [? 3] 0x00007f55661624e7 MATLABROOT/bin/glnxa64/builtins/rtw_core/../../../../bin/glnxa64/libmwdmr.so+00812263  [? 4] 0x00007f55661628f1 MATLABROOT/bin/glnxa64/builtins/rtw_core/../../../../bin/glnxa64/libmwdmr.so+00813297  [? 5] 0x00007f554c698c2e MATLABROOT/bin/glnxa64/builtins/sl_graphical_classes/../../../../bin/glnxa64/libmwdmr_datasource.so+00289838 _ZN14dmr_datasource10DataSource20doOpenAndInitialSyncERKNSt7__cxx1112basic_stringIDsSt11char_traitsIDsESaIDsEEEN2mf4zero10datasource10DataSource11ToModelSyncENSC_16ToDataSourceSyncENSC_10SyncTimingE+00001950  [? 6] 0x00007f554c6999be MATLABROOT/bin/glnxa64/builtins/sl_graphical_classes/../../../../bin/glnxa64/libmwdmr_datasource.so+00293310 _ZN14dmr_datasource10DataSource7connectERKNSt7__cxx1112basic_stringIDsSt11char_traitsIDsESaIDsEEEN2mf4zero10datasource10DataSource11ToModelSyncENSC_16ToDataSourceSyncENSC_10SyncTimingE+00000622  [? 7] 0x00007f554c699aba MATLABROOT/bin/glnxa64/builtins/sl_graphical_classes/../../../../bin/glnxa64/libmwdmr_datasource.so+00293562 _ZN14dmr_datasource10DataSource6createERKNSt7__cxx1112basic_stringIDsSt11char_traitsIDsESaIDsEEERKN2mf4zero5ModelENSA_10datasource10DataSource11ToModelSyncENSF_16ToDataSourceSyncENSF_10SyncTimingE+00000138  [? 8] 0x00007f5548751b62 MATLABROOT/bin/glnxa64/builtins/dmr_datasource_mi/../../../../bin/glnxa64/libmwdmr_datasource_mi.so+00109410

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Simulink model containing a Demux block connected to a Stateflow chart might generate incorrect code
Description: Simulink model containing a Demux block connected to a Stateflow chart might generate incorrect code as shown below: RootModel_P.ConstantValue[-127]  This issue is caused due to integer overflow in the matrix index.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 5, R2021b

Summary: The SizeChangedFcn is slower in Figures created by the uifigure function compared to those created by the figure function.
Description: If the SizeChangedFcn ?for a Figure created with the uifigure  function is attached to a long-running callback function, the events build up over time and continue executing long after the Figure is resized. fig = uifigure;fig.AutoResizeChildren = 'off';fig.SizeChangedFcn = @onResized;  function onResized(src,  evt)? ? rand( 10000 ); ? ? fprintf(1,  'Figure width is %f\ n',  src. Position(3)); end

Summary: Compilation error when simulating uavDubinsConnection object as a referenced model using MATLAB function block in Accelerator mode
Description: Simulink returns a compilation error when you run a model containing a uavDubinsConnection  object as a referenced model created using MATLAB function block in Accelerator mode. The error is caused in the linking stage because the parent model generates singlethreaded MEX file and the referenced model generates multithreaded MEX file.

Summary: Polyspace compilation error when using option -osek-multitasking or -autosar-multitasking
Description: If you analyze multitasking programs with Polyspace using the option -osek-multitasking  or -autosar-multitasking , the analysis might be incomplete or might fail. Depending on the implementation of the OSEK or AUTOSAR APIs within your application, you might get a compilation error with a message similar to this: error: identifier "OSEK_task_polyspace_XXXX" is undefined.?
Workaround: Remove the -osek-multitasking or -autosar-multitasking option and?set up your multitasking analysis manually.
Products: Polyspace Code Prover Server, Polyspace Code Prover

Summary: Reference frame name-value argument is not taken into account when adding surface or custom mesh into uavScenario
Description: When you add a mesh of type "surface" or "custom" into a uavScenario  object, the addMesh  method ignores the ReferenceFrame  name-value argument. For example, in this code, the added surface is not rotated according to the MAP reference frame. s = uavScenario;addInertialFrame(s,"ENU","MAP",eul2tform([pi/3 0 0]));[x,y] = meshgrid(0:10,0:10);z = rand(11,11);addMesh(s,"surface",{x,y,z},[0.6 0.6 0.6],"ReferenceFrame","MAP");s.show3D

Summary: First request to a deployed MATLAB?function that uses Python 3.7 or later might hang if the MATLAB Production Server instance was started on a Windows machine using mps-start or mps-restart
Description: The first request to a MATLAB function deployed on a MATLAB Production Server instance hangs if all of the following conditions are true: The MATLAB function uses Python 3.7 or a later version. The server instance was started from the Windows command line using?mps-start ?or?mps-restart .   The first request to a deployed MATLAB?function that uses Python 3.7 does not hang when you start the server using any of the following methods: On Linux and MacOS. As a service on Windows. From the dashboard on Windows or Linux.
Workaround: Open the?script?folder in the?MATLAB Production Server installation folder.Open?mps-start.cmd?in a text editor. Locate the line "%MPS_BIN_ARCH%\mps_start.exe" %*. Update it to?"%MPS_BIN_ARCH%\mps_start.exe" %* < NUL.Open?mps-restart.cmd?in a text editor.?Locate the line?"%MPS_BIN_ARCH%\mps_stop.exe" %* && "%MPS_BIN_ARCH%\mps_start.exe" %*. Update it to?"%MPS_BIN_ARCH%\mps_stop.exe" %* && "%MPS_BIN_ARCH%\mps_start.exe" %* < NUL.

Summary: Code generation might fail when a referenced export-function model contains Simulink functions and has many function-call input ports
Description: When generating code for a model that contains a Model block that references an export-function model with many function-call input ports, the build might fail if the referenced export-function model also contains Simulink functions.
Workaround: Avoid including Simulink functions in the export-function model so that the model exports only functions triggered by root-level?function-call Inport blocks.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: edfwrite?object functions error out when an EDF/EDF+ file is not in the current directory
Description: The addAnnotations ?function throws an error when an EDF/EDF+ file is on the MATLAB path but does not exist in the current directory. The error message is:  Error using datestr (line 131)  Not enough input arguments.  Error in edfwrite/getFileProperties (line 1738)  ? ? ? ? ? ? obj.FileModDate =? string(datestr(fileInfo.datenum));  Error in edfwrite/addAnnotations (line 1337)  ? ? ? ? obj = getFileProperties(obj, [], false);  The  addSignals  function throws the same error.  The?deleteAnnotations ?and deleteSignals ?functions silently create a new EDF/EDF+ file in the current directory.
Workaround: Change the current MATLAB directory to the location where the EDF/EDF+ file is saved.

Summary: False positive violation of AUTOSAR-C++14 A20-8-6 when assigning one const std::shared_ptr to another const std::shared_ptr
Description: Polyspace raises a false positive violation of AUTOSAR-C++14 A20-8-6 when assigning a const std::shared_ptr  to another const std::shared_ptr . For example, class C {? public:??? explicit C(const std::shared_ptr<int> aB) noexcept : b(aB) { } // False positive violation when assigning aB to b.? private:??? const std::shared_ptr<int> b;};

Summary: Error when comparing requirement set file revisions?
Description: When you compare different versions of a requirement set file with highlighting enabled, you might see an error message that says?Unable to locate Requirement Set . This occurs when a version of the requirement set that you are comparing is already opened in the Requirements Editor .
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 1, R2023a

Summary: SimBiology project saved in R2019b cannot be opened in later releases
Description: A SimBiology project saved in R2019b with empty plot matrix plots cannot be opened in later releases.
Workaround: To workaround this bug, re-open the project in R2019b, delete all empty plot matrix plots and re-save.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 4, R2021b, R2022a

Summary: Write firmware to SD card step errors out in HDL Coder Support Package for Xilinx RFSoC Devices
Description: In the hardware setup wizard of the HDL Coder Support Package for Xilinx RFSoC Devices, when running the Write firmware to SD card  step, an error message shows up as: "Cannot find the archive file with name:? <SPKGROOT>\3P.instrset\zynqrfpetalinuxbinaries.instrset", in the download folder, ...zip"  where <SPKGROOT> is the MATLAB installation directory and can be found using the matlabshared.supportpkg.getSupportPackageRoot command in a MATLAB session.?
Workaround: The zip files that the support package is looking for are actually in a folder one level further down. <SPKGROOT>\3P.instrset\zynqrfpetalinuxbinaries.instrset\zynqrf_linuxbinariesAs a workaround,? move the zip files up one level, to under:<SPKGROOT>\3P.instrset\zynqrfpetalinuxbinaries.instrset\?Then run following steps in MATLAB:1. >>rehash toolboxreset2. Close and re-open MATLAB.??

Summary: MATLAB crashes during update diagram of AUTOSAR model if bus element Inport and bus element Outport are directly connected
Description: MATLAB crashes when you perform an update diagram of an AUTOSAR model if the model contains a bus element Inport that is directly connected to a bus element Outport.

Summary: MATLAB crashes when you use models with Variant Subsystems in the Fixed-Point Designer workflow
Description: MATLAB crashes when you set the data type of 'MinMaxOverflowLogging' property to 'MinMaxAndOverflow' using DataTypeWorkflow.Converter on a model with Variant Subsystem.?  An example of reproduction steps is shown below: load_system(modelName);? % load model with Variant Subsystem  converter = DataTypeWorkflow.Converter(modelName); % set up MMO  set_param(modelName, 'MinMaxOverflowLogging', 'MinMaxAndOverflow'); % simulate system  converter.simulateSystem;  ps = DataTypeWorkflow.ProposalSettings(); % remove MMO  converter.proposeDataTypes(converter.CurrentRunName, ps); % propose data types  converter.applyDataTypes(converter.CurrentRunName); % apply data types  converter.simulateSystem();  ?

Summary: DO Qualification Kit test case for Simulink Design Verifier fails to execute when Stateflow is not available
