Description: The DO Qualification Kit test procedure for Simulink Design Verifier?requires Stateflow to execute test case TC_AOB_RPT_101 .?  In this situation, it is expected that the test procedure would exclude the tests that require Stateflow and return results as "EXCLUDED: no Stateflow license " in the qualification report. Instead, the test returns unexpected failure message "EXCLUDED: Simulink Design Verifier fault ".
Workaround: If you don't have a Stateflow license:1. In the trace matrix artifact (qualkitdo_sldv_trace.xlsx), remove the row that correspond to test case TC_AOB_RPT_101.2. Rerun the Simulink Design Verifier test procedure in the DO Qualification Kit.?

Summary: Deleting a single connector that shares a source port with other connectors using the destroy?function removes all the connectors connected to that source port
Description: When using the destroy  function? on a?systemcomposer.arch.Connector  object to remove a connector that shares a source port with other connectors, all connectors sharing that source port are deleted. To share a source port with other connectors, use branching for System Composer connectors.

Summary: MATLAB may freeze when a real-time application is stopped
Description: When a real-time application is stopped, MATLAB may freeze for a long time or crash?when any one of the following conditions is true: The ExportToBaseWorkspace  option is set before starting the Simulink Real-Time application. The external mode simulation is started by using the?Run on Target ?button in the Simulink Editor and the Signal Logging  option is checked in the Data Import/Export section of the Model Configuration Parameters.
Workaround: To avoid freezing or crashing MATLAB when stopping simulation of a real-time application use any of the following:Disable ExportToBaseWorkspace option before you start the real-time application.Disable the Signal logging?option in the Data Import/Export section of the Model Configuration Parameters when running external mode simulation. This disables all logging within the model.For release R2021a and later,?set the Record Mode option within settings of the Simulink Data Inspector to View during simulation only. The data for signals is not available after the application run in the Simulink Data Inspector or Base Workspace when using this Record Mode option.
Fixed In: R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 3, R2021b, R2022a

Summary: Incorrect AUTOSAR?IncludedDataTypeSet?entries exported for Simulink enumerated types with custom storage class Exported Global
Description: Incorrect AUTOSAR IncludedDataTypeSet ?entries are exported for Simulink enumerated types that meet all of the following conditions. The enumerated types are: Configured with custom storage class Exported Global . Internal to the component model. Not present on the software component interface.  For each enumerated type, the exported AUTOSAR data type and generated Rte_Type.h  data type definition incorrectly refer to the data type of a member of the enumerated type.

Summary: Error might occur during simulation that logs data with sample value larger than 21 MB
Description: When you simulate a model that logs data, Simulink might throw the following error if a logged element has sample values larger than 21 MB:? Value max size exceeded for database (string or blob too big) ?  For example, this error might occur if you log a matrix signal with dimensions?2000x2000  and data type double ?because each sample of this signal requires 32 MB.

Summary: Writing a command to an instrument with VISA-USB generates a "Query UNTERMINATED" error
Description: Writing a command to a VISA-USB controlled instrument with the visadev  interface, might result in the instrument displaying a "Query UNTERMINATED"  error. For example:  vd = visadev("MY_DMM");writeline(vd,"CONF:VOLT:DC 10");s = writeread(vd,":SYSTEM:ERROR?")s =    "-420,"Query UNTERMINATED""
Workaround: You can avoid this issue with either of the following options:Use the visa interface instead of the visadev interface.If you prefer to continue using the visadev interface, contact technical support at https://www.mathworks.com/support/contact_us.html.

Summary: PLC Code generation for MultiProg and PCWorx targets may fail for some models
Description: PLC Code generation for MultiProg and PCWorx targets may fail for some models that do not contain any functions of the format y = fcn(y) The following error message would be displayed: The model uses in-place MATLAB Function Block argument of array or struct type. Simulink PLC Coder does not generate code of InOut variables for such models on the selected target. Please either change the model to use scalar type for in-place MATLAB Function Block argument or select a compatible target.

Summary: Expected Output field in sldvData during Test Generation workflow is not populated completely
Description: When you configure Simulink Design Verifier to save expected outputs of the test cases, the saved data does not have values for individual time steps when the model contains a constant block feeding a non-virtual bus. Therefore,? in this case, when you configure Simulink Test to use Simulink Design verifier, the workflow fails with the following similar message:?Index in position 2 exceeds array bounds.

Summary: MATLAB might crash while compiling a model with a signal viewer connected to an inactive variant port in normal mode
Description: Compiling a model in normal mode might cause MATLAB to crash when a signal viewer, such as a Floating Scope block, is connected to an inactive variant port. For example, this crash might occur when, in the Simulink Toolstrip, on the Modeling ?tab, you click Run ?or Update Model .
Workaround: Remove signal viewers that are connected to inactive variant ports. Optionally, replace them with signal viewers in the variant subsystem or model.

Summary: remove method of SimData does not remove data
Description: For most types of queries, the remove  method does not remove any data. Instead, it returns a SimData  object with all the data present in the input object.
Workaround: Use a different SimData method to create the desired SimData object. For example, use the removebyname method to specify which data columns to be removed from SimData. Alternatively, invert the query and use the select method to specify the data to keep.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 3, R2021b

Summary: SimBiology Model Analyzer data might show up in the incorrect axes when plotting multiple data sources with independent scenarios
Description: In the SimBiology Model Analyzer  you can drag and drop multiple data sources onto a time plot. ?If there is no matching or correlation between different scenarios (groups or runs) across the data sources and you set Scenarios  to use the?Grid , Horizontal ,?or Vertical  style in the?Property Editor , some scenarios are plotted in the same axes instead of separate axes.
Workaround: To view the data correctly, set Scenarios?to use the?Color,?Linestyle,?or?Transparency style in the?Property Editor.

Summary: Code-generation fails for Simulink model containing custom ROS messages when deploying to localhost.
Description: Code-generation for Simulink model containing custom ROS messages fails with build errors when Deploy to  option is set to Localhost . An example snippet of the errors thrown on diagnostic viewer is shown below: <snip>?  ?Top model targets built:    Model         Action  Rebuild Reason                                      ========================================================================  <modelname> Failed  Code generation information file does not exist.      0 of 1 models built (0 models already up to date)  Build duration: 0h 0m 10.522s   BuildDependencies{1}  Caused by:  Brace indexing is not supported for variables of this type. ?  </snip>?
Workaround: Deploy the code to a remote device on a Linux Virtual machine or remote robot device. Under?ROS?toolstrip?tab,?in the?CONNECT?section,?select Remote Device option from the Deploy to drop-down.Use Manage Remote Device button in the drop-down?to set the Device address, username, password, ROS install folder and Catkin workspace.

Summary: convertToGUIDECallbackArguments function throws an error when running an app migrated from GUIDE to App Designer
Description: After migrating certain GUIDE apps to App Designer, running the app can result in the following error from within the?convertToGUIDECallbackArguments ?function:  Unrecognized method, property, or field 'Type' for class 'matlab.graphics.shape.internal.ScribePeer'   This error is most often encountered when the App Designer app contains a?UIAxes ? component.

Summary: In parallel model build where model blocks contain Speegoat fixed FPGA blocks, error occurs fl:filesystem:PathNotFound
Description: In a parallel model build where the model includes model blocks that contain Speegoat fixed FPGA blocks, an error occurs?fl:filesystem:PathNotFound .  When Speedgoat FPGA blocks are using a prebuilt FPGA bitstream, the parallel build encounters a problem finding the bitstream at the end of the model build.
Workaround: The model build completes successfully when all of these conditions apply:The parallel build feature is disabled.The slprj?folder for the build is deleted, forcing a complete rebuild.

Summary: Duplicate HDL files are generated for identical atomic subsystems inside two or more different hierarchical paths of conditional networks
Description: Identical atomic subsystems inside two or more different hierarchical paths of conditional networks create duplicate HDL files. This should not happen unless any optimizations are required.

Summary: False positive violation of A15-4-4 with explicitly declared noexcept constructors
Description: Rule A15-4-4 specifies that "A declaration of non-throwing function shall contain noexcept specification."Polyspace raises a false positive violation on?noexcept ?constructors that are defined outside the class. For example:class C {  public:  ? ? C() noexcept(true);  };   C::C() noexcept(true) {} // False positive.

Summary: Target computer crashes when global bus read from Data Store Read block during simulation that is started by using Run on Target button
Description: A target computer crash might occur when both of the following are true: A real-time application is generated from a model that has a Data Store Read block with a global bus.  External mode simulation is started by using the Run on Target  button in the Simulink Editor.
Workaround: Avoid the target computer crash for this model by using the Target object programmatic interface commands to load, run,and stop the real-time application.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code generated when logical variable is initialized using if-elseif-else containing a call to error()
Description: MATLAB Coder might generate incorrect code if your MATLAB code satisfies all  of these conditions: A boolean variable is initialized using an if-elseif-else  tree. Two of the branches of the if-elseif-else  tree contain a single assignment of a literal true  or false  to the boolean variable. The other branch of the if-elseif-else  tree contains an unconditional call to error() . The boolean variable is read later in the program to determine the condition of another if  statement.  When the issue happens, the later if  statement that reads the boolean variable is incorrectly simplified assuming that the variable always takes on the value true  or false . For example, in the following program intermediate  is initialized in an if-elseif-else  tree: function out = issue_2492753(selector)    if selector == 1intermediate = false;    elseif selector == 2intermediate = true;    elseerror('Unknown selector');    end    temp = 100;    if ~intermediatetemp = 1000;    end    out = temp.^2;end When code is generated for the function issue_2492753  by running this command: codegen -config:lib -args 0?issue_2492753  In the generated code for?issue_2492753, the program is incorrectly simplified assuming intermediate always gets assigned the value false : double issue_2492753(double selector){  (void)selector;  return 10000.0;} The correct generated code is: double issue_2492753(double selector){  int a;  a = 100;  if (selector == 1.0) {    a = 1000;  }  return a * a;}
Workaround: Handle the error check and variable initialization in independent if trees. Here is a modification of the example program issue_2492753:function out = issue_2492753(selector)    if selector ~= 1 || selector ~= 2error('Unknown selector');    end    if selector == 1intermediate = false;    elseintermediate = true;    end    temp = 100;    if ~intermediatetemp = 1000;    end    out = temp.^2;endNote that the error check is performed before an if-else statement that initializes?intermediate.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 7, R2021b, R2022a

Summary: Saving model with test harnesses?and immediately closing it might crash MATLAB?
Description: If you save a model that has one or more test harnesses, and then immediately close the model, MATLAB might crash.

Summary: Error exporting IBIS-AMI models with MinGW as default complier.
Description: When exporting IBIS-AMI models from the SerDes IBIS-AMI Manager with MinGW as the default complier, an error containing the following message is generated:?Error Report: Command "mingwSetupFcn();" threw an error: Not enough input arguments. ?

Summary: When model uses data dictionary that contains bus object or boolean type variable, post-build failure occurs
Description: When a model uses a data dictionary that contains bus objects or boolean type variables, a post-build failure occurs and no real-time application is generated.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generated for?ndims?with unformatted dlarray input might produce incorrect answer
Description: Code generated for the ndims ?function might produce incorrect answers if all  these conditions are satisfied:?? The input to the ndims  function is an unformatted dlarray ?object  The data format of this?dlarray ?object?is different from 'SCBTU'  This?dlarray  object has trailing singleton dimensions  This is an example MATLAB code snippet that is affected by this issue: dlX = dlarray(1:12, 'BSC');dlY = stripdims(dlX);nd = ndims(dlY);

Summary: Codegeneration limitations for tcpclient not documented
Description: Propertiesand methods of tcpclient  added in R2020b do not support code generation using MATLAB Coder and code generation with a MATLAB Function block. The following tcpclient  properties and methods do not support code generation: Properties  NumBytesAvailable  NumBytesWritten  ByteOrder  UserData  Terminator  BytesAvailableFcn  BytesAvailableFcnCount  BytesAvailableFcnMode  ErrorOccurredFcn    Methods  readline  readbinblock  writeline  writebinblock  writeread  flush  configureTerminator  configureCallback     Forexample, the following code does not support code generation.   functioncodegentcpclient()    t =tcpclient("127.0.0.1",4000);   t.ByteOrder= "big-endian";   end   >>codegen codegentcpclient   ??? Class'tcpclient' does not have a property with name 'ByteOrder'.   Error in==> codegentcpclient Line: 3 Column: 1   Codegeneration failed: View Error Report   Error usingcodegen (line 223)

Summary: Error about unrecognized field name "map_axi2dut" in SoC Builder with certain blocks
Description: When you run SoC Builder on a processor only model that contains Stream Read, Stream Write, Register Read, or Register Write block, you get this error during the Generate external mode model  or Build software application  step in the Build screen: ? ?"Unrecognized field name "map_axi2dut" in SoC Builder

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated code in Simulink may be incorrect for fixed-point operations when the settings for Signed integer divisionrounds to?do not match between the production hardware and the test hardware
Description: In order to trigger the incorrectgenerated code, all the following must betrue: 'ProdIntDivRoundTo'  is set to'Undefined'  'TargetIntDivRoundTo'  is set to 'Zero'  or'Floor'  'ProdEqTarget'  is set to'off'  'UseDivisionForNetSlopeComputation'  is set to'on'   Additionally, the model must contain oneor more of the following blocks:DotProduct Reciprocal Square Root Magnitude-Angleto Complex  Finally, the model must contain fixed-point types/operations with slopes that are not a power of 2 and/or havenon-zero bias.
Workaround: Ensurethat the settings for 'ProdIntDivRoundTo' and 'TargetIntDivRoundTo' match. Mosttargets will round to 'Zero', so ensuring that both parameters are set to'Zero' will avoid this bug.

Summary: System Extension Blocked message during real-time kernel installation on macOS
Description: When trying to install the real-time kernel on macOS, a dialog titled System Extension Blocked  appears during the installation process. Although the dialog offers to open System Preferences, there is no option to enable the system extension in the System Preferences dialog and the real-time kernel installation cannot continue.

Summary: Demux block with partially dead output ports might produce disconnected cosimulation model and incorrect test bench
Description: If a Simulink design contains Demux blocks with partially dead output ports:  The generated cosimulation model might be disconnected The test bench generated from the design might be incorrect   Dead output ports are those ports that do not contribute to the functional behavior of the design. A Demux block is said to contain partially dead output ports if some of the block's output ports are dead while others are not. This issue does not affect the generated HDL code. It affects only the cosimulation model and the generated test bench.

Summary: Unexpected error during Rapid Accelerator or Real-time simulation of Stateflow charts or MATLAB Function blocks with variable-size data
Description: In models that contain Stateflow charts withvariable-size local data or MATLAB Function blocks with persistentvariable-size data, simulating in Rapid Accelerator or Simulink Real Time modesresult in the following error: "Code generation and simulation targets registered different sets of persistent variables for the block. External or Rapid Accelerator mode simulation requires code generation and simulation targets to register the same set of persistent variables for this block. This discrepancy is typically caused by MATLAB functions that have different code paths for simulation and code generation targets where these code paths define different sets of persistent variables. Please identify these code paths in the offending block and rewrite the MATLAB code so that the set of persistent variables is the same between simulation and code generation."?

Summary: Referenced model build might fail because current model configuration differs from the model configuration used to generate the shared utility code folder
Description: Referenced model build might fail when these conditions apply:? The model reference hierarchy is set up for AUTOSAR. Some models in the hierarchy reference a coder dictionary stored in the model or in a referenced data dictionary.  Code generation incorrectly tries to fetch the shared utility memory section configuration from the coder dictionary. Example of error message: Error building model 'engstdl_detector'. ?The current model configuration differs from the model configuration used to generate the shared utility code folder, ?'E:\ MATLAB_Document\ 2020b\ 02. demo\ slprj\ autosar\ _sharedutils'  Click here to review parameter differences between 'engstdl_detector' and 'E:\ MATLAB_Document\ 2020b\ 02. demo\ slprj\ autosar\ _sharedutils'.Suggested Actions:Configure settings for 'engstdl_detector' so that there are no parameter differences.Remove 'E:\ MATLAB_Document\ 2020b\ 02. demo\ slprj\ autosar\ _sharedutils'.Consider generating code to a different folder structure.  The suggested actions might not resolve the error because the configuration sets may be the same or the models may use configuration references.

Summary: Multistage nonlinear MPC block fails to compile when multiple slack variables are used in the MPC formulation
Description: If an nlmpcMultistage  object has more than one slack variable (for example, nlobj.Stages(i).SlackVariableLength = 2) then when using this object in a Multistage Nonlinear MPC ?block, the Simulink model fails to compile because it wrongly expects a scalar slack variable. The error is as follows:? Inferred size ('[xx]') for data 'e' does not match specified size ('scalar').?

Summary: Error that occurs during real-time application execution disables the UDP send socket
Description: When an error occurs while sending data during real-time application execution, the error disables the UDP send socket. The error description indicates the issue disabling the socket. A disabled socket cannot be re-enabled during application execution.
Workaround: Stop the application. Resolve the issue that disabled the UDP send socket. Re-start the real-time application.

Summary: Generated model after HDL code generation may give incorrect results when Clock-rate pipelining and Sharing optimizations turned on.
Description: Generated model after HDL code generation may give incorrect results when following conditions are met: Model having Clock-rate pipelining   optimization turned on Model having Oversampling    factor value greater than 1 Subsystem is set with SharingFactor ?  value greater than 0 in HDL Block Properties dialog.  The mismatches can be observed in the validation model  by enabling the following option: Configuration parameters => Global Settings => Model Generation => Enable the Validation model   check box  The above issue will be observed for 4-D/5-D Lookup Table block when following configuration is set: Subsystem having the 4-D/5-D Lookup Table   block Interpolation method parameter? set as  Linear point-slope   HDL Block Property  AreaOptimization   set as Serial   under Native Floating Point  tab Model having Oversampling   factor greater than 1?
Workaround: The following command can be run in command window as Workaround:? >>hdlset_param(<modelname>, 'ReduceMatchingDelays', 'off')?

Summary: Specifying an input type for a function as gpuArray?during MEX generation and then calling the generated MEX with non-gpuArray input might cause? a memory leak
Description: This issue occurs if all  these conditions are true:? When generating a MEX, you specify the type of an input x ?of a certain MATLAB function foo  as a?gpuArray  During the code generation process, the code generator optimizes out an argument that appears after x in the signature of ? foo ?  When executing the generated MEX, you pass a normal MATLAB array for the input x , instead of a  gpuArray   In such situations, the generated code converts the normal MATLAB array to a gpuArray ?before passing it to the generated function  foo . This gpuArray  memory might not be freed at the end of the MEX call, causing a memory leak. This issue also occurs when selecting the 'mex'  acceleration option?in the deep learning toolbox functions.
Workaround: In the function signature, place the?gpuArray?arguments before all other arguments.

Summary: Performance regressions in MEX acceleration
Description: Using MEX acceleration during inference with DAGNetwork  objects shows performance regressions.?? The performance regressions are observed when using the option?'Acceleration','mex'  with t he following functions: activations  predict ? classify   When MEX acceleration is enabled, the execution time is significantly higher than when using the option 'Acceleration','auto' . You might experience slowdowns of 20 times or more when compared to using the option 'Acceleration','auto' .

Summary: Web apps running on MATLAB Web App Server do not record or play sound
Description: Web apps running on MATLAB Web App Server that access a client computer microphone or speakers do not record or play sound. Failure to record or play sound is captured in the app session logs.

Summary: MATLAB freezes if?a System Composer component is added to the canvas when both the Property Inspector and Model Data Editor tabs are open
Description: MATLAB will freeze after following these steps:? Open the Property Inspector  tab and the Model Data Editor  tab. Add a System Composer component to the canvas.?

Summary: Unexpected results when the initial value of a signal object is changed when in fast restart mode
Description: When in fast restart mode, if you change the initial value of a signal object that is used as a data store, the new value is not reflected in the simulation result. In this case, Simulink does not report a warning.

Summary: MATLAB Function containing?bitorreduce,?bitandreduce?or?bitxorreduce?might produce HDL code that causes compilation error
Description: The generated HDL code from a Simulink model that contains MATLAB Function blocks or from MATLAB files might cause compilation error when the MATLAB code contains?bitorreduce ,?bitandreduce ?or?bitxorreduce  that is not a standalone expression.
Workaround: Pull out the bit operation and assign it to a temporary variable, and then use that temporary variable at the original location. Please check below example or the attached model.MATLAB code that causes compilation error in generated HDL Coder?function y = fcn()? ? a = fi(0,0,4,0);? ? b = fi(0,0,4,0);? ? y = fi(a + (bitorreduce(bitand(b, fi(8,0,4,0)))),0,4,0);Workaround?function y = fcn()? ? a = fi(0,0,4,0);? ? b = fi(0,0,4,0);? ? c = bitorreduce(bitand(b, fi(8,0,4,0)));? ? y = fi(a + (c),0,4,0);

Summary: Reading from PDF using extractFileText causes MATLAB to crash.
Description: For some PDF files, the extractFileText  function encounters an issue that ends the MATLAB session.

Summary: Editedvalues close to largest representable value of data type result in incorrectoverflow calculation
Description: Theoverflow message might be incorrect in the Signal Editor tabular area when the edited value is close to the maximum representable valuedefined by the data type.

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier does not generate tests for relational boundary objectives when Add Tests for Missing Coverage is clicked in Simulink Test Manager
Description: When Simulink Design Verifier is invoked from Simulink Test Manager by clicking Add Tests for Missing Coverage  button, relational boundary coverage objectives are not recognized for the tests generation.

Summary: JMAAB check for db_0126 displays incorrect warnings for events using send syntax
Description: Model Advisor JMAAB check for db_0126 : Defining Stateflow events  reports Stateflow events are not defined at their smallest scope  for events using the syntax send(event,  state) . This warning message is incorrect as the events cannot be scoped only to the state in which it is used but also to the state sending the events.

Summary: Memory Channel block in Random Access mode loses data during simulation
Description: When simulating the Memory Channel block in Random Access  mode, if the wr_ready ?signal changes from 1 to 0 in the middle of a transaction and the writer writes one more data with the wr_valid  signal high, then this last data is not written into memory and is lost. This behavior occurs when the pipeline is full and the wr_ready  signal goes low due to backpressure. The expected behavior is that a data pushed immediately (next clock cycle) after wr_ready  signal changes to low is not lost.
Workaround: Current workarounds without this fix are:- Do not use pipelined mode and wait for a transaction complete before pushing the next one.- When using pipelined mode, only do transactions that are smaller than the pipeline size.

Summary: Model Advisor check?Check for optimal bus virtuality?does not display warning for subsystem boundaries
Description: Model Advisor check?Check for optimal bus virtuality  (id:?mathworks.design.OptBusVirtuality) ? does not flag virtual bus signal crossing a subsystem boundary.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Embedded Coder might not initialize an exported or imported signal with a constant value
Description: Embedded Coder might not initialize an exported or imported variable that is mapped to a constant (infinite sample time) signal if the model is using bus element ports at the root-level.
Workaround: Avoid using bus element ports at the root-level and replace those with regular Inports, Outports, and Bus Selector blocks.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 1, R2022b

Summary: Coverage simulation with a filter applied is very slow
Description: If you apply a coverage filter to a model using the Coverage filter filename  parameter or CovFilter   command line parameter and then simulate the model, the simulation is slower than normal. This performance regression is especially noticeable for very large models.
Workaround: Avoid using the CovFilter parameter. Instead, apply the coverage filter after analysis. For example, apply the filter to the cvdata object.set_param('MyModel', 'CovFilter', '');?cvd = cvsim('MyModel');?cvd.filter = 'MyFilter.cvf';?This approach is especially useful if you are attempting to collect cumulative coverage aggregated from the results of multiple simulations and only need to see filtering applied to this cumulative coverage result.? Rather than applying filtering (via the?CovFilter parameter) during every simulation, apply filtering only to the final cumulative cvdata object. This significantly reduces the number of coverage filtering operations that need to be performed.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 7, R2021b

Summary: MATLAB does not destroy MATLAB objects on the call stack after a runtime error occurs in models containing MATLAB Function or Stateflow blocks
Description: When a run-time error stops the simulation of a model that contains MATLAB Function or Stateflow blocks that use MATLAB as the action language, MATLAB does not destroy temporary objects on the function call stack.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 4, R2021b

Summary: MATLAB crashes when saving a project using the "Save As" option from any of the MATLAB Compiler apps?on macOS 10.15
Description: MATLAB crashes when saving a project using the "Save As " option from any of the MATLAB Compiler apps?on macOS 10.15. This issue occurs due to a bug in the macOS "Save As " dialog.?? The crash occurs when the following conditions are met: The suggested name in the "Save As "  dialog is not altered. A file matching the suggested name already exists in the default location the "Save As " ? dialog is pointing to.? The option to overwrite the existing file is selected when prompted.?
Workaround: There are two options to workaround the crash:Alter the suggested name for the project file in the "Save As"?dialog when prompted for a name.Update the version of macOS from 10.15 to 11.0.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Model Configuration parameters like 'MaxStep'? are ignored in rapid accelerator mode when they are specified as a fractional values
Description: Following Model configuration parameters are?ignored in rapid accelerator mode when they are specified as a fractional values, eg : 1/5. MaxStep?  MinStep?  InitialStep?  MaxConsecutiveMinStep?  MaxConsecutiveZCs?  RelTol?  AbsTol?  ZcThreshold?   These parameters can be tuned either using 'set_param ' API or can be tuned through GUI via? 'Configuration Parameters/ Solver/ Solver details '? window of the corresponding model.?
Workaround: Specify the affected configuration parameters as decimal numbers (eg 0.2) instead of as a fraction.

Summary: The link computations are incorrect when receiving a signal from directions not aligned with the z-axis of the receiver.
Description: When the z-axis of the receiver is not pointing in the direction from which the signal is received, the link computations are incorrect. In the following code, the z-axis of the receiver (rx) is pointing towards zenith, and not towards the transmitter (tx). The direction from which the receiver receives the signal is not aligned with the z-axis and the computed Eb/N0 results are incorrect. >> startTime = datetime(2021,6,8,15,37,18);  >> stopTime = startTime + days(1);  >> sampleTime = 60;   >> sc = satelliteScenario(startTime, stopTime, sampleTime);  >> sat =? satellite(sc, 10000000, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0);  >> gs = groundStation(sc, 10, 0);  >> tx = transmitter(sat);  >> rx = receiver(gs, "MountingAngles", [0;180;0]);  >> pointAt(sat, gs); ? >> lnk = link(tx, rx);  >> [e, t] = ebno(lnk);

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generated using GPU Coder or MATLAB Coder for deep neural networks containing averagePooling2DLayer with PaddingValue property set to mean?produces incorrect results
Description: Deep learning code generation for averagePooling2DLayer ? supports only zero padding of the input by setting the layer 'PaddingValue'?  property to 0. If you try to pad the input with the mean of the pooling region by setting the 'PaddingValue'  property is to 'mean' , the? ?code generator proceeds with code generation without throwing any errors. This incorrect code might produce different inference results when compared with MATLAB. For example, the code generated for Network 1 produces correct results but that for Network 2 produces incorrect results. Network 1?  ? ? ? layers = [imageInputLayer([30 30 3],"Name","imageinput",'Mean',0)? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? averagePooling2dLayer(5,"Name","pool","Padding",[1 1 1 1],"Stride",[3 3])? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? regressionLayer];  ? ? ? net1 = SeriesNetwork(layers); ?? Network 2  ? ? ? layers = [imageInputLayer([30 30 3],"Name","imageinput",'Mean',0)? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? averagePooling2dLayer(5,"Name","pool","PaddingValue","mean","Padding",[1 1 1 1],"Stride",[3 3])? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? regressionLayer];  ? ? ? net2 = SeriesNetwork(layers);  This issue can also be seen when generating deep learning code using MATLAB Coder.

Summary: Code Generation For Lidar Object Detection Using PointPillars Deep Learning example may throw an error
Description: Code Generation For Lidar Object Detection Using PointPillars Deep Learning example throws an error when generating the mex file. As a result you will not be able to run the?Generate CUDA MEX section of the example. The error message is:  Input dlarray of 'double' type to predict method of dlnetwork is not supported for code generation. Cast inputs to 'single' type before invoking predict.
Workaround: In the CodeGenerationForLidarObjectDetectionUsingPointPillarsExample.mlx file, replace the following code (line 29,30) :pillarFeatures = dlarray(processedPtCloud{1,1},'SSCB');pillarIndices = dlarray(processedPtCloud{1,2},'SSCB');?with this code:?pillarFeatures = dlarray(single(processedPtCloud{1,1}),'SSCB');pillarIndices = dlarray(single(processedPtCloud{1,2}),'SSCB');?

Summary: Code Generation Advisor error when model uses autosar.tlc or?a custom system target file
Description: When using an AUTOSAR model or a model that has a customized system target file that includes target-specific code generation options, the Code Generation Advisor check Check model configuration settings against code generation objectives  displays an error message in the Result  section. The check will not be run. If using an AUTOSAR model, the error is:? Abnormal exit: Parameter 'AutosarCompilerAbstraction' is not defined  If using a customized system target file, the error reports that the first code generation option listed in the TLC file is not defined.

Summary: Code generation for a ROS node containing both ROS messages and variable size parameters throws an error
Description: When trying to generate code for a ROS node containing both ROS messages and custom variable size parameters, code generation using MATLAB Coder fails with the following error in the build log: d:\quickprototype\src\cg\include\cg\mlroscpp_msgconvert_utils.h(35): error C2653: 'coder': is not a class or namespace name  d:\quickprototype\src\cg\include\cg\mlroscpp_msgconvert_utils.h(35): error C2065: 'array': undeclared identifier? d:\quickprototype\src\cg\include\cg\mlroscpp_msgconvert_utils.h(35): error C2065: 'structPtr': undeclared identifier?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Parameters are evaluated incorrectly when starting with double literal followed by more than approximately 4100 whitespace characters followed by additional operations
Description: When setting a block parameter to a value consisting of a double literal followed by more than approximately 4100 characters of whitespace (depending on the length of the double literal) followed by subsequent operations, the block ignores the subsequent operations.? This issue applies to any block numerical parameter.
Workaround: Ensure that the first 4096 characters of a block parameter include code in addition to the double literal and whitespace. ?For example, either of the following produces correct results:>> set_param(gcb, 'Value', ['0 + 1.5' repmat(' ', 1, 5000) '+ 7'])>> set_param(gcb, 'Value', ['1.5 + 0' repmat(' ', 1, 5000) '+ 7'])??
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 3, R2021b

Summary: Code generation error when performing parallel build for model reference hierarchy with 3 or more levels
Description: When you call rtwbuild ?(ctrl+B ) on the top model of a model reference hierarchy, the code generator reports an error when the hierarchy is 3 or more levels deep and the following conditions exist:?A referenced sub-model (Model A ) defines a model argument with a non-Auto  storage class mapping. A parent model (Model B ) directly references Model A  in a Model block, and promotes the model argument again by selecting the Argument  property. Model B  is used as a sub-model in a model reference hierarchy with Model T  as the top model. Model T  is configured to build referenced models in parallel.  In this case, the code generator reports this error: Simulink Coder Error: RT_MODEL record CoderGroup_InstP not found

Summary: Generating coverage report takes very long time
Description: It takes a very long time to generate a coverage report, especially when attempting to generate one report for multiple cvdata  objects, such as in this example: cvhtml('covReport', cvdata1, cvdata2, cvdata3, cvdata4);?
Workaround: To generate a report showing the total cumulative coverage result accumulated from multiple simulations, do not pass multiple cvdata as input arguments to cvhtml command. Instead, sum the cvdata objects from each individual simulation together and then, pass this cumulative result as an? input argument to cvhtml.?cvdataSum = cvdata1 + cvdata2 + cvdata3 + cvdata4;cvhtml('covReport_Cumulative', cvdataSum);?
Products: Requirements Toolbox, Simulink Check, Simulink Coverage
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 3, R2021b

Summary: ARXML ImplementationDataTypes and Rte_Type.h type definitions are not generated for some AUTOSAR lookup tables
Description: Building an AUTOSAR model that contains a lookup table block does not generate the expected ARXML ImplementationDataType  descriptions and Rte_Type.h  type definitions if any of the following conditions exists. In the lookup table block dialog box: Breakpoints specification  is set to Even spacing . Breakpoints specification  is set to Explicit values ?and at least one of the breakpoint parameters is not mapped to an AUTOSAR parameter. Number of table dimensions  is greater than 3.  In the lookup table object dialog box: Breakpoints specification ?is set to Reference? and at least one of the breakpoint parameters is not mapped to an AUTOSAR parameter. Number of table dimensions ?is greater than 3.

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier analysis on a subsystem fails if model parameter SolverPrmCheckMsg is disabled
Description: If SolverPrmCheckMsg  model parameter is disabled then Simulink Design Verifier analysis on any subsystem inside that model fails with the following error message: Changing property 'SolverPrmCheckMsg' is not allowed .
Workaround: This issue can be avoided by using either of the following two workarounds:Ensure that the?SolverPrmCheckMsg model parameter is enabled.Generate subsystem harness using Simulink Test and invoke Simulink Design Verifier analysis on that harness model.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b

Summary: Code generation for smoothdata requires a nanflag option when nonfinite support is disabled
Description: Code generation for smoothdata ?might be ?unsuccessful if both these conditions are true: Nonfinite support is turned off for code generation. Specifically, in a coder.codeConfig  or coder.EmbeddedCodeConfig  object, SupportNonFinite  is set to false . Alternatively, in the MATLAB Coder App, in the project build settings, on the Speed   tab, the Support nonfinite numbers  check box is cleared.? The?nanflag  option is not specified for the smoothdata  function.?  In such situations, if you generate code for smoothdata , you might get the following error:  Error Message:  ??? Expression could not be reduced to a constant.? Error in ==> smoothNumericArray Line: 51 Column: 23?
Workaround: Explicitly specify a nanflag option when generating code for smoothdata with non-finite support turned off.?For example:?smoothdata(data,'includenan');

Summary: MATLAB crashes when opening or loading a System Composer model that references another model
Description: MATLAB crashes when opening or loading a top-level System Composer model, when all of the following are true: The System Composer model has one or more reference components. The ports on the model referenced by one or more reference components have been renamed. All models are saved, closed, and then reopened.

Summary: Error when using matlabFunction on matrices containing large piecewise expression
Description: MATLAB might throw an "Index exceeds the number of array elements" error if you use?matlabFunction  on a matrix containing large piecewise expression. For example, the following code results in the error: A = sum(sym('A',[1,100]));B = sum(sym('B',[1,100]));C = sum(sym('C',[1,100]));syms q1 q2pw1=piecewise(q1 ~= 0, A, B);pw2=piecewise(q2 > 5, A+B, B+B);pw3=piecewise(q1 ~= 0 & q2 > C, A+B+C);pw4=piecewise(q1 < 0 & q2 > 5, 3*C,C);M=[pw1,pw2;pw3,pw4];g = matlabFunction(M, "File", "Mfun", 'Optimize', false)

Summary: Zooming on?timescope?causes signals to disappear
Description: In the timescope  window, when you zoom in on multiple signals with different sample rates, the data for the first signal disappears.

Summary: Non-virtual bus elements cause C++ code generation to fail
Description: If a model contains at least two graphical, non-virtual In Bus Elements or Out Bus Elements blocks, then C++ code generation fails.

Summary: Incorrect handling of linear term cancellation in conditional statements
Description: Inside a conditional statement, linear term cancellation is not performed, which might result in the network either being differentiated more times than necessary or remaining a high-index problem. For example, in the following equation  ? ? ?? x == if u > 0.0, y - y + z else z end;   if linear term cancellation is not performed, the solver concludes that the equation depends on x, y, and z (instead of just x and z). As a result, the solver might draw the wrong conclusion about the index of the problem, and the model might fail to initialize correctly, or the simulation might fail to converge.
Fixed In: R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 5, R2021b, R2022a

Summary: Variant Manager fails to add?Simulink.VariantControl?to a configuration
Description: When you try to have a control variable of?Simulink.VariantControl ?type added to ?a variant configuration in Variant Manager, the below incorrect behaviors are observed:UI: Import control variables operation fails to discover control variables of type Simulink.VariantControl? that are used by variant blocks.    Command line: Adding control variable of type Simulink.VariantControl? using?Simulink.VariantConfigurationData.addControlVariables()  fails with the following error   Invalid vector of control variables.  Caused by:  ? ? Invalid value(s) of field 'Value'.  ? ? ? ? Invalid element at index '1'.  ? ? ? ? ? ? Invalid value of a control variable.   Note:? If the model has variant parameters (Simulink.VariantVariable  objects) visible and using them, then Simulink.VariantManager.findVariantControlVars()  API does not find Simulink.VariantControl  objects that are solely being used by these variant parameters. This is also applicable for the import control variables operation in Variant Manager.

Summary: fimath properties of MATLAB Functions in 'C' Action Language Charts cannot be modified
Description: The fimath  properties for MATLAB functions in C action language charts are disabled and cannot be modified using the Property Inspector or Properties dialog.
Workaround: Specify the fimath properties using the Stateflow API. For example,chartH = find(sfroot(), '-isa', 'Stateflow.Chart', 'Name', 'FIChart');funcH = find(chartH, '-isa','Stateflow.EMFunction', 'Name', 'myFunction');%set MATLAB function fimath to 'Same as MATLAB'funcH.EmlDefaultFimath = 'Same as MATLAB Default';

Summary: The value of 'ChildConfigSet' is invalid error from rerun of parallel simulation or parallel code generation
Description: The following steps might result in an error: For a model reference hierarchy, run a parallel simulation or generate code in parallel.? Make a change to one of the models that alters the model interface without affecting the generated code. For example, change the value of an interface variable. Run the parallel simulation or generate code in parallel again. Run the parallel simulation or generate code?in parallel once more.  ?Step 4 might produce this error: The value of 'ChildConfigSet' is invalid

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results might occur if grounded Function-Call Subsystem with IC specified on subsystem's outport is present inside model reference block
Description: Incorrect code might occur for a model that meets the following conditions: The Function-Call Subsystem is present inside model reference block.? Non-zero initial condition is specified on Function-Call Subsystem's outport.? The outport with initial condition is connected to model reference block's outport.? The Function-Call Subsystem is fed by Ground block.?
Fixed In: R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 3, R2021b

Summary: Boolean constants are not initialized properly in <model>_data.c.
Description: In <model> _data.c , boolean values are initialized using 1 ?or 0 ?instead of using true  or false .

Summary: Results for nested suites cannot be exported under certain conditions on Windows
Description: Results for nested suites cannot be exported on Windows if all of these conditions exist: There are test nested suites. An inner nested suite has iterations. Coverage is enabled.??  This error occurs: Relationship ID already exists

Summary: MATLAB crashes or an error occurs when adding a variant choice to a Reference Component in System Composer
Description: ?An error occurs when you try to add a variant choice to a reference component in System Composer. MATLAB crashes if you try to add a variant choice to the reference component again after seeing the error. ?For example: Create an architecture model in System Composer with root-level architecture ports. Connect those ports to a component named TestComponent . Save the model as ModelA . Create a second architecture model in System Composer and add a component named Component . Right-click the component and select Link to Model . Select the model ModelA . Right-click the reference component named Component ?that references ?ModelA ?and select  Add Variant Choice .  This error occurs: Source component '___TempVariantComponentName___' and Destination Component 'TestComponent' should belong to the same Parent Architecture.  MATLAB crashes if you try again to add a variant choice to the reference component.

Summary: Ground stations cannot be added to satellite scenario after performing certain actions on satellite scenario
Description: Ground stations cannot be added to the satellite scenario after performing any one of the following: Calling any of the following methods: play  accessIntervals?  accessPercentage  accessStatus ?without?time? input? gimbalAngles ?without?time? input? states ?without?time? input? aer ?without?time? input?  Clicking the?play ?button on the satellite scenario viewer   This bug is present in Aerospace Toolbox and Satellite Communications Toolbox. As an example, running the following code results in an error:  >> sc = satelliteScenario;  >> sat = satellite(sc, 1e7, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0);  >> gs = groundStation(sc);  >> viewer = satelliteScenarioViewer(sc);  >> play(sc);  >> gs2 = groundStation(sc, "Latitude", 0, "Longitude", 0);?  Error using horzcat  Number of fields in structure arrays being concatenated do not match. Concatenation of structure arrays requires that these arrays have the same set of  fields.    Error in matlabshared.satellitescenario.internal.Simulator/addGroundStation (line 119)  ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? simObj.GroundStations = [existingGroundStations, gs];    Error in matlabshared.satellitescenario.GroundStation (line 472)  ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? simID = addGroundStation(simulator, lat(idx), ...    Error in satelliteScenario/groundStation (line 290)  gs = matlabshared.satellitescenario.GroundStation(names, latitude, ...
Workaround: Completely set up the scenario first, and then call the listed functions or interact with the?play?button of the satellite scenario viewer only when there are no more ground stations to be added.

Summary: satelliteScenario?play?function returns error when visualizations involve ground tracks of satellites with same name
Description: The?satelliteScenario?play ?function returns an error if you try to visualize ground tracks of different satellite objects that have the same value assigned for the? Name? property. For example, the following code causes an error:  >> sc = satelliteScenario;  >> sat1 = satellite(sc, 10000000,0,0,0,0,0,"Name", "Satellite 1");  >> sat2 = satellite(sc, 20000000,0,0,0,0,0,"Name", "Satellite 1");  >> groundTrack(sat1);  >> groundTrack(sat2);  >> play(sc);  Error using globe.animation.internal.CZMLWriter/addPacket (line 340)  Graphic name already exists. If the name ends with 'PositionReference', it was likely  added by 'addPolyline'. Please provide a different name.    Error in globe.animation.internal.CZMLWriter/addPath (line 98)  addPacket(writer, name, type, packetString);    Error in matlabshared.satellitescenario.GroundTrack/addCZMLGraphic (line 260)  ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? addPath(writer, name, groundTrack, times, 0, gt.TrailTime, ...    Error in matlabshared.satellitescenario.Viewer/writeCZML (line 32)  ? ? ? ? addCZMLGraphic(obj, w, times);    Error in satelliteScenario/play (line 128)  ? ? ? ? writeCZML(currentViewer);  This bug is present in Aerospace Toolbox and Satellite Communications Toolbox.
Workaround: Assign unique?Name?property values to satellite objects?when visualizing their ground tracks simultaneously.

Summary: Error occurs while writing a vector of data as foreground signal generation
Description: When using certain NI devices such as the USB-6356, foreground signal generation errors out while writing a vector of data.? For example, the following code generates an error when writing of a vector of ones: d = daq("ni");ch = addoutput(d, "Dev1", "ao0", "Voltage");write(d, ones(100,1))"Unexpected exception in plug-in: 'Input data can only contain ASCII characters.'"

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results are possible for a model with local block outputs enabled and a Zero-Order Hold block with multiple rates inside a For Each subsystem
Description: Incorrect generated code is possible for a model that meets all of the following conditions: The? Enable local block outputs  model?configuration parameter is selected. A Zero-Order Hold block is inside a For Each subsystem  The Zero-Order Hold block's input and output ports have different sample times. The Treat each discrete rate as a separate task  model?configuration parameter is selected.
Workaround: Insert a Signal Copy block with the Exclude this block from Block Reduction optimization?parameter selected between the Zero-Order Hold block and the For Each subsystem output.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 3, R2021b

Summary: connect: Connection refused error when?using non-standard SSH ports to interact with Git source control
Description: SSH URLs may specify a non-standard port number to connect to, for example,  ssh://git@myserver:2222/myrepo.git.  MATLAB Git integration does not use the specified port number. When using clone, push, pull, or fetch, MATLAB produces a  connect: Connection refused error message .

Summary: Searching for words or sequence patterns in the Sequence Viewer app causes an error
Description: When you attempt to find characteristic words or sequence patterns in the Sequence Viewer  app by selecting Sequence  > Find Word  and entering the word or pattern, clicking the Find  button displays the error message: SEQSHOWWORDS has been removed .

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Rapid accelerator mode simulation might produce incorrect results when Initialize Function block is inside a For Each subsystem
Description: Simulating your model in rapid accelerator mode might produce incorrect results when all of the following conditions are met: The model contains an Initialize Function block inside a For Each subsystem. The For Each block inside the For Each subsystem has the block parameter MultithreadedSim ?set to its default value of?auto .  The model configuration parameter MultithreadedSim   is set to its default value of?auto .

Summary: Cannot tune parameters for UDP Send block or UDP Receive block from block mask in normal mode
Description: During normal mode simulation, when you tune parameters for the UDP Send block or UDP Receive block by using the block mask, the parameter value changes do not take effect. Also, the masks for these blocks cannot be used to tune parameters in a subsystem.

Summary: JMAAB check for jm_0012?displays abnormal exit warning for non-default sub-id selection
Description: Model Advisor?check for?jm_0012  (Usage restrictions of events and broadcasting events ) displays abnormal exit warning  when sub-id a1  is selected. The check also inappropriately displays warnings of sub-id a1  when sub-id a2  is selected, and warnings of sub-id a3  when sub-id a2  is selected.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results might occur for a model with a function-call subsystem that has feedback loop of output going into the input
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets all of the following conditions:?? The output of a function-call subsystem is fed back to the input. The function-call subsystem is executed using a Function-Call Generator block.  In the generated code, one or more temporary variables might be incorrectly optimized out of the for  loop.

Summary: Mix-up of m and km in a Satellite Link Budget Analyzer P.618 propagation model example
Description: Incorrect usage of station height in m  instead of km  (in P.618losses calculation) in the Satellite Link Budget Analyzer  documentation example.The station height to p618PropagationLosses  function must be in km . In theexample, station height in m  is used with the p618PropagationLosses  function.

Summary: Traceability links for the Delay blocks followed by Lookup Table blocks are broken when the?DistributedPipelining setting for the parent subsystem is changed
Description: The traceability links for the Delay blocks placed after the Lookup Table blocks are broken when you apply the DistributedPipelining ?parameter setting on the subsystem at the higher hierarchy.

Summary: Traceability link for the Lookup Table block is broken in HDL Code Generation report when the input to the block is a vector
Description: The traceability link for the Lookup Table block is broken in the HDL Code Generation report for vector input to this block. The traceability link exists only for the first input of the Lookup Table block on providing a vector input.

Summary: makehdl()?for a top model with a Variant Subsystem in sim codegen switching variant control mode can error under certain scenario.
Description: Invoking makehdl()  for a top model with a Variant Subsystem in sim codegen switching  variant control model can error under the following scenario.Top model has a variant subsystem with settings - Variant control mode: sim codegen switching  and Variant activation time: update diagram  Top model has one or more model blocks referencing other models  ?The error thrown is similar to below.? ?### Failure to initialize the model Model.  Error using slhdlcoder.SimulinkConnection/initModel  Cannot connect to model 'Model'; please try Update Diagram (Ctrl-D).  Error in slhdlcoder.HDLCoder/inithdlmake  Error in slhdlcoder.HDLCoder/initMakehdl  Error in slhdlcoder.HDLCoder/makehdl  Error in privmakehdl  Error in makehdl (line 66)  privmakehdl(varargin{:})  Caused by:  Error using slhdlcoder.SimulinkConnection/initModel  The active variant of the block 'Model/Variant Subsystem' was modified by a callback on a?  block other than itself. Such changes to the variant control variable is not permitted.? ?
Workaround: To resolve this issue, one of the following workarounds can be applied:Set the sim codegen switching?of variant subsystem's Variant Activation Time to update diagram analyze all choices (instead of update diagram)Do not use model blocks referencing other models?in the top model

Summary: Stateflow charts inside Simulink based states might execute during actions on entry
Description: Stateflow charts in models using variable step solvers might experience unexpected behavior if the chart contains a Simulink based state, and that Simulink based state models a subsystem with a Stateflow chart in it.? When the Stateflow chart inside the Simulink based state contains both?entry  and during  actions, and during this step the state becomes active,  the during  actions are unexpectedly executed on entry.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect HDL code generation for a subsystem in the presence of Bus signals with fixed-point datatypes configured with 'Slope and Bias'.
Description: For a subsystem that has Bus signals with the fixed-point datatypes, the generated HDL code might be incorrect when you specify the fixed-point datatype with 'Slope and Bias'.?? HDL code generation does not supports fixed-point datatype specification with 'Slope and Bias'. For more information on the fixed-point datatypes support in HDL Coder, open the following documentation link:?? https://www.mathworks.com/help/hdlcoder/ug/supported-data-types-and-matlab-operators-and-control-flow-statements.html
Workaround: Change the specified datatype to 'Word length' and 'Fraction length' format.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 6, R2021b, R2022a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated code results might be incorrect when solver setting Automatically handle rate transition for data transfer is enabled
Description: The generated HDL code for a Simulink model might be incorrect when the following settings are applied: Configuration Parameters > Solver >? Solver details > Tasking and? sample time options > Automatically handle rate transition for data transfer  checkbox is enabled? Model has multiple sample times??  The incorrect HDL code can result in simulation failures in ModelSim when verifying the generated code.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results might occur if reusable subsystem is connected to Conditional Subsystem's outport through Selector block with storage class on it's inport.
Description: Incorrect code might occur for a model that meets the following conditions: Model contains a reusable subsystem. The model configuration parameter Buffer for reusable subsystems  (BufferReusableBoundary ) is selected.? Reusable Subsystem's outport is connected to a Conditional Subsystem's outport through Selector block.? Number of elements present in Selector block's output signal are less than number of elements present on it's input signal.? The input signal to Selector block has storage class with initial value specified on signal object.?
Workaround: Clear the Buffer for reusable subsystems (BufferReusableBoundary) model configuration parameter.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 4, R2021b

Summary: PLC Coder could generate incorrectly typed matrix address expressions
Description: PLC Coder may generate code that has incorrect matrix address expressions when such addresses are not constants. This may lead to errors while compiling the code in the target IDE.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 5, R2021b

Summary: Checkpoint contains a structure instead of a network object when using the train function in parallel
Description: When training a network using the train ? function with?'UseParallel'  set to 'yes'  and 'CheckpointFile'  set to a valid MAT file, the resulting checkpoint struct contains a field  net  which contains a structure  and not a network  object.  For example, when training a network using the command net = train(net,x,t,'CheckpointFile','MyCheckpoint.mat','UseParallel','yes');?  the MAT file MyCheckpoint.mat contains a variable   checkpoint, where   checkpoint.net  is a structure and not a network  object.
Workaround: Convert the structure to a valid network using the network function.validNet = network(checkpoint.net);?

Summary: Running Upgrade Advisor programmatically for external test harnesses might produce an error
Description: The Upgrade Advisor might fail when both of the following conditions are true: The Upgrade advisor is run programmatically: upgradeadvisor('modelname').  The Simulink model is an external test harness.  The error message is: Unable to resolve the name nextReference. name.

Summary: Calling step function?of a persistent instance of?generalizedInverseKinematics?object fails to generate code
Description: Code generation fails when the?step ?function of? a persistent instance of the?generalizedInverseKinematics ?object? is called outside the? if isempty(gik) ?conditional block in the following pattern:  persistent robot gik if isempty(robot)  ? % Create rigidBodyTree, "robot"?  ? robot =  twoJointRigidBodyTree("row");  end  if isempty(gik)   ? % Create a?generalizedInverseKinematics?solver instance "gik"  ? gik = generalizedInverseKinematics('RigidBodyTree', robot);  end  % Obtain a solution configuration "qSol" given "q0" as an initial guess.?  qSol = gik(q0);        end  The following error message is seen:? Cannot allocate this handle object. For code generation, if a persistent variable references a handle object, you can create the handle object only once per program.?

Summary: Incorrect lane boundary information of drivingScenario actors
Description: The laneBoundaries function of a driving.scenario.Actor object returns incorrect curvature values for the left and right lane boundaries.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Simulink might log wrong answer at root output port with automatic unit conversion
Description: Simulink automatic unit conversion might log the wrong answer at a root output port during simulation when all of the following are true: The logging SaveFormat  is set to Dataset .  The signal before the root output port is branched.  The root output port unit does not match the source signal unit and the units are convertible.  Simulink automatically converts units at the root output port.  At least one of the other branches has a different unit from this root output port.
Workaround: Manually insert a Unit Conversion block at the root output port instead of relying on automatic unit conversion.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 3, R2021b

Summary: Recovered Stateflow Charts block inserted when Subsystem Reference block added to test harness
Description: When you add a Subsystem Reference block outside the component under test in a test harness, and that referenced subsystem model contains Stateflow charts or MATLAB Function blocks,?you might see a Recovered Stateflow Charts block inserted when you reopen the harness. If you delete the corrupted charts manually and then simulate the test harness, MATLAB might crash.

Summary: AUTOSAR Dictionary left-hand tree view not updated when interfaces renamed in list view
Description: In the AUTOSAR Dictionary, when using an interfaces list view to interactively rename interfaces, the corresponding names are not updated in the left-hand tree view.

Summary: extractFileText and readPDFFormData fail to read PDF files with non-ASCII characters in the file name or file path
Description: On Windows platforms, extractFileText  and readPDFFormData  fail to read PDF files when the file name or file path includes non-ASCII characters. The function throws an error suggesting that the file is not found. For example, >> str = extractFileText("H:\¤˘¤˘¤˘.pdf");  Error usingtextanalytics.internal.pdfparser.extractFragmentsFile 'H:\¤˘¤˘¤˘.pdf' not found.
Workaround: Rename files and folders such that they contain ASCII characters only.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect simulation result for a model with Variant blocks and Block reduction enabled
Description: When a model is composed of Variant blocks, the inactive part is removed from the block diagram during Normal mode simulation and may enable dead code path elimination to remove more blocks. In Normal mode simulation, the removal could separate a connected block group into multiple unconnected block groups. In the ERT generated code, the inactive part is not removed from the block diagram. This leads to different sorted order than the order in the generated code.

Summary: Exception from mex file when importing or converting ONNX or Caffe models on macOS
Description: An unexpected standard exception can sometimes occur when using functions of the?Deep Learning Toolbox Converter for ONNX Model Format or Deep Learning Toolbox Importer for Caffe Models support package in MACs. The reason is that the support package MEX-file is unable to open its corresponding library, which is located in the folder "<Support Package Root>/bin/maci64". The software returns the following error message for Deep Learning Toolbox Converter for ONNX Model Format. Error using nnet. internal. cnn. onnx. onnxmex  Unexpected Standard exception from MEX file . What() is:Failed to load ONNX Shared library . . . Error in nnet. internal. cnn. onnx. ModelProto/ writeToFile (line 52 )ModelPtr = onnxmex(int32(FuncName. EnewModelProto));Error in nnet. internal. cnn. onnx. exportONNXNetwork (line 38 )writeToFile(modelProto, ?Filename);Error in exportONNXNetwork (line 38 ) nnet. internal. cnn. onnx. exportONNXNetwork(Network, ?filename, ?varargin {:});  You can see this bug when using these functions: importONNXNetwork  importONNXLayers  importONNXFunction  exportONNXNetwork  importCaffeNetwork  importCaffeLayers
Workaround: To work around this problem, contact MathWorks Technical Support. They can provide a MEX file with instructions to use it as a workaround.https://www.mathworks.com/support/contact_us.html

Summary: Training dlnetwork objects requires large memory usage when compared to previous releases.
Description: Training a dlnetwork  object in a custom training loop can require up to twice as much memory when compared to a previous release.

Summary: Plotting many satellites or ground stations in the satellite scenario viewer is very slow
Description: Plotting more than 50 satellites or ground stations with labels visible in the satellite scenario viewer might take over a minute. This time increases as the number of satellites and ground stations increases. This issue exists in Aerospace Toolbox and Satellite Communications Toolbox.
Workaround: To significantly reduce the time to plot graphics, before starting the satellite scenario viewer, hide the labels of satellites and ground stations. For example:sc = satelliteScenario;sat = satellite(sc, 'leoSatelliteConstellation.tle');sat.ShowLabel = false;% Open the viewer after hiding labels as a workaround.viewer = satelliteScenarioViewer(sc);??

Summary: Simulink Web View does not show coverage results
Description: slwebview_cov  does not generate a Simulink Web View that shows coverage information.?The bottom pane that shows coverage results is missing.

Summary: MATLAB coder configuration parameter EnableMemcpy is not respected
Description: To implement copy of vector assignments, the code generator uses a memcpy  call if the number of bytes to copy exceeds the MemcpyThreshold  (bytes) coder configuration parameter and the EnableMemcpy ?configuration parameter is enabled. In rare circumstances, the code generator emits memcpy  even though EnableMemcpy  is disabled.?? Following is the generated code to expect when the EnableMemcpy  is disabled. for (i = 0; i < 15; i++) {  ? ?A[i] = B[i];  }?  Instead, the code generator produces the following:? memcpy(&A[0], &B[0], 15U * (sizeof(int32)));  See also?Bug Report ID 2454826 .
Workaround: Set the MemcpyThreshold (bytes) parameter to a very high value such as inf or intmax('int32'). For example:cfg = coder.config;?cfg.MemcpyThreshold = inf;

Summary: A model that uses EtherCAT blocks and connects to one or more of the thermocouple input devices (for example, EL3028, EL3314, or EL3318) generates an error 'bad format for xml file'.
Description: When the ENI file that configures a model that uses EtherCAT blocks includes one or more of the thermocouple input devices (for example, EL3028, EL3314, or EL3318) in its EtherCAT network, any attempt to use the model generates an error?'bad format for xml file'? due to an unexpected character.

Summary: sldvmakeharness fails for models with Dimension Variants
Description: When the model has dimension variants, analysis completes as expected.?But generating harness fails with the following error:?? Error using prod:  PROD does not work with char inputs.  Error in Sldv.HarnessUtils/getReconstructionParams>getMaxRectLength (line 161):  ? ? ? ? dimension = prod(inportInfo.Dimensions);  Error in Sldv.HarnessUtils/getReconstructionParams>getMaxRectLength (line 158):  ? ? ? ? ? ? [maxRectLength,cumSignalSize] = getMaxRectLength(inportInfo{i},maxRectLength,reconsParams,cumSignalSize);

Summary: Report generation using saved sldvData fails in the subsystem extraction workflow
Description: Simulink Design Verifier errors out while generating a report on loading saved sldvData  in subsystem extraction workflow with the following error message:Unrecognized function or variable 'data'.  Error in Sldv.ReportUtils/prepareAnalysisInfo>addDataConstraintInfo (line 325)  ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? modelname = Sldv.ReportUtils.getAnalyzedModelName(data);  Error in Sldv.ReportUtils/prepareAnalysisInfo>prepareAllConstraints (line 301)  ? ? ? ? constraints = addDataConstraintInfo(data, data.Constraints.Analysis, ...  Error in Sldv.ReportUtils/prepareAnalysisInfo (line 55)  ? ? [dvconstraints, minmaxconstraints]= prepareAllConstraints(data, titles, createLink);

Summary: Memory leak in SUNDIALS solver can slow system performance
Description: When simulating a SimBiology model with the SUNDIALS solver, allocated computer memory is not released. This results in an increased memory consumption that can slow down computations during parameter estimation and repeated simulations.
Workaround: To release allocated memory, close and restart MATLAB.

Summary: MATLAB Git integration is unable to authenticate using pass-phrase protected SSH keys
Description: MATLAB Git integration?fails to authenticate if?all ?of these conditions are true: You attempt to clone, push or fetch a repository with an SSH URL You use a pass-phrase protected SSH keys to authenticate  MATLAB issues an error. An example error when you use a pass-phrase protected SSH key: Unable to clone repository from git@github.com:myrepo.git to C:\Users\MyRepo.   Caused by: Failed to retrieve list of SSH authentication methods: Failed getting response (Error code = -1, Error class = 23)?

Summary: Moving an area by dragging it might result in diagonal line routing
Description: Moving an area by dragging it to a new location might result in diagonal line routing for lines that connect to blocks outside the area.

Summary: Signal Editor UI can appear to have unsaved changes when no edits have been made.
Description: After saving changes in the Signal Editor UI clicking in the signal tree and other areas can result in a the save icon indicating unsaved changes even though no edits have been made.

Summary: Signals connected to a FileLog block are not shown in Test Manager results
Description: When you use the Test Manager to test a model that has signals connected to a FileLog block, those signals do not appear in the test results.  This issue also occurs if you upgrade an earlier model to R2020b or later because signals marked for buffered logging are changed to signals connected to FileLog blocks during the upgrade.
Workaround: Enable logging?for signals connected to FileLog blocks.

Summary: The Classification Learner app produces errors and becomes unresponsive after performing feature selection and training a KNN model
Description: Training KNN models in the Classification Learner app after performing manual feature selection can cause error messages to appear in the Command Window and the app to become unresponsive. The problem occurs when you import a mixed data set with numeric and categorical predictors, select either exclusively numeric or exclusively categorical predictors as part of feature selection, and train a KNN model. The KNN model disappears from the Models pane list, and error messages appear in the Command Window if you try to retrain the model.? The app no longer allows you to create or train models. Clicking the model preset or train buttons creates more error messages in the Command Window. You must close and relaunch the app to continue training models.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results are possible for a model hierarchy in which one model has a single instance of a reusable library subsystem and another model has multiple instances of the same reusable library subsystem
Description: Incorrect generated code might occur for a model hierarchy containing two models that meet all of these conditions:?Model A references a reusable library subsystem once for a given specialization such as dimensions or data type. Model B references the same reusable library subsystem at least twice with the same specialization. The reusable library subsystem contains a block whose output is determined to be constant.  The generated code might have the wrong number of elements in the initialization code for the constant block outputs structure relating to the reusable library subsystems in Model B. The target compiler might not catch this incorrect code and produce an error. This code is an example of the constant block output structure for the reusable library subsystem:  typedef struct {? const int32_T Width;?????????? } ConstB_RLS_5QZ55YRG;  This code is an example of the constant block output structure for Model B:  /* Invariant block signals (default storage) */typedef struct {? ConstB_RLS_5QZ55YRG RLS1;????? ? ConstB_RLS_5QZ55YRG RLS2;????? } ConstB;   This code an example of the incorrect initialization of the model constant block output structure including the reusable library subsystem constant block output structure in Model B: const ConstB rtConstB = {? {??? 4,???????????????????????????? ??? 3????????????????????????????? ? }? ? {??? 4,????????????????????????????? ??? 3?????????????????????????????? ? }}; ?
Workaround: You can either make the reusable library subsystem a nonreusable library subsystem, or in all models that reference the reusable library subsystem, override the reusable library subsystem settings to break reuse with other models.?
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 2, R2021b

Summary: Incorrect data plotted on Simulink Scope block during external mode simulation running on target computer
Description: Incorrect data may be displayed on a Simulink Scope block for a real-time application under these conditions: An external mode simulation is running on the target computer. In the real-time application which is connected to the model for the simulation, multiple signals in the same sample rate are uploading signal data for display on one or more Simulink Scope blocks. At some point during the simulation, incorrect data from one of the multiple signals is displayed on a scope. If viewing the data in the Simulation Data Inspector, the signals display correctly.

Summary: MISRA-C:2012 20.1 False Positive violation after 2 consecutive?#elif
Description: When an #include  follows two consecutive #elif , Polyspace generates a violation on the #include  even if it is  only preceded by preprocessor directives or comments. #define FOO 0?  #if (FOO == 0)? #define BAR#elif (FOO == 1)#elif (FOO == 2)??/ /* this second #elif causes the violation.  */#endif #ifdef BAR#include "file. h" // False Positive violation#endif
Products: Polyspace Bug Finder Server, Polyspace Bug Finder

Summary: MATLAB crashes when reading GeoTIFF files using readgeoraster
Description: MATLAB crashes when these conditions are met: You specify a GeoTIFF file as input to the geotiffinfo ?function or the?Tiff  class.? The GeoTIFF file contains TIFF tags that are not supported by the Tiff  class. You read the GeoTIFF file using the readgeoraster  or georasterinfo  function, ?then call clear all , ?then read a GeoTIFF file using readgeoraster  or georasterinfo .  ?The beginning of the stack trace might be similar to this: [? 0] 0x00007ffa7cd24d0a? C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2020b\bin\win64\tiff.dll+00019722 TIFFVSetField+00002106  [? 1] 0x00007ffa7cd237be? C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2020b\bin\win64\tiff.dll+00014270 TIFFGetField+00000094  [? 2] 0x00007ffa7bc1e119 C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2020b\bin\win64\gdal300.dll+02416921 GDALRegister_GTiff+00056169
Workaround: Replace calls to clear all with calls to clear variables.

Summary: Simulation Mode missing from Simulation Metadata section for imported tests
Description: When you import a set of saved tests results back into Simulink Test, the Simulation Mode might be missing from the Simulation Metadata section of the test case results. This issue might occur for either of these situations: The system under test is a test harness. No signals are logged for the test case, and thus, there are no Simulation Outputs in the test case results.?

Summary: Icons for axes toolbar are missing on deployed versions of?timescope and dsp.ArrayPlot?objects
Description: When you deploy the timescope ?and?dsp.ArrayPlot ?object using MATLAB Compiler, the zoom, pan, auto-scale, and full-display icons are missing. These icons normally appear in the hover toolbar on each axes. On the deployed scopes, buttons appear and are usable, but they are blank.? This issue does not appear during normal operation within a full MATLAB session.
Workaround: There is no workaround at this time.
