The incorrect highlighting does not affect the behavior of the Inport blocks. Highlighting of duplicate In Bus Element blocks is not affected.

Summary: Model configuration parameter Use memcpy for vector assignment is not respected
Description: To implement copy of vector assignments,?the code generator uses a?memcpy ?call if the number of bytes to copy exceeds the?Memcpy threshold (bytes) ?model configuration parameter and the?Use memcpy for vector assignment ?model configuration parameter is enabled. In rare circumstances,?the code generator emits?memcpy ?even though Use memcpy for vector assignment ?option is disabled.?Following is the generated code to expect when the?Use memcpy for vector assignment ?option is disabled. for (i = 0; i < 15; i++) { ? ?A[i] = B[i];  }? Instead, the code generator produces the following:? memcpy(&A[0],?&B[0],?15U * (sizeof(int32))); See also?Bug Report ID?2477020 .
Workaround: Set the?MemcpyThreshold (bytes)?parameter to a very high value such as?inf or intmax('int32'). For example:set_param(modelName, 'MemcpyThreshold', intmax('int32'));
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 3, R2021b

Summary: Mixed-Signal Analyzer app shows incorrect data while plotting waveforms and trend charts. Also, Add Analysis button does not work.
Description: There are three major issues with the Mixed-Signal Analyzer  app :The right-hand side table of the app incorrectly represents the waveforms plotted.  ?The table also needs to work as a legend, ?where each row corresponds to a single waveform. The?x -axis data in the trend chart does not accurately display all the corner field information coming from the dataset.? Add Analysis  button in the toolstrip does not work.

Summary: Atomic subsystem SIL/PIL simulations do not support GetSet storage class
Description: An atomic subsystem software-in-the-loop (SIL) or processor-in-the-loop (PIL) simulation fails if Inport or Outport blocks of the subsystem are mapped to the GetSet  storage class. For Inport blocks, the simulation produces this error: Connectivity:target:CodeInfoInportUnsupportedImplementation   For Outport blocks, the simulation produces this error: Connectivity:target:CodeInfoOutportUnsupportedImplementation
Workaround: Do not use the GetSet storage class at the subsystem interface.

Summary: Exported marking points have incorrect width and length in OpenDRIVE.
Description: The reported width and length of marking <object> entries exported to OpenDRIVE were incorrectly calculated.

Summary: Coverage not recorded for referenced Subsystem simulated using a test harness
Description: Coverage is not recorded for a Subsystem model referenced in a test harness. Even if you enable coverage collection, no coverage results are returned.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 4, R2021b

Summary: Unity displays LOD group warning when importing a scene from RoadRunner
Description: RoadRunner exports nested LODs, which game engines, such as Unity and Unreal, do not support. These scenes do not have LODs correctly applied when imported.

Summary: Analysis Viewer cells might not update after running analysis function if columns have been resized
Description: When you run an analysis function by clicking Analyze  in the Analysis Viewer in System Composer, the cells in the app might not show updated numerical values, especially if the columns in the Analysis Viewer have been resized. The correct value from the most recent analysis displays when you click an individual cell.

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier Compatibility check fails with error changing property '<parameterName>' is not allowed
Description: When Compatibility check or SLDV analysis is run on a model having locked (read only) configuration parameter, the operation fails with error:Changing property '<parameterName>' is not allowed  For example if compatibility check is run on a model having configuration parameter BusObjectLabelMismatch ?set to read only, compatibility check fails with error: Changing property 'BusObjectLabelMismatch' is not allowed
Workaround: Before running Simulink Design Verifier analysis, ensure that the parameter (e.g. BusObjectLabelMismatch,?MultiTaskRateTransMsg) mentioned in the error message is not marked as read-only.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 7, R2021b

Summary: Subsystems containing?Bus Creator, Bus Selector?or Bus Assignment?blocks generates 'Delay balancing unsuccessful' error message
Description: HDL Coder generates the following cryptic error during code generation for Bus Expansion subsystems that contains Bus Creator, Bus Selector, or Bus Assignment? ?Blocks when Delay Balancing  is turned on for 'Multi-Rate' Designs or? single Rate 'Bus Selector/Bus Creator/Bus Assignment  ' Blocks contains unused/unconnected signals. Error Message:? ?For the block '<model_name> '?Delay balancing unsuccessful because Signal rate of value 0. 000000 found. Offending Block:'<path_to_block> '.
Workaround: Consider setting?'BalanceDelays'??to off .

Summary: Explicit MPC object and block may return non-optimal actions at run-time
Description: The explicit MPC algorithm fails to explore all the critical regions at the design stage. Therefore, at run-time, the controller may return incorrect?control actions when operating in unexplored regions.

Summary: power_analyze function might return incorrect value for SourceBranchNames?if the model has only one source block
Description: If you call the power_analyze  function with the 'sort'  input argument inside a model that contains only one block defined as a source, the function might return an incorrect value for the?SourceBranchNames? output field.

Summary: Target computer crashes when external interrupt starts real-time application that includes File Log block
Description: When an external interrupt starts a real-time application that includes a File Log block, the target computer crashes. If the real-time application start is not initiated by an external interrupt (for example, from a Speedgoat IO module), the target computer does not crash.

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier may error out for models containing Stateflow Charts with exported functions that contain reads or writes to global variables
Description: In some cases, Simulink Design Verifier may error out for models containing Stateflow Charts with exported functions that contain reads or writes to global variables  When this error occurs, one of the following error messages will occur: Assert Type checker is unable to infer the type of Node '@'. Please provide the type explicitly.    Exception occurred while parsing cgel file.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 2, R2021b

Summary: Performance regressions in custom training loops with?batchNormalizationLayer?
Description: Custom training loops involving dlnetwork   objects that contain a batchNormalizationLayer   can be significantly slower when compared to past releases.

Summary: Axes in a grid layout appears stretched out during maximize or resize operation
Description: When an axes in a grid layout is resized by a large amount, the axes appears stretched out or badly rendered. This might occur when a figure that contains the grid layout is maximized.
Workaround: To restore the axes to its expected appearance, hover your mouse over the axes.

Summary: Statuses of test objectives reported incorrectly while trying to run Coverage Top off workflow
Description: While trying to run Coverage top off workflow in a model with test objectives, the status in the following conditions are incorrect:Stateflow chart and MATLAB function block If an objective is covered by the given coverage file  or coverage data object , SLDV still runs the analysis on the objective. So instead of Satisfied by coverage data ,? the status will be the status of the last SLDV analysis  Test Objective Block If an objective is covered by the given coverage file  or coverage data object, ? SLDV ignores the objective. The objective status can not be seen as the objective does not exist in the final result.

Summary: FMCOMMS5 Transmitter and FMCOMMS5 Receiver blocks do not apply?center frequency?input port value to channels 3 and 4
Description: In the Communications Toolbox Support Package for Xilinx Zynq-Based Radio, the FMCOMMS5 Transmitter and FMCOMMS5 Receiver blocks do not apply the center frequency value on the?center frequency ?input port?to?channels 3 and 4.
Workaround: To set the center frequency values for all 4 channels, follow these steps.Set the Source of center frequency?block parameter to Dialog.?Set the center frequency value by using the?Center frequency?(Hz) block parameter.

Summary: Unsuccessful update of target computer from R2020b to R2021a if development computer does not have Speedgoat R2021a I/O Blockset for Simulink Real-Time installed
Description: Update of the target computer software from R2020b to R2021a is unsuccessful if the development computer does not have the Speedgoat R2021a I/O Blockset for Simulink Real-Time installed. The target computer update fails to configure the Ethernet port for communication with the development computer.
Workaround: Use any one of these workarounds to regain proper operation of your target computer.Reinstall the target computer files by using the Speedgoat target migration tool.?Log into the target as root by using the monitor and keyboard on the target computer.Install the R2021a version of the Speedgoat I/O Blockset to your R2021a version of MATLAB and Simulink Real-Time.Switch from the status monitor to the system prompt. On the target computer use the keyboard and monitor to switch virtual consoles with <ctrl><alt>2. The status monitor uses console 1. Log in as user root and password root.Run the ifconfig utility to set the IP address on the host-target Ethernet port. The Baseline machine uses wm3 while Performance machines use wm0.? These are the names known to the target computer RTOS. Contact Speedgoat if you are not sure which port to configure. For example, to?configure Ethernet port wm0 to use IP address x.y.z.w on a Performance target computer, at the target computer system prompt, type:ifconfig wm0 x.y.z.w netmask 255.255.255.0After configuring the target computer, in the MATLAB Command Window, type:tg=slrealtime('yourtargetname'); tg.update Reboot the target computer.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: 'BinWidth'?option can cause?histcounts and histcounts2?to omit values
Description: In some circumstances, the bins calculated by histcounts   and histcounts2 ?using the data and the bin widths specified by the 'BinWidth'  option may exclude the largest data values due to roundoff error.? This impacts code executed in MATLAB and code generated by MATLAB Coder. For example: >> histcounts([-8.8 1.2], 'BinWidth', 1.2)  ans =  ? ? ?1? ? ?0? ? ?0? ? ?0? ? ?0? ? ?0? ? ?0? ? ?0? ? ?0??
Workaround: If the largest value has been omitted from the first output of histcounts or histcounts2, the correct result can be produced as follows:>> [~, edges] = histcounts(data, 'BinWidth', binWidth);>> edges(end) = max(data, [], 'all');>> N = histcounts(data, edges);

Summary: Setting ExcitationSource to 'External' does not error on failure
Description: Setting an RTD channel on an NI device to use an external excitation source is not a valid configuration. If you attempt to do this, the excitation source remains set to 'Internal' , without generating an error. For example, using an NI 9219: dq = daq("ni");ch = addinput(dq, "cDAQ1Mod1", "ai0", "RTD");ch.RTDType = "Pt3928";ch.RTDConfiguration = "FourWire";ch.R0 = 100;ch.ExcitationSource = 'External'; % Silently fails; should error.ch.ExcitationSourceans =    'Internal'

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Casts from non-power-of-2 scaled fixed-point types to Boolean with the parameter 'UseDivisionForNetSlopeComputation' set to 'on' may result in incorrect generated code
Description: When the model parameter 'UseDivisionForNetSlopeComputation'  is set to 'on' , conversions from non-power-of-2 scaled fixed-point types to Boolean may result in incorrect generated code. This also impacts accelerator and rapid accelerator modes.?
Workaround: Set the model parameter 'UseDivisionForNetSlopeComputation' to 'off' or?'Use division for reciprocals of integers only'.

Summary: On M1 Macs MATLAB Connector crashes after logging into a MathWorks account
Description: Occasionally the MATLAB Connector crashes after you sign into your MathWorks account from an M1 Mac. This issue occurs when the sign-in window is closed.

Summary: MATLAB crashes when running a large block of code by pasting into the Command Window or using F9 inside the Editor
Description: Pasting a code snippet into the Command Window or running it by pressing F9 in the Editor can crash MATLAB if the code snippet is longer than a few hundred lines and contains several instances of either of the following expressions:  Ellipsis (i.e., '... ').  Statements inside if , for , or while  blocks.  For example, the crash might occur if you paste a snippet containing a for  loop whose body is several hundred lines long.
Workaround: Minimize the number of lines of code ending in '...', or inside if, for or while blocks, by moving these lines into a new function.

Summary: Simulink.BlockAuthoringTemplate.internal.callBlockSet?error when publishing a blockset with Simulink Blockset Designer
Description: If you publish a blockset without any blocks in Simulink Blockset Designer, ?Simulink throws an error similar to: Error using Simulink.BlockAuthoringTemplate.internal.callBlockSet?Failure occurred when applying operations.?Caused by:?Brace indexing is not supported for variables of this type.

Summary: ALIGNMENT_LONG keyword in ASAP2 file should align with ASAM standard and have a value of 4
Description: The C  data type long  is an 8 byte data type which aligns?to 8 bytes, ?but the ASAM standard defines long  as always being a 4 byte data type. As a result, the ALIGNMENT_LONG keyword in the ASAP2 file should have a value 4 and not 8.

Summary: Polyspace products crash in ps_middle_end
Description: Polyspace Bug Finder, Code Prover, or Polyspace As You Code ends with a crash and a message in the log. On Windows the error message is: Error: An unexpected error (9) occurred in "ps_middle_end.exe".   On Linux the error message is: terminate called after throwing an instance of 'std::out_of_range'? what():? basic_string::replace: __pos (which is 18446744073709551615) > this->size() (which is 0)Error: The process "ps_middle_end" received the signal 6.

Summary: The?New Session from Workspace?dialog box in the Classification Learner and Regression Learner apps?does not work as expected when the workspace contains more than 31 variables
Description: When the workspace contains more than 31 variables, the New Session from Workspace  dialog box in the Classification Learner and Regression Learner apps does not behave as intended. Regardless of the Data Set Variable ?selection, the Response  section of the dialog box remains disabled, and the Start Session  button remains grayed out. As a result, you cannot start an app session.
Workaround: To avoid this problem, launch Classification Learner or Regression Learner from the Command Window, and specify the data arguments in the call to classificationLearner or regressionLearner, as shown in this example code.>> load fisheriris>> classificationLearner(meas,species)

Summary: AUTOSAR map block: error message for invalid fixed point word length configuration of two input ports is not clear
Description: The following error message for cross port validation isunclear when generating code for an incorrectly configured Map block:  The word length of the first port must be greater than or equal to the word lengthof the second port. If both have the same word length, a port with an unsignedtype is considered greater than a port with a signed type. In this error message, the description of first and second ports refer to the signal constraints registration and not the physical port index of the block underneath.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results might occur when a reusable subsystem input connects to a Model block input which connects to a Simulink Function block
Description: Incorrect code might occur for a model that meets the following conditions: The model contains a nonvirtual subsystem. The nonvirtual subsystem contains a Model block.? For the nonvirtual subsystem, the?Function packaging? parameter is set to?Reusable function .? One of the inputs to the?nonvirtual subsystem?connects to the Model block's input which connects to a Simulink Function block.? In the model configuration settings of the referenced model, the?Pass fixed-size scalar root inputs by value for code generation? configuration parameter is cleared (that is,? ModelReferencePassRootInputsByReference ?is set to 'off ').
Workaround: For the subsystem containing the referenced model, in the?Subsystem Block Parameters?dialog box, on the?Code Generation?tab, set Function packaging?as?Inline?or?Nonreusable.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 3, R2021b

Summary: Run button might run the wrong file in MATLAB Online after debugging a program file with a .m extension
Description: In MATLAB Online, the Run  button or F5? keyboard shortcut might run the wrong file if both  these conditions are true: There are multiple files open in the Editor. A program file with a .m extension was recently debugged (e.g. stopping at a breakpoint)
Workaround: Select a different document tab in the Editor and then switch back to the desired document. The?Run button then runs the correct file until a program file with a .m extension is debugged once again.

Summary: Invalid ARXML exported when AUTOSAR mapping specifies C type qualifiers for static or constant memory elements typed by data types of category TYPE_REFERENCE
Description: AUTOSAR code generation exports invalid ARXML for a model in which AUTOSAR mapping specifies C type qualifiers for static or constant memory elements typed by data types of category TYPE_REFERENCE. In the ARXML for the affected AUTOSAR implementation data type, the tags ADDITIONAL-NATIVE-TYPE-QUALIFIER and IMPLEMENTATION-DATA-TYPE-REF appear in the wrong order. Re-importing the exported ARXML might generate an error that starts with: Failed to validate AUTOSAR XML file "reproduction.arxml":[ line: 8869, col:111 ]: cvc-complex-type.2.4.a: Invalid content was found starting with element 'IMPLEMENTATION-DATA-TYPE-REF'. ...

Summary: In the SimBiology Model Analyzer, plots might not have finished updating when the busy (waiting) icon disappears
Description: When you make a change in the Property Editor  pane, the SimBiology Model Analyzer  app displays a busy icon while the plot updates. ?This icon might disappear even though the plot has not finished updating, making it difficult to know when the plot has actually finished updating.

Summary: The Task Manager block does not trigger the correct task when a model includes both Interrupt Channel and Memory Channel blocks.
Description: When??a model includes both Interrupt Channel and Memory Channel blocks, and  The Memory Channel event port is connected directly to the?Task Manager The Interrupt Channel output is connected to the Task Manager  The following occurs:The task tied to the event originating in the?Interrupt Channel is never executed. The task tied to the event originating in the?Memory Channel??is executed for both events originating in the Memory Channel?and?Interrupt Channel blocks.
Workaround: When your model includes both Memory Channel and?Interrupt Channel, connect the Memory Channel event port to the Interrupt Channel, and not directly to the?Task Manager block.

Summary: Simulink Code Inspector might fail to verify code generated from a model containing From or Demux blocks
Description: Code inspection fails for a model that meets all of these conditions: The model contains a From or a Demux block. The output signals of the block have the?Signal name must resolve to Simulink signal object ?property selected.
Workaround: Attach the signal object to the source of the signal that is connected to the virtual From or Demux block.

Summary: PIL simulation error when target package with debugger provides connectivity and target hardware byte ordering is Big Endian
Description: If you use the target  package with a debugger to?provide connectivity between Simulink and the target hardware ?and Byte ordering  for the target hardware is Big Endian , running a processor-in-the-loop (PIL) simulation produces an error.

Summary: The MATLAB High-integrity checks need Stateflow license to appear in the Model Advisor
Description: The Model Advisor checks for MATLAB High-integrity (hism.himl_****)  do not appear in the Model Advisor if Stateflow license is missing.

Summary: PLC code generation errors out for some Stateflow models
Description: Models that have Stateflow charts which do not contain any parallel states may error out during PLC code generation as follows: object[id] != nullptrError during converting atomic subcharts to regular subcharts.?

Summary: Traceability matrix does not open from Model Advisor report
Description: For the  mathworks.hism.hisl_0070 ?check, the traceability matrix does not open from the Model Advisor report.

Summary: When using IP Core Register Read block with vector output, the vector length is displayed incorrectly as 1 in the Memory Map tool
Description: When using an IP Core Register Read block with a vector output (the output dimension is greater than 1) the following occurs: the vector length is displayed incorrectly as 1 in the Memory Map tool offset addresses for subsequent registers are not calculated properly In SoC Builder, an error occurs during the "Generate IPCore" step due to the address conflict

Summary: Percentage efficiency at rated output power parameter of?DC-DC Converter block resets to default value
Description: In R2021a, if you open a model saved in a previous release that contains a DC-DC Converter block, the Percentage efficiency at rated output power  parameter resets to the default value of 80%.

Summary: Adding a legend to an axes in a uifigure may cause the AxesToolbar to disappear
Description: When you perform the following actions in order, theAxesToolbar within a uifigure disappears: Create axes in auifigure. Interact with the axes in some way, for example hovering over theaxes to make the AxesToolbar appear.? Add a legend to the axesin the uifigure.? Hover over the axes. The AxesToolbarno longer appears.  If you add a legend to the axes in the uifigure before interacting with the axes, the AxesToolbar will not disappear.

Summary: Take DDS Sample block show receive status parameter is always set to false when using eProsima
Description: When using eProsima, the Take DDS Sample block show receive status  parameter does not correctly indicate if a message has been successfully received. Instead, the parameter always returns  false  regardless of whether or not the message has been received successfully.

Summary: imregtform?can crash MATLAB when using?MattesMutualInformation metric with flat images
Description: The? imregtform ?function can crash MATLAB when using the? MattesMutualInformation ?metric with flat images.? A flat image is one where all pixels have the same value. This behavior is observed for both 2-D and 3-D images when either the fixed image or the moving image is a flat image.  For example, this code can crash MATLAB:  I1 = im2gray(imread('peppers.png'));I2 = 120*ones(size(I1),class(I1));[optimizer,metric] = imregconfig('multimodal');tform = imregtform(I1,I2,'translation',optimizer,metric);

Summary: Simulink Test Observers output incorrect value for self activity in a Stateflow chart
Description: When observing Stateflow state self activity, the data type in of the Observer Port will incorrectly be uint32 and the output values will be incorrect.

Summary: Non-ASCII output from mexPrintf might be truncated on Windows
Description: Calling mexPrintf  with arguments that produce lengthy (>1000 characters) output containing non-ASCII characters might result in a truncated output on Windows.
Workaround: If this issue is encountered, there are several possible workarounds:Call mexPrintf with shorter arguments?Convert arguments to UTF-8 before calling mexPrintf.?

Summary: Cannot create a subsystem reference from ROS Simulink blocks
Description: Cannot create a subsystem reference from ROS Simulink blocks because of errors thrown in block mask dialog.  An example of the error message thrown when creating a subsystem from a ROS Blank message block is shown below:  Error evaluating 'MaskDialog' callback of ROS Blank Message block (mask) 'untitled/ Blank Message'.  Callback string is 'ros. slros. internal. block. MessageBlockMask. dispatch('entityTypeEdit', ?gcb);'

Summary: The play?function results in an error when visualizations involve ground tracks of satellites that have the same name
Description: The play ?function will result in an error if you attempt to visualize ground tracks of different satellite objects that have the same value assigned for the  Name  property. For example, the following code will result in an error:  >> sc = satelliteScenario;  >> sat1 = satellite(sc, 10000000,0,0,0,0,0,"Name", "Satellite 1");  >> sat2 = satellite(sc, 20000000,0,0,0,0,0,"Name", "Satellite 1");  >> groundTrack(sat1);  >> groundTrack(sat2);  >> play(sc);  Error using globe.animation.internal.CZMLWriter/addPacket (line 340)  Graphic name already exists. If the name ends with 'PositionReference', it was likely  added by 'addPolyline'. Please provide a different name.    Error in globe.animation.internal.CZMLWriter/addPath (line 98)  addPacket(writer, name, type, packetString);    Error in matlabshared.satellitescenario.GroundTrack/addCZMLGraphic (line 260)  ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? addPath(writer, name, groundTrack, times, 0, gt.TrailTime, ...    Error in matlabshared.satellitescenario.Viewer/writeCZML (line 32)  ? ? ? ? addCZMLGraphic(obj, w, times);    Error in satelliteScenario/play (line 128)  ? ? ? ? writeCZML(currentViewer);  This bug is present in Aerospace Toolbox and Satellite Communications Toolbox.
Workaround: Assign unique Name property values to satellite objects?when visualizing their ground tracks simultaneously.

Summary: Longer execution time for command setparam(tg)
Description: The setparam(tg)  command may require longer execution time than in R2020a or prior releases.

Summary: Simulink crashes when refreshing a model block that contains a non-existent protected model
Description: Simulink will crash when all of the following conditions are satisfied: There is a hierarchy of three or more models, each included in the previous model in the hierarchy using a Model block. The lowest level model is a protected model that has parameter arguments. The lowest level model is renamed or deleted. The highest level model is refreshed.?  MATLAB crashes with a stack trace that looks like: [? 0] 0x00007ff89ac56696 Z:\matlab\bin\win64\sl_lang_blocks.dll+01009302 GraphRemoveBlockTemplate::useSLM3I+00245702 [? 1] 0x00007ff89b04bdcb Z:\matlab\bin\win64\sl_lang_blocks.dll+05160395 mdlref::GetExternalLibrarySet+00002891 [? 2] 0x00007ff89b0502e2 Z:\matlab\bin\win64\sl_lang_blocks.dll+05178082 mdlref::GetExternalLibrarySet+00020578 [? 3] 0x00007ff89b044d82 Z:\matlab\bin\win64\sl_lang_blocks.dll+05131650 slGetGraphicalInterface+00002434 [? 4] 0x00007ff89b079d71 Z:\matlab\bin\win64\sl_lang_blocks.dll+05348721 mdlref::ModelGraphicalInterface::writeGraphicalInterface+00013153 [? 5] 0x00007ff89b07a264 Z:\matlab\bin\win64\sl_lang_blocks.dll+05349988 mdlref::ModelGraphicalInterfaceFactory::createGraphicalInterface+00000148

Summary: Error during ARXML import with schema notations of style 000xx for Classic AUTOSAR
Description: When importing ARXML for Classic AUTOSAR using schema notations of the modern style AUTOSAR_000xx.xsd, the import workflow shows an error message like the following: Did not recognize schema version 00045,  expected either of 4. 0,  4. 1,  4. 2,  4. 3,  4. 4, R19-11.  Schema version 00045 is equivalent to R4.3.1, which is supported for import.
Workaround: Adjust ARXML files to use the old-style schema notation (i.e. "AUTOSAR_4-y-y.xsd") instead of the modern-style notation (i.e. AUTOSAR_000xx.xsd) before importing into Simulink.

Summary: Name clash between normal MATLAB variables and enums can cause incorrect code generation with Variants
Description: When normal MATLAB variables are used as variant control expression of variant blocks it can lead to incorrect code generation when all the below conditions are met.Another Variant block uses enums as variant controls. and the elements of this enum has name clash with the variant control of the block Model is configured to generate code with ert.tlc and variant blocks have code-compile /generate pre-processor conditionals  selected.?
Workaround: When enums are used in variant expressions, ensure that elements of the enums are not used variant control for other blocks

Summary: show() object?function does not open OSLC resources in specified configuration context
Description: When you create and configure an oslc.Client  object, you can use setConfigurationContext()  to set the configuration context for the client object. If you have a resource object (such as an oslc.rm.Requirement  object) from querying the server or from creating a new resource, you can use show()  to view the resource in the service provider. However, show()  opens the base version of the corresponding resource, not the version that corresponds to the specified project configuration.

Summary: JMAAB check for jc_0651?displays an incorrect warning for root-level Outport blocks
Description: Model Advisor JMAAB check for jc_0651 : Guideline for implementing a type conversion reports operation blocks explicitly specify output data type  for root-level Outport blocks. ?This warning message is incorrect as root-level Outport blocks are considered as operation blocks and explicit data type conversion is expected which is incorrect as strong data typing can be set at Inport and Outport blocks.

Summary: Compiler error for?xil_interface.c in SIL or PIL simulation that logs AUTOSAR signals and states
Description: You can?test an AUTOSAR software component  by using a harness model that references the AUTOSAR component model. If you run a simulation with the Model block?Simulation mode ?set to either?Software-in-the-loop (SIL) ?or?Processor-in-the-loop (PIL) ?and you log??AUTOSAR??component?signals or states that are mapped to?StaticMemory , the simulation produces a compiler error for the file xil_interface.c .

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: AUTOSAR C code does not contain definitions of true and false with Simulink.Variant objects.
Description: The generated C code does not contain definitions of true  and false  when all the following conditions are met:The model uses variants and the variant conditions are defined using Simulink.Variant objects. The Simulink.Variant objects use true/false MATLAB keywords to denote 1/0 respectively. The model is configured for AUTOSAR code generation.

Summary: Reading HTML for extractFileText, extractHTMLText, and htmlTree stops at NUL characters
Description: While reading HTML, embedded NUL characters (char(0) ) are treated as end-of-file or end-of-string, respectively. This stops import of data after the first such character.
Workaround: For HTML code specified as a string, remove char(0) in the input before using htmlTree or extractHTMLText. For example:inp = strrep(inp, char(0), '');out = extractHTMLText(inp);?For HTML files, first read the HTML code into memory as a string, then remove char(0) in the input before using htmlTree or extractHTMLText. For example:inp = fileread(fileName);inp = strrep(inp, char(0), '');out = extractHTMLText(inp);?

Summary: status = logical 0?printed on MATLAB command prompt?with SLDV Test Generation workflow
Description: Additional message? status = logical 0 ?printed on MATLAB command prompt?with SLDV Test Generation workflow.

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier analysis may provide incorrect results when observing a constant signal
Description: When any Observer Port  block is mapped to a constant signal in the design model, Simulink Design Verifier analysis of the design model may provide incorrect results. For example, in Property Proving mode, some Valid  objectives might be reported as Falsified .

Summary: NaN%?reported for excluded code coverage in Simulink Test Manager coverage report
Description: For coverage that is completely excluded by coverage filters, the Test Manager coverage report indicates that coverage is NaN% , instead of showing it as "--."

Summary: Error occurs when compiling a System Composer model after changing the name of an interface
Description: An error occurs when compiling a System Composer model when you update the diagram or run a simulation after changing an interface name. The error message references the previous interface name, which is Interface1  in this example message: Simulink. DataType object 'Interface1' is not in scope from 'interface_ex/ Component/ Bus Element Out1'.

Summary: Incorrect latency reported in Approximate subsystem generated by lookup table optimization in HDL-optimized mode with linear interpolation
Description: The latency reported by the Function Approximation Solution block generated by lookup table optimization using FunctionApproximation.Problem  is incorrect when the following conditions are met:? The HDLOptimized  property of FunctionApproximation.Options  is set to true  The Interpolation  property of FunctionApproximation.Options  is set to Linear ?  For example, p = FunctionApproximation.Problem('tanh');?p.Options.HDLOptimized = true;?s = solve(p);s.approximate?  returns an Approximate subsystem that reports Latency:8, but the subsystem contains 6 Delay blocks.

Summary: uavSensor?not compatible with gpsSensor using geodetic position input format
Description: If you use a?gpsSensor ?object with the geodetic position input format to create a uavSensor  object, the latitude-longitude-altitude output returns wrong outputs, and the following warning is returned on the command line: Warning: The ReferenceLocation property is not relevant in this configuration of the System object.?  In uav.internal.scenario.sensor/GPSAdaptor/setup (line 37)  In uav.internal.scenario.sensor.SensorAdaptor (line 65)  In uav.internal.scenario.Sensor (line 51)  In uavSensor (line 160)??   Here is an example of the code that produces the warning and returns wrong outputs: s = uavScenario("ReferenceLocation", [42,-76,2000]);  p = uavPlatform("UAV",s);  % Using Geodetic position input format in gpsSensor leads to warning from uavSensor? g = uavSensor("GPS",p,gpsSensor("PositionInputFormat","Geodetic"));  % Notice warning in command line.  setup(s);  % The lla output is wrong.  [~,~,lla, vel, gs, course] = read(g)?

Summary: EtherCAT Init block generates error for EtherCAT ENI file that defines multiple cyclic frames
Description: An EtherCAT Init block generates an error when configured to use an EtherCAT ENI file that defines multiple cyclic frames. The error occurs when the block evaluates the ENI file to populate the variable names list for the PDO blocks. Reasons for definition of multiple cyclic frames include: Separate LR and LW frames are selected, instead of selecting a single LRW frame. The number of variable names for the PDO blocks exceeds the maximum allowed frame size. To accommodate the allowed frame size, the configuration program splits the variables into multiple frames.

Summary: Generating Custom Message on MacOS BigSur fails while building custom message package
Description: When executing?ros2genmsg ?for a custom message package, the following build error is encountered on latest versions of MacOS BigSur machines(11.1 and 11.2.1).  No rule to make target '/usr/lib/libz.dylib', needed by 'libros2_msg_matlab.dylib'.?

Summary: AUTOSAR-C++14 A10-03-03 false positive violation on function member marked as "override final"
Description: When you mark function members "override final" or "virtual final" in a class marked "final", Polyspace incorrectly raises a violation of AUTOSAR-C++14 A10-03-03.

Summary: Deleting Stateflow objects highlighted by Model Advisor might cause MATLAB to crash
Description: MATLAB might crash or throw an assertion error if you: Use Model Advisor on a model containing Stateflow chart or charts, and Model Advisor flags objects in those charts. Click a link in the Model Advisor results pointing to a flagged object, and Model Advisor highlights the object. Delete the highlighted object. Navigate away from the Stateflow chart to the Simulink editor. Open the Stateflow chart again.
Workaround: Click on another link from the Model Advisor report to highlight a different object and remove highlight from the previous object. Now you can delete that previous object.

Summary: Large regressions in convolutional network execution on GPU.
Description: Convolution operations on GPU show significant performance regressions.?? All deep learning models that involve convolution are affected, including?  DAGNetwork  models? SeriesNetwork  models? dlnetwork  models? dlarray  convolution operations?   You might see performance regressions of up to 3x. The regressions are more pronounced for smaller inputs, such as when the batch size is small.? The performance regression is due to additional overhead for the convolution operation introduced by the upgrade of the NVIDIA library cuDNN, required for support for Ampere-generation GPUs.

Summary: Polyspace Access server times out frequently
Description: The Polyspace Access server times out after less than 20 seconds and you see this error message in the web interface: The serverresponse timed out.    This issue commonly occurs in these situations: You review projects with many files of findings.  You have a slow network connection. Multiple users are connected at the same time.

Summary: Missing zero crossing types #include in subsystem header
Description: If a subsystem header/source requires zero crossing types such as?ZCSigState , there should be a #include  for the zero crossing header?zero_crossing_types.h ? in order to supply the definition of the types. In certain cases where the subsystem is a reusable library subsystem from which code is generated into the _sharedutils folder and there is an inlined subsystem within that reusable subsystem that needs zero crossing types, the parent subsystem may not include the zero crossing header. This will result in a compilation error in the final generated code similar to: error C2061: syntax error: identifier 'ZCSigState'?

Summary: RL agents train poorly if an observation or action?is an array of 3 or more dimensions
Description: When the observation or the action of an environment is an array of 3 or more dimensions (e.g., image width, height, color), agents incorrectly concatenate and reshape observations or actions during minibatch creation. As a consequence, the agents are not able to learn a good policy in such environments. This bug does not affect training in any environment in which actions and observations are arrays of one or two dimensions (vectors or matrices).
Workaround: Separate a 3D array observation into multiple 2D arrays in your environment. Use multiple input layers for neural networks and concatenate them along the third dimension. You must modify your environment and neural network architecture. For example, if the observation is an 80-by-80-by-3 image, you can then split the observation into three 80-by-80 images (Image1, Image2, and Image3). In this case, you can modify the 3D (80-by-80-by-3) observation into a cell array of 3 observations in step and reset functions if you use a MATLAB environment, as follows: Image1 = Observation(:,:,1);Image2 = Observation(:,:,2);Image3 = Observation(:,:,3);Observation = {Image1, Image2, Image3};Observation information for multiple image inputs can be defined as follows:ObservationInfo(1) = rlNumericSpec([80 80]);ObservationInfo(2) = rlNumericSpec([80 80]);ObservationInfo(3) = rlNumericSpec([80 80]);Then the multiple input layers can be concatenated as follows:lgraph = layerGraph();tempLayers = imageInputLayer([80 80 1],"Name","Image1",'Normalization' ,'none');lgraph = addLayers(lgraph,tempLayers);tempLayers = imageInputLayer([80 80 1],"Name","Image2",'Normalization' ,'none');lgraph = addLayers(lgraph,tempLayers);tempLayers = imageInputLayer([80 80 1],"Name","Image3",'Normalization' ,'none');lgraph = addLayers(lgraph,tempLayers);tempLayers = concatenationLayer(3,3,"Name","concat_1");lgraph = addLayers(lgraph,tempLayers);lgraph = connectLayers(lgraph,"Image1","concat_1/in1");lgraph = connectLayers(lgraph,"Image2","concat_1/in2");lgraph = connectLayers(lgraph,"Image3","concat_1/in3");The rest of the network is the same as the original network.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 1, R2021b

Summary: System Composer models might have extraneous systemcomposer.arch.Component objects that corrupt the model file
Description: Working with components in System Composer might result in the creation of extraneous systemcomposer.arch.Component  objects such that the number of Component  objects associated with the model no longer matches the number of components displayed in the canvas. This issue is more likely to occur for models that include components with links to Simulink Requirements and when using these System Composer functions:? saveAsModel  linkToModel  inlineComponent   To determine whether a System Composer model has this issue and identify extraneous systemcomposer.arch.Component  objects, enter these commands in the MATLAB Command Window, replacing the value for modelName  with the name of the System Composer model: >> modelName = "mySystemComposerModel";  >> scModel = systemcomposer.openModel(modelName)  >> constraint = systemcomposer.query.PropertyValue('SimulinkHandle') == -1;  >> [invalidCompPaths, invalidComps] = find(scModel,constraint,'Recurse',true)   If either return argument for the find ?function is not empty, the model contains extraneous Component ? objects.?? If you encounter this issue,?contact MathWorks technical support .

Summary: Model build time much longer when generating A2L file
Description: The model build time is much longer when generating an A2L file.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect PCIe register initial value for vectors with data type <= 16 bits
Description: When using Simulink Real-Time FPGA I/O workflow from HDL Coder, Simulink model ports mapped to the "PCIe" interface can specify a value for RegisterInitialValue ?using the Interface Options  dialog in the Target Interface table. When specifying an initial value on vector ports whose data type has <= 16 bits, the initial value is incorrectly set in the generated code. Register initial values set on scalar ports or on vector ports with data type > 16-bits are not affected. These initial values are correctly set in the generated code.

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier compatibility check fails with an error Empty character vector corresponding to an enumerated value
Description: When compatibility check is run on an Export Function model having Model Arguments  of type Simulink.Parameter , the check fails with an error?Empty character vector corresponding to enumerated value .
Workaround: Remove Model Arguments from the Export Function model.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b

Summary: If?RatesConvention?property is?'Annualized',?Sharpe ratio is incorrect with?summary?function
Description: If the?RatesConvention ?property of the backtestEngine ?object is specified as 'Annualized' , running the summary ?function with the?backtestEngine ?object produces an incorrect value for the Sharpe ratio for all strategies.

Summary: Unexpected indexing error when evaluating a SimFunction object
Description: Using a?SimFunction  object to simulate a model can fail with an unexpected indexing error of the form:   Index in position 2 exceeds array bounds. Index must not exceed 4. The numbers in the error message might be different. This error is thrown if The simulation fails, and The?SimFunction ?object?has an?Observable ?object as a logged state.  This error might also occur when you use other functionality that relies on a SimFunction  object, such as fitting, global sensitivity analysis, or scanning.
Workaround: To work around this issue, use the following strategies to avoid simulation failures:Avoid extreme parameter values.Turn off absolute tolerance scaling.Adjust solver tolerances.The?Model Debugger for SimBiology?might also help to identify unexpected behavior during simulation.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 1, R2021b

Summary: impzest?produces wrong answers when used with multichannel input
Description: The impzest  function swaps channels when used with a multichannel?recording. For example, the output channels in this example are swapped when the input is stereo. exc = sweeptone(2,100/44100);ir1 = impzest(exc,exc);ir2 = impzest(exc,0.5*exc);stereo = [exc 0.5*exc];ir = impzest(exc,stereo);subplot(2,1,1), stem([ir1 ir2])subplot(2,1,2), stem(ir)  This bug does not affect the Impulse Response Measurer  application because the app records only one channel at a time.
Workaround: Use impzest with single-channel inputs only. Process multichannel signals one channel at a time. (See example in Description of Problem.)
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 1, R2021b

Summary: fwrite does not write UTF-8 character data correctly
Description: Using fwrite  with character or string data results in the wrong bytes written to a file with UTF-8 encoding. For example, this code returns 255 . The correct output is [207;168] . >> fid = fopen('file.txt','w','n','UTF-8');>> fwrite(fid,char(1000));>> fclose(fid);>> fid = fopen('file.txt','r','n','UTF-8');>> bytes = fread(fid,'uint8')>> fclose(fid);bytes =   255% But bytes should equal [207;168]
Workaround: Instead of fwrite(fid,textdata) use fprint(fid,'%s',textdata).For example:>> fid = fopen('file.txt','w','n','UTF-8');>> fprintf(fid,'%s',char(1000));>> fclose(fid);>> fid = fopen('file.txt','r','n','UTF-8');>> bytes = fread(fid,'uint8')>> fclose(fid);bytes =   207   168
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 1, R2021b

Summary: Error when using dlarray mask value as input to crossentropy and huber
Description: You see an error when using dlfeval  with  crossentropy  and huber  with name-value option?'NormalizationFactor', "mask-included"  if the mask value used in name-value option  'Mask', mask , is a dlarray.  For example, create predictions, targets, dlarray mask value, and a function handle to a named function that calls crossentropy , and pass them as input arguments to dlfeval : dlY = dlarray(rand(2), 'CB');?  T = zeros(2); T(1, :) = 1;?  mask = dlarray(ones(2));loss = dlfeval(@lossFunc, dlY, T, mask);function L = lossFunc(X, Y, M) L = crossentropy(X, Y, 'Mask', M, 'NormalizationFactor', "mask-included");  end  When you run the example, you see an error like the following: Check for incorrect argument data type or missing argument in call to function 'recordNary'. Error in deep. internal. recording. RecordingArray/ internal_catcrossentropy (line 10)X = recordNary(X, ?T, ?weights, ?mask, ?1, ?op);Error in deep. internal. recording. operations. CrossentropyOp/ forward (line 38)????????????????X = internal_catcrossentropy(X, ?T, ?weights, ?mask, ?op. NormalizationFactor);Error in deep. internal. recording. RecordingArray/ internal_catcrossentropy (line 10)X = recordNary(X, ?T, ?weights, ?mask, ?1, ?op);Error in dlarray/ crossentropy (line 199)????loss = internal_catcrossentropy(xdata, ?tdata, ?wdata, ?mask, ?normFactor);Error in Untitled>lossFunc (line 11)loss = crossentropy(X, Y,' Mask',M,' NormalizationFactor',"mask-included");Error in deep. internal. dlfeval (line 18)[varargout{1:nargout}] = fun(x{:});Error in dlfeval (line 41) ? ??[varargout{1:nargout}] = deep. internal. dlfeval(fun, varargin{:});?  Error in Untitled (line 8)  loss = dlfeval(@lossFunc,X,Y,M);
Workaround: To avoid this issue, call extractdata on the dlarray mask value.For example, add a call to extractdata after creating the mask: mask = dlarray(ones(2));mask = extractdata(mask);

Summary: Simulink Test Manager degraded performance when test case has many baseline signals
Description: If you have a test case with more than a few hundred signal criteria objects, the performance of the Test Manager might be degraded.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 5, R2021b

Summary: Calling embed with a permuted dlarray can return an incorrect result
Description: When you call the embed ?function with an input dlarray that is formatted and has had its S or U dimensions permuted, the result might not match the dimension permutation of the input. For example:? ? ? X = dlarray(randi(201,[10 20 1 8]), 'SSCB');? ? weights = rand(15,201);? ? % Permute the first two spatial dimensions in X?? ? X = permute(X,[2 1 3 4]);? ? Z = embed(X,weights);??  produces a dlarray Z of size 10x20x15x8 instead of the expected 20x10x15x8.

Summary: Simulations run using the sim and parsim functions take a long time to start
Description: When you run multiple simulations of models that contain a large number (>100) of model references, setup prior to the start of simulation takes a long time if you configure the simulations using an array of Simulink.SimulationInput  objects with the sim  or parsim  functions.

Summary: The CUDA code generated by GPU Coder may fail to compile due to a register count nvlink?error
Description: The NVIDIA compiler (nvcc) may fail in the linking stage due to a mismatch in the maximum number of registers computed at compilation. You may encounter an error message with the following pattern: nvlink error : entry function 'xxx ' with max regcount of n calls function 'yyy ' with regcount of m.  where 'xxx  ' and 'yyy  ' are the mangled function names, n and m are integers, and m is larger than n.?
Workaround: Use the?'-maxrregcount n'?compiler flag of NVCC to specify the maximum amount of registers. Use the compiler flags option in the GPU code configuration parameters to pass compiler flags to NVCC. For example,cfg = coder.gpuConfig;?cfg.GpuConfig.compilerFlags = '-maxrregcount n';where n?is the smallest integer number of register count in the error message thrown by NVLINK.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Using an assignment block with 2-D matrix and vector assignment with for-iterator indexing could result in incorrect code generation
Description: When the subsystem for code generation uses an assignment block with 2-D matrix?as assignment block?Y0 input, vector as U input, and for-iterator block as indexing port input, the generated code and test bench could fail during verification due to computation of incorrect results.

Summary: Generating a code coverage report using cvhtml might cause MATLAB to crash
Description: If you create a coverage report by calling cvhtml  while all of the following conditions are true: You pass more than one cvdata  object to cvhtml .  All of the cvdata  objects contain code coverage results. Products: Requirements Toolbox, Simulink Coverage, Simulink Check

Summary: Polygon label creation in the Labeler apps remain enabled after deleting the Polygon label definition.
Description: The ability to create Polygon labels on an image remains enabled in the Image Labeler ,   Video Labeler , and the GroundTruth Labeler  apps after deleting the Polygon label definition. No data in the session is saved for any of the polygon labels created after the Polygon label is deleted.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Using an assignment block with partial element assignments could result in incorrect code
Description: When the subsystem for code generation uses an assignment block with output elements partially assigned by input data port values, the generated code and test bench could fail during verification due to computation of incorrect results.

Summary: SinCos Cordic blocks do not participate in distributed pipelining if Distributed pipelining priority is set to Performance.
Description: When generating code from a subsystem with?Distributed pipelining priority ?set to Performance , SinCos Cordic block is classified as a blocker because it has multiple rates in the generated optimization report. However, this classification is wrong and the block should participate in Distributed pipelining.
Workaround: If all of the following conditions hold: 1. Distributed Pipelining in `on` 2. Distributed Pipelining priority is set to Performance3. SinCos Cordic prevents delays from being distributed across it4. Only OutputPipeline?is specifed, or only InputPipeline?is specifiedManually move pipelines in the system around sin/cos blocks.?

Summary: Models generated in Simscape to HDL workflow might contain unconnected blocks
Description: If you select the partition solver, the implementation model and the validation model that the Simscape to HDL workflow advisor generates might contain unconnected blocks.

Summary: Receiver Accumulator (2P) outflow from vapor or liquid ports might not be fully vapor or fully liquid, respectively
Description: Fluid leaving ports AV  and BV  should be saturated or superheated vapor, and fluid leaving ports AL  and BL  should be saturated or subcooled liquid. However, when there is condensation or vaporization occurring within the Receiver Accumulator (2P), fluid leaving ports AV  and BV  might be slightly below the vapor saturation line, and fluid leaving ports AL  and BL  might be slightly above the liquid saturation line.

Summary: Code Importer errors during global I/O analysis if CopySourceFiles option is false
Description: When you use the unit testing workflow and set CopySourceFiles  to false , the Code Importer might produce incorrect parsing errors during global Input/Output analysis.

Summary: Several Aero.FixedWing methods return errors when MultiplyStateVariable is off?for coefficient object
Description: When a coefficient object on a fixed-wing aircraft has MultiplyStateVariables   set off  , these?Aero.FixedWing ?methods fail to flatten the coefficient matrix to a column vector and returns an incorrect dimensions for matrix multiplication error:forcesAndMoments  nonlinearDynamics  staticStability  linearize

Summary: Model disconnects from real-time application during external mode simulation
Description: Intermittently, the connected model disconnects from the real-time application during external mode simulation. The unexpected disconnection in external mode occurs due to occasional target connection issues. Observers see the RUN ON TARGET  button transition from Stop execution ?state to Run on Target ?state. This change gives the impression that the real-time application is not running on the target. When this condition occurs, inspecting the state of the target computer confirms that the real-time application is still running.
Workaround: Stop the execution of the real-time application on the target computer by using the stop(tg) command.

Summary: Generating stubs for imported code with multiple source files creates duplicate stub definitions
Description: When you import C code that has multiple source files and use automated stubbing for undefined symbols, the generated auto_stubs.h  file might contain duplicate definitions. If this happens, the following error occurs:? Symbol has already been defined in the current scope .?

Summary: High memory consumption during HDL code generation for large models resulting from Simscape to HDL workflow
Description: Simscape models with large number of switches result in a very high memory consumption during HDL code generation after running Simscape to HDL workflow. The large memory is consumed to perform the various internal operation during HDL code generation.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect answer may be observed with reusable subsystems receiving N-D signals in the row-major mode
Description: Incorrect code may be generated under the following conditions: Model configuration parameter Array layout  is set to Row-major . Multiple subsystems have Reshape block(s) inside and the subsystem block parameter Function packaging  is set to Reusable  or Auto . The subsystems receive different N-D signals that have the same size overall. For example, [4 x 3] ,  [3 x 4] ,  [6 x 2]  have different individual dimensions but the same overall size, which is 12 in this case.
Workaround: Use one of the following:Set model configuration parameter Array layout to Column-major.Set the subsystem block parameter Function packaging to anything other than Reusable or Auto.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 3, R2021b

Summary: Import or export of AUTOSAR architecture models with execution order constraints may fail for branched inputs
Description: Importing or exporting ARXML for an AUTOSAR architecture model that contains execution order constraints information may error if the components in the composition have branched inputs. When this occurs, it throws the following error message: Partition D1 is notmapped to one source block SIDs .

Summary: Deep Network Designer errors when importing a layerGraph extracted from a dlnetwork with recurrent layers.
Description: Deep Network Designer throws an unexpected error when importing a layerGraph  extracted  from a dlnetwork created using   lstmLayer , bilstmLayer,   or gruLayer . For example, extract a layer graph containing an LSTM layer from a dlnetwork  object. layers = [  ??? sequenceInputLayer(1)  ? ? lstmLayer(16,"OutputMode","sequence")  ? ? fullyConnectedLayer(1)];  net = dlnetwork(layers);  lgraph = layerGraph(net);  Importing the layer graph into Deep Network Designer throws an error:  deepNetworkDesigner(lgraph)  Error using deepNetworkDesigner (line 30)  Expected input to be one of these types:    single, double    Instead its type was dlarray.?

Summary: Custom code functions from multiple libraries not all imported into Simulink
Description: If you specify more than one custom code library in Additional build information ?in the Simulation target ?pane of the model configuration parameters, Simulink might not import custom code functions from libraries other than the first one specified.? If so, in the dialog of C Caller blocks the Function name ?parameter?lists only the functions defined in the first library, and functions defined in the other libraries cannot be called from C Caller blocks, C Function blocks, or Stateflow diagrams.
Workaround: Go to the model configuration parameters, Simulation Target?pane, and set?Undefined function handling?to Do not detect.?

Summary: Running implementation model produced during HDL code generation from Simscape model might produce an assertion
Description: While generating HDL code from a Simscape model, the Simscape HDL Workflow Advisor generates an implementation model. Running the generated implementation model might produce an "Assertion detected" error. The error is thrown by a Bitwise Operator block inside a "Mode Vector to Index" subsystem in the implementation model.

Summary: Simulink Code Inspector reports an Abnormal exit: Assertion failed error when calling MATLAB eps function with an argument
Description: When running the compatibility checker on a model with MATLAB function block that calls the built-in?eps ?function with an argument, the check "Check Usage of Code in MATLAB Functions" fails with the error message "Abnormal exit:?Assertion failed ". This is because the function eps ?used in the MATLAB function block has an argument, which is not supported by Simulink Code Inspector.

Summary: Masked subsystems containing logic that folds into to a constant value during HDL code generation causes code generation assertion
Description: MATLAB might crash during HDL code generation if both these conditions are true:?? Your model contains a masked subsystem This subsystem contains logic that might get reduced to a constant value during HDL code generation?  The stack trace for such a crash might look like this: [??0] 0x00007f946782e99f void pir::SetCompositeBlocksTagsImpl::propagateMantMul<pir::GainComp>(pir::GainComp&) at / matlab/ src/ cgir_hdl/ pir_transforms/ SetCompositeBlocksTags. cpp:140[??1] 0x00007f946782e10d pir::NetworkCompVisitor::run(pir::Network*, ?pir::CompIterator::TrType) at / matlab/ src/ cgir_hdl/ export/ include/ cgir_hdl/ dom_pir_core/ iterator. hpp:483[??2] 0x00007f946782e382 pir::SetCompositeBlocksTags::run(pir::GlobalCtx*) at / matlab/ src/ cgir_hdl/ pir_transforms/ SetCompositeBlocksTags. cpp:244 [??3] 0x00007f946733717f metadatafactory::udd::UDDMetaDataFactory_MethodInfo::invokeMethod(UDMethodSignature*, ?int*, ?void**, ?int, ?void**) at / matlab/ src/ cgir_hdl/ . . / . . / derived/ glnxa64/ src/ include/ pir_core/ udd_factory/ private/ . . / . . / . . / . . / . . / . . / . . / src/ pir_core/ export/ include/ pir_core/ udd_factory/ private/ UDDMethodCreatorPrivate. hpp:49   ?
Workaround: Inside masked subsystems, replace the logic that can be reduced to constants by equivalent constant blocks.?

Summary: Simscape HDL Workflow Advisor fails to generate the implementation model when the Simscape model contains model references
Description: Simscape HDL Workflow Advisor fails at the 'Generate implementation model' step of the workflow when the original Simscape model contains model references. An error message similar to the following error is encountered when running the workflow steps individually on a Simscape model containing model reference: Model 'sscModel' directly, or indirectly via referenced models, references model 'refModel'. The simulation status for the referenced model 'refModel' is invalid. When updating, simulating or generating code for a model containing Model blocks, none of the referenced models can be in compiled phase. Please stop compilation or simulation of the referenced model.
Workaround: Running all the workflow steps together prevents this error from occuring.

Summary: Performance estimation hangs when convolution input/output memory size is set equal or less than theinput image size
Description: For a network with an image input size of NxNxM,? >> imageInputSize = NxNxM?   if the input and output memory size in processor configuration is set to AxAxB such that N is greater than or equal to A,  >> hPC.setModuleProperty('conv', 'InputMemorySize', [A A B]);?   performance estimation goes into an infinite loop.  >> hW.estimate('Performance') % R2020b  or? >> hPC.estimatePerformance(net) %R2021a and later
Workaround: Set the input and output memory size in processor configuration greater than input image size.For a network with an image input size of NxNxM,>> imageInputSize = NxNxM %Example [12 12 7]Set the input and output memory size in processor configuration to AxAxB such that N (for example, 12) is greater than or equal to A (for example, 13),>> hPC.setModuleProperty('conv', 'InputMemorySize', [A A B]); %Example [13 13 256]?>> hPC.setModuleProperty('conv', 'OutputMemorySize', [A A B]); %Example [13 13 256]

Summary: False positive indication of updated referenced requirements when updating from some ReqIF files
Description: You can author requirements in third-party tools that use the Requirements Interchange Format (ReqIF). You can then export the requirements to a ReqIF file and import the requirements to Simulink Requirements.  If, in the Importing Requirements  dialog, you select Allow updates from external source , the requirements are imported as referenced requirements (slreq.Reference ?objects) and the source of truth for the requirement set remains in the third-party tool. If you make a change in the third-party tool, you can update the referenced requirements to reflect the change. ?You can view the updates to the referenced requirements by opening the Requirements Editor , selecting the Import node for the requirement set and, in the Details ?pane, displaying the Comments ?section. You can also view the revision history for a referenced requirement in the Details ?pane under Properties . However, when you make changes in the third-party tool and export the requirements (represented as SpecObjects ?in ReqIF) ?to a ReqIF file, some third-party tools update the lastChange ?attribute for all  SpecObjects ?even if some  SpecObjects ?were not changed. As a result, when you update the referenced requirements in Simulink Requirements, the Requirements Editor  incorrectly indicates that there were changes to all of the referenced requirements.

Summary: SimBiology Model Analyzer might display incorrect data in time plots using response sets
Description: When using response sets in time plots created in the SimBiology Model Analyzer  app, the wrong data might be displayed in the following circumstances: Only one response (state) is shown if multiple responses with the same name from different data sources (program results or imported datasets) are included in the same response set. Axes labels and data might not match if response sets are being used, and responses are shown using the "grid", "horizontal", or "vertical" style.

Summary: CAN and CAN FD blocks error out with custom defined Simulink bus types
Description: The functions canMessageBusType  and canFDMessageBusType  create standard versions of CAN_MESSAGE_BUS  and CAN_FD_MESSAGE_BUS . If you modify or create a custom version of a bus, it might generate an error when used with the following blocks: CAN Pack CAN Receive  CAN Replay  CAN FD Log  CAN FD Pack CAN FD Receive  CAN FD Replay   The resulting error message looks like this:   "Invalid Simulink Bus object CAN_MESSAGE_BUS. Use canMessageBusType function to generate a valid Simulink Bus object." You might encounter this error when modifying the bus definition to support AUTOSAR code generation with the CAN Pack or CAN FD Pack block.

Summary: Simulink Code Importer incorrectly reports missing source file or include directory
Description: When Simulink Code Importer or Simulink Test Code Importer detects a missing user-specified source file or include directory, it may incorrectly?report the missing file or directory using the name of another, existing file or directory.

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier analysis finishes quickly with all objectives reported as Undecided
Description: If the absolute path of the model being analyzed contains non-ASCII characters, Simulink Design Verifier analysis terminates with all the objectives reported as Undecided .

Summary: Angular acceleration outputs of rottraj function are incorrect when TimeScaling parameter has nonuniform value
Description: In the rottraj  function, if you specify nonuniform time scaling by using the TimeScaling  name-value argument, the returned angular accelerations of the generated trajectory are incorrect. For example, in the following code snippet, the output angular acceleration, a2 , is effectively zero when it should be linear. tvec = 0:0.01:5;tpts = [1 4];q0 = quaternion([0 pi/4 -pi/8],'euler','ZYX','point');qF = quaternion([3*pi/2 0 -3*pi/4],'euler','ZYX','point');[s, sd, sdd] = cubicpolytraj([0 1], tpts, tvec);[qInterp2, w2, a2] = rottraj(q0, qF, tpts, tvec, 'TimeScaling', [s; sd; sdd]);?
Workaround: The rottraj function returns an analytical solution to the angular acceleration, but you can also use numerical differentiation to obtain a close approximate from the returned velocity outputs:tvec = 0:0.01:5;tpts = [1 4];q0 = quaternion([0 pi/4 -pi/8],'euler','ZYX','point');qF = quaternion([3*pi/2 0 -3*pi/4],'euler','ZYX','point');[s, sd, sdd] = cubicpolytraj([0 1], tpts, tvec);[qInterp2, w2] = rottraj(q0, qF, tpts, tvec, 'TimeScaling', [s; sd; sdd]);% Use numerical differentiation to instead get an accurate approximation?angAcc = diff(w2,1,2) ./ diff(tvec);?
Products: Navigation Toolbox, ROS Toolbox, Robotics System Toolbox

Summary: Test case cleanup callback for parallel test execution runs on host MATLAB
Description: For parallel test execution using Simulink Test, the test case post-load callback run on the parallel worker MATLAB but the cleanup callback runs on the host MATLAB.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Nested if statements or switch statements may generate incorrect HDL Code when used in System objects or?with optimizations enabled
Description: System objects using nested if statements or switch statements as shown below may produce HDL Code with incorrect logic for the "if" condition. Incorrect HDL code may also be generated when nested if statements are used along with optimization options like?MapPersistentVarsToRAM . if (logicA)  else??if (logicB)????/ / code ??end  end  or? ?switch?(State)  ????case?Wait_Request  ????????nextState = Shift_Bit_Value;  ????case?Shift_Bit_Value  ????????nextState = Compute_Sqrt;  ????case?Compute_Sqrt  ????????nextState = Wait_Request;  end

Summary: Experiment Manager displays confusion matrix with axes reversed
Description: When Experiment Manager computes the confusion matrix for a classification experiment, the true classes and the predicted classes are transposed. As a result, false positives are marked as false negatives, and the other way round.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 2, R2021b

Summary: Code inspection fails if the code in a MATLAB Function block contains a switch statement with an enum type
Description: Simulink Code Inspector fails to verify the code generated from a model that meets all of these conditions: The model contains a MATLAB Function block.? The code in the MATLAB Function block contains a switch statement.? The condition of the switch statement is of enum data type.? The switch statement is inside of a if statement.
Workaround: Replace the enum type with an integer type, or replace the switch statement with an equivalentif statement.
