Summary: Model in .mdl format opened in 21a with saved C++ mapping may issue warnings on load or lose mapping specifications
Description: Warnings issued when loading a .mdl  model file in 21a that has the 'ClassName'  property of C++ mapping specified. The value of the 'ClassName'  property is not preserved in .mdl  files.
Workaround: Save models with C++ mapping in .slx format.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: JIT MEX generated from MATLAB function that accepts four or more input arguments might produce incorrect answers if at least one argument is of half type
Description: The generated MEX function might produce incorrect answers if all  these conditions are true: In the coder.MexCodeConfig  object, the EnableJIT  property is set to true. Alternatively, in MATLAB Coder app, in the Generate  step, the Use JIT compilation  check box is selected. This setting instructs the code generator to generate the MEX function by using the just-in-time (JIT) compilation technology. Your MATLAB code contains a function that accepts four or more input arguments. At least one of these arguments is passed data of half type.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: C/C++ code generated for MATLAB code that uses handle classes and inheritance might be incorrect
Description: Code generation might produce incorrect C/C++ code if your MATLAB code satisfies?all  the following conditions:? Your MATLAB code creates a handle class B. The constructor of handle class B instantiates another handle class C. The handle class B has a base class A, whose constructor also instantiates handle class C. Both allocations of C are stored in a cell array or structure, which are then stored as properties of A and B, respectively.?  Example MATLAB code that satisfies all these conditions: classdef A < handle    properties        property1    end    methods        function this = A            this.property1 = {C(1)};        end    endendclassdef B < A    properties        property2    end    methods        function this = B            this.property2 = {C(2)};        end    endendclassdef C < handle    properties        property    end    methods        function this = C(property)            this.property = property;        end    endendfunction [a, b] = mainx = B;a = x.property1{1}.property;b = x.property2{1}.property; In such situations, the generated code might use the same memory for both instances of C, instead of allocating separate memory for the two instances. This issue affects MATLAB Coder, MATLAB Function blocks and MATLAB System blocks in Simulink, and MATLAB Functions in Stateflow.
Workaround: Add a dummy field to the nested type. (In the above description, the nested type is class C.) Make sure that the derived and base constructors assign different types to this?dummy field.For example, modify the code in the previous section by adding the field dummy?to the implementation of class C. Notice that the constructor of class A is modified to assign a character type to dummy, while?the constructor of class B is modified to assign a double type to?dummy.classdef A < handle    properties        property1    end    methods        function this = A            this.property1 = {C('a', 1)};        end    endendclassdef B < A    properties        property2    end    methods        function this = B            this.property2 = {C(1, 2)};        end    endendclassdef C < handle    properties        dummy        property    end    methods        function this = C(dummy, property)            this.dummy = dummy;            this.property = property;        end    endendfunction [a, b] = mainx = B;a = x.property1{1}.property;b = x.property2{1}.property;

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Constant string element in virtual bus output from submodel loses initial value in generated code
Description: In generated code, a string signal might be incorrectly initialized to an empty string when all of these conditions are true: The string signal has a constant sample time. The string signal is an element of a virtual bus. The virtual bus is the output of a referenced model. The initial value of the string signal is assigned in a referenced model.

Summary: Uninstalling a previous version of a toolbox fails when the toolbox includes apps and multiple versions are installed
Description: If a toolbox includes apps and multiple versions of that toolbox are installed, uninstalling a previous version of the toolbox fails.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 1, R2021b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: MATLAB simulation may return incorrect output datatypes for X^0 and X^1 when X is a fi object
Description: Simulation in MATLAB can return incorrect output datatypes for?X^0 ?and?X^1.? This occurs when both of the following conditions are met: X ?is a?fi ?object  X  specifies a? fimath ?with the properties? 'ProductMode'  and 'SumMode' ?both set to 'SpecifyPrecision'   In simulation,? X^0 ?always returns a 1-bit?fi ?object for any?fi ?input and?X^1 ?always returns a?fi ?object with the same?numerictype ?as the input,? X. This does not affect code generation. For example, consider the following function used for simulation and code generation. function y = foo(X,k)?  ? ?y = X^k;?  end?  For demonstration purposes, customize the fipref  settings to display the relevant output.? >> p = fipref('NumericTypeDisplay','short','FimathDisplay','none');  Define the input X  to be a fi  object. The math settings of X  are specified by the fimath  object F , where ?'ProductMode' ?and?'SumMode' ?both set to?'SpecifyPrecision' .  >> F = fimath('ProductMode','SpecifyPrecision',...  ? ? ? ? ? ? ? 'ProductWordLength',32,...  ? ? ? ? ? ? ? 'ProductFractionLength',2,  ? ? ? ? ? ? ? 'SumMode','SpecifyPrecision',  ? ? ? ? ? ? ? 'SumWordLength',32,  ? ? ? ? ? ? ? 'SumFractionLength',2);  >> X = fi(ones(3,3),1,18,18,F);  Generate code for the function foo . >> codegen foo -args {X, 0};  Compare the output of MATLAB simulation with the output of the generated MEX file for?k=0 ?and?k=1 .? >> y_sim = foo(X,0)?  y_sim =?? ? ?1? ? ?0? ? ?0? ? ?0? ? ?1? ? ?0? ? ?0? ? ?0? ? ?1 ? ? ? numerictype(0,1,0)  >> y_mex = foo_mex(X, 0)?y_mex?=?? ? ?1? ? ?0? ? ?0? ? ?0? ? ?1? ? ?0? ? ?0? ? ?0? ? ?1 ? ? ? numerictype(1,32,2)  >> y_sim = foo(X,1)y_sim =?? ? 0.5000? ? 0.5000? ? 0.5000? ? 0.5000? ? 0.5000? ? 0.5000? ? 0.5000? ? 0.5000? ? 0.5000 ? ? ? numerictype(1,18,18)  >> y_mex = foo_mex(X,1)  y_mex =?? ? 0.5000? ? 0.5000? ? 0.5000? ? 0.5000? ? 0.5000? ? 0.5000? ? 0.5000? ? 0.5000? ? 0.5000 ? ? ? numerictype(1,32,2)
Workaround: Wrap the call to X^k?in a function. When the conditions described above are met, use cast to set the data type of the output.For example:function y = myPow(x, k)?if (k==0) || (k==1) && (strcmpi(x.SumMode,'SpecifyPrecision') && strcmpi(x.ProductMode, 'SpecifyPrecision'))y = cast(x^k, 'like', x.*x);?else?y = x^k;?endGenerate code using the input X?from the problem description.>> codegen myPow -args {X, 0}??The resulting MEX function shows that the results now agree.>> myPow(X, 0)ans =?? ? ?1? ? ?0? ? ?0? ? ?0? ? ?1? ? ?0? ? ?0? ? ?0? ? ?1? ? ? numerictype(1,32,2)>> myPow_mex(X, 0)ans =?? ? ?1? ? ?0? ? ?0? ? ?0? ? ?1? ? ?0? ? ?0? ? ?0? ? ?1? ? ? numerictype(1,32,2)>> myPow(X, 1)ans =?? ? 0.5000? ? 0.5000? ? 0.5000? ? 0.5000? ? 0.5000? ? 0.5000? ? 0.5000? ? 0.5000? ? 0.5000? ? ? numerictype(1,32,2)>> myPow_mex(X, 1)ans =?? ? 0.5000? ? 0.5000? ? 0.5000? ? 0.5000? ? 0.5000? ? 0.5000? ? 0.5000? ? 0.5000? ? 0.5000? ? ? numerictype(1,32,2)?

Summary: Rule A8-4-7 raises a false positive for types that are not trivially copyable
Description: In A8-4-7, the rule states that the input parameters of functions should be passed by value if they are trivially copyable. Polyspace incorrectly raised violations of this checker when inputs that are not trivially copyable are passed by reference.  For example:   #include <cstdint>? ? union Small   { std::int8_t m_1 ; wchar_t m2;   } ; ?void f(Small& arg) ; //False positive: arg is not trivially copyable

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: HDL Code generated from a MATLAB code containing division by a constant may result in incorrect code
Description: When generating HDL code from a MATLAB function or a subsystem containing a MATLAB function block, HDL Coder may generate incorrect code for an expression such as "y = x/constVal" in the following cases:  ?If:  "x" has an integer type "constVal" is a power of 2  then the generated code will have "floor" rounding behavior. Integers in MATLAB have "nearest" rounding behavior.  If:  "x" has a fi type with: OverflowMode = Wrap RoundingMethod = Floor or Zero  "constVal" is in the range "2^n" to "(2^n)+1", exclusive  then the generated code will be as if "constVal" had the value of "2^n" instead.

Summary: If Simulation Data Inspector is open when you start simulation of real-time application that includes matrix signals, signal data logging can be incomplete or MATLAB can crash.
Description: If the Simulation Data Inspector is open when you start simulation of a real-time application that includes matrix signals, the signal data logging can be incomplete or MATLAB can crash. When the Simulation Data Inspector is incomplete, the matrix signal data can show only the first channels for some matrices.  When the real-time application is configured for automatic export to the Simulation Data Inspector at the end of simulation, MATLAB can crash.
Workaround: There are two workarounds. Either:?To avoid crashing MATLAB in the end of simulation, make sure that the Simulation Data Inspector is closed before you start simulation of the real-time application. If you want to observe signals during simulation, open the Simulation Data Inspector after you start simulation.OrTo avoid crashing MATLAB during export of data that you collected for a simulation that you started while the Simulation Data Inspector was open, use the attached script to cleanup the logged data. The script fixes the metadata of the incorrect signals and makes the data exportable.Unzip the attachment and save the?cleanupDataForExport.p?file to a folder on your MATLAB path. Run the?cleanupDataForExport?before exporting this kind of data from the Simulation Data Inspector.

Summary: Step 5.1 Generate Simulink Real-Time interface?in HDL Workflow Advisor fails for models with bus data types
Description: When going through Simulink Real-Time workflow in HDL Workflow Advisor, for the subsystem with bus data type, running? step 5. 1 Generate Simulink Real-Time interface ?errors out as follows: Failed: Dot indexing is not supported for variables of this type.
Workaround: Consider using?scalaror vector data type for the inputs/outputs ports

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generated for handle object might be incorrect if the object is created and used inside a for or while loop with break statements
Description: The C/C++/CUDA code generated for handle objects in your MATLAB code might be incorrect if?all  the following conditions are true: Your code contains a?for  or while  loop with a break  statement. ? Handle objects are created inside the loop body.? The objects are used in the same block of code, immediately preceding the break  statement.?  This code pattern might invoke an object's delete  method too early (before the last use of the object).? This issue affects MATLAB Coder, MATLAB Function blocks and MATLAB System blocks in Simulink, and MATLAB functions in Stateflow.
Workaround: Duplicate the logic that triggers the break statement.? For example, suppose that your code has this for loop:for i = 1:n    x = A;    if foo(bar)        use(x);        break    endendRewrite this code as:for i = 1:n    x = A;    t = foo(bar);    if t        use(x);    end    if t        break    endend

Summary: Inconsistent results between Model Advisor check detailed and summary reports
Description: For a given Model Advisor check, when one of these options for defining the error severity of the check is true: Input parameter Check result when issues are flagged ?is set to Fail  The " severity" ? attribute in the Model Advisor check configuration file (.json ) is defined as? "severity":required   The results in the Model Advisor detailed and summary reports are inconsistent.? The detailed report correctly labels the severity of the flagged results as error .? However, the summary report incorrectly labels the severity as?warning .?

Summary: 'Multi-Input Sum Block' that has configuredwith complex vector or matrix inputs results in an internal assertion
Description: HDL Coder generates the following error during code generation when the 'Multi-input Sum Block' has been configured with complex vector or matrix inputs: "Unexpected or internal error encountered in "in CGIR: hdl_emitters/ CGIRCompEmitter. cpp line 819: In registerIOSignals: Assertion failed: var?
Workaround: Consider splitting multi-input sum-block or combinations of signs (+, -, +, +)? ?with the series of two inputsadd or subtract blocks with sign combinations.

Summary: Exported DDS XML does not include QoS information for Domain Participants
Description: When you build and export XML for a modeled DDS application, the exported XML files do not include QoS information for Domain Participants. The exported XML can include QoS information specified for DataReaders or DataWriters in the application.

Summary: Starting values not properly used in glmfit?and fitglm?in singular problems
Description: The?glmfit ?and?fitglm ?functions carry out an iterative estimation process to estimate regression coefficients. If the iterations do not converge, it might be helpful to provide starting values. These functions accept the name-value argument 'B0'  that specifies starting values for the coefficient estimates, as shown in this call to fitglm . mdl = fitglm(X,y,'Distribution','binomial','B0',startvals) If the input matrix X  is rank-deficient, then the functions reduce X  into a matrix of full rank. However, the starting values are incorrectly applied to the reduced?X  matrix. The result is an error because the full-size startvals ?vector has too many entries to multiply the reduced matrix.
Workaround: Remove redundant columns from X before passing it into the function. The functions rank?and qr?might be useful in discovering redundant columns.

Summary: The Simulink configuration parameter?Support long long?is greyed out and cannot be re-enabled when Generate GPU code is enabled and the Device type?is changed.
Description: When the Generate GPU code ?parameter in the Code Generation  pane of the Simulink Model Configurations window is enabled, the ProdLongLongMode ?parameter which is labelled Support long long ?in the UI is enabled automatically and greyed out. However changing?the field labelled?Device type ?in the Hardware Implementation pane, which corresponds to the ProdHWDeviceType  parameter, may automatically disable Support long long  and leave it greyed out.
Workaround: If the?Generate GPU code?parameter in the Code Generation pane is toggled off and on again, Simulink will automatically enable the?ProdLongLongMode?parameter which is labelled?Support long long?in the UI.

Summary: Invalid Model version value on model save
Description: Incorrect appended Model version  value is seen in the history section when Prompt to update model history ?is set to When saving model . The Model version  value is displayed as double value.Example: Incorrect Model version observed: <username> -- Tue Feb 0217:09:19 2021 -- Version 2.100000e+00  <username> -- Tue Feb 02 17:08:00 2021-- Version 2  Expected Model version: <username>-- Tue Feb 02 17:09:19 2021 -- Version 1.2

Summary: System Design Description (SDD) reports generated by the Simulink Report Generator test suite are incomplete
Description: The results of executing the Simulink Report Generator tests suite are provided in System Design Description (SDD) reports. The SDD reports do not provide information for parameter BusVirtuality .? As a result, several of the generated SDD reports do not match the expected reports provided with the DO Qualification Kit.?? This issue impacts the reports for these test models: SDD_SF_ArithLogic.slx SDD_eml_call_gf.slx SDD_logic.slx SDD_lookup.slx SDD_signals.slx SDD_sldemo_mdlref_basic.slx SDD_subsys.slx SDD_truth_table_model.slx

Summary: Incorrect constraints might be in the generated code for a model that uses constants in symbolic expression
Description: Incorrect constraints might be in the generated code for a model that uses constants in a symbolic expression (for example, D+1 ). The generated code might have invalid constraints from -Inf  to +Inf ?even though D  is valid and bounded.? The code might look like this:? #if (D+1) <= -2147483648 #error "The preprocessor definition '(D+1)' must be greater than '-2147483648'"  #endif?  This code might cause a compiler error as the generated constraints cannot be met.

Summary: When importing multiple ReqIF specifications into one requirement set, manually mapping attributes only works for one specification
Description: If you have a Requirements Interchange Format (ReqIF) file that contains more than one specification and you import all specifications into a single requirement set, the imported requirement set has multiple Import nodes or multiple top-level requirements that each correspond to a specification. After you import the ReqIF requirements, you can map their attributes to requirement properties in Simulink Requirements. When you import multiple ReqIF specifications into a single requirement set, you can map the ReqIF requirement attributes from each specification to Simulink Requirements requirement properties. If two or more specifications have ReqIF requirement attributes with the same name, those requirement attributes only map properly for the first specification. The Requirements Editor accepts the mapping for the other specifications, but the values do not populate correctly.

Summary: Stateflow charts containing?bitconcat of constant values might produce HDL code that causes compilation error
Description: The generated HDL code might cause compilation error if both  these conditions are true:? Your Simulink model contains a Stateflow chart The Stateflow chart uses the?bitconcat ?function with constant inputs

Summary: Task  4.2 Run Synthesis may fail in HDL Workflow advisor when using any version including and after Microsemi Libero SoC 12.1
Description: HDL Coder only supports Microsemi Libero SoC Version 12.0. However, when using any other version, HDL Workflow Advisor errors out during??Task 4.2 Run Synthesis  step with an unintuitive error message in the log window: Error:??The 'SYNTHESIZE' tool run failed.?Please select a root before running this tool.  ????????You can select a root in Tcl using the 'set_root' Tcl command.  Error:??The command 'run_tool' failed.  Error:??Failure when executing Tcl script.?[ Line 45 ]  Error:??The Execute Script command failed.?
Workaround: Follow the steps listed below to support Microsemi Libero SoC 12.4 or any unsupported tool version:Run HDL Workflow Advisor till task 4.1 Create Project?Launch the Microsemi Libero SoC 12.4 or any other unsupported tool version?In the left pane, go to Design HierarchyRight click on the top module in the design and click on "Set As Root"After this, you can either Run Synthesis and other steps from Microsemi Libero SoC tool or continue with task 4.2 in HDL Workflow Advisor.?
