Summary: Gain block with boolean output type generates incorrect HDL code
Description: The generated HDL code for a Simulink model might be incorrect when the following settings are applied: The model contains a Gain block The Output data type   parameter set to 'boolean'  for the Gain block  The incorrect HDL code can result in simulation failures in ModelSim when verifying the generated code.

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier analysis may provide incorrect results on models containing Simulink Functions with certain settings
Description: Simulink Design Verifier analysis may provide incorrect results on a model containing a Simulink Function, when any one of the below conditions is true:Uses the same variable as input and output arguments, or Configures the generated C/C++ function arguments to be in a different order compared to that of the Simulink Function

Summary: Generated VHDL code might fail to compile because of collision between component type names and the library name
Description: Assigning the same name to Simulink blocks and the HDL model property VHDLLibraryName ?might generate VHDL code that does not compile. This is because of a collision between the identifiers used to define the generated the VHDL library and the names of VHDL component types.?

Summary: Icons on push buttons do not appear in web apps?
Description: When a web app deployed to MATLAB Web App Server has multiple push buttons with icons, some of the icons do not render. This issue occurs only on servers running on Linux systems.
Workaround: Refreshing the app page one or more times corrects the problem.

Summary: High-Integrity check Check usage of recursions?does not flag all instances of duplicate Charts and MATLAB Function Blocks
Description: The Model Advisor check?Check usage of recursions ?(ID:?mathworks.hism.hisf_0004 ) does not flag all instances of duplicate Charts and MATLAB Function Blocks.

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier analysis report may contain garbled text on non-English MATLAB on Windows platform
Description: The objective descriptions in the Simulink Design Verifier?analysis report may contain garbled text when all the following conditions are true: The analysis is performed on a non-English MATLAB on Windows platform. The model contains S-Function or C/C++ custom code. The model representation is reused from the Simulink cache file (SLXC).
Workaround: Modify value of?RebuildModelRepresentation?option to?Always,?run the analysis and generate the report.

Summary: Test for Model Component?wizard in Test Manager incorrectly adds new test case to existing test file
Description: If you have a test file open in the Test Manager and you use the Test for Model Component wizard to autogenerate test cases, the new test cases are always added to that test file even if you specify a new test file location.
Workaround: Before using the Test for Model Component wizard to create test cases and save them to a new test file, close all open test files in Test Manager.?

Summary: Signal Editor block silently fails the execution of some set_param commands
Description: For the Signal Editor block, the execution of the set_param ?function with? the parameters 'ActiveScenario'  or 'ActiveSignal' ?might silently fail if set to invalid entries. For example, if the specified scenario or signal does not exist, then the function appears to succeed but actually fails without error and the parameter is set to the first alphabetical scenario or signal in the MAT-file.

Summary: Model with inactive caller functions produces SIL/PIL simulation error
Description: If you run a SIL or PIL simulation of a model that contains function callers inside inactive variants, you might see this error:  Variable is not defined in memory.  Object's 'Owner' property is empty

Summary: MATLAB might crash when passing in model arguments as values to a masked Model block
Description: Simulating a model hierarchy might crash MATLAB when all of these conditions apply: The model hierarchy contains at least three levels of models. For example, suppose the top model (A ) directly or indirectly references a model (B ) that directly references another model (C ). The Model blocks that reference models B  and C  specify accelerator mode simulation. Models B  and C  specify model arguments. Model C  is either a function-call model or a model with a virtual bus at a root input or output port. Model C  has a system mask. The Model block that references model C  passes a model argument as the value for one of the parameters on the mask.

Summary: Poorly formatted Iteration names and Excel tab names when generated test cases are exported to Simulink Test
Description: Iteration names and tab names in the Excel file corresponding to the iterations are generated in the form Scenarios_1 , Scenarios_1_1 , Scenarios_1_1_1  instead of Scenarios_1 , Scenarios_2 , Scenarios_3  if test-cases generated by Simulink Design Verifier are exported to Simulink Test when:Using the Export test cases to Simulink Test  link in the Simulink Design Verifier Results Summary  Windows and selecting Excel  from the Test Data Format  drop-down selection in the Export Design Verifier Test Cases  dialog Using the Create Test for Component Wizard ?in Simulink Test Manager, select the Use Design Verifier to generate test input scenarios ?option in the Test Inputs ?page, and select EXCEL ?in the Specify the file format ?drop-down selection of the Generated Test ?page.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 3, R2022a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: MATLAB Function block containing sub-function with in-place specification on inputs with variable dimensions might result in incorrect code
Description: Incorrect generated code might be generated for a MATLAB Functionblock that meets these conditions: The MATLAB function contains a sub-function. Thesub-function has an in-place specification (that is, the same input and outputvariable). The in-place input variable has variable dimensions.  For example, in the below representative code pattern, a MATLAB Function block is the function top_function ?which contains the? sub_function . The P1  input to sub_function  is in-place and has variable dimensions. function [P1,P2] = top_function(In1)...[P1,P2] = sub_function(In2,P1);...end  function [P1,P2] = sub_function(In2,P1)...end  Note, you might get the following warning during code generation:? warning: passing argument 2 of 'sub_function' from incompatible pointer type [-Wincompatible-pointer-types]?

Summary: Some editor features might be missing when MATLAB starts
Description: Editor features in some editors might be missing when MATLAB starts. These features might include: syntax highlighting Live Tasks visualization types debugger buttons  Anything that impacts MATLAB startup behavior like the -r  startup option or startup.m  can increase the probability that this bug occurs, especially when the cd  command is called.
Workaround: Call addpath with a valid directory that is not currently on the MATLAB path after MATLAB starts and before opening the editor to re-enable the missing editor features. You only need to do this once per MATLAB session.

Summary: copyfile overwrites read-only files without using the force flag
Description: The copyfile  function overwrites any read-only files in the destination folder having the same names as files in the source folder, even when not using the force flag. For example:?? Create two folders and populate with subfolders of the same name. mkdir(fullfile("folder1", "subfolder"));mkdir(fullfile("folder2", "subfolder")); Populate with files of the same name. writematrix(randi(5,5), fullfile("folder1", "subfolder", "file.txt"));writematrix(randi(5,5), fullfile("folder2", "subfolder", "file.txt")); Set file.txt  in the destination folder to be read-only. fileattrib(fullfile("folder1", "subfolder", "file.txt"), "-w"); The following operation succeeds in overwriting, but should not. copyfile('folder2', 'folder1');

Summary: Error when compiling a model containing a variant subsystem with a library link to a subsystem containing a Stateflow Chart or MATLAB Function Block
Description: When you update or run a model containing a variant subsystem, Stateflow throws an error if all of the following conditions are true: One of the variant subsystems contains a linked library to a model with a Stateflow Chart or MATLAB Function Block The model has already been compiled or run at least once, or the library model has been opened The variant control expression relies on a variable defined in the InitFcn of the model, but that variable has been cleared from the workspace  The error is similar to: Undefined function 'myModel_sfun' for input arguments of type 'double'.
Workaround: Initialize all variant control parameters before compiling the model.?

Summary: Internal error generated during HDL code generation from 1-D Lookup Table block when data types of Input and Breakpoints 1 parameter are different
Description: An internal error is generated during HDL code generation when following conditions are met: Subsystem contains 1-D Lookup Table  block with Fixed-Point  input types Data types of Input    and Breakpoints 1   parameter are different (E.g.?Input (ufix5) , Breakpoints 1 (uint32))?  The error is:             BITAND/BITOR/BITXOR must have matching operand types
Workaround: Set the? Breakpoints 1 parameter data type setting to 'Inherit: Same as corresponding input'.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Simulink Normal?and Accelerator mode simulations may yield incorrect results for complex fixed-point types with word length greater than 64 bits
Description: Simulation results for?Normal ?and?Accelerator ?mode?may be inaccurate in models containing Simulink blocks with a complex parameter of fixed-point type which has word length greater than 64 bits and the block output datatype does not match the parameter type. For example, in a model containing a Constant block attached to an Outport block, if you specify the Constant value  as fi(1.5+2.5i,1,67,1) ?and specify the Output data type ?as fixdt(1,77,1) , an incorrect output is generated during simulation.? Observed in but not limited to the following blocks:  Constant block Gain block Unit Delay block Delay block
Workaround: The key condition which triggers this bug is that the block parameter undergoes data type conversion from its original fixed-point type to a different fixed-point type.Use alternative designs that avoid implicit data type conversions within the blocks for complex values which use fixed-point datatypes and have word length greater than 64 bits.This can be achieved by either one of these:Maintain data type consistency within the block by setting?Output data type?to?Inherit.Use a Data Type Conversion block when data type conversions are needed.Perform any data type conversions needed within the parameter or expression declaration in the block. For example, fi(fi(1.5+2.5i,1,67,1),1,77,1).
Fixed In: R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 1, R2021b

Summary: MATLAB occasionally crashes when saving MAT-files containing objects with long chains of references
Description: MATLAB crashes when saving MAT-files containing objects with long chains of references, i.e. a series of objects where the first has a property containing the second, the second has a property containing the third, etc. In version 2021a, a limit on the depth of the recursion during saving a MAT-file was introduced.? In certain rare circumstances, enforcing that limit in the MATLAB implementation code might result in a crash.
Workaround: For class instances that will be be saved into MAT-files, avoid long chains of references.? The 'save' operation works in a recursive manner, so objects referencing each other in a chain can cause a very tall recursion stack.? Objects that can, directly or indirectly, reference themselves can cause additional problems.? Instead, keep the multiple objects in an array, which breaks the chain of references and replaces those references with a container holding all the (previously) referenced objects in order. ?An example contrasting those two approaches is attached to this bug report.

Summary: Requirement links are incorrectly copied from Subsystem Reference blocks to instances in Simulink models
Description: If you create a requirement link from a Subsystem Reference block and create an instance of the Subsystem Reference block in a Simulink model, when the model is opened the requirement links from the Subsystem Reference block are automatically copied into the instance in the Simulink model.This happens depending on the selection of the Duplicating outgoing links when copying Simulink and Stateflow object ?preference in the Requirement Settings dialog. If the default value of Always duplicate links when copying objects ?is selected, then the links are copied. If?Duplicate links only when model requirements are highlighted ?is selected, then the links are only copied if the links are highlighted in the Simulink model. You can view this setting in Simulink by navigating to the Apps ?tab and opening the Requirements Manager ?app. Select Layout ?and ensure that Requirements Browser ?is selected. In the Requirements ?pane, in the View ?drop-down, select Links . In the toolstrip, select Link Settings ?> Default Link Storage . To toggle link highlighting, in the toolstrip, click Highlight Links .  You can also view this setting at the MATLAB command prompt by entering:? rmipref('DuplicateOnCopy')  If MATLAB returns a logical  1 ?(true), then? Always duplicate links when copying objects ?is selected. If MATLAB returns a logical 0 ?(false), then? Duplicate links only when model requirements are highlighted ?is selected.
Workaround: Before opening the model containing an instance of a Subsystem Reference block, change the selection of the?Duplicating outgoing links when copying Simulink and Stateflow object?preference. Navigate to the?Apps?tab and open the?Requirements Manager?app. Select?Layout?and ensure that?Requirements Browser?is selected. In the?Requirements?pane, in the?View?drop-down, select?Links. In the toolstrip, select?Link Settings?>?Default Link Storage. Then select?Duplicate links only when model requirements are highlighted. Ensure that link highlighting is turned off by toggling Highlight Links?to off.You can also change the selection of the?Duplicating outgoing links when copying Simulink and Stateflow object?preference at the MATLAB command line by entering:rmipref('DuplicateOnCopy',false)

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Full assignments of buses in Stateflow charts might not produce correct results during simulation
Description: A full bus assignment in the form A = B in a Stateflow Chart might not be performed correctly in simulation when all of the following conditions are true: The chart property Execute (enter) chart at initialization  is enabled. The assignment is executed at time 0. The assignment reads or writes to output data of the chart.  In simulation, Stateflow might incorrectly assign zeroes or uninitialized values to the signals in A.

Summary: IEC Certification Kit validation suite for Simulink Requirements fails in time zones different than?Eastern Time Zone (ET)
Description: The IEC Certification Kit auto-compares the Simulink Requirements reports that are generated in the user environment against baseline reports that are shipped with the product (expected results). The time stamps in the expected results are generated in Eastern Time Zone (ET), but the time stamps in the user-generated reports represent the local time zone. The discrepancy in these values results in a failed validation status.?
Workaround: When the validation status is evaluated as failed, manually compare the content of the test reports to the baseline reports,?ignoring the time stamp discrepancies. The baseline reports are available in slreq/expected_results.Alternatively, change the operating system time zone of your computer to Eastern Time Zone and re-execute the validation tests.

Summary: When using UIFigure or App Designer, MATLAB sporadically crashes during shutdown
Description: When closing MATLAB after using UIFigure or App Designer, it might crash and leave a stack trace. Stack Trace (captured): 0x00007fff1b8e38b9                    D:\MATLAB\bin\win64\libmwms.dll+015873850x00007fff0cf21909                  D:\MATLAB\bin\win64\webwindow.dll+000719450x00007fff0cf40419                  D:\MATLAB\bin\win64\webwindow.dll+001976570x00007fff0d011d22 D:\MATLAB\bin\win64\libmwconnectorbinarycefimpl.dll+000729940x00007ffef2ea33db            D:\MATLAB\bin\win64\hgweb_interface.dll+00275419

Summary: Compilation error occurs if reusable Subsystem inside Variant Subsystem is connected to Scope block
Description: Compilation error might occur for a model that meets the following conditions: Model contains a reusable Subsystem placed inside a Variant Subsystem block.? Outport of reusable Subsystem is connected to Variant Subsystem's Outport.? The same signal also connects to a Scope block.?

Summary: Running Iterations with fast restart might error when Simulation Mode is not Model Settings
Description: In the Test Manager, if you set the Simulation Mode  to anything other than Model Settings ?and run Iterations? with fast restart enabled, an error similar to the following might occur: The following parameters are not supported by the sim command when Fast Restart is enabled: 'SimulationMode' ?
Workaround: Instead of setting the Simulation Mode in the Test Manager, set the simulation mode in the model or test harness before running iterations with fast restart enabled.

Summary: Polyspace-configure command fails on Windows
Description: The polyspace-configure ?command might fail to generate a project or options file if you run the command on Windows and the path to your compiler executable contains dots and ends with .exe . For instance: C:\path.with.dots\gcc.exe   In the command output, you see an error message similar to this: ERROR: Fatal error (exception): mwboost::filesystem::last_write_time
Workaround: To work around this issue, try one of the following:Remove the .exe extension from your compiler name:C:/folder.with.dots/gcc.exe --> C:/folder.with.dots/gccRename your compiler folder to remove all dots in the folder path:C:/folder.with.dots/gcc.exe --> C:/folder_without_dots/gcc.exe

Summary: Capturing baseline data in previous release produces error when callbacks are used in Test Manager
Description: If you use a multiple release test in the Test Manager to capture baseline data from an previous release and include a pre-load callback, this error occurs: Pre-load callback errored out. Variable 'message' and/or class 'message.publish' is undefined.   If you use a multiple release test with post-load callback that contains a model close command, this error occurs: SlicerDebugStatus: No variable found ?

Summary: Assertion or MATLAB crash during HDL code generation in the presence of unconnected output ports of Stateflow charts with input events.
Description: When using Stateflow charts that contain input events for HDL code generation, user may see the following assertion in the presence of dead or unconnected output ports of the chart: ?### Begin VHDL Code Generation for 'sfchart_event_dead_out'. failed assertion in matlab\ src\ cgir_hdl\ hdl_emitters\ CGIRCompEmitter. cpp at line 825:????In registerIOSignals: Assertion failed: sig[??0] matlab\ bin\ win64\ cgir_hdl. dll+09026218 pir::CGIRCompEmitter::globalPhase+00001482[??1] matlab\ bin\ win64\ cgir_hdl. dll+09129704 pir::PIREmitter::commonPostTransformPhase+00000360[??2] matlab\ bin\ win64\ cgir_hdl. dll+09497484 pir::VHDLPIREmitter::postTransformPhase+00000028[??3] matlab\ bin\ win64\ cgir_hdl. dll+09198422 pir::PIREmitter::runThroughNameUniquification+00001622[??4] matlab\ bin\ win64\ cgir_hdl. dll+09200034 pir::PIREmitter::startEmission+00000386[??5] matlab\ bin\ win64\ cgir_hdl. dll+05296563 metadatafactory::udd::UDDMetaDataFactory_MethodInfo::invokeMethod+00000259[??6] matlab\ bin\ win64\ udd. dll+00395264 UDMethodInfo::invokeMethodE+00000032[??7] matlab\ bin\ win64\ udd_mi. dll+00477095 ouAddBuiltInMethod+00001575 Users may also encounter the following MATLAB crash in some cases: [? 0 ]? pir::CGIRCompEmitter::registerIOSignals(pir::CGIRComp? const &, mwboost::shared_ptr<pir::PortAccessor>)? const ?at /matlab/src/cgir_hdl/hdl_emitters/CGIRCompEmitter.cpp: 816 ? [? 1 ] ?pir::CGIRCompEmitter::registerIOSignals(pir::CGIRComp&)? const ?at /matlab/src/cgir_hdl/hdl_emitters/CGIRCompEmitter.cpp: 842  [? 2 ] ?pir::CGIRCompEmitter::globalPhase()? const ?at /matlab/src/cgir_hdl/hdl_emitters/CGIRCompEmitter.cpp: 466 ?

Summary: Update target computer generates Java FTP exception
Description: Update target computer can generate this exception: Cannot update software on target 'TargetPC1': Cannot copy file'C:\ProgramData\MATLAB\SupportPackages\R2020b\toolbox\slrealtime\target\supportpackage\qnxtools.tar' to'/home/slrt/.config/updateos/qnxtools.tar' on target 'TargetPC1': Java exception occurred:org.apache.commons.net.io.CopyStreamException: IOException caught while copying.at org.apache.commons.net.io.Util.copyStream(Util.java:129)at org.apache.commons.net.ftp.FTPClient.__storeFile(FTPClient.java:385)at org.apache.commons.net.ftp.FTPClient.storeFile(FTPClient.java:1379)
Fixed In: R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 1, R2022b

Summary: Grouping components in a System Composer model and then synchronizing the related allocation set causes error "Uniqueness constraint violation"
Description: The error "Uniqueness constraint violation" appears when synchronizing an allocation set in the Allocation Editor. The error appears if at least one of the following actions were taken on either of the allocated System Composer models: Select one or more components in the model editor and select "Group Selected Components" from the context menu. Select one or more components in the model editor and press CTRL+G on the keyboard.

Summary: The Interpreter?property of label UI components?does not respond correctly to Enable, FontSize, or specific label text
Description: When using the Interpreter  property with a label UI component, there are the following inconsistencies. When Interpreter  is 'html'  or 'latex' , the Enable  property has no effect. When?Interpreter ?is 'html'  or 'latex' , the FontSize  property has no effect when it is set before or at the same time as?Interpreter . When?Interpreter ?is 'html' , the label disappears from the App Designer design view when changing other properties. When?Interpreter ?is 'latex' , display equations in multiline input might appear on an incorrect line.
Workaround: Here are possible workarounds for some of the issues above.To update the font size, set?FontSize after creating the label or in the startupFcn of App Designer. The value of FontSize?must change for this workaround to have an effect. For example, the code below would not work if the font size was already 25.>> drawnow>> app.Label.FontSize = 25;If the label in App Designer disappears, resize the label in the design view.To place LaTeX display equations on separate lines, specify Text as a cell array of character vectors or a string array, with each display equation as a separate element. For example:>> l = uilabel(uifigure,'Interpreter','latex','Position',[100 100 100 70]);?>>?l.Text = ["Inline: $f(x)= x^2$" "Display:" "$$f(x)= \frac{1}{x}$$"];

Summary: Configuration reference in a data dictionary might cause MATLAB to crash
Description: MATLAB might crash when a data dictionary has a configuration reference object that points to a configuration object in the data dictionary, and the configuration reference object is updated to point to a different configuration object in the data dictionary.

Summary: Internal error during HDL code generation from subsystem containing DocBlock in Native Floating Point mode
Description: An internal error is thrown during HDL code generation when following conditions are met: HDL Block Property Architecture    of the Subsystem is set to BlackBox  DocBlock ? is present inside the Subsystem with  Floating-Poin t input ports  Floating Point IP Library ?is set to?Native Floating Point   The error message is:? Native-floating point code generation can not complete for the following reason(s): 'Signal rate of value 0.000000 found. It may be caused by unconnected input ports' ?

Summary: Satisfied - No Test Case?reported for all the model coverage objectives, even when analysis runs for a longer time
Description: Test generation analysis reports the status of all the model coverage objectives as?Satisfied - No Test Case  when the below conditions are TRUE:The design model is driven by at least one root level in-port The number of model coverage objectives is in thousands  In some cases, the test generation may report the objective status as satisfied  without producing test cases.  The objective status remains unchanged even if the analysis timeout increases.
Workaround: Set the?Maximum test case steps parameter in Test Generation options to few steps - (say 5) and repeat the analysis.

Summary: Reset function in model reference generated code has incorrect signature
Description: Code generation might generate an incorrect signature for a reset function if all the following conditions exist: "Configure arguments for Step function prototype" is selected Model has a Reset Function block Model is built as a model reference target

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect Code Generation:?GPU Coder generated code might produce incorrect output in the presence of gpucoder.reduce with large inputs
Description: GPU Coder generated code produces incorrect output when the following conditions are met: The design function contains gpucoder.reduce.?  The number of elements in the input array to gpucoder.reduce  is greater than 67107841 (?numel(inputArray) > 67107841 ).
Fixed In: R2020a Update 6, R2020b Update 3, R2021a Update 1, R2021b

Summary: JMAAB check for jc_0531?does not comply with its JMAAB 5.1 modeling guideline??
Description: Model Advisor?check?Check default transition placement in Stateflow charts? (id:?mathworks.jmaab.jc_0531)? does not support sub-id selection configuration as per?JMAAB 5.1 modeling guideline??.

Summary: dlquantizer reports incorrect average precision output for yolov2ObjectDetector and ssdObjectDetector
Description: When quantizing a yolov2ObjectDetector  or ssdObjectDetector , the Deep Network Quantizer app and the dlquantizer  object report an incorrect average precision for both the floating-point and the quantized networks during the validate stage of the quantization workflow.

Summary: CurrentPoint?value is incorrect?for horizontally scrollable figures
Description: The value of the CurrentPoint ?property scales incorrectly in scrollable figures created using the uifigure  function. The value is correct when scrolled all the way to the left, but is incorrect after scrolling horizontally. For example, this code creates a horizontally scrollable figure that displays the location of the mouse pointer. If you scroll to the right and mouse over the figure, the displayed values are incorrect. >> f = uifigure;  >> pnl = uipanel(f,'Position',[1000 100 100 100]);  >> f.Scrollable = 'on';  >> f.WindowButtonMotionFcn = @(src,event)disp(src.CurrentPoint);

Summary: Video formats might be missing when using webcam devices with linuxvideo?adaptor
Description: When using the linuxvideo   adaptor in any of the latest Linux distributions, a detected webcam device might return fewer or no supported video formats available for acquisition. All available video formats appear when using the same device in MATLAB on older Linux distributions. The issue occurs in Linux distributions with GStreamer version 1.14 and later because GStreamer by default disables the libv4l2 ? library that supports format conversions.
Workaround: To work around this issue, use the following steps to enable the?libv4l2?library and start MATLAB from the command line.1) Open the Terminal application.?2) Set the environment variable that enables the libv4l2 library:export GST_V4L2_USE_LIBV4L2=1?3) Navigate to the <MATLAB>/bin folder in Terminal.4) Start MATLAB using the following command:./matlabThis allows the?linuxvideo?adaptor to list all video formats in the supported formats list when using the webcam device.

Summary: Assertion error when generating code for MATLAB function when variable sized matrix is assigned a scalar value
Description: MATLAB might display an assertion error message when you generate code for a MATLAB function that meets all of the following conditions: The left-hand side of an assignment contains a variable sized matrix The right-hand side of the above assignment evaluates to a scalar value and a full write of the variable sized matrix is performed using this scalar value  Assertion at dom_core/analysis/range/LocalAnalysisImpl.cpp line 2098: In rangeOfElementwiseCall: Assertion failed: matInput != nullptr
Fixed In: R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b, R2021b Update 3

Summary: Mismatch between total conv module latency and sum of layer latencies under the conv module
Description: While retrieving prediction results from the deployed network, when you turn on the profiler, you might see a mismatch between the total conv module latency and the sum of the individual layer latencies under the conv module. For example, you might see conv_module latency = 47574 cycles when the sum of individual layer latencies under the conv module is (10068+6977+11357+5473+13767), which equals 47642 cycles. This mismatch between the displayed value and value calculated by hand might cause confusion. This mismatch does not affect the functionality and the prediction results.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect answers in rapid accelerator mode when Stateflow charts call multi-instance model blocks in export-function mode
Description: Incorrect results are possible in rapid simulation mode fora model that meets the following conditions: The model contains two or more identical Stateflow charts. The Stateflow charts call multi-instance Model blocks.  The multi-instance Model blocks are in export-function mode.  For the above scenario, incorrect answers might occur at the model block outputs.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 3, R2021b

Summary: Test generation shows objectives status as?Undecided with Testcases for certain models with Block Reduction off?
Description: Certain Simulink models can be optimized further with?Simulation Target->Block Reduction ?parameter is off. For these models, SLDV test generation shows objective status as?Undecided with Testcases.
Fixed In: R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 7, R2021b, R2022a

Summary: 'Cannot be child'?error message is unreadable in some languages
Description: If you execute a command that assigns an object to an invalid parent, MATLAB might return an error message that is unreadable. For example, create a Line  object with the plot  function. Then pass the Line  object to the imagesc  function. p = plot(1:10);imagesc(p,rand(5,5));  MATLAB should return this message: Image cannot be a child of Line.  In some languages, this message is incorrectly translated as unreadable text. For example, the message in the preceding example looks like this in Chinese:?? Ą¨Ąé©`: image Image ?? Line ??q????Çń??????????B?

Summary: In some locales, the Deep Learning HDL Toolbox errors on compile
Description: In some locales, when you try to compile with Deep Learning HDL Toolbox, you see an error message of the form: Error using dnnfpga. tool. Text/ concatToLine The text only has 23 lines.  Error in dnnfpga. apis. Workflow/ displayCompileStartBanner  Error in dnnfpga. apis. Workflow/ compile   The issue can occur with European and Japanese locales.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Using non-fixed-point constant literals in fixed-point division in Stateflow charts might cause incorrect HDL code generation
Description: Suppose that your Stateflow chart contains fixed-point division operation like: varA = input / 2;  If both?varA ? and input ?are? fixed-point numbers, then the HDL code generated from this chart might be incorrect.?
Workaround: Specify the fixed-point type of the constant literal by using this code pattern:varA = input / fi(2, s, w, f)? ?% s == signedness, w == wordlength, f == fractionlength?

Summary: Unexpected error encountered when using multiple frame input in predict function of dlhdl.Workflow object
Description: When providing more than 1 frame input to the predict() function of dlhdl.Workflow workflow, the predict() function errors out. For example: >> hW = dlhdl.Workflow('Network',net,'Bitstream','zcu102_single.bit','Target',hTarget); >> hW.predict(img) when img contain 15 frame of input data  Error message:? Error using dnnfpga.apis.Workflow/predictOnNetwork  Input frame number 15 exceeds the limit. To fix the problem, provide an input frame number that is less than or equal to 1. Or you can re-compile the network with a larger InputFrameNumberLimit property value in the input argument, for example hW.compile('InputFrameNumberLimit', 50).?

Summary: Switching the active variant in variant subsystem that contains a Stateflow library chart might cause an error during upgrade diagram
Description: When your Simulink model uses a variant subsystem and its variants are Stateflow library charts, Simulink might throw an?Invalid object id.  error when you try to switch the active variant. This error occurs if the model is compiled more than once and the active variant is switched between compilations, while the inactive library is unloaded and its path is removed.  You might also see this assertion:? failed assertion in D:\sf4\matlab\toolbox\stateflow\src\stateflow\fsm\fsm.cpp at line 380:? id != 0
Workaround: Keep the inactive library loaded.?

Summary: Changing a bus datatype in a referenced dictionary from the Model Explorer does not update the Interface Editor table for that interface
Description: The interface editor does not reflect changes made to a Simulink.Bus  object in the Model Explorer.  This might happen in the following situation:  1. The interface editor is open for a model linked to a SLDD (e.g. primaryDD.sldd).  2. That SLDD is referencing another SLDD (e.g. refDD.sldd).  3. In the Model Explorer you modify a Simulink.Bus that is defined in the referenced dictionary (e.g. refDD.sldd)
Workaround: Close and reopen MATLAB. After reopening MATLAB and the System Composer model, the changes to the reference dictionary are reflected in the Interface Editor.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Variant Subsystem in a triggered system with initial condition may cause simulation and software-in-the-loop results mismatch
Description: The results of the simulation of a model in the normal, and software-in-the-loop modes does not match when Variant Subsystem is present in a triggered subsystem  When you create a model with a triggered subsystem which contains a variant subsystem connected to an output port bearing non zero initial condition, the results of the simulation in the normal, and software-in-the-loop modes do not match if any of the following conditions are met: The Variant Subsystem has no active choices and Allow Zero Variant Controls  is selected. The input signal of the active choice of the Variant Subsystem is not connected to the output port. The active choice of the Variant Subsystem has a direct connection but there is another triggered subsystem in the path which has an output port inside bearing a non zero initial condition. For example, a triggered subsystem triggered by a Pulse Generator.
Workaround: Insert a signal conversion block between the Variant Subsystem and the output port bearing initial condition.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Missing zero protection logic in the MATLAB to HDL workflow may result in simulation mismatch or?out of bounds indexing errors
Description: While generating HDL from MATLAB function, missing zero protection logic may permit zero initial values to propagate through the generated design unchecked. In certain cases, this can result in a simulation mismatch or out of bounds indexing error during simulation of the generated code.
Workaround: Turn off distributed pipelining to prevent the insertion of pipeline registers which introduce zero initial values in the design.? Distributed pipelining can be disabled through the HDL Coder code generation configuration object:>> hdlcfg = coder.config('hdl')?>> hdlcfg.DistributedPipelining = false;

Summary: The find_mdlrefs?function fails to find the active variant block with variant filters post model compilation
Description: When a model is either in the Accelerator  or Rapid Accelerator  modes, the find_mdlrefs  function fails to identify the active model with variant filters blocks post-compilation. Example:? The code below is expected to return only active model blocks, however inactive model blocks will also be noticed.  load_system(modelName);?  % load top model with model reference hierarchy set to accelerator mode set_param(modelName,'SimulationCommand','update');?  [models,blocks] = find_mdlrefs(modelName,'MatchFilter',@Simulink.match.activeVariants);   The code below is expected to return only code active model blocks, however inactive model blocks will also be noticed.  load_system(modelName); ? % load top model with model reference hierarchy set to accelerator mode modelName([],[],[],'compileForCodegen');?  modelName([],[],[],'term');?  [models,blocks] = find_mdlrefs(modelName, 'MatchFilter',@Simulink.match.codeCompileVariants);?

Summary: RoadRunner Asset Library props exported to OpenSceneGraph are missing textures
Description: When props that require a RoadRunner Asset Library license (assets ending with "_rrx") are exported to OpenSceneGraph, the exported model is missing textures if they are embedded.

Summary: Numerical difference between Simulink and HDL Coder in Sqrt block for certain input values and datatypes.
Description: Numerical difference between Simulink and HDL Coder may occur during code generation for these conditions: 1. Sqrt block 'Function' parameter set to 'Sqrt' 2. Input datatype is a fixed-point datatype with non-zero fraction length? 3. Sqrt block 'Output data type' set to an integer datatype? 4. 'Intermediate results' data type is set to 'Inherit: Inherit via internal rule'

Summary: MATLAB may crash or take long time during HDL code generation from a subsystem with a large matrix multiplication
Description: When generating HDL code for subsystems with large matrix multiplications, MATLAB uses up a lot of memory causing long code generation time or crashes due to out-of-memory issue.?Depending on the matrix types and hardware, code generation can take upwards of 4-5 hours and consume all the memory on a 16G RAM machine?for 200x200 times 200x1 matrix multiplication.
Workaround: Please see attached model for a possible workaround. Use the MATLAB Function Block in the attached model instead of built-in Matrix Multiply Block in Simulink. This MATLAB Function Block contains a script that will generate a more memory efficient implementation for HDL code generation.

Summary: Sporadic crash might occur when running Simulink model with Sequence Viewer block and Model block in Accelerator mode
Description: Sporadic crash might occur when running a Simulink model that contains Sequence Viewer blocks and Model blocks?in Accelerator  mode.
Workaround: Run the model with Sequence Viewer block and Model block in normal mode.

Summary: batchsim is not able to find models or files in referenced projects
Description: When using batchsim  on a non-local cluster, models or files that are in referenced projects are not transferred to the remote cluster.? This may cause the simulations to error with a message similar to: Error: No system or file called 'referencedModel' found.
Workaround: Manually attach the required files using the AttachedFiles option with batchsim.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: HDL code generated for integer division by a constant power of two in MATLAB code might be incorrect
Description: HDL Code generated for this MATLAB code pattern might be incorrect: out1 = int8(in1) / 2;?  out2 = uint32(in2) / 4;?  For code that performs integer division by a constant power of two, HDL Coder generates a bit shift operation that rounds towards zero. However, integers in MATLAB have round-to-nearest behavior. This difference might cause the generated HDL code to produce incorrect answers.

Summary: PLL models result in error in Accelerator mode.
Description: When running PLL models in Accelerator mode, it results in a sporadic error. This is true for both PLL models provided under reference architecture and PLL models created using basic building blocks in the Mixed-Signal Blockset?.The error is caused because the decrease in the propagation delay in Variable Pulse Delay block under VCO in the PLL model is more than the increase in the?variable step discrete input sample time, causing the output sample times to occur out of sequence.

Summary: Multicycle path constraint file may not be generated when clock rate pipelining is requested on a model
Description: Multicycle path constraints are extracted from the Simulink model. Multicycle path constraints identify paths between clocked elements driven by the same clock enable. To use multicycle path constraints for certain parts in your design, it is important to retain the multicycle paths in that region from being altered by clock rate pipelining. In certain cases, with clock rate pipelining on, the Multicycle path constraints are not generated.

Summary: Incorrect defect?Unmodified variable not const-qualified?on reference variable
Description: In C++, if you take a reference to a variable and then use the reference to modify the variable, Bug Finder incorrectly shows results for the defect checker?Unmodified variable not const-qualified.  For instance, in this example, the checker incorrectly flags the variables var  and?ref  even though they are modified later:     int var = 0;     int& ref { var };     ref = 34; The variables cannot be marked const  as the checker suggests.

Summary: Graphics flicker when hovering mouse over the figure
Description: Figure content might flicker as you hover the cursor over the figure while the content is being updated. For example, if you display video frames in a loop as you hover the cursor over the figure, the content might flicker.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Stateflow charts with nonzero initial values for outputs and containing input events might generate incorrect HDL code
Description: The HDL code generated from a Stateflow chart might be incorrect if both these conditions are true: The chart contains input events ?The initial values specified for the outputs of the chart are not equal to zero

Summary: Error?Unable to launch the MATLABWindow application on?Red Hat based distributions
Description: This issue is applicable to the following Linux distributions:  Red Hat Enterprise Linux 8.2 and later CentOS 8.2 and later Fedora 32 and later  The following error can be observed while installing MATLAB or while running certain features in MATLAB (such as Simulink Start Page, Help Browser, Live Editor, UIFigure, App Designer, Simulink Data Inspector, etc.): terminate called after throwing an instance of 'std::runtime_error'??  ????? what():? Unable to launch the MATLABWindow application ??? Aborted    You might also see: MATLABWindow application failed to launch. Unable to launch the MATLABWindow application.  Or if you enter this command in MATLAB: system(fullfile(matlabroot, '/bin/glnxa64/MATLABWindow'))   MATLAB displays: ./MATLABWindow: symbol lookup error: /lib64/libk5crypto.so.3: undefined symbol: EVP_KDF_ctrl, version OPENSSL_1_1_1b
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 1, R2021b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Simulation of referenced model block having a sample time specification of [Inf Inf] in rapid acceleration mode returns incorrect results
Description: A Simulink model will produce an incorrect results if it satisfies all the following conditions:  1. The model is running in rapid acceleration mode  2. The model has a referenced model that has a block output port with the sample time specification [inf inf] and directly connected to the root outport block. ?Example blocks could be Width, probe? an SFunction that can register the [inf inf] sample time specification at their respectively output ports.? No error is reported for this modeling pattern during model compilation. Usually, the signal value at the corresponding output port on the model block is zero at all time steps.

Summary: Harness created with slvnvmakeharness(model) function throws error during simulation when model has partitions
Description: The harness model simulation for?periodic? partitions reports an error message when the below is true: The name Test Unit   of the model block is not a valid C identifier. The Test Unit   model block has incorrect setting for ScheduleRates  and ScheduleRatesWith  block parameters.    Error message:? Model blocks which reference models with explicit partitions must schedule rates in block <Test Unit>
Workaround: When the model has periodic?partitions, then apply the following workaround:Set the name Test Unit of model block to a valid C identifier e.g. Test_Unit.For Test Unit model block, set the parameter ScheduleRates to On?and ScheduleRatesWith to Schedule Editor.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generated for certain sparse assignment operations might produce incorrect answers
Description: Code generated for the statement?S(:,X) = R ?in your MATLAB code might produce?incorrect answers if all ?of these conditions are true: ? S is a sparse row vector R is a row vector (either sparse or full) X is a valid column index for S

Summary: HDL blocks with 'inf' sample time may result in delay balancing failure.
Description: HDL blocks with 'inf' sample time may result in delay balancing failure with the following error message: ? ? ? Delay balancing unsuccessful because Signal rate of inf found.?  There is no way to change this sample time manually.? Below is the list of blocks that may affect delay balancing:  Compare to constant  Compare to zero?  Interval Test  Detect Rise Positive  Detect Rise Negative  Detect Fall Negative  Detect Fall Non-positive  Counter Free-Running  Counter Limited  HDL Counter  Wrap To Zero  Decrement Real World  Decrement Stored Integer  Increment Real World  Increment Stored Integer  Multiply-accumulate  Serializer 1d  Deserializer 1d  Single Port RAM  Dual Rate Dual-Port RAM  HDL FIFO
Workaround: Consider replacing the blocks from the above list with alternate blocks which have the same functionality. For example, replace 'compare to constant' and 'compare to zero' blocks with 'switch' block.?

Summary: Simulation of newly generated tests fail in Simulink Test Manager for export function models
Description: When Add Tests for Missing coverage  is invoked in an export function model, the newly generated tests by Simulink Design Verifier throws runtime error during coverage aggregation,?Example error message:? An error occurred ('Simulink:Logging:InvInputLoadNameList') when calling 'sim':  The number of external inputs must be equal to the number of root-level input port blocks. Root-level input port blocks include Enable blocks and Trigger blocks with 'Trigger type' set to rising, falling, or either. The model contains 3 root-level input ports, but 2 external inputs were specified in Configuration Parameters -> Data Import/Export -> Input.?
Workaround: Export function models have at least one inport with a signal attribute Output function call as on. Consider creating a MATLAB scheduler harness for an export function model first and then invoke Add Tests for Missing Coverage workflow on the harness.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Distributed Pipelining adds invalid registers when a model contains non-constant source blocks
Description: When applying Distributed Pipelining to a design with non-constant source blocks, such as a counter, invalid registers can be moved to the output of the block. The invalid registers break functional equivalence to the original model.  Using optimizations like Sharing or Streaming in conjunction with Clock Rate Pipelining will also introduce counters in the design which could be susceptible to this issue.  Checking simulation of the Validation Model can expose whether or not a design experiences this bug.

Summary: Only the first model will use fast restart when executing different models using Simulink Test Manager
Description: In Simulink Test, if multiple test cases are executed at once which have different system under tests, then only the first one is executed in fast restart mode.

Summary: Running a compiled app that loads a trained model exported by Classification Learner or Regression Learner generates multiple warning messages
Description: A compiled application displays multiple warning messages when it is generated using the following workflow in Classification Learner or Regression Learner: Export a trained model to the MATLAB workspace. Save the trained model structure in a MAT-file.  save mymodel trainedModel    Write the code to be compiled. This code must load the trained model and use it to make a prediction. It must also have a pragma, so the compiler recognizes that Statistics and Machine Learning Toolbox code is needed in the compiled application. This pragma can be any model training function used in the app (for example, fitctree ).  function ypred = mypredict(tbl)%#function fitctreeload('mymodel.mat');ypred = trainedModel.predictFcn(tbl);end   Compile the code as a standalone application using MATLAB Compiler.  mcc -m mypredict.m    The warning messages typically state that files are "not in the application's expanded CTF archive" and refer to different files in the following?folder: matlab/toolbox/stats/mlearnapp/+mlearnapp/+internal/  for R2020b or earlier releases matlab/toolbox/stats/mlearnapps/+mlearnapp/+internal/  for R2021a release  The compiled application still behaves as expected, despite these additional warning messages.

Summary: False positive violations of MISSING_CONSTEXPR performance defect checker
Description: BugFinder MISSING_CONSTEXPR  performance defects are improperly raised in cases where adding the constexpr  specifier to a variable definition does not provide any performance gain. For instance: const int value = 42; // False positive violation of MISSING_CONSTEXPR

Summary: Sampling internal nodes of PLL model makes the model?unstable and runs extremely slow
Description: If you sample one of the internal nodes of a PLL model, for example, the loop filter output, using a Zero-Order Hold block, the PLL model becomes unstable. The model slows down considerably. This behavior is not dependent on the sample time chosen on the Zero-Order Hold block.

Summary: Arithmetic operators? with half precision floating point types generates incorrect HDL code when input/output numbers falls under denormal range.
Description: The generated HDL code for a Simulink model might be incorrect when the following settings are applied: The model contains arithmetic operators The inputs are of half  precision floating point values under denormal  range  The incorrect HDL code can result in simulation failures in ModelSim when verifying the generated code.
Workaround: Enable the following option from configuration parametersConfiguration Parameters --> HDL Code Generation -->? Floating Point --> Enable 'Handle Denormals' option

Summary: Inefficient code generation for models with Clock Rate Pipelining and?SqrtNewton architectures
Description: When a Sqrt block with?SqrtNewton ?architecture is present in the slow rate of a multirate model that has Clock Rate Pipelining enabled, HDL Code generation is successful, but the code is inefficient. Only the Sqrt logic will run at the base clock rate, and the fast regions introduced by Clock Rate Pipelining will run three times slower than the base clock rate rather than at the clock rate.
Workaround: Change the Architecture settings of Sqrt block to a single rate architecture within the HDL Block Properties.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Accumulation operations modeled in Simulink using feedback loops might cause HDL mismatch due to latency introduced by optimizations upstream
Description: HDL code generation for a Simulink model might result in a numeric mismatch if the following conditions are met: Operations in a Simulink model involve performing arithmetic using a prior value from a feedback loop,? Latency is introduced from the upstream logic during code generation due to optimizations such as pipeline insertion, and resource sharing/streaming  HDL Coder does not properly schedule or reset this accumulation due to the latency introduced upstream. In the generated model, the feedback loop executes on the introduced cycles of latency, which can result in a numeric mismatch. Note that there are two similar situations to this bug report which are not addressed with this fix. Review the two bug reports below for further information. https://www.mathworks.com/support/bugreports/2439242  https://www.mathworks.com/support/bugreports/2433888
Workaround: The mismatch can be resolved by introducing an enable logic on the Delay block. Either add an Enable port on the Delay block, or use an Enabled Subsystem for other blocks with state that do not support enable ports. If the boolean signal driving the Enable is routed up to the DUT, the delay balancing optimization inserts the appropriate matching latency to align with the latency introduced by the other optimization settings. This signal can be treated as a valid input signal, and allows the appropriate timing for the accumulation operation.

Summary: Accessibility Issue: Screen readers do not announce new figure creation in MATLAB Online
Description: Screen readers do not announce when new figures are created in MATLAB Online. For example, executing code such as plot(1:10) ?that creates a new figure at the command line does not cause the screen reader to use aria-live to announce the new figure.
Workaround: For our best accessibility support, use?MATLAB Online. MathWorks is committed to improving the accessibility of its software products, focusing first on the accessibility of MATLAB Online. See the?Accessibility Statement for MATLAB?site for the latest information on accessible workflow support.

Summary: readcell might error when a spreadsheet file contains dates on machines with a non-English locale
Description: On non-English locales, readcell  cannot read in dates as datetime elements from spreadsheets. For example: c = readcell(filename);  Could not recognize the date/time format of '01-dic-2020'. You can specify a format using the 'InputFormat' parameter. If the date/time text contains day, month, or time zone names in a language foreign to the 'en_US' locale, those might not be recognized. You can specify a different locale using the 'Locale' parameter.   ?
Workaround: To workaround this issue, call readcell with 'DatetimeType','text'. For example:c = readcell(filename,'DatetimeType','text');Then you can get a logical array indicating which elements in the cell array are datetimes using the getDatetimeElements function defined below.isdate = getDatetimeElements(c, locale);where getDatetimeElements is defined as: function isdate = getDatetimeElements(c, locale)   % Returns a logical matrix representing which elements in the cell array c   % can be converted into datetime variables.    isdate = true(size(c));      for i = 1:numel(c)         try             datetime(c(i), "Locale", locale);        catch            isdate(i) = false;        end     endendWhen you have the logical vector isdate indicting which elements are datetimes, you can convert these elements into datetime variables with the following command:c(isdate) = cellfun(@(text) datetime(text, "Locale", locale), c(isdate), 'UniformOutput', false);?

Summary: Model Advisor checks do not report Reserved Keywords
Description: Model Advisor checks checking for Reserved  Keywords do not report C Reserved  Keywords,?C++ Reserved  Keywords, Reserved  Keywords for Code Generation but reports only MATLAB  keywords.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Conditionally executed subsystem blocks that propagate variable-size signals, when enabled might return incorrect numerical results
Description: Normal, accelerator and rapid accelerator simulation might cause incorrect output values for models that meet all these conditions: The model contains a conditionally executed subsystem block. The model propagates variable-size signals into the conditionally executed subsystem block  The conditionally executed subsystem block Propagate sizes of variable-size signals  is set to Only when execution is resumed  for an Action Port.  Only when enabling  for Enable and Trigger or Function-Call Port.

Summary: Code Generation Advisor does not run on a model that has a Polyspace options object in its configset
Description: You cannot run Code Generation Advisor on a model if a Polyspace options object is attached to its configset.? The process exits with this message: Abnormal exit: Dot indexing is not supported for variables of this type.

Summary: When starting a MATLAB process on Windows via certain external interfaces, the MATLAB process may stall or hang.
Description: When using MATLAB C++, Java, or Python engine APIs to start a MATLAB process, the process might stall or hang. The stall or hang might appear as a timeout while trying to find graphics devices during MATLAB graphics initialization.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 2, R2022a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Native floating-point with hierarchical clock-rate pipelining might cause validation model mismatch
Description: The clock-rate pipelining optimization has the ability to work across hierarchical boundaries. For more information, see?Clock-Rate Pipelining and Hierarchy Flattening .? When this optimization is combined with floating-point libraries, the result of delay balancing on the design might be incorrect.?
Workaround: Hierarchical clock-rate pipelining is not applied if no hierarchy exists. For the parts of the design that are affected by this issue, apply FlattenHierarchy on the subsystem to avoid the mismatch. To perform this action, run this command:hdlset_param(<path_to_subsystem>, 'FlattenHierarchy', 'on')?To disable hierarchical clock-rate pipelining for the entire model, run the following command. Doing so might cause clock-rate pipelining results to be less optimal.hdlset_param(<model_name>, 'CRPWithoutFlattening', 'off')

Summary: Signal selection changes in bus selector blocks might occur when reordering or deleting outputs of Stateflow charts or MATLAB Function blocks
Description: When the output port of a Stateflow chart or a MATLAB function block is deleted or reordered, bus selector blocks which are connected to these outputs might change their signal selection. For example, consider a Stateflow chart which has two outputs "out1" and "out2". These outputs are connected to a bus creator block which combines them into a single bus. There is a bus selector block connected to the output of this bus creator which then extracts the signal "out1" from this bus. If the port numbers of the outputs in the chart are changed, then the signal selection of the bus selector block might change from "out1" to "out2".?

Summary: MATLAB crashes when using a DBC-file that contains inconsistencies
Description: When trying to open a DBC-file that contains inconsistencies, MATLAB might crash without generating any crash log. The impacted workflows include:? Calling the function canDatabase  with such a DBC-file. Specifying such a DBC-file to use with the following blocks: CAN Pack, CAN Unpack, CAN FD Pack, CAN FD Unpack, J1939 Network Configuration.
Workaround: If accessing a DBC-file causes MATLAB to crash, you can use Vector CANdb++ to perform a consistency check for the file in question. Fixing the reported inconsistencies should prevent the crash.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 3, R2022a, R2022b

Summary: Crash when using Java Access Bridge on Windows
Description: MATLAB might crash during startup when other applications, such as screen readers, are using the Java Access Bridge.?The first line of the generated stack trace references: Java_com_sun_java_accessibility_AccessBridge_popupMenuWillBecomeVisible
Workaround: Choose one of the following?possible workarounds:If you do not need the Desktop or Java, run:???????? matlab -nojvmDisable Java Access Bridge before starting MATLAB, and re-enable it after startup. The command to disable Java Access Bridge is:???????? jabswitch /disableRemove the following files from the MATLAB installation directory. Error messages are displayed at startup, but no crash dialog appears. MATLAB continues to run normally.sys\java\jre\win64\jre\bin\JavaAccessBridge-64sys\java\jre\win64\jre\bin\WindowsAccessBridge-64.dll

Summary: Saving graphical content produces very large vector graphics files
Description: Starting in R2014b, print , saveas , and other features that generate vector graphics files produce output that is larger than in previous releases.?Vector formats include PostScript (.ps), Encapsulated PostScript (.eps), PDF, Windows Metafile (.emf), and Scalable Vector Graphics (SVG).
Workaround: 1) On some systems, you can use the the OpenGL renderer to create smaller files. To use the OpenGL renderer, specify the '-opengl' option when calling the print function:print(fig, '-opengl', '-depsc', 'outfile.eps')Alternatively, set the Renderer property of the figure to 'opengl' before generating the output:set(fig, 'Renderer', 'opengl')?2) Another workaround is to try the R2019a MATLAB release which addresses most of the file size issues when the figure has a large number of filled polygon graphics objects.
